question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Cut high purity copper foil into 4x4 cm pieces
2. Draw a line 0.5 cm from one edge of each square foil
3. Flatten each foil using a cylindrical roller on a clean surface
4. Clean copper foils by bath sonication in acetone for 15 minutes
5. Clean copper foils by bath sonication in isopropanol for 15 minutes
6. Dry copper foils in a glass dish
7. Position substrate on center of copper electrode using tape
8. Rinse substrate electrode assembly with water for one minute
9. Rinse substrate electrode assembly with isopropanol
10. Rinse substrate electrode assembly again with water
11. Attach bare copper electrode to anode
12. Attach substrate electrode assembly to cathode
13. Place both electrodes in 100 milliliter glass beaker
14. Adjust electrode separation to one centimeter
15. Add 31.6 grams of zinc hexahydrate solution
16. Add 31.6 grams of zinc hexahydrate solution and 35 grams of benzimidazole solution to a 100 milliliter beaker
17. Stir mixture for 30 seconds at room temperature
18. Transfer precursor sol to beaker containing electrodes
19. Immerse both electrodes up to 3.5 centimeter mark
20. Adjust height of beaker to maintain electrode immersion depth
21. Carry out electrophoretic deposition
22. Carry out electrophoretic deposition at 1 volt for 4 minutes by turning on the power source
23. Lower the beaker slowly at the end of deposition to avoid disturbing weak adhesion between freshly deposited nuclei and substrate
24. Mix 31.6 grams of zinc hexahydrate solution and 35 grams of 2-Methylimidazole solution in a 100 milliliter beaker
25. Place microscopic glass slide with substrate vertically in precursor solution
26. Leave substrate undisturbed for 10 hours at 30 degrees Celsius
27. Thoroughly mix equal proportions of epoxy and hardener
28. Leave mixture for one hour
29. Place ZIF-8 membrane on 24mm steel disc with 5mm diameter center hole
30. Apply epoxy along substrate edges and cover substrate while exposing 5mm center hole
31. Allow epoxy to dry overnight
32. Scan membrane and reference scale using stereomicroscope
33. Calculate exposed membrane area from scanned image using graphical software
34. Place steel disc with membrane in stainless steel permeation cell
35. Place permeation cell in oven
36. Place Viton O-rings above and below steel disk
37. Fasten screws to ensure leak-tight fit
38. Set gas flow rates to 30 milliliters per minute on feed and sweep sides
39. Heat membrane cell at 130 degrees Celsius for two hours using hydrogen feed gas and argon sweep gas
40. Maintain 0.1 megapascal pressure at feed and sweep sides by adjusting needle valves on retentate and permeate sides
41. Change feed side gas flow to target gas
42. Set gas flow rate to 30 milliliters per minute
43. Set oven temperature to desired temperature
44. Calculate permeance in Excel using mass spectrum data after steady state establishment

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Set differential pressure to 4 Pascal (30 mTorr) via feedback system
2. Turn on ozone generator by passing dry air through ultraviolet lamp
3. Set ozone flow rate to 0.1 standard liter per minute
4. Set relative humidity of bag to designated values
5. Set chamber temperature to 25 ± 0.1°C
6. Connect instrument inlets to environmental chamber
7. Start self-developed software by clicking Start button
8. Check real-time data on self-developed software with feedback control
9. Dissolve ammonium sulfate in high purity water using 100 milliliter volumetric glass
10. Atomize ammonium sulfate solution at 3 standard liters per minute flow rate
11. Pass aerosol flow through diffusion drier to achieve 10% relative humidity
12. Pass dried aerosol through bipolar charger and differential mobility analyzer for electric mobility-based size selection
13. Withdraw 1 milliliter of isoprene solution using syringe
14. Rinse syringe three times with isoprene solution prior to final withdrawal
15. Place syringe into syringe injector
16. Insert syringe needle tip through rubber seal into round bottomed flask
17. Preheat flask to 90 ± 1°C using heating tape
18. Turn on syringe injection and set to appropriate value
19. Introduce flow of 2 standard liters per minute of purified air
20. Start aerosol measurement software
21. Create new file by clicking 'Create a New File'
22. Click 'Okay' button to record particle number diameter distributions exiting the bag
23. Measure aerosol flow using high-resolution time-of-flight aerosol mass spectrometer
24. Start data acquisition software by pressing 'Acquire' button on lower left panel
25. Open sampling valve of PTFE Teflon tube inside bag
26. Start data acquisition using software: Access Acquisition menu in top deck and press Start
27. Record time series of each ion through software
28. Stop injection of gas phase precursors
29. Stop injection of aerosol seed particles
30. Continuously inject pure air at 40 liters per minute into bag
31. Turn on all ultraviolet lights
32. Set ozone concentration to 600 parts per billion
33. Set temperature to 40 degrees Celsius

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Cut hole in bottom of one-ounce cup
2. Glue screen over hole for air exchange
3. Aspirate insect colony into 15 milliliter conical tube
4. Chill insects on ice briefly
5. Identify female insects by presence of ovipositor on ventral abdomen
6. Transfer identified females into prepared cup
7. Seal cup with 5x5 cm paraffin wax film after collecting 15 females
8. Apply 400 microliters of 10% sucrose solution to top of film
9. Place second 5x5 cm paraffin wax film over sucrose solution
10. Place adult chamber on egg collection dish with plastic paraffin wax film contacting oviposition medium
11. Wrap entire egg-laying chamber with plastic wrap while avoiding air holes
12. Place chamber at 25°C with 70% humidity and 14-hour light/10-hour dark cycle
13. Apply 1x15 mm double-sided tape strip onto 22x30 mm cover slip
14. Place cover slip onto oviposition medium of egg-laying chamber
15. Transfer individual semi-transparent embryos from agar surface to double-sided tape using fine brush under dissecting microscope
16. Arrange approximately 25 banana-shaped embryos laterally on tape with larger ends adhered
17. Place cover slip onto 100 × 15 ml Petri dish containing 1% agar
18. Position setup under dissecting microscope inside humidified hood
19. Place tip of quartz injection needle in water drop
20. Initiate injection cycle for pressure calibration
21. Insert needle into larger end of embryo approaching from left side of cover slip
22. Deliver injection solution into egg
23. Withdraw needle from embryo
24. Transfer cover slip to new 1% agar dish surface
25. Place dish into humidity chamber
26. Transfer surviving embryos using fine brush to 35x10mm Petri dish containing water-moistened filter paper
27. Seal Petri dish with plastic paraffin wax film
28. Place sealed Petri dish in 25°C incubator
29. Transfer emergent first instar nymphs using fine brush to Petri dish containing leaf clippings
30. Seal nymph dish with plastic paraffin wax film
31. Transfer two-day-old nymphs to rearing cage with corn plants using fine brush
32. Separately cage insects with visible phenotype for target trait recovery
33. Rear insects under controlled temperature/humidity with regular transfers to fresh corn plants
34. Screen progeny regularly for expected phenotypes
35. Place phenotype-positive individuals into separate cages to establish homozygous lines

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Inoculate S. cerevisiae cells
2. Grow S. cerevisiae overnight at 30°C with 150 RPM agitation
3. Measure optical density at 600 nm (OD600)
4. Dilute S. cerevisiae culture to OD600 0.1 in 100 ml YPD medium
5. Grow S. cerevisiae until OD600 reaches 0.8
6. Measure OD600 of S. pombe overnight culture
7. Dilute S. pombe culture to OD600 0.1 in 500 ml YAS medium and grow until OD600 reaches 0.8
8. Prepare fresh solution of 2M four-thiouracil
9. Keep prepared solution at room temperature
10. Protect solution from light
11. Add four-thiouracil solution to S. cerevisiae and S. pombe cultures to achieve 5 mM final concentration
12. Incubate S. cerevisiae culture at 30°C with constant agitation
13. Incubate S. pombe culture at 32°C with constant agitation
14. Maintain incubation duration of 6 minutes
15. Remove small aliquot from each culture
16. Count cells using automatic cell counter or Neubauer chamber
17. Centrifuge at 2,500 × g and 4°C for 5 minutes to collect cells
18. Discard supernatant
19. Wash cells with ice-cold 1× PBS
20. Centrifuge at 2,500 × g and 4°C for 5 minutes
21. Resuspend cells in 5 mL ice-cold 1× PBS
22. Mix S. cerevisiae and S. pombe cells at 3:1 ratio
23. Centrifuge mixed samples at 2,500 × g and 4°C for 5 minutes
24. Remove PBS
25. Flash-freeze cells in liquid nitrogen
26. Store sample at -80°C until use
27. Thaw cells on ice for 20 to 30 minutes
28. Transfer filter cartridge to final collection tube
29. Elute RNA with 50 microliters of DEPC-treated RNase-free water preheated to 100°C
30. Centrifuge at 16,000 × g for 1 minute
31. Elute RNA again with 50 microliters of preheated DEPC-treated RNase-free water into same tube
32. Centrifuge at 16,000 × g for 1 minute
33. Verify entire sample volume has passed through filter
34. Centrifuge again for longer period if sample volume not fully passed through filter
35. Quantify and check purity of RNA sample using appropriate equipment
36. Adjust RNA concentration to 2mg/ml using DEPC-treated nuclease-free water
37. Aliquot 200 micrograms of total RNA
38. Heat RNA aliquot at 60 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes
39. Chill sample on ice for two minutes
40. Add 600 microliters of DEPC-treated RNase-free water
41. Add 100 microliters of biotinylation buffer
42. Add 200 microliters of biotin HPDP
43. If precipitation occurs, increase DMSO/DMF volume to 40% of reaction volume
44. Protect sample from light
45. Incubate at room temperature with gentle agitation for three hours
46. Add approximately equal volume of chloroform to tubes
47. Mix chloroform-containing tubes vigorously
48. Centrifuge at 13,000 × g at 4°C for 5 minutes
49. Transfer upper aqueous phase to new 2 mL tubes
50. Add 5M sodium chloride equal to ~1/10 sample volume
51. Mix sample after sodium chloride addition
52. Heat biotinylated RNA at 65°C for 10 minutes
53. Chill samples on ice for five minutes
54. Add 100 microliters of Streptavidin-coated magnetic beads to biotinylated RNA
55. Incubate mixture at room temperature with slight shaking for 90 minutes
56. Place kit columns in magnetic stand
57. Add 900 microliters of room temperature washing buffer to columns
58. Apply 200 microliter bead and RNA mixture to columns
59. Collect flow-through in 1.5 milliliter tubes
60. Reapply flow-through to same magnetic column
61. Retain final flow-through as unlabeled RNA fraction
62. Synthesize cDNA for RT-qPCR validation
63. Amplify cDNA by real-time qPCR using standard protocol

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 1 mL of 10 mM HEPES in DMEM to tumor fragments
2. Mince tissue with sterilized razor blade
3. Add 9 mL of DMEM with HEPES, collagenase type 1/2, and thermolysin to tumor pieces
4. Incubate mixture for 1.5-2 hours at 37°C and 5% CO₂ on orbital shaker
5. Transfer cell suspension to 50 milliliter tube
6. Centrifuge tube at 400 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C
7. Aspirate supernatant
8. Resuspend pellet in 5 milliliters of ACK lysing buffer
9. Incubate tube at room temperature for 3-5 minutes to lyse red blood cells
10. Coat one well of 24-well plate with 150 microliters of ice-cold growth factor reduced basement membrane matrix
11. Place coated plate in incubator for 30 minutes
12. Add 20 milliliters of DMEM to cell-containing tube
13. Centrifuge cell suspension
14. Aspirate supernatant from centrifuged cells
15. Resuspend cell pellet in 1 milliliter of 0.25% trypsin EDTA and 10 micromolar Y-27632 dihydrochloride
16. Incubate tube for 5 minutes in 37°C water bath
17. Neutralize trypsin by adding 10 milliliters of DMEM with 10% FBS
18. Filter cell suspension through 100 micrometer cell strainer into new 50 milliliter tube
19. Centrifuge tube at 400 g for 5 minutes at 4°C
20. Aspirate supernatant
21. Resuspend pellet with 1 milliliter of DMEM
22. Count cells using hemocytometer
23. Transfer 3-4 × 10⁴ tumor cells to 1.5 ml microtube on ice
24. Centrifuge microtube at 400 g for 3 minutes at 4°C
25. Carefully remove supernatant
26. Resuspend cells in 500 microliters of prewarmed organoid medium with 10 µM Y-27632
27. Transfer cell suspension into membrane matrix coated well
28. Place 24-well plate into incubator
29. Replace medium every two days with 500 microliters of prewarmed organoid medium
30. Split tumor organoids
31. Thaw virus-containing aliquot in 37°C water bath
32. Add thawed virus to 250 microliters organoid medium containing 10 μM Y-27632 and 8 μg/mL hexadimethrine bromide
33. Replace organoid medium in 24-well plate with 500 microliters virus-containing medium
34. Incubate plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 12-16 hours
35. Add 500 microliters of collagenase dispase to organoid medium in 24-well plate
36. Pipette basement membrane matrix and medium up and down to mix
37. Incubate plate for 20 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
38. Collect cells into 15 milliliter tube
39. Add five milliliters of prewarmed DMEM
40. Centrifuge tube at 400 g for three minutes at 4 degrees Celsius
41. Aspirate supernatant
42. Resuspend pellet with 1 milliliter of DMEM
43. Transfer solution to 90 millimeter Petri dish
44. Pick up 10-100 tumor organoids under microscope using P200 pipette
45. Collect organoids into microtube placed on ice

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Seed BT-474 cancer cells at density of 2 × 10⁶ cells into T-75 flask using 10 ml complete growth media
2. Culture cells until reaching 70-80% confluency
3. Discard growth media
4. Wash cell monolayer with PBS
5. Wash cell monolayer with PBS (second wash)
6. Add 5 ml pre-warmed cell culture dissociation reagent
7. Incubate at 37°C for 5 minutes or until cell detachment
8. Remove flask from incubator
9. Gently tap flask to aid cell detachment
10. Add 5 ml complete growth media with 10% FBS to quench dissociation reagent
11. Gently resuspend cells by pipetting to reduce clumps
12. Transfer cell suspension to 50-milliliter tube
13. Centrifuge at 180 G for 3 minutes at room temperature
14. Decant supernatant
15. Resuspend cell pellet in 10 ml HBSS without calcium and magnesium
16. Centrifuge cell suspension at 180 G for 3 minutes at room temperature
17. Decant supernatant to remove residual serum or dissociation reagent
18. Resuspend cell pellet in 3 ml HBSS
19. Place 100-micrometer cell strainer on fresh 50-milliliter conical tube
20. Pass cell suspension through strainer to remove cell clumps
21. Calculate number of viable cells using trypan blue
22. Dilute cell suspension with HBSS to concentration of 2.5 × 10⁶ cells/mL
23. Place tube horizontally on ice
24. Gently rock tube periodically to minimize clumping
25. Store cell suspension on ice
26. Ear-punch mouse for identification
27. Shave fur from neck region using electric clippers
28. Clean excess hair from exposed skin using tape
29. Apply ocular lubricant to eyes to prevent drying
30. Secure mouse by hooking upper incisor teeth using thread taped to surgical board
31. Tape front and hind legs to surgical board
32. Wipe neck with povidone iodine antiseptic (center to outward)
33. Perform three alternating rounds of povidone iodine and 70% ethanol disinfection
34. Perform pinch test to verify anesthesia depth
35. Lay sterile surgical drape over animal
36. Position under dissection microscope
37. Make 15-mm vertical midline neck incision with scissors from 5mm below jaw to thoracic inlet
38. Part skin and salivary glands using two pairs of angled forceps
39. Apply retractors to maintain tracheal exposure
40. Bluntly dissect muscle and fat tissue adjacent to trachea using two pairs of fine angled forceps
41. Clear surrounding fascia from a segment of the common carotid artery caudal to the carotid bifurcation
42. Separate the common carotid artery from the vagus nerve and veins
43. Isolate and clear the carotid bifurcation from surrounding nerves and fascia
44. Position fine forceps under the external carotid artery
45. Pass a 5-0 silk suture under the external carotid artery
46. Knot and tighten the suture on the external carotid artery
47. Position fine forceps under the common carotid artery
48. Pass a 5-0 silk suture under the common carotid artery
49. Tie a knot and tighten the suture proximal to the proposed injection site
50. Cut excess suture leaving approximately 10 millimeters of line
51. Cut and moisten a strip of low-lint disposable wipers
52. Cut and moisten a 10-by-5 millimeter strip of low-lint disposable wipers
53. Fold the moistened wiper strip
54. Place folded wiper strip underneath carotid artery at proposed injection site
55. Place third ligation with loose knot on common carotid artery rostral to injection site
56. Gently agitate cell suspension
57. Draw 200 microliters of cell suspension into insulin syringe with 31-gauge needle
58. Load syringe into syringe driver connected to activating foot pedal
59. Attach fine cannula with 31-gauge needle to syringe
60. Prime the line
61. Check carotid artery positioning and pressurization
62. Gently tension the end of the first ligature using fine-angled forceps
63. Hold 31-gauge needle with fine-angled forceps
64. Slowly insert needle with bevel up into blood vessel lumen while avoiding puncture
65. Inject 100 microliters of prepared cell suspension into common carotid artery at 10 microliters per second (delivering 2.5×10⁵ cells)
66. Visually confirm successful injection via blood clearance from carotid vessel
67. Withdraw needle from blood vessel
68. Immediately lift and tighten loose ligature to prevent backflow and bleeding
69. Trim excess sutures
70. Remove moistened low-lint disposable wipers
71. Rinse surgical cavity twice with 150-200 microliters of sterile water or saline using P-200 pipette
72. Check for bleeds
73. Reposition soft tissue, salivary glands, and skin over carotid artery and trachea
74. Close skin layer of incision using suture needle-holder, forceps, and 6-0 monofilament suture in continuous pattern
75. Inject 50 μg/kg buprenorphine and 1 mg/kg meloxicam via subcutaneous injection for post-surgical pain relief
76. Move animal to warm and clean cage for anesthesia recovery

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Handle animals daily for at least five days with sessions of 10 to 15 minutes per animal
2. Weigh animals during handling sessions
3. Allow animals to acclimate to handler's hand in cage
4. Gently lift each animal in safe manner
5. Weigh each animal to establish baseline body weight prior to food deprivation
6. Initiate starvation protocol
7. Provide 0.05 grams of standard diet per gram of body weight daily
8. Ensure unrestricted daily access to water
9. Monitor body weight to prevent exceeding 10% loss during food restriction
10. Feed animals separately when housed in multi-animal cages to prevent food monopolization
11. Place rat into training box for 10-15 minutes daily for five days prior to pre-training
12. Place several pellets into training box to familiarize animal with smell and taste
13. Clean training box between exposures to each animal
14. Return rat to training box
15. Place sugar pellet on slide in front of slit opening
16. Instruct rat to reposition (run to rear of cage and return to slit opening, or step back and turn around) for next pellet
17. Allow rat to explore cage during task execution
18. If repositioning fails, gently tap rear cage with forceps to attract attention
19. After rat moves to rear, gently tap front cage to guide back to slit opening
20. Replace slide in front of training box with pedestal
21. Place pellet on pedestal positioned 1.5 cm from window
22. Move pellet close to rat's mouth using forceps
23. Retract pellet using forceps
24. Continue forceps-based teasing until rat stretches forelimb
25. Shift pedestal to window border contralateral to preferred limb
26. End training session after either 150 pellet grasps or 1 hour (whichever occurs first)
27. Record successful first-attempt pellet retrievals meeting monolithic movement criteria (no hesitation/repetition)

Complete step list: Remove spent medium from wells
2. Rinse cells in each well with 1 milliliter of 1X DPBS
3. Incubate stem cell colonies in each well with 1 milliliter of 0.5 millimolar EDTA at 37°C for approximately 5 minutes
4. Check colony morphology for curled edges with loose gaps
5. Aspirate EDTA from wells
6. Gently rinse cells in each well with 6 milliliters of medium A containing 6 microliters of 10 millimolar rock inhibitor Y27632 solution
7. Gently tap bottom of dish to rinse cells
8. Observe cells under microscope to confirm embryo body formation
9. Collect embryo bodies into 15 milliliter centrifugal tube using 10 milliliter pipette
10. Wait 5 minutes for embryo bodies to settle to bottom
11. Carefully aspirate supernatant
12. Re-suspend embryo bodies in 6 milliliters of fresh medium A
13. Transfer suspension into new low-adhesion well of six-well plate
14. Culture in 37 degrees Celsius incubator
15. Coat 10 cm dish with 3 ml of 0.1% fish gelatin solution
16. Incubate coated dish for ≥1 hour at 37°C in 5% CO₂ incubator
17. Remove gelatin solution from dish
18. Rinse dish once with 5-6 ml of 1X DPBS
19. Collect embryo bodies into 15 milliliter centrifuge tube
20. Wait 5 minutes for embryo bodies to settle at tube bottom
21. Re-suspend embryo bodies with 15 milliliters of medium B
22. Transfer suspension to coated 10 centimeter dish
23. Culture in 37°C 5% CO₂ incubator for one week
24. Centrifuge cells at 200G for five minutes at room temperature
25. Aspirate supernatant carefully
26. Re-suspend cells with two milliliters of fresh medium C
27. Transfer cell suspension into new low-adhesion well of six-well plate
28. Incubate plate in 5% CO₂ incubator at 37°C
29. Replace spent medium with 2 milliliters of medium C per well
30. Examine plate under microscope to identify branched microglia adhering to bottom of low adhesion well
31. Gently replace medium C in each well with 1 mL Accutase
32. Incubate at 37°C for 3 minutes
33. Collect microglia cells into 15 mL tube using 5 mL pipette
34. Centrifuge cells at 200G for 5 minutes at room temperature
35. Discard supernatant
36. Suspend microglia with 1 mL of medium E
37. Obtain human microglia from human embryonic stem cells and harvest on day 56
38. Culture human embryonic stem cells in stem cell medium until cell density reaches 80-90%
39. Add 1 mL Dispase per well to cultured cells
40. Incubate at 37°C for 5 minutes
41. Add 1 mL Medium D per well after Dispase removal
42. Cut cells into small pieces using 10 μL pipette
43. Gently collect all cell pieces
44. Centrifuge tube at 200G for 5 minutes
45. Remove supernatant
46. Gently re-suspend cells in 200 microliters of cold matrix
47. Transfer 1.5 milliliter micro centrifuge tube to incubator
48. Prepare 10 cm dish with 15 ml of medium D
49. Re-suspend matrix with medium D
50. Shake Petri dish gently
51. Place dish in incubator for five days
52. Replace medium with 3 milliliters of Dispase
53. Aspirate Dispase after 5 minutes incubation
54. Add 15 milliliters of medium E
55. Introduce harvested microglia to digested retinal organoids
56. Gently swirl plate to aggregate organoids to center of dish
57. Replace medium E with medium F
58. Transfer organoids with medium F to new suspension dish

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Label a 15 milliliter conical tube for tissue sample
2. Add 2.5 milliliters of type four collagenase mixture and 30 units of elastase to the tube
3. Move lung to clean 1X HBSS well
4. Swirl lung in HBSS with forceps to remove remaining blood
5. Transfer lung to empty 3.5 centimeter tissue culture plate
6. Mince lung with scissors
7. Rinse plate with 2.5 milliliters of HBSS
8. Transfer HBSS with lung pieces to prepared 15 milliliter conical tube containing collagenase/elastase cocktail
9. Incubate tube for 75 minutes at 4°C on rocker/rotating wheel
10. Label one 50 ml centrifuge tube and one 10 cm tissue culture plate per mouse (including additional plates for dilutions if required)
11. Bring tube volume to 10 ml with 1X HBSS, pour contents over 70 μm cell strainer into 50 ml conical tube
12. Place 70 micrometer cell strainer into 50 milliliter conical tube
13. Use plunger of 1 milliliter syringe to gently grind sample through strainer
14. Centrifuge tube for 5 minutes at 350 × g
15. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
16. Wash pellet twice with 10 milliliters of 1X HBSS
17. Resuspend pellet in 10 milliliters of 60 micromolar 6-thioguanine complete culture media
18. Plate samples in 10 centimeter cell culture plates
19. Incubate plates at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 5 days
20. Pour culture media off plates into appropriate waste container
21. Add 5 ml undiluted methanol to each plate
22. Incubate plates with methanol for 5 minutes at room temperature
23. Swirl methanol to ensure full plate coverage during incubation
24. Pour methanol off plates into appropriate waste container
25. Rinse each plate with 5 ml distilled water
26. Add 5 ml of 0.03% methylene blue per plate
27. Incubate plates with methylene blue for 5 minutes at room temperature
28. Swirl methylene blue solution to ensure full plate coverage during incubation
29. Pour methylene blue into appropriate waste container
30. Rinse each plate with 5 ml distilled water
31. Turn plate upside down
32. Blot plate against paper towel to remove excess liquid
33. Place plate on its lid for overnight air drying
34. Remove labeled lids from plates while ensuring clear sample identification
35. Line up stained lung plates on clean light surface
36. Take picture of plate collection in well-lit area while minimizing reflections
37. Take several pictures from multiple angles
38. Crop image to exclude lids and background elements
39. Open image in Fiji ImageJ software
40. Convert image to black and white using Image→Adjust→Color Threshold menu path
41. Set thresholding method to 'Default'
42. Set threshold color to 'Black and White'
43. Set color space to 'Lab'
44. Deselect 'Dark background' option
45. Select circle tool for analysis area
46. Draw consistent circular analysis area across all plates to maximize analyzed area while minimizing edge noise
47. Analyze selected circle to determine percentage of white area
48. Click 'Analyze' and select 'Analyze particles'
49. Set particle size range: 0 to infinity
50. Set circularity range: 0 to 1
51. Check 'Summarized' box and confirm analysis
52. Move circle to next plate by grabbing center
53. Repeat analysis for relocated circle
54. Copy and paste percent area result into spreadsheet
55. Average percent area results across images for each plate

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Dissolve 2g low-melting temperature agarose in 100mL sterilized distilled water
2. Heat mixture in microwave with interval shaking to dissolve agarose completely
3. Add 520μL melted agarose to 256-well-format mold
4. Allow agarose in mold to solidify
5. Transfer agarose microplate to single well of 12-well plate
6. Add 1.5mL DPBS with calcium and magnesium to each well
7. Pipette DPBS up and down to remove bubbles
8. Aspirate medium from undifferentiated human pluripotent stem cell (HPSC) culture
9. Add 5 mL room-temperature calcium/magnesium-free DPBS to rinse cells
10. Remove DPBS buffer after rinsing
11. Add 5 mL cell dissociation reagent to cells
12. Incubate at 37°C for 6-8 minutes
13. Observe cell detachment under microscope
14. Remove cell dissociation reagent
15. Add 5 mL fresh MT serve one medium to stop reaction
16. Remove cell dissociation reagent to stop reaction
17. Add 5 mL of fresh MT serve one medium supplemented with 10 μM rock inhibitor Y27632
18. Pipette mixture up and down several times to dissociate cells
19. Count viable cells using hemocytometer and trypan blue staining
20. Transfer desired number of cells to sterile 15 mL or 50 mL centrifuge tube
21. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes to pellet cells
22. Discard supernatant
23. Resuspend cell pellet in MT serve one medium with 10 μM rock inhibitor at 2.1 million cells/mL concentration
24. Add 190 microliters of prepared cell suspension per agarose microwell
25. Incubate agarose microplates at 37°C in 5% CO₂ for 2 hours
26. Add 1 mL of fresh warm T-serve one medium with rock inhibitor to each well
27. Return plates to incubator for 24-hour incubation
28. Examine hepatosphere formation
29. Dissolve 2 grams of poly-HEMA in 100 milliliters of 95% ethanol
30. Stir solution overnight on 55°C hot plate
31. Add 250 microliters of solution to each well of 24-well plate
32. Dry plate overnight in 60°C oven
33. Remove MT serve one medium from previously seeded cells
34. Add 1 ml of fresh endoderm differentiation medium to cells
35. Refresh endoderm differentiation medium every 24 hours for 3 days
36. Switch medium to hepatoblast differentiation medium
37. Replace hepatoblast differentiation medium every 2 days for 5 days (including final change on last day)
38. Wash hepatospheres with hepatocyte maturation medium
39. Add 1 mL hepatocyte maturation medium supplemented with 10 ng/mL HGF and 20 ng/mL OSM
40. Pipette solution up and down several times to lift hepatospheres from agarose microplate
41. Transfer hepatospheres to poly-HEMA coated well
42. Wash agarose microplate with 1 mL supplemented hepatocyte maturation medium
43. Transfer wash medium to poly-HEMA coated well
44. Aspirate excess medium using P-100 pipette until approximately 1 milliliter remains in well, avoiding hepatosphere removal
45. Refresh medium every 48 hours for 12 days
46. Remove maturation medium (for embryonic stem cells at day 20)
47. Wash cells with maintenance medium
48. Add 1 milliliter of supplemented maintenance medium
49. Refresh medium every 48 hours after medium switch

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Line tissue culture hood with under pad
2. Place 1L beaker with stir bar, 150mm sterile tissue culture dish, and whole kidney into hood
3. Fill beaker with 500ml of 1% SDS solution
4. Place kidney in sterile tissue culture dish
5. Remove perirenal fat by lightly shaving around renal capsule with scalpel
6. Make shallow 8-10cm incision across superior end of kidney to open renal capsule without damaging cortex tissue
7. Use two hemostat clamps to remove renal capsule by peeling away from cortex tissue
8. Bisect kidney along coronal plane using scalpel along lateral side
9. Isolate cortex tissue from both halves by carving out medullar region with scalpel
10. Dice cortex tissue into 0.5 centimeter cubed pieces
11. Remove any large visible vessels
12. Place diced cortex tissue into beaker containing SDS solution
13. Cover beaker with autoclaved aluminum foil
14. Place covered beaker on stir plate at 400 RPM outside tissue culture hood
15. Bring beaker into tissue culture hood after 24 hours of stirring
16. Bring beaker into tissue culture hood
17. Add 40 micron sterile nylon mesh cell strainer
18. Fill separate 1000 milliliter beaker with 200 milliliters of bleach
19. Place bleach-containing beaker in tissue culture hood
20. Dispose of SDS solution through cell strainer into bleach-containing beaker
21. Pipet out remaining SDS solution until only decellularized tissue and cell strainer remain
22. Transfer decellularized tissue (kidney ECM) into 30 milliliter self-standing conical tube
23. Fill conical tube with cell culture grade water until all tissue is submerged
24. Homogenize kidney ECM in conical tube using tissue homogenizer for 2 minutes until opaque solution with no visible ECM pieces is obtained
25. Submerge conical tube containing kidney ECM in liquid nitrogen
26. Store kidney ECM at -4°C overnight
27. Slightly loosen conical tube cap to allow gas exchange
28. Place tube into lyophilization machine
29. Lyophilize kidney ECM for three days or until powdered
30. Add carefully weighed porcine gastric pepsin
31. Add 0.01 normal hydrochloric acid
32. Transfer mixture to scintillation vial containing stir bar
33. Stir mixture at approximately 500 RPM
34. Continue stirring until all pepsin dissolves
35. Transfer lyophilized kidney ECM to scintillation vial
36. Agitate solution on stir plate at ~500 RPM for three days
37. Pipet required volumes of cell culture media, sodium hydroxide, and M199 supplement in tissue culture hood
38. Transfer mixture to sterile 30 milliliter self-standing conical tube
39. Mix neutralizing reagent solution with micro spatula
40. Transfer appropriate volume of stock kidney ECM hydrogel to neutralizing reagent using sterile 1 milliliter syringe
41. Gently mix solution with micro spatula until homogeneous hydrogel forms
42. Avoid air bubbles by stirring slowly and gently
43. Resuspend 300,000 cells in 10 microliters of medium per hydrogel
44. Pipet 10 microliters of cell suspension into final kidney ECM gel
45. Stir solution with micro spatula until cells are evenly distributed
46. Fill 1 mL syringe with kidney ECM hydrogel
47. Transfer hydrogel from syringe to cell culture device
48. Incubate hydrogel-filled device at 37°C for 1 hour to set gel
49. Prepare cells for transfer onto set hydrogel in culture device

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place patient in French position
2. Abduct patient's left arm to 90 degrees
3. Position first surgeon between patient's legs
4. Position first assistant on left side and second assistant on right side
5. Keep suprapubic region free for Pfannenstiel incision
6. Place needle transcutaneously in epigastric region around Teres ligament
7. Retract ligament to ventral abdominal wall
8. Mobilize hepatic flexure
9. Perform wide Kocher maneuver
10. Expose inferior caval vein, origin of superior mesenteric artery, and ligament of Treitz
11. Expose superior mesenteric vein and its confluence with gastroepiploic vein
12. Dissect gastroepiploic vein
13. Transect gastroepiploic vein using clips and articulating sealer
14. Clear distal stomach 1 cm proximal to pylorus
15. Transect stomach using endostapler
16. Identify hepatic artery, portal vein, and gastroduodenal artery
17. Encircle gastroduodenal artery with vessel loop
18. Divide gastroduodenal artery leaving two plastic locking clips on patient's side and one on specimen's side
19. Create pancreatic tunnel using blunt dissection
20. Sling pancreas using articulating sealer
21. Secure pancreas sling with quarter-length vessel loop
22. Mobilize ligament of Treitz
23. Divide first jejunal loop with endostapler using vascular cartridge
24. Complete mobilization of first jejunal loop up to mesenteric root
25. Suture two ends of jejunum to each other
26. Pass sutured jejunum under mesenteric root to patient's right side
27. Dissect uncinate process with articulating sealer from lateral aspect of SMV and first jejunal branch
28. Work in layers from ventral to dorsal
29. Retract SMV medially to expose SMA
30. Transect pancreas using monopolar diathermy
31. Manage bleeding with bipolar diathermy
32. Transect pancreatic duct with scissors
33. Dissect retroperitoneal nodes while visualizing SMA
34. Identify hepatic duct and divide it between laparoscopic bulldog (proximal) and plastic locking clip (distal)
35. Place specimen in plastic retrieval bag
36. Temporarily leave specimen in lower abdomen
37. Initiate pancreatic reconstruction phase using modified Blumgart technique
38. Prepare four barbed sutures for anastomosis
39. Pass sutures through pancreas tissue approximately 1 cm from cut edge
40. Pass sutures dorsally through bowel tissue
41. Return sutures back through pancreas tissue
42. Drive needle through suture loop
43. Tighten suture
44. Place tightened suture far from pancreas
45. Repeat suturing procedure for each suture while avoiding pancreatic duct obstruction
46. Create a two-millimeter enterotomy in the jejunum using a diathermy hook
47. Place four to six duct-to-mucosa sutures using a small blunt needle, starting with eight and five o'clock sutures with internal knots
48. Insert a 12-centimeter 6 or 8 French pediatric nasogastric feeding tube as an internal pancreatic stent
49. Place 11 and two o'clock sutures with external knots
50. Complete anastomosis by securing ventral jejunum to pancreas using original barbed sutures: insert through jejunal wall, then through ventral pancreas, securing each with a clip
51. Suture gall bladder to ventral abdominal wall for liver retraction and exposure of hepatoduodenal ligament
52. Create 2-5 mm antimesenterial enterotomy on jejunal loop using diathermy hook
53. Perform end-to-side hepaticojejunostomy with interrupted absorbable 5/0 sutures
54. Place initial suture between 6 o'clock position on bile duct and 12 o'clock position on enterotomy
55. Continue suturing from caudal to cranial direction
56. Alternate suturing between left and right sides of hepaticojejunostomy
57. Create small enterotomy
58. Create small gastrostomy ventral to staple line
59. Perform side-to-side stapled antecolic gastrojejunostomy
60. Close remaining opening with single 3/0 barbed suture
61. Place second drain through right-sided five-millimeter trocar caudal to pancreaticojejunostomy with tip under gastrojejunostomy
62. Cover pancreaticojejunostomy and GDA stump with greater omentum

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 1 mL of ice-cold 0.9% sodium chloride to 1.5-mL tube containing larvae
2. Close tube lid
3. Vertically flip tube to wash larvae
4. Place tube on ice for 30 seconds
5. Remove sodium chloride solution using 1-mL pipette
6. Centrifuge samples at 2,000 g for 1 minute at 4°C
7. Remove residual solution using 200-μL pipette and immediately freeze sample in liquid nitrogen
8. Label side of 2-mL screw-cap bead tube with ethanol-proof marker
9. Tare mass of labeled bead tube using analytical balance (0.01 mg accuracy)
10. Remove 1.5-mL sample tube from liquid nitrogen dewar using long forceps
11. Grab frozen tube while wearing nitrile gloves and invert it
12. Sharply pound inverted tube lid against benchtop to dislodge frozen pellet
13. Pour dislodged pellet into pre-tared 2-mL screw-cap bead tube
14. Measure combined mass of larval pellet and bead tube
15. Place sample tube into liquid nitrogen
16. Place sample tubes in -20°C benchtop cooler
17. Add 0.8 mL pre-chilled 90% methanol containing 2 μg/mL succinic-D4 acid to each sample tube
18. Return samples to -20°C benchtop cooler
19. Add 0.8 mL pre-chilled 90% methanol containing 2 μg/mL succinic-D4 acid to empty bead tube for negative control
20. Homogenize samples for 30 seconds at 6.45 meters per second using bead mill homogenizer in 4°C temperature control room
21. Return homogenized samples to -20°C benchtop cooler
22. Transfer cooler to -20°C freezer for at least one hour
23. Centrifuge tubes at 20,000 g for 5 minutes at 4°C to remove precipitate
24. Transfer 600 microliters of supernatant to new 1.5-milliliter microcentrifuge tube without disturbing precipitate
25. Open all sample tubes and place them in vacuum centrifuge
26. Dry samples at room temperature until all solvent is removed
27. Prepare solution of 40 mg/mL methoxylamine hydrochloride (MOX) in anhydrous pyridine
28. Store MOX and anhydrous pyridine in desiccator
29. Dry 1-mL glass syringe with heat gun for 5 seconds
30. Insert syringe needle into bottle of anhydrous pyridine
31. Withdraw 1 mL of anhydrous pyridine using syringe
32. Add withdrawn pyridine to microfuge tube containing 40 mg MOX
33. Flush bottle of anhydrous pyridine with argon
34. Seal the bottle
35. Return sealed bottle to desiccator
36. Dissolve MOX in pyridine by incubating in thermal mixer at 35°C for 10 minutes at 600 rpm
37. Add 40 microliters of 40 mg/mL MOX in anhydrous pyridine solution to dried sample
38. Vortex sample for 10 seconds
39. Centrifuge sample briefly
40. Incubate sample in thermal mixer
41. Centrifuge sample at 20,000 g or maximum speed for 5 minutes
42. Transfer 25 microliters of supernatant to autosampler vial with 250-microliter deactivated glass insert
43. Add 40 microliters of MSTFA containing 1% TMCS to vial
44. Place cap on autosampler vial
45. Seal vial using crimper tool
46. Incubate sample at 37°C for 1 hour with shaking at 250 rpm
47. Add 3 microliters of fatty acid methyl ester (FAMES) standard solution to vial using robotic autosampler

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Prepare solution of lipids in chloroform at 1 mg/mL concentration with total mass 0.6 mg
2. Wrap glass vial containing lipid mixture with metal foil to protect from ambient light
3. Place metal foil-wrapped sample vial inside glass beaker
4. Evaporate chloroform solvent in rotary evaporator for 3 hours at 7 kPa vacuum and 80 rpm
5. Add 600 microliters of phosphate-buffered saline solution onto dried lipid film
6. Add six microliters of glycerol to sample
7. Seal glass vial with Parafilm
8. Cover sealed vial with metal foil for light protection
9. Store vial at four degrees Celsius overnight
10. Sonicate glass vial containing solution for one minute using ultra-sonication bath at room temperature
11. Remove Parafilm and pipette solution until visually uniform small lipid vesicles form
12. Aliquot 30 microliters of small lipid vesicle solution into individual 0.5 milliliter plastic tubes
13. Thaw plastic tube containing aliquot of frozen suspension of small lipid vesicles at room temperature
14. Vortex tube four times for 1-2 seconds using vortex mixer at maximum speed
15. Place 5 microliters of small lipid vesicle suspension onto surface of uncleaned glass coverslip to form small round droplet
16. Place glass coverslip in vacuum desiccator for 20 minutes
17. Store dried lipid film at room temperature for 4 minutes
18. Pipette 50 microliters of 10 millimolar HEPES buffer onto dry lipid film
19. Wait 5 minutes for GUV-MLV complex formation
20. Center glass coverslip on microscope stage
21. Pipette 300 microliters of HEPES buffer onto coverslip
22. Position droplet center above microscope objective
23. Transfer 50 microliters of GUV-MLV solution into HEPES solution
24. Wait 25 minutes for GUV-MLV complexes to adhere to coverslip
25. Round edges of borosilicate glass capillaries by placing capillary ends into candle flame
26. Pull at least three glass capillaries using automatic laser puller to achieve tip opening of approximately 0.3 microns diameter
27. Filter 5 mM calcium chloride solution in HEPES buffer using 0.2-0.5 micron syringe filter
28. Back-fill each micropipette with 8 microliters of filtered calcium chloride solution using Microloader
29. Connect micropipette holder to micromanipulator
30. Mount micropipette into micropipette holder tightly
31. Connect injection pump to system
32. Connect capillary holder using supply tube
33. Start injection pump
34. Adjust injection pump settings to 20 hectopascal injection pressure
35. Set compensation pressure to 5 hectopascal
36. Set microscope to bright-field mode
37. Configure microscope for differential interference contrast
38. Position micropipette above droplet containing GUV-MLV complexes using coarse micromanipulator
39. Locate the tip of the micropipette above the objective
40. Bring the micropipette down to the droplet using the coarse micromanipulator
41. Set the microscope to fluorescence mode
42. Position the micropipette tip at a distance of three microns from the membrane surface
43. Inject calcium chloride solution from the micropipette to allow MTPs to form
44. Translate micropipette tip around GUV surface using fine micromanipulator
45. Maintain constant distance between GUV surface and micropipette tip
46. Continue calcium ion injection during manipulation
47. Turn off injection pump to stop microinjection

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Harvest mixed population of cells from mouse testes
2. Load harvested cells into stay put apparatus
3. Load BSA gradient into stay put apparatus
4. Allow cells to sediment through BSA gradient
5. Collect sedimented fractions
6. Combine fractions based on cell composition
7. Secure two 2-liter cylinders to top platform
8. Secure cell loading chamber to top platform
9. Connect components with two small tubing pieces
10. Apply tube clamps to connections
11. Clamp all tubes closed
12. Seal spout on rightmost 2-liter cylinder
13. Place small stir bar in cell loading chamber
14. Place large stir bar in leftmost 2-liter cylinder
15. Place 2-liter sedimentation chamber on platform
16. Position metal anti-vortex baffle over sedimentation chamber opening
17. Place lid on sedimentation chamber
18. Apply vacuum grease to ground glass joint of three-way stopcock
19. Clamp stopcock to sedimentation chamber by connecting ground glass joints
20. Connect cell loading chamber to right outlet of stopcock with tubing
21. Close stopcock
22. Attach cell fractionation tubing to left outlet of stopcock
23. Clamp small tube at the very bottom
24. Pour 2% BSA solution into left two liter cylinder
25. Pour 4% BSA solution into right two-liter cylinder
26. Verify absence of large bubbles in tubing connecting cylinders
27. Pour Krebs buffer into cell loading chamber and fill connecting tubing to sedimentation chamber
28. Verify absence of large bubbles that might disturb gradient
29. Confirm Krebs buffer is in tube and not in cell chamber
30. Allow small amount of Krebs buffer to flow into sedimentation chamber
31. Drain almost all buffer into cell fractionation tubing
32. Fill fractionation tubing and remove large bubbles
33. Place fraction collector directly under sedimentation chamber
34. Confirm all fraction tubes are in place
35. Cover fraction tubes with plastic wrap to prevent contamination
36. Deencapsulate testes in plate containing 8 milliliters of Krebs buffer
37. Incise membrane surrounding seminiferous tubules using one pair of forceps
38. Hold membrane with second pair of forceps and push tubules out of membrane
39. Transfer 4 milliliters of Krebs containing tubules to conical tube with 25 milliliters collagenase solution
40. Shake at 33°C for 10 minutes until tubules develop spaghetti-like appearance
41. Allow tubules to settle for 5 minutes at bottom of tube
42. Pour out supernatant
43. Wash tubules twice with 25 milliliters Krebs at room temperature, allowing settling to bottom after each wash
44. Leave approximately 5 milliliters Krebs in each tube
45. Incubate tubules with trypsin
46. Agitate tubule-trypsin solution using wide-bore pipette
47. Pipette solution in and out approximately 10 times to dissociate cells
48. Filter 30 milliliter single-cell suspension through 100-micron mesh cell strainer
49. Position stopcock to direct flow from cell chamber to sedimentation chamber
50. Turn both stir bar plates to low setting
51. Pour cell suspension into cell chamber
52. Open stopcock to allow cell flow into sedimentation chamber at 10 ml/min
53. Close stopcock
54. Pour 5 ml of 0.5% BSA solution into cell chamber and drain into sedimentation chamber at 10 ml/min
55. Close stopcock and repeat BSA wash four times
56. Open clamps between cell chamber and cylinders to drain liquid into sedimentation chamber at 40 ml/min
57. Adjust flow rate to load BSA gradient over 20-30 minutes
58. Attach fraction tube to fraction collector
59. Adjust flow rate using stop cock to collect 10 milliliters per tube every 45 seconds
60. Microscopically analyze collected fractions for different cell populations
61. Transfer 10 microliters of DAPI stained cells onto slide
62. Place cover slip on slide
63. Analyze sample with fluorescence microscope
64. Pool fractions with similar cell size and nuclear morphology

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Weigh 100 milligrams of poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) (PLGA)
2. Transfer weighed PLGA into scintillation vial
3. Dissolve PLGA in 5 milliliters of dichloromethane by vortexing
4. Place homogenizer in beaker containing 50 milliliters of 1% polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) solution
5. Position homogenizer close to bottom of beaker without touching
6. Turn on homogenizer to appropriate speed
7. Add PLGA solution to beaker for 1 minute of homogenization
8. Pour polyvinyl alcohol PLGA micro-particle solution into beaker containing 100 milliliters of 0.5% PVA solution on stir plate in chemical hood
9. Stir solution for at least four hours
10. Pour particle solution into 50 milliliter conical tubes
11. Collect particles by centrifugation
12. Replace supernatant with 20 milliliters of de-ionized water
13. Re-suspend particles by vortexing
14. Bring final volume in each tube to 50 milliliters with de-ionized water
15. Wash particles two more times using demonstrated procedure
16. Dissolve 200 milligrams of PLGA in 5 milliliters of dichloromethane
17. Place sonicator probe in beaker containing 50 milliliters of 1% PVA solution on ice without touching beaker bottom
18. Begin sonication at 12 watts for 2 minutes
19. Add PLGA solution to beaker during sonication
20. Pour 1% polyvinyl alcohol PLGA nano-particle solution into beaker containing 100 milliliters of 0.5% PVA solution
21. Place beaker on stir plate in chemical fume hood for 4-hour solvent evaporation
22. Pour evaporated particle solution into 50 milliliter conical tubes
23. Centrifuge tubes to remove micro-particles
24. Remove supernatants after centrifugation
25. Transfer nano-particles to high-speed centrifuge tubes for three washes in de-ionized water
26. Re-suspend particles in 1 milliliter of fresh de-ionized water
27. Add film casting solution to achieve 2.5 mg/mL particle concentration
28. Transfer 10 mL aliquots of particle suspension into 75x50 mm rectangular Petri dishes
29. Transfer 15 mL aliquots of particle suspension into 100x100 mm square Petri dishes
30. Remove dried films using tweezers after overnight drying
31. Trim film edges with scissors
32. Place short edges of film between two neoprene rubber pieces
33. Mount film on aluminum blocks of automated thin film stretching device
34. Secure film using Allen wrench to screw metal grips on rubber
35. Mount all four film edges onto aluminum blocks for 2D stretching
36. Measure and record film length between aluminum blocks for 1D or 2D stretching
37. Calculate required stretch distance in one or two directions based on desired fold stretch
38. Place film-loaded stretching device in 90°C oven with water-containing beaker for 10-minute thermal conditioning
39. Let stretched film cool to room temperature for 20 minutes
40. For 1D stretching: Cut film out of stretching device at edges
41. For 2D stretching: Cut out and save center square of uniformly stretched film
42. Place films in 50 mL conical tubes (maximum two films per tube)
43. Add approximately 25 mL de-ionized water to each tube for vortexing
44. When films dissolve: Wash particles three times in de-ionized water as demonstrated
45. After last wash: Re-suspend particles in approximately 1 mL fresh de-ionized water per tube
46. Freeze particles at -80°C for 1 hour or overnight for lyophilization
47. Re-suspend lyophilized micro/nano-particles at 20 mg/mL in freshly prepared MES buffer with vortexing
48. Transfer 100 μL of particle solution to polypropylene micro centrifuge tube containing 900 μL MES buffer
49. Add 100 μL of freshly prepared EDC NHS solution to tube
50. Mix particles by vortexing
51. Incubate particles on inverter at room temperature for 30 minutes
52. Collect particles by centrifugation
53. Re-suspend micro-particle pellets in 1 mL PBS by vortexing
54. Re-suspend nano-particles in 1 mL PBS by 5-second sonication at 2-3 watts
55. Add 8 micrograms of signal one protein and 10 micrograms of anti-mouse CD28 antibody to micro-particles
56. Add 16 micrograms of signal one protein and 20 micrograms of anti-mouse CD28 antibody to nano-particles
57. Bring final volume in each tube to 1.1 milliliter with PBS
58. Incubate particles overnight on inverter at 4 degrees Celsius
59. Wash particles three times in de-ionized water

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Dissolve 2.6 grams of P nipam in 2 milliliters of 60% toluene and 40% hexane solution
2. Heat mixture to 60 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes
3. Stir mixture until P nipam is dissolved
4. Cut filter paper into 60 millimeter diameter circle
5. Place filter paper in Buchner funnel
6. Filter solution through Buchner funnel into pre-weighed glass beaker
7. Keep beaker and contents in bell vacuum overnight
8. Weigh beaker with P nipam
9. Add isopropyl alcohol to P nipam
10. Create 50:50 weight/weight solution
11. Place two milliliters of solution on tissue culture plate surface
12. Coat plate for five minutes under UV light
13. Wash cells with two milliliters of warm PBS
14. Add three milliliters of trypsin or cleaving dissociating solution to cells
15. Incubate for five minutes
16. Add 3 mL of culture media or PBS containing 10% FBS to cells
17. Collect cells in conical tube
18. Count cells
19. Centrifuge cells at 1000 RPM for 5 minutes
20. Aspirate supernatant
21. Resuspend cells in growth media
22. Plate cell suspension on 35mm thermosensitive plate at concentration for 100% confluence
23. Incubate plate at 37°C overnight
24. Collect endothelial cells
25. Disperse cells into single-cell solution
26. Deactivate trypsin enzyme with equal amount of soybean trypsin inhibitor or 10% FBS in PBS
27. Collect cells in 15 milliliter conical tube
28. Count cells with hemocytometer and calculate volume needed for patch dimensions
29. Extract 2 million endothelial cells
30. Place extracted cells in new 15 milliliter conical tube
31. Centrifuge cells for five minutes
32. Aspirate supernatant, leaving cell pellet in conical tube
33. Mix high aurin and liquid gelatin in 1:1 ratio
34. Transfer 80% of final hydrogel-gelatin volume to conical tube containing endothelial cell pellet and resuspend cells
35. Transfer suspended cells into fibrous matrix
36. Add 20% of total final volume of crosslinker
37. Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour
38. Remove 35 millimeter P nypa treated plate containing cells from incubator
39. Place plate in cell culture hood
40. Aspirate media from cells
41. Add two milliliters of 37°C 6% normal gelatin to plate
42. Place metal lattice into gelatin
43. Submerge metal lattice below surface of normal gelatin
44. Place entire plate on ice for 5-7 minutes to harden gelatin
45. Use spatula to carefully separate gelatin edges from plate side
46. Lift metal lattice from plate with forceps
47. Transfer cell sheet to dish and place on fibrous matrix hydrogel combination
48. Add 2 milliliters of 37°C media to tissue culture plate
49. Incubate overnight to allow cell sheet adherence to hydrogel surface

16. Remove liquid

18. Centrifuge at 21,000 x g for 1.5 minutes

20. Resuspend cells in 425 microliters of digestion master mix and vortex briefly
21. Add 5 microliters of zymolyase to tube
22. Vortex for 2-3 seconds to mix
23. Place tube on roller drum and digest at 30°C for 15-30 minutes
24. Check cells by microscopy every 5 minutes after 15 minutes until 80% appear non-transparent and non-refractive
25. Centrifuge tube at 376 x g for three minutes
26. Remove supernatant completely
27. Gently resuspend cells with 1 mL Buffer B by pipetting up and down 1-2 times
28. Centrifuge tube
29. Remove buffer
30. Resuspend cells in 1 mL of 70% ethanol
31. Incubate at room temperature for 3.5-4 hours
32. Centrifuge tube of cells at 376 x g for three minutes
33. Remove 500 microliters of 70% ethanol from tube
34. Gently pipette up and down to resuspend remaining cells
35. Transfer cell suspension to low adhesion tube
36. Centrifuge low adhesion tube
37. Remove all ethanol from low adhesion tube
38. Add one milliliter of 10% formamide wash buffer to tube
39. Gently pipette up and down 2-3 times to resuspend cells
40. Incubate cells at room temperature for 20 minutes
41. Centrifuge sample
42. Remove supernatant into hazardous waste
43. Add at least 50 microliters of hybridization solution to tube
44. Flick tube to mix solution
45. Incubate at 30°C on roller drum in dark for at least 16 hours
46. Place tube into foil-covered box to protect from light
47. Centrifuge sample at 376 × g for 3 minutes
48. Remove supernatant into hazardous waste by pipetting
49. Resuspend sample in 1 mL of 10% formamide wash buffer by gently pipetting up and down 2-3 times
50. Place tube into foil-covered box to protect from light
51. Incubate at 30°C for 30 minutes
52. Centrifuge sample at 30-minute mark
53. Remove supernatant to hazardous waste by pipetting while leaving approximately 50 microliters
54. Resuspend sample in 1 milliliter of DAPI solution by gentle pipetting (2-3 up/down cycles)
55. Place tube in foil-covered box
56. Incubate at 30°C for 30 minutes
57. Centrifuge sample
58. Completely remove supernatant by pipetting
59. Resuspend pellet in 50 microliters of enzyme-free GLOX buffer
60. Mix by pipetting (3-4 up/down cycles)
61. Store in foil-covered box at 4°C until imaging

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Fill PDMS negative with 400 microliters of MY-133-V-2000 resin
2. Remove MY-133-V-2000 from vacuum chamber
3. Press glass slide against top of overfilled negative to create flat surface
4. Insert MY-133-V-2000 into 400 watt UV oven
5. Set UV radiation to 50% maximum intensity for 300 seconds to cure microchannel
6. Place two small drops of glue on edge of acrylic
7. Spread glue evenly using disposable tool
8. Place glass substrate onto acrylic
9. Allow glue to dry using glass weight for positioning
10. Coat exposed glass with 100 microliters of PDMS using positive displacement pipette
11. Insert base layer into vacuum spin coater
12. Spin coat at 1500 RPM for 2 minutes to achieve 10 micrometer PDMS film
13. Remove base layer from spin coater
14. Wipe excess PDMS from acrylic using acetone and paper towel
15. Bake base layer with PDMS in oven at 65°C for 2 hours
16. Pipette 1 milliliter of PDMS onto glass slide
17. Spread PDMS evenly using second glass slide until it oozes out sides
18. Cure PDMS on hot plate at 150°C for 10 minutes
19. Cut cured PDMS into rectangle matching MY-133-V-2000 device dimensions
20. Punch reservoir holes in PDMS using midlayer acrylic mold
21. Cut square viewing window in PDMS to complete gasket
22. Remove cured MY-133-V-2000 device from UV oven
23. Remove channel from negative mold and place channel-side up on flat substrate
24. Remove base layer with cured PDMS from 65°C oven
25. Place base layer onto substrate
26. Place substrate with channel and base layer into oxygen plasma cleaner
27. Set vacuum pressure to 200 millitorr
28. Set radio frequency level to high
29. Surface treat channel and glass substrate for 30 seconds
30. Remove microchannel and base layer from plasma cleaner
31. Place MY-133-V-2000 channel side down into rectangular cutout of base layer acrylic using forceps to contact PDMS
32. Place PDMS gasket on top of MY-133-V-2000 device
33. Align holes in PDMS gasket with reservoirs in the device
34. Place unfinished device on raised platform above bench
35. Extract 3 milliliters of acrylic cement using syringe
36. Distribute acrylic cement on base layer to form thin even coating, avoiding channel seepage
37. Place mid-layer acrylic piece on base layer, aligning holes with MY-133-V-2000 channel reservoirs
38. Clamp mid-layer tightly onto base layer and hold for 2 minutes for cement hardening and bonding
39. Remove pressure from the device
40. Apply acrylic cement to midlayer at anticipated top layer contact points
41. Place top acrylic layer onto midlayer
42. Align holes with reservoirs
43. Allow assembly to rest on bench for 2 minutes for cement drying
44. Add 10 microliters of food dye or deionized water into one reservoir hole
45. Insert tube into opposite reservoir
46. Connect tube end to vacuum trap
47. Turn vacuum on to pull dye/water through channel
48. Check under microscope for leaks in channel or reservoir
49. Remove dye from both reservoirs using vacuum
50. Rinse reservoirs with ethanol
51. Place ethanol in one reservoir
52. Pull ethanol through channel using vacuum
53. Allow ethanol to sit at room temperature for 10 minutes
54. Spray channel thoroughly with ethanol
55. Place device into sterile polystyrene dish
56. Wrap dish tightly with Parafilm for storage

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Select male Sprague Dawley rats with no overt pathology weighing 300-350 grams
2. House three rats per cage with ad libitum chow and water
3. Allow minimum two-week adaptation period on 12-hour light-dark cycle
4. Place rats in individual cages in housing room during dark cycle
5. Place 100 milliliters of 1% sucrose solution bottle in each cage for 24-hour adaptation
6. Remove bottles from cages
7. Deprive rats of food and water for 12 hours
8. Place bottle containing 100 milliliters of 1% sucrose solution in each cage
9. Place bottle containing 100 milliliters of tap water in each cage
10. Leave bottles in cages for four hours
11. Record consumed volume (in milliliters) of sucrose solution and water
12. Calculate sucrose preference affinity
13. Eliminate rats testing positive for depression
14. Eliminate rats housed in same cages as depressed rats
15. Exclude additional rats at random if necessary for group formation
16. Tag rats using numbered stickers
17. Subject 60 rats in chronic unpredictable stress group to two randomly selected stressors daily for five consecutive weeks
18. House rats in overcrowded conditions (six rats per cage instead of three) for 18 hours
19. Place rats in cage tilted 45 degrees along vertical axis for three hours
20. Food deprive rats for 18 hours
21. Water deprive rats for 18 hours with exposure to empty water bottles after one hour
22. Place rats in cage with wet bedding for eight hours during night cycle
23. Expose rats to continuous lighting for 24 hours in reverse light-dark cycle twice weekly
24. Place rats in 40°C environment for five minutes during night cycle
25. Perform sucrose preference test to confirm depression development
26. Place two CUS-exposed rats per cage across 30 cages
27. Add one depression contagion group rat to each cage containing two CUS-exposed rats
28. Allow cohabitation for five weeks under standard conditions
29. Subject all groups to sucrose preference, open field, and forced-swim tests
30. Clean apparatus with 5% alcohol prior to introducing each animal
31. Place rat at corner of open field facing the wall
32. Record rat's behavior with video camera for five minutes
33. Analyze video recordings for total travel distance, velocity in central part, and time spent in central part
34. Place rat in glass cylinder containing room-temperature water
35. Conduct 15-minute habituation swimming session
36. Remove rat from cylinder
37. Dry rat with paper towels
38. Place rat in warm cage for 15 minutes
39. Return rat to home cage
40. Videotape 5-minute swim session
41. Analyze recordings for immobility, climbing, and defecation

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Ask participant to sit comfortably in chair
2. Clean participant's skin by rubbing preparation paste in hand regions for electrode placement
3. Remove excess preparation paste with clean gauze pad
4. Attach EMG surface electrodes in belly-tendon montage on hand muscle of interest
5. Connect ground electrode
6. Turn on magnetic stimulator
7. Charge capacitor by pressing corresponding charge button
8. Place figure-of-eight TMS coil on participant's scalp over motor cortex region
9. Hold TMS coil at 45-50° angle relative to inter-hemispheric fissure with handle oriented backwards
10. Discharge magnetic stimulator using trigger button, foot switch, or software program after charging
11. Set stimulation intensity to low level
12. Monitor EMG amplifier for motor evoked potentials (MEPs)
13. Increase stimulation intensity if no MEP is detected
14. Increase stimulation intensity until MEP is clearly present
15. Repeat stimulation by pressing trigger button
16. Move coil radially in 1cm steps around initial site to find spot with largest MEP response
17. Apply single TMS pulses during coil movement
18. Move coil again to secure hotspot position
19. Mark hotspot position and coil orientation with non-permanent skin marker
20. Connect cables to rubber electrodes
21. Place electrodes inside sponge bags
22. Ensure electrode size and sponge bag size match
23. Soak sponge bags on both sides with isotonic sodium chloride solution, avoiding excessive soaking to prevent salt bridges or dripping
24. Find head markings for motor cortical hot spot
25. Separate hair around the marked area
26. Clean skin with swab soaked with 40-50% alcohol or skin preparation paste
27. Remove excess cleaning agent with swab
28. Clean area again with isotonic sodium chloride solution
29. Dry the area
30. Place one electrode on head marking for motor cortex area of interest, ensuring sponge maintains direct skin contact
31. Place second electrode for unilateral stimulation
32. Place second electrode over contralateral supra-orbital area for unilateral stimulation
33. Place second electrode on opposite motor cortex (following head marking ipsilateral to study limb) for bilateral stimulation
34. Route all electrode cables toward participant's back to avoid disturbance during stimulation and ease device connection
35. Cover head twice with elastic bandage circularly in mediolateral direction to stabilize M1 electrode
36. Cover head with remaining bandage circularly in anterior-posterior direction to stabilize both electrodes
37. Fix end of bandage and secure cables with adhesive tape on participant's neck or shirt
38. Connect electrode cables to non-invasive brain stimulation device
39. Switch on brain stimulation device
40. Adjust device settings (stimulation type, intensity, duration, ramping parameters, frequency spectrum) for transcranial random noise stimulation
41. Inform participant about potential side effects associated with brain stimulation
42. Start stimulation and check for continuity during ramping up and throughout session
43. Disinfect electrodes and sponges with 40 to 50 percent alcohol
44. Thoroughly rinse electrodes and sponges in water
45. Let materials dry before storing

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Select similar-sized Triticum aestivum (wheat) seeds
2. Disinfect seeds with 8% hydrogen peroxide solution for 15 minutes
3. Rinse disinfected seeds thoroughly with deionized water
4. Place seeds on humid filter paper in dark at room temperature for 5-day germination
5. Select 9 uniform-sized germinated seedlings
6. Transfer seedlings to plastic beakers containing 250 milliliters of 1/4 strength Hoagland's solution
7. Cultivate seedlings in growth chambers for 7 days under temperature cycle: 14 hours at 22°C and 10 hours at 27°C
8. Prepare centrifuge tubes A and B with 50 milliliters of 1/4 strength Hoagland's solution
9. Dissolve PFOA and PFOS in methanol
10. Dilute dissolved PFOA/PFOS with sterile nutrient solution to prepare stock solution
11. Add stock solution to tube B at 100 micrograms per liter of PFOA and PFOS
12. Separate wheat seedling roots into two equal parts using tweezers
13. Insert separated roots into tubes A and B respectively
14. Seal both tubes with aluminum foil
15. Culture tubes in incubator for 7 days with 14 hours at 22°C and 10 hours at 27°C cycle
16. Collect wheat seedlings after 7-day incubation
17. Separate seedlings into shoots and roots using scissors (3 parts: spiked solution shoots/roots, unspiked solution shoots/roots)
18. Freeze-dry plant samples in lyophilizer at -55°C for 48 hours
19. Homogenize freeze-dried samples
20. Weigh root and shoot samples
21. Collect spiked and unspiked solution samples
22. Add 2 milliliters of sodium carbonate buffer to 15-milliliter polypropylene tube
23. Add 1 milliliter of tetrabutylammonium hydrogen sulfate to tube
24. Add 5 milliliters of methyl tert-butyl ether to tube
25. Add homogenized root or shoot sample to tube
26. Shake tube at 250 RPM for 20 minutes
27. Centrifuge tube at 2000 g for 10 minutes at room temperature
28. Pool similar treatment extracts
29. Evaporate pooled extracts to dryness in gentle nitrogen stream
30. Reconstitute dried extracts with 5 milliliters methanol
31. Vortex solution at constant speed for 30 seconds
32. Condition PestiCarb cartridge with 5 milliliters 0.1% ammonium hydroxide in methanol
33. Condition cartridge with 5 milliliters water
34. Condition cartridge with 5 milliliters methanol
35. Add 5 milliliters extracting methanol solution through PestiCarb cartridge to remove pigments
36. Elute cartridge with 5 milliliters methanol into sample tubes
37. Evaporate 10 milliliters methanol solution to dryness
38. Reconstitute with 200 microliters methanol
39. Centrifuge sample at 10,000 g for 20 minutes at room temperature
40. Condition polar-enhanced polymer extraction cartridge with 5 milliliters of methanol and 5 milliliters of water
41. Add sample solution (1 milliliter spiked solution or 50 milliliters unspiked solution) through cartridge
42. Elute target PFAAs with 10 milliliters of methanol
43. Evaporate extract using gentle nitrogen stream
44. Reconstitute evaporated extract with 200 microliters of methanol

Complete step list: Add IDU label to cell suspension at 25 micromolar final concentration
2. Mix cell suspension
3. Incubate cells for 20 minutes at 38°C and 5% CO₂
4. Add CLDU label to cell suspension at 250 micromolar final concentration
5. Mix cell suspension
6. Wash DT40 cells with ice-cold PBS
7. Suspend cell pellets in cold PBS and maintain on ice
8. Spot 2 microliters of cell suspension onto one end of glass slide
9. Air dry until drop volume is greatly reduced but not completely dry
10. Add 7 microliters of fiber lysis solution on top of cell suspension
11. Gently mix solutions by stirring with pipette tip
12. Incubate for 2 minutes
13. Tilt slide to 15 degrees to allow fibers to spread along slide
14. Place slide horizontally to air dry after fiber solution reaches bottom
15. Mark beginning of stretched fibers with pencil
16. Immerse slides in 3:1 methanol to acetic acid solution for 10 minutes
17. Wash slides in distilled water
18. Immerse slides in 2.5 M hydrochloric acid for 80 minutes
19. Wash slides three times in PBS for five minutes with dabbing
20. Collect excess PBS onto paper towel
21. Orient slides horizontally
22. Pipette 5% BSA in PBS solution onto each slide
23. Place cover slip on each slide
24. Incubate slides for 20 minutes
25. Gently move cover slip down glass slide
26. Remove excess BSA using paper towel
27. Pipette 50 microliters anti-BRDU primary antibody solution onto each slide
28. Place cover slip on each slide
29. Incubate slides in humidified chamber for 2 hours
30. Remove cover slips from slides
31. Wash slides three times in PBS (5 minutes per wash)
32. Apply 50 microliters of secondary antibody solution to slides
33. Position cover slips on slides
34. Protect slides from light
35. Incubate slides for 1 hour
36. Remove cover slips from slides
37. Wash slides three times in PBS (5 minutes per wash)
38. Add drop of VectorShield mounting medium to each slide
39. Gently press cover slip onto slide
40. Remove excess fluid around cover slip
41. Seal cover slips with transparent nail polish
42. Air dry sealed slides
43. Place drop of immersion oil onto slide near pencil mark
44. Start locating DNA fibers using immersion oil
45. Move microscope away from main fiber bundle to find areas with clearly separated fibers
46. Take approximately 10 pictures of each sample using one color channel to avoid bias
47. Move along slide to capture images from different areas for representative sampling

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Enter nucleotide sequence in empty box
2. In Basic Options box, select minimum free energy and avoid isolated base pairs
3. In Output Options box, select interactive RNA secondary structure plot
4. Click Proceed button
5. Select three different 21 to 24-nucleotide-long sequences within mRNA of interest
6. Prioritize sequences in regions lacking extensive secondary structures and located in 3-prime untranslated regions
7. Select regions with guanidine/cytosine ratio close to 50% and lacking nucleotide tandem repeats
8. Design 2-prime-methoxy modified RNA oligonucleotides bearing biotin moiety at 3-prime end
9. Ensure oligonucleotides are fully complementary to selected target mRNA regions
10. Use online tool to adjust RNA hybrid melting temperature to 60-65°C
11. Optimize linguistic sequence complexity using online tool
12. Select Nucleotide BLAST
13. Insert ASO sequence into query box
14. Select target organism database
15. Keep BLAST parameters as default
16. Initiate BLAST analysis
17. Mix 2 μg reporter gene with 20 μL transfection reagent
18. Add transfection mixture to HEK293 cells (70% confluency in 10-cm dish)
19. Incubate transfected cells at 37°C for 48 hours
20. Remove culture medium using serological pipette
21. Wash cells twice with 10 milliliters of PBS pre-warmed to 37 degrees Celsius
22. Add six milliliters of PBS to dish
23. Place dish on ice
24. Expose cells to UV light at 100 millijoules per square centimeter in UV-crosslinker
25. Scrape off cells and PBS from dish and transfer to 15-milliliter tube
26. Centrifuge cells at 250 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
27. Remove supernatant using pipette
28. Resuspend cell pellet in 2 ml pre-chilled lysis buffer by pipetting up/down 5-6 times while maintaining tube on ice
29. Transfer lysate to 5 ml tube using pipette while keeping tube on ice
30. Sonicate lysate for 3 rounds (20-second bursts at 10-micron amplitude with 30-second ice-cooling intervals between rounds)
31. Transfer lysate to 2 ml tubes
32. Centrifuge lysate at 15,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
33. Collect supernatant and transfer to new tube
34. Equilibrate 1 mg oligo(dT)-coupled magnetic beads in 500 μl lysis buffer
35. Remove tube from rotator
36. Place tube on magnetic rack
37. Remove lysis buffer
38. Combine 4 mg HEK293 protein extract with oligo(dT)25-coupled magnetic beads
39. Mix samples vigorously
40. Incubate mixture for 10 minutes at 25°C
41. Place tubes on magnetic stand for 10 seconds
42. Remove supernatant
43. Keep supernatant on ice for subsequent recovery rounds
44. Add 500 microliters of wash buffer A to beads
45. Vortex mixture for five seconds
46. Collect beads using magnet
47. Wash beads twice with 500 microliters of wash buffer B
48. Add 30 microliters of 10-millimolar Tris-HCl to beads
49. Incubate tube at 80 degrees Celsius for two minutes with continuous shaking at 1,000 RPM
50. Transfer tube to magnetic stand and collect eluate within 10 seconds to prevent RNA rebinding
51. Add 30 microliters of streptavidin-coupled magnetic beads to 1 mL binding and wash buffer containing 0.1 mg/mL E. coli tRNA
52. Equilibrate bead mixture on rotator for 1 hour at room temperature
53. Wash beads three times with 750 microliters binding and wash buffer
54. Vortex tube containing washed beads
55. Remove binding and wash buffer from tube
56. Resuspend beads in 30 microliters buffer
57. Store resuspended beads on ice until use
58. Dilute approximately 35 micrograms of total protein from poly(A) isolation in 100 microliters of binding and wash buffer
59. Add 200 picomoles of appropriate antisense oligonucleotide
60. Incubate mixture at 70 degrees Celsius for 5 minutes to facilitate annealing
61. Remove heat block from device and place at room temperature for 10 minutes to cool slowly
62. Add 30 microliters of equilibrated streptavidin-coupled magnetic beads to sample
63. Incubate mixture for 30 minutes at 25 degrees Celsius with constant shaking at 950 rpm
64. Place tube on magnetic stand and remove supernatant
65. Wash beads three times with 750 microliters of pre-warmed binding and wash buffer at 55 degrees Celsius
66. Add 20 microliters of 10-millimolar Tris-HCl
67. Place sample at 90 degrees Celsius with constant shaking at 950 rpm for 10 minutes to elute RNA
68. Place tube in magnetic stand
69. Immediately collect eluate
