question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Harvest human endothelial EAHY 926 cells at 95% confluency
2. Wash cells with 10 milliliters of PBS to remove growth media
3. Incubate cells in trypsin-EDTA solution for detachment
4. Transfer detached cells to conical tube
5. Centrifuge cells
6. Resuspend cells in 25 ml of complete DMEM medium at 1 million cells/ml concentration
7. Place one Thermon cover slip in each well of 24-well tissue culture plate using sterile forceps
8. Orient cover slip with opaque/cell-adherent side facing upwards
9. Add 500 microliters of cell suspension to each well
10. Incubate plate at 37°C for 48 hours or until cells reach confluency
11. Use clean forceps to remove each cover slip
12. Dip cover slips in DMEM to remove soluble secreted proteins
13. Transfer washed cover slips to individual wells of fresh 24-well plate containing 490 microliters per well of complete DMEM while maintaining orientation (cells facing upward)
14. Prepare 10 milliliter culture of Staphylococcus aureus in brain heart infusion broth
15. Transfer bacterial culture into conical tube
16. Centrifuge bacteria at 5,000 times G for 10 minutes
17. Remove supernatant after pelleting cells
18. Resuspend pellet in 10 milliliters of sterile DMEM
19. Centrifuge cells again to repeat wash
20. Perform wash procedure three times total
21. Resuspend bacteria in DMEM after final wash
22. Measure optical density of bacterial suspension
23. Adjust concentration to obtain OD600 of 1.0 (approximately 10^9 CFU/mL)
24. Add 10 microliters of bacterial suspension to each well of 24-well plate containing cover slips
25. Incubate plate at 37 degrees Celsius for 15-90 minutes to allow bacterial attachment and invasion
26. Aspirate supernatant from each well and replace with 10% FBS-DMEM supplemented with Gentamycin
27. Incubate plate at 37 degrees Celsius for 60 minutes to kill extracellular bacteria
28. Wash each cover slip in DMEM three times to remove antibiotic
29. Transfer cover slips to new 24-well plate containing 500 microliters of 0.5% Triton X-100 in PBS per well
30. Maintain cover slips with cell-covered side facing up during transfer
31. Vigorously rinse each cover slip by pipetting Triton solution up and down directly onto cover slip to lyse cells and release intracellular bacteria
32. Perform serial dilutions of the lysate to quantify infecting bacteria
33. Collect lysate and plate serial dilutions on TSA plates

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 2 microliters of 1 mg/mL antibody to 200 microliters of cell lysates
2. Incubate lysate-antibody mixture at 4°C for 1 hour while rocking
3. Wash protein A-Sepharose beads 2-3 times by adding lysis buffer
4. Centrifuge beads at 4°C for 30 seconds to 1 minute at 5000 × g
5. Remove supernatant from beads using pipet
6. Resuspend beads in buffer
7. Add 30 microliters of 50% protein A-Sepharose bead slurry in lysis buffer to lysates
8. Incubate lysate-bead mixture at 4°C for 1 hour while rocking
9. Perform touch spin at 4°C to pellet beads
10. Remove supernatant after bead pelleting
11. Wash beads three times with 1 mL bead wash buffer including touch spinning
12. Wash beads once with 1X kinase reaction buffer
13. Perform touch spin prior to buffer removal
14. Remove maximum buffer without disturbing beads
15. Add entire reaction mixture to kinase sample
16. Incubate reaction at 30 degrees Celsius
17. Place reaction on ice
18. Add 7.5 microliters of 5X Laemmli sample buffer
19. Heat reaction at 100 degrees Celsius for 3-5 minutes in heat block
20. Load 20 microliters of sample per well onto 10-15% SDS-PAGE gel
21. Shield gel apparatus to limit phosphorus-32 radiation exposure
22. Run SDS-PAGE gel to separate kinase and substrate by molecular weight
23. Remove gel from glass and aluminum plates
24. Place gel in 50 milliliters of Coomassie stain
25. Shake gel on orbital shaker at 50 RPM for one hour
26. Transfer gel from stain to fixing solution
27. Place foam pieces or knotted laboratory wipes in container with gel
28. Rock gel in 600-700 milliliters of fixing solution on orbital shaker at 50 RPM overnight
29. Remove gel from fixing solution
30. Soak gel in 200 milliliters of methanol with gentle agitation
31. Continue agitation for 1-2 minutes until gel turns milky white
32. Wet a 14 cm × 14 cm piece of qualitative filter paper with methanol
33. Place methanol-treated filter paper onto slab gel vacuum dryer
34. Lay gel onto filter paper with front side facing up
35. Cover gel carefully with plastic wrap
36. Turn on and run vacuum dryer to apply vacuum
37. Roll out air bubbles with soft rubber brayer after vacuum attainment
38. Continue vacuum drying run for 90 minutes
39. Remove dried gel
40. Attach autorad marker (e.g., phosphorescent ruler or dot) to filter paper on gel side
41. Place gel in cassette containing intensifying screen
42. Use Geiger counter to check radioactive signal intensity
43. Place X-ray film on top of dried gel inside film cassette with intensifying screen
44. Remove film from cassette after exposure
45. Develop film in medical/X-ray film processor
46. Mark bands corresponding to protein standards on film
47. Label protein standards and reaction lanes for reference
48. Excise bands from gel corresponding to bands of interest on film
49. Place excised bands in seven milliliter scintillation vials
50. Add four milliliters of scintillation fluid to vials
51. Count bands using liquid scintillation counter
52. Confirm scintillation counter is set to phosphorus-32 energy spectrum window

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Weigh 100 grams of Taiwanese green propolis
2. Grind propolis into fine powder using spice grinder (no large particles)
3. Set out five flasks and add 100 milliliters of various ethanol concentrations to each
4. Mix 10 grams of ground propolis into ethanol solution in each flask
5. Incubate flasks at 25°C with shaking at 250 RPM for 48 hours
6. Filter ethanol extracts using 25 micrometer pore size filter paper
7. Reconstitute filtrates to original volume of 100 milliliters with 95% ethanol using volumetric flask
8. Store reconstituted extracts at -20°C until ready to use
9. Concentrate 10 milliliters of extract using vacuum evaporation at 40 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes
10. Bake dry matter at 45 degrees Celsius for 24 hours
11. Reconstitute dry matter sample with 10 milliliters of 95% ethanol
12. Filter extract using 0.22 micrometer syringe filter and collect filtrate for HPLC analysis
13. Prepare 1 liter of mobile phase methanol-to-water solution at 88.8:11.2 ratio
14. Prepare serial dilutions of propolin standard concentrations using mobile phase as solvent
15. Inject 20 microliters of each standard concentration into reverse phase columns
16. Sequence injections from lowest to highest concentration
17. Set HPLC column temperature to 30 degrees Celsius
18. Set UV detector wavelength to 280 nanometers
19. Set flow rate to 1 milliliter per minute and recorder time to 20 minutes
20. Analyze standards in triplicate
21. Inject 20 microliters of each extract into reverse phase column
22. Set HPLC column temperature to 30 degrees Celsius
23. Set HPLC flow rate to 1 milliliter per minute
24. Set UV detector wavelength to 280 nanometers
25. Set recorder time to 20 minutes
26. Analyze extracts at least three times
27. Use standard curve to determine concentration
28. Add 10 microliters of diluted ethanol extract (0.156-640 μg/mL) to wells of 96-well plate
29. Adjust well volumes to 100 microliters using broth while maintaining 5% DMSO concentration
30. Inoculate 100 microliters of bacterial culture into each well of 96-well plate
31. Incubate plate at 37°C for 48 hours
32. Measure bacterial growth turbidity at 590 nm using microplate reader to determine MIC
33. Inoculate 10 microliters of liquid culture from no-growth wells onto agar plate
34. Incubate agar plate at 37 degrees Celsius for 24 hours

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place eight-day-old specific pathogen-free embryonated chicken eggs in rotating egg tray with stamped ends facing upward
2. Set rotating tray inside egg incubator set to 36°C with 50% humidity
3. Incubate eggs for 48 hours
4. Place incubated eggs in egg rack with stamped ends up
5. Transfer egg rack to laminar flow hood
6. Turn off room and hood lights
7. Hold stamped end of egg lightly to egg candler to expose vasculature of chorioallantoic membrane (CAM) and air sack
8. Make a mark between two major blood vessels with a pencil
9. Make a hole at egg tip above air sack at stamped end using sterile push pin (3mm deep)
10. Make a second hole at pencil mark using sterile push pin (3mm deep)
11. Squeeze air from safety bulb
12. Press safety bulb firmly against hole above air sack
13. Slowly release bulb to apply suction at pencil mark hole
14. Confirm air sack displacement to pencil mark using candler
15. Make both holes deeper if air sack displacement unsuccessful
16. Deepen both holes slightly
17. Reapply suction using safety bulb
18. Hold egg with one hand and position rotary tool with 1.5-1.6 inch cutoff wheel attachment
19. Make two transverse cuts (2 cm length, 1 cm apart) through shell without touching CAM
20. Make longitudinal cuts at ends of transverse cuts by lightly touching cutoff wheel to shell
21. Slide sterile forceps under and parallel to shell piece
22. Grab shell piece with forceps and cleanly remove it
23. Place basement membrane protein mixture (e.g., matrigel) on ice to prevent polymerization
24. Use 4-5 ml of 2 mM EDTA solution per flask to detach tumor cells
25. Place flask in tissue culture incubator for 5 minutes
26. Resuspend detached cells in medium
27. Count cell number
28. Transfer volume required for 500,000-2 million cells to new tube
29. Centrifuge tube at 500 × g for 5 minutes
30. Discard supernatant
31. Resuspend cell pellet in 40 μl PBS containing calcium
32. Peel tape off the egg
33. Drop sterile 1 mm silicone ring from cryogenic vial through opening onto CAM
34. Pipette 60 microliter solution of cells, PBS plus plus, and basement membrane mixture into center of silicone ring
35. Retape the egg window
36. Carefully move egg tray back to incubator
37. Allow tumors to grow for 5-7 days
38. Place underpad and biohazard bag in hood
39. Prepare 35 mm × 10 mm plates containing 1 ml PBS plus plus and paraformaldehyde containers
40. Insert pointed end of sterile dissection scissors into egg at hole near stamped end and cut around length of entire egg
41. Extract tumor from CAM using dissection scissors and forceps
42. Place tumor in 35 mm × 10 mm plate
43. Measure and weigh tumor
44. Fix tumor in paraformaldehyde for paraffin embedding
45. Mince harvested tumor in plate using dissection scissors
46. Transfer minced tumor to 15 mL centrifuge tube
47. Remove PBS plus plus from tube by pipetting
48. Add 2 mL of 1 mg/mL collagenase solution in PBS plus plus
49. Mix solution by inverting tube several times
50. Incubate tube at 37°C for 30 minutes to several hours
51. Add 5 mL medium to tube and mix by pipetting 20-40 times
52. Transfer supernatant to fresh tube after sedimentation
53. Centrifuge at 500 × g for 5 minutes and discard supernatant

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place eight-day-old specific pathogen-free embryonated chicken eggs in rotating egg tray with stamped ends facing upward
2. Set rotating tray inside egg incubator set to 36°C with 50% humidity
3. Incubate eggs for 48 hours
4. Place incubated eggs in egg rack with stamped ends up
5. Transfer egg rack to laminar flow hood
6. Turn off room and hood lights
7. Hold stamped end of egg lightly to egg candler to expose vasculature of chorioallantoic membrane (CAM) and air sack
8. Make a mark between two major blood vessels with a pencil
9. Make a hole at egg tip above air sack at stamped end using sterile push pin (3mm deep)
10. Make a second hole at pencil mark using sterile push pin (3mm deep)
11. Squeeze air from safety bulb
12. Press safety bulb firmly against hole above air sack
13. Slowly release bulb to apply suction at pencil mark hole
14. Confirm air sack displacement to pencil mark using candler
15. Make both holes deeper if air sack displacement unsuccessful
16. Deepen both holes slightly
17. Reapply suction using safety bulb
18. Hold egg with one hand and position rotary tool with 1.5-1.6 inch cutoff wheel attachment
19. Make two transverse cuts (2 cm length, 1 cm apart) through shell without touching CAM
20. Make longitudinal cuts at ends of transverse cuts by lightly touching cutoff wheel to shell
21. Slide sterile forceps under and parallel to shell piece
22. Grab shell piece with forceps and cleanly remove it
23. Place basement membrane protein mixture (e.g., matrigel) on ice to prevent polymerization
24. Use 4-5 ml of 2 mM EDTA solution per flask to detach tumor cells
25. Place flask in tissue culture incubator for 5 minutes
26. Resuspend detached cells in medium
27. Count cell number
28. Transfer volume required for 500,000-2 million cells to new tube
29. Centrifuge tube at 500 × g for 5 minutes
30. Discard supernatant
31. Resuspend cell pellet in 40 μl PBS containing calcium
32. Peel tape off the egg
33. Drop sterile 1 mm silicone ring from cryogenic vial through opening onto CAM
34. Pipette 60 microliter solution of cells, PBS plus plus, and basement membrane mixture into center of silicone ring
35. Retape the egg window
36. Carefully move egg tray back to incubator
37. Allow tumors to grow for 5-7 days
38. Place underpad and biohazard bag in hood
39. Prepare 35 mm × 10 mm plates containing 1 ml PBS plus plus and paraformaldehyde containers
40. Insert pointed end of sterile dissection scissors into egg at hole near stamped end and cut around length of entire egg
41. Extract tumor from CAM using dissection scissors and forceps
42. Place tumor in 35 mm × 10 mm plate
43. Measure and weigh tumor
44. Fix tumor in paraformaldehyde for paraffin embedding
45. Mince harvested tumor in plate using dissection scissors
46. Transfer minced tumor to 15 mL centrifuge tube
47. Remove PBS plus plus from tube by pipetting
48. Add 2 mL of 1 mg/mL collagenase solution in PBS plus plus
49. Mix solution by inverting tube several times
50. Incubate tube at 37°C for 30 minutes to several hours
51. Add 5 mL medium to tube and mix by pipetting 20-40 times
52. Transfer supernatant to fresh tube after sedimentation
53. Centrifuge at 500 × g for 5 minutes and discard supernatant

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Seed adipose-derived stromal cells (ASC) at approximately 80% confluency in conventional tissue culture plates
2. Seed ASC at approximately 80% confluency in PNIPAAm coated tissue culture plates of corresponding size
3. Maintain ASC in complete culture medium at 37°C and 5% CO₂
4. Change culture medium every two days
5. Wrap rim of plunger disk with lab tape at least two times to prevent leakage
6. Spray down 15 ml conical tube rack containing empty uncapped tubes with 70% ethanol in biosafety cabinet
7. Spray each tape-wrapped plunger with 70% ethanol and place into empty conical tube
8. Spray approximately 6.3 gram metal washers and place on rack with hooked forceps
9. Close cabinet sash and turn on UV light for overnight drying/sterilization
10. Add 0.75 grams of gelatin powder to 10 milliliters of HBSS
11. Balance solution pH with 100 microliters of sodium hydroxide
12. Place gelatin solution in 75-degree Celsius water bath
13. Shake tube vigorously every five minutes until powder dissolves
14. Strain homogeneous gelatin solution through syringe filter
15. Apply filtered gelatin solution to plungers
16. Allow gelatin to solidify in plunger mold
17. Unwrap tape from solidified gelatin mold
18. Remove gelatin from outer edge of plunger using hooked forceps
19. Ensure remaining central gelatin is completely level
20. Gently apply gelatin-removed plungers to PNIPAAm coated ASC plates
21. Use metal washers to weigh down plungers
22. Transfer plates to ice-water bath
23. Clean bottom of PNIPAAm coated plates to remove non-sterile water
24. Add cold cell culture medium to one 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube per culture to be seeded
25. Wash large segments of adipose tissue three times in sterile PBS
26. Remove as much PBS as possible between washes
27. Coarsely mince tissue using forceps and sterile razor
28. Remove vasculature and fascia during mincing
29. Continue mincing until solution achieves thick liquid consistency with minimal visible tissue segments
30. Transfer minced tissue to prepared 1.5 ml tubes using modified P1000 micro-pipette tip
31. Briefly mix minced tissue with medium within tubes
32. Replace medium in each ASC culture with one volume of white adipose tissue solution
33. Transfer gelatin plungers to white adipose tissue mixture on base ASC plates
34. Examine monolayers in PNIPAAm coated plates under microscope to confirm cellular detachment
35. Move plates to 37-40°C heat block surface in biosafety cabinet
36. Add 2-3 milliliters of warmed culture medium to incubate cells and facilitate gelatin melting
37. Gently remove plungers from plate surface
38. Cover cultures with appropriate plate lids
39. Place plates in cell culture incubator
40. Aspirate and replace cell culture medium with phenol red-free M199 medium supplemented with insulin, dexamethasone, and antibiotics

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Take polymer precursor and pattern it
2. Convert patterned precursor to carbon at 900-1000°C in inert atmosphere
3. Set hotplate to 95 degrees Celsius
4. Turn on spin coater
5. Turn on connected vacuum pump
6. Program two-step spin coating sequence: first step 10 seconds at 500 rpm, second step 30 seconds at 1,000 rpm, both with 100 rpm/s ramp rate
7. Place silicon wafer or substrate on spin coater vacuum holder
8. Start vacuum to fix substrate to holder
9. Apply sufficient epoxy resin-based negative photoresist to cover substrate surface
10. Run two-step spin coating sequence to achieve 1-250 micron photoresist thickness
11. Release substrate from holder
12. Transfer substrate using tweezers to 95 degrees Celsius hotplate without disturbing thin film
13. Bake substrate at 95 degrees Celsius for 1 to 40 minutes depending on photoresist thickness
14. Turn on photolithography UV system during soft bake
15. Set UV exposure time to 12 seconds
16. Transfer substrate to UV system after soft bake completion
17. Clamp patterned mask with vacuum mask holder
18. Place mask holder with mask on UV exposure system
19. Place photoresist-coated substrate on UV exposure system stage
20. Cover substrate with patterned mask and gently press mask against substrate
21. Expose substrate to UV light for set duration
22. Separate mask from substrate after UV exposure
23. Perform post-exposure bake at 95 degrees Celsius for 1 to 15 minutes
24. Immerse substrate in appropriate developer solution for 1 to 20 minutes with gentle shaking
25. Dry patterned wafer under stream of nitrogen gas or compressed air
26. Inspect wafer under microscope at 50x magnification to verify pattern quality
27. Place patterned wafer in pressurized open-ended tube furnace
28. Set flow rate for first 15 minutes of sequence
29. Set gas flow rate to 1.5 times furnace tube volume per minute for first 15 minutes
30. Set gas flow rate to furnace tube volume per minute for remainder of sequence
31. Program temperature ramp to 300°C at 5°C per minute
32. Program hold at 300°C for 1 hour
33. Program temperature ramp to 900°C at 5°C per minute
34. Program hold at 900°C for 1 hour
35. Program cooling to 300°C at 10°C per minute
36. Initiate heating and cooling sequence
37. Cool pyrolyzed sample to room temperature under nitrogen stream
38. Turn off furnace and gas flow
39. Remove pyrolyzed sample from furnace
40. Proceed to sample characterization
41. Wash human hair sample with deionized water
42. Dry hair sample under stream of nitrogen gas
43. Obtain silicon wafer or ceramic boat
44. Arrange individual hairs in desired configuration on substrate
45. Fix hairs to silicon wafer with epoxy-based polymer (if using wafer)
46. Place sample in pressurized open-ended tube furnace
47. Run pyrolysis sequence for sample stabilization and carbonization
48. Cool sample to room temperature under nitrogen flow
49. Remove sample from furnace using tweezers

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Select plump and undamaged tartary buckwheat seeds
2. Soak seeds in 28°C water for 20 minutes
3. Place 100-200 peeled seeds into sterilized 100 ml conical flask containing 75% ethanol
4. Sterilize seeds in 75% ethanol for 30 seconds
5. Replace ethanol with 5% sodium hypochlorite
6. Decant sodium hypochlorite after 15 minutes
7. Wash seeds with sterile deionized water five times
8. Blot seeds dry with sterile bibulous paper
9. Add 10 tartary buckwheat seeds per bottle to 300 ml plant tissue culture bottles containing 50 ml MSSA medium
10. Germinate seeds in culture room at 25±1°C under light conditions
11. Select robust seedlings with two unfolded cotyledons after 7-10 days
12. Cut seedlings from roots
13. Place seedlings in sterile petri dish
14. Cut hypocotyls into 0.8-1 cm segments
15. Shear cotyledons into ~0.5 cm pieces
16. Pre-culture explants under light conditions for 24 hours
17. Thaw A. rhizogenes culture on ice
18. Evenly plate bacteria onto YEB solid medium with antibiotics
19. Incubate plate at 28°C for 12-16 hours
20. Pick monoclonal colony from incubated plate
21. Culture picked colony in new petri dish
22. Incubate new culture at 28°C for 12-16 hours
23. Inoculate monoclonal colonies in sterilized 100ml conical flask containing 20ml YEB medium with antibiotics
24. Incubate flask at 28°C with 200 RPM shaking for 16-18 hours until OD600 reaches 2.0
25. Transfer A. rhizogenes culture to sterile 50 milliliter conical tube
26. Collect bacteria by centrifugation
27. Resuspend pellet in MSSAS medium
28. Adjust suspension to optical density of 0.2
29. Infuse suspension into flask of explants
30. Remove explants from flask after 10 minutes
31. Blot explants with sterile bibulous paper
32. Place sterile 9cm diameter filter paper onto dish of MSSAAS medium
33. Overlay explants on filter paper
34. Incubate at 25°C in dark for three days
35. Transfer approximately 20 infected explants onto MSSACK medium
36. Vertically incubate plants under light conditions at 25 ± 1°C
37. Select hairy roots demonstrating white appearance and rapid growth
38. Cut selected roots into 2-3 centimeter pieces
39. Number root pieces on clean bench
40. Subculture root pieces in sterilized 100 ml conical flask containing MSSK medium
41. Incubate at 25°C and 80 rpm in dark until roots overspread flask bottom
42. Remove tawny and contaminated hairy roots
43. Select hairy roots with white appearance
44. Check roots for fluorescence under blue/UV transilluminator (if reporter gene used)
45. Select hairy roots exhibiting strong fluorescent signal
46. Dry roots with absorbent paper
47. Wrap roots in marked aluminum foil for immediate analysis
48. Lyophilize roots in liquid nitrogen for long-term storage
49. Place lyophilized roots at -80°C until investigation

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Harvest spinal cord via laminectomy
2. Cut spinal cord transversely into 1 cm segments
3. Dissect paraventricular region from each spinal cord segment
4. Mince tissue into 1 mm pieces
5. Enzymatically dissociate minced tissue
6. Separate intact cells by centrifugation through discontinuous density gradient
7. Plate cell suspension
8. Sterilize rat's skin and remove dorsal surface using large dissection scissors to expose vertebral column
9. Hold dissection scissors perpendicular to dorsal surface and transversely cut vertebral column above hind limbs
10. Use small scissors to longitudinally cut dorsal muscle over vertebral column in rostral direction to expose spinous processes
11. Insert small blunt bone cutting instrument extraorally into lateral aspect of spinal canal between spinal cord and vertebral column starting at exposed caudal end
12. Place blade shallow and parallel to spinal cord
13. Make small cuts into lamina
14. Peel away lamina to expose spinal cord
15. Continue removing lamina in rostral direction to expose thoracic and cervical spinal cord
16. Using blunt tissue forceps, gently lift spinal cord from vertebral column
17. Sever spinal roots with micro scissors to release cord
18. Place excised spinal cord in Petri dish containing ice-cold sterile rat dissection buffer
19. Rinse tissue with ice cold dissecting buffer
20. Cut spinal cord transversely into 1cm segments using scissors
21. Hold tissue segment with fine forceps
22. Use micro scissors to carefully remove white matter and most gray matter
23. Isolate periventricular region of spinal cord
24. Transfer dissected periventricular tissue to 10cm sterile Petri dish containing ice cold rat dissection buffer
25. Mince dissected periventricular tissue into 1 cubic millimeter pieces using micro scissors
26. Add 5 milliliters of Earl's balanced salt solution to vial of papain from dissociation kit
27. Incubate vial at 37 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes until papain dissolves and solution appears clear
28. Add 500 microliters of Earl's balanced salt solution to vial of DNAs
29. Gently mix DNAs solution
30. Add 250 microliters of DNA solution to vial containing papain
31. Gently mix papain-DNA solution
32. Add approximately 0.2 grams minced tissue to vial
33. Place vial on rocker platform at 37 degrees Celsius for 45 minutes to one hour
34. Triturate mixture with 10 milliliter pipette to dissociate tissue pieces
35. Yield cloudy cell suspension
36. Transfer cell suspension into sterile 15 milliliter conical tube
37. Centrifuge at 300 G for 5 minutes at room temperature
38. Prepare OVO moid solution by mixing 2.7 milliliters of Earl's balanced salt solution with 300 microliters of reconstituted albumin OVO MOID inhibitor solution in 15 milliliter conical tube
39. Add 150 microliters of DNA solution to OVO moid solution
40. Mix solution by inverting tube
41. Discard supernatant from pelleted cells
42. Immediately resuspend cell pellet in diluted DNA albumin inhibitor mixture
43. Add 5 ml of albumin inhibitor solution to a 15 ml tube
44. Gently layer cell suspension on top of albumin inhibitor solution using a 5 ml pipette
45. Centrifuge sample at 70 g for 6 minutes at room temperature
46. Discard supernatant and resuspend cell pellet in 1 ml of prewarmed rat EFH medium
47. Count live cell density using a hemocytometer
48. Plate cells into T25 culture flask at density of 10 cells/μl in EFH medium
49. Incubate flasks at 37°C with 5% CO₂ for one week without disturbance

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place gallium-68 labeling kit on labeling module according to manufacturer specifications
2. Position materials in correct locations on module
3. Attach solutions from gallium-68 labeling kit to manifolds
4. Attach sterile vial with non-vented filter to labeling module output
5. Place vial in lead shield rated for positron emitters
6. Connect output of gallium-68 generator to input of labeling module
7. Dissolve 50 micrograms of peptide analog into 1.5 milliliters of 1.5 molar HEPES buffer solution
8. Transfer dissolved peptide solution to reaction vial
9. Close lead shielding around labeling module
10. Start gallium-68 DOTATATE production via tablet computer attached to labeling module
11. Remove needles with filters from glass vial
12. Close lead shield around vial
13. Place vial in dose calibrator
14. Measure activity of gallium-68 DOTATATE
15. Note activity reference time
16. Remove 0.5 milliliters of labeled peptide from vial for quality control
17. Prepare syringes for patient administration
18. Visually inspect gallium-68 DOTATATE product to ensure colorless liquid without particles
19. Measure pH of solution using pH indicator strip
20. Add 20 microliters of labeled peptide to 500 microliters of water
21. Homogenize dilution by careful shaking of vial
22. Add 5 microliters of dilution to 7cm × 1cm instant thin-layer chromatography silica gel glass fiber paper strip, 1.5cm from bottom
23. Place paper in tube containing 2mL of 50:50 methanol to 1M ammonium acetate solution, ensuring no peptide-solution contact
24. Close tube to prevent evaporation
25. Wait until solvent travels at least 5 cm above peptide application point
26. Cut chromatography paper in half
27. Place bottom and upper paper halves in separate tubes
28. Place tubes in well counter
29. Determine counts in each vial for 30 seconds in 400-600 eV energy window
30. Dilute 20 microliters of gallium-68 DOTATATE in 1 mL of 5 mM EDTA
31. Dilute 10 microliters of peptide EDTA solution in 1 mL of sterile water
32. Place diluted solution in well counter for radioactivity counting
33. Slowly flush C-18 cartridge with 1 mL of 100% ethanol
34. Flush C-18 cartridge with 1 mL of sterile water
35. Flush diluted sample through C-18 cartridge into collection tube
36. Rinse sample tube with 1 mL of water
37. Flush water wash through column into collection tube
38. Place collecting tube, empty sample tube, and syringe in well counter
39. Determine counts in each material using 400-600 eV energy window for 30 seconds
40. Obtain short medical history of patient including date of last somatostatin analog administration
41. Place intravenous cannula in patient's arm
42. Flush catheter with saline to verify cannula placement
43. Inject 100 megabecquerels of gallium-68 DOTATATE into cannula
44. Place patient on PET/CT scanner with arms above head
45. Instruct patient to remain still throughout exam
46. Acquire survey image with patient in position
47. Select scan area from pituitary gland to mid-thigh
48. Perform low-dose CT scan with parameters: 40 milliamps, 140 electron volts, 5 millimolar slices
49. Perform PET scan starting at patient's head with 150 seconds per bed position

Complete step list: Add 5.00 grams of methyl 4-nitrobenzoate to oven-dried 250 milliliter two-neck round-bottom flask

3. Add magnetic stir-bar to flask

6. Perform three vacuum-refill cycles to replace air with nitrogen gas
7. Add 138 milliliters of anhydrous tetrahydrofuran to flask using airtight syringe
8. Cool and stir reaction mixture at 0°C
9. Add 1.47 milliliters of hydrazine monohydrate drop-wise using airtight syringe at 0°C

11. Confirm complete consumption of methyl 4-nitrobenzoate
12. Filter reaction mixture through Celite pad in 60 mL frit Buchner funnel using vacuum filtration
13. Wash filter with 20 milliliters of ethyl acetate three times
14. Concentrate filtrate in vacuo using rotary evaporator to afford crude methyl 4-hydroxyamino benzoate
15. Add 2.55 grams sodium bicarbonate to oven-dried 500ml two-neck round-bottom flask
16. Add all crude methyl 4-hydroxyamino benzoate to flask
17. Add stir-bar to flask
18. Cap one flask neck with septum
19. Connect other flask neck to nitrogen-vacuum manifold
20. Perform three vacuum-refill cycles to replace air with nitrogen
21. Add 138ml anhydrous diethyl ether using airtight syringe
22. Cool reaction mixture to 0°C and stir for 15 minutes
23. Prepare solution of 2.17 milliliters acetyl chloride and 138 milliliters anhydrous diethyl ether
24. Add solution to reaction mixture at 0°C using syringe pump at 10.0 milliliters per hour

26. Wash filter with 20 milliliters ethyl acetate three times

28. Purify crude product with flash-column chromatography eluting with mixture of hexane and ethyl acetate

32. Place magnetic stir-bar into oven-dried 250 ml round-bottom flask inside glove box
33. Add 95.6 milliliters of dried and degassed chloroform to reaction flask

36. Filter reaction mixture through filter funnel to remove solid residue
37. Concentrate filtrate in vacuo using rotary evaporator

39. Add 2.51 grams of methyl 4-trifluoromethoxy acetamido benzoate, magnetic stir-bar, and 9.05 milliliters of anhydrous nitromethane to 15 milliliter pressure vessel
40. Cap pressure vessel with screw cap

42. Transfer cooled reaction mixture to 100 milliliter round-bottom flask
43. Concentrate reaction mixture in vacuo using rotary evaporator
44. Purify crude product using flash-column chromatography eluting with hexanes/ethyl acetate mixture
45. Obtain 2.13 grams of methyl 4-acetamido 3-trifluoromethoxy benzoate
46. Characterize new compounds by proton-carbon-13 NMR spectroscopy and high-resolution mass spectroscopy
47. Characterize fluorine-containing compounds using fluorine-19 NMR spectroscopy

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Homogenize 100 grams of fresh radish using kitchen blender for 10 minutes
2. Transfer resulting slurry to 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
3. Centrifuge at 14,000 × g at 4°C for 20 minutes to remove insoluble material
4. Transfer supernatant to new 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
5. Add equal volume of 2M trichloroacetic acid solution
6. Centrifuge at 14,000 × g at 4°C for 30 minutes
7. Remove supernatant
8. Wash pellet with 20mL deionized water
9. Centrifuge at 14,000 × g at 4°C for 5 minutes
10. Resuspend pellet in 1mL deionized water
11. Transfer protein solution to fresh 1.5mL centrifuge tube
12. Mix 50 microliters of protein solution with 0.2 milliunits of recombinant PNGase H+ in ten millimolar acetic acid
13. Incubate mixture at 37 degrees Celsius for 12 hours
14. Centrifuge mixture at 14,000 × g for five minutes to remove residual protein and enzyme
15. Transfer supernatant to fresh 1.5 milliliter centrifuge tube
16. Wash solid phase extraction column with 3 mL water
17. Activate column with 3 mL of 80% acetonitrile containing trifluoroacetic acid
18. Equilibrate column with 3 mL deionized water
19. Transfer sample onto column
20. Discard flow-through
21. Wash column with 1.5 mL deionized water
22. Add 1.5 milliliters of aqueous 20% acetonitrile solution containing 0.1% TFA to elute N-glycans into a two milliliter tube
23. Remove solvent by centrifugal evaporation at room temperature
24. Evaporate sample until completely dry
25. Add 5 microliters of prepared 2-AB solution to dried samples
26. Vortex samples until completely dissolved
27. Incubate samples at 65 degrees Celsius for 2 hours
28. Cool samples to room temperature for 5 minutes
29. Add 5 microliters of deionized water and 40 microliters of acetonitrile to cooled samples
30. Centrifuge samples at 14,000 × g for 3 minutes
31. Transfer 48 microliters of supernatant to 300 microliter HPLC vial
32. Store samples at -20 degrees Celsius
33. Connect UPLC glycan column to online fluorescence detector
34. Set fluorescence detector excitation wavelength to 330 nm and emission wavelength to 420 nm
35. Set column temperature to 60 degrees Celsius
36. Inject 45 microliters of sample into UPLC system
37. Elute N-glycans by UPLC using 15-40 minute retention window
38. Collect each UPLC peak fraction into 2-milliliter centrifuge tubes
39. Dry samples using centrifugal evaporation
40. Inject 1 picomole of 2-AB labeled dextran standard for calibration, elution time assignment, and glucose unit standardization
41. Dissolve dried samples in 5 microliters of LCMS grade water
42. Mix 1 microliter of dissolved sample with 1 microliter of ATT solution
43. Pipet mixture into MALDI-TOF sample carrier
44. Load sample carrier into MALDI-TOF mass spectrometer for analysis

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 5% sulfinitated polyetheretherketone fibers to 250 milliliter round bottom flask
2. Dissolve fibers in dimethylacetamide solvent
3. Shake flask for 10 minutes to settle ionomer polymers
4. Place mixture into silicon oil bath with magnetic stir bar
5. Vigorously stir solution at 500 rpm for 24 hours at 80°C
6. Filter 30 milliliters of solution through 0.45 micrometer PTFE filter into circular 18 centimeter diameter glass dish
7. Remove bubbles using air blower
8. Place dish in oven at 90 degrees Celsius for 24 hours to generate approximately 50 micrometer thick freestanding membrane
9. Fill dish with warm distilled water
10. Wait 10 minutes for membrane detachment
11. Immerse membrane in 1M sulfuric acid at 80°C for 2 hours
12. Wash membrane three times with 1L distilled water (10 minutes per wash at room temperature)
13. Dissolve 10% by weight FAA-3 ionomer in NMP solvent at room temperature with 500 RPM stirring for 2 hours
14. Filter approximately 30 ml of solution through 100 micron pore strainer into 18 cm glass Petri dish
15. Place dish in 100°C oven for 24 hours after air bubble removal
16. Extract dried membrane using hot distilled water
17. Activate membrane in 1 L sodium hydroxide solution for 2 hours
18. Wash activated membrane three times with 1 L distilled water per wash
19. Position 3 cm thick PMMA plate with electrode facing up
20. Place rubber gasket and spacer onto electrode
21. Place cation exchange membrane
22. Place anion exchange membrane on opposite side of gasket
23. Place silicon gasket and spacer onto each membrane
24. Position second PMMA plate onto spacers and gaskets
25. Secure stack using digital wrench drive at 25 newton meter force with nuts, bolts, and washers
26. Place iridium/ruthenium-coated titanium mesh electrode at end of each plate
27. Connect electrodes to source meter using crocodile clips
28. Add 5 liters of 0.01 M sodium chloride solution to a large container connected to a peristaltic pump
29. Add 5 liters of 0.6 M sodium chloride solution to a separate large container connected to a peristaltic pump
30. Stir both sodium chloride solutions continuously at room temperature
31. Add 0.05 M rinse solution to a third container
32. Connect all three containers to the RED assembly via rubber tubes through the peristaltic pump and pressure gauges
33. Set rinse solution flow rate to 50 mL/min
34. Set salt solutions flow rate to 100 mL/min
35. Check tubing to eliminate crossflow or leakage

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Disinfect operating surface with 70% ethanol
2. Sterilize all surgical instruments using hot bead sterilizer
3. Adjust homeothermic blanket system temperature to maintain 37±1°C
4. Prepare cotton gauze and applicators for bleeding management
5. Record weight and identifying characteristics of healthy animals scheduled for surgery
6. Administer dose of buprenorphine to animals
7. Anesthetize the rat
8. Shave fur from neck and chest areas
9. Disinfect shaved areas with Betadine solution
10. Apply 70% ethanol to disinfected areas
11. Repeat disinfection cycle (Betadine + ethanol) three times
12. Place animal on homeothermic blanket system
13. Position animal lying on its right side
14. Bevel edges of polyethylene endotracheal tube
15. Insert endotracheal tube using surgical stereo microscope while manipulating tongue and visualizing trachea
16. Connect endotracheal tube to ventilator set at 80 breaths/minute and 1.2 mL/100g body weight tidal volume
17. Place sterile drape over rat with surgical area exposed
18. Open chest at left fourth intercostal space while minimizing tissue cutting to reduce bleeding risk
19. Position chest retractor within fourth intercostal space and spread ribs to expose left ventricle (LV) while avoiding lung damage
20. Open pericardium and leave in place
21. Identify proximal left anterior descending (LAD) artery using surgical stereo microscope
22. Ligate LAD with slip knot to induce ischemia while avoiding heart exteriorization
23. Confirm LAD ligation by observing myocardial tissue blanching distal to suture and anterior wall dysfunction
24. Begin cardiac reperfusion after 30-minute ischemic period
25. Release ligature around LAD using microsurgical scissors to initiate cardiac reperfusion
26. Verify reperfusion by observing return of red color to myocardial tissue and recovery of anterior wall motion
27. Remove chest retractor
28. Suture ribs together using interrupted pattern
29. Create negative chest pressure by drawing air from chest cavity
30. Close skin with continuous suture pattern
31. Transfer rat to recovery cage with heating pad
32. Monitor body temperature (rectal thermometer), respiratory rate (visual inspection), heart rate (palpation), and pain signs until ambulatory
33. Administer sterile saline
34. Administer buprenorphine if signs of dehydration observed
35. Place re-anesthetized rat into surgical area
36. Visualize left ventricle
37. Ligate LAD or retie slipknot
38. Inject 1 mL of 5% Evans blue and saline into right ventricular chamber
39. Allow Evans blue solution to perfuse for 2 minutes
40. Inject 1 mL saturated potassium chloride solution into right ventricular chamber to stop heart
41. Rapidly excise heart
42. Embed heart in warmed 2% agar phosphate-buffered saline
43. Wait for agar to solidify at room temperature
44. Cut heart into 1-2 mm sections
45. Stain viable myocardium with 2% triphenyltetrazolium chloride (TTC) for 20 minutes
46. Capture image of each heart slice

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Open thoracic cavity
2. Clear blood from vasculature through intraoral perfusion of PBS with heparin
3. Inject casting agent into left ventricle to perfuse circulation system
4. Remove perfused brain and fix in Paraformaldehyde
5. Dehydrate and clarify brain tissue with ethanol and methyl salicylate
6. Anesthetize mouse via intraperitoneal injection of ketamine and xylazine diluted in saline
7. Confirm full anesthesia by paw pinch test
8. Make chest incision below xiphoid process using scissors
9. Cut through diaphragm
10. Cut between dorsal and ventral segments of ribcage
11. Fold up sternum and adjacent chest wall
12. Secure folded tissue with hemostat to expose pericardial sac
13. Open pericardial sac using tweezers if necessary
14. Open pericardial sac with tweezers to expose heart
15. Begin perfusing PBS with 5 units/mL heparin at 37°C using pump set to 100 mmHg
16. Insert blunt 22-gauge needle connected to pump outflow into left ventricle near heart apex
17. Cut open right atrium to enable blood outflow and continue perfusion until blood is removed from circulation
18. Mix 5 mL of diluent with 4 mL of filtered compound
19. Add 450 μL of curing agent to mixture
20. Mix casting agent solution immediately before use
21. Draw casting agent solution into 10 mL syringe
22. Attach blunt 20-gauge needle to syringe
23. Fill attached needle with solution
24. Insert needle into left ventricle through existing hole
25. Secure needle in place with hemostat clamp
26. Manually inject casting agent solution into left ventricle at 3 ml/min
27. Monitor for perfusion indicators: casting agent in intestine/liver vasculature, distal limb discoloration, nose/tongue discoloration
28. Readjust needle position if necessary to achieve proper perfusion
29. Remove brain
30. Place brain in 4% paraformaldehyde overnight at 4°C
31. Dehydrate brain in 25% ethanol at room temperature (initiating 7-day ethanol series)
32. Immerse perfused brain in methyl salicylate for 2 days at room temperature to clarify tissue around blood vessels

2. Resuspend isolated RNA at concentration ≥2 mg/ml

14. Submerge slides in isopropanol
15. Dry slides using centrifuge with slide adapter (100xg, 5 minutes, 22°C)
16. Rinse lifter slips in water
17. Rinse lifter slips in ethanol
18. Air-dry lifter slips
19. Place slide in microarray hybridization chamber base
20. Position lifter slip on slide to cover probe area and white strips
21. Contact slide in dark
22. Spin down precipitated labeled RNA
23. Wash RNA pellet once with 70% ethanol
24. Air dry RNA pellet
25. Prepare hybridization solution using 1/15 to 1/100 volume purified labeled reference DNA per RNA sample
26. Dissolve RNA pellet completely with approximately 60 microliters hybridization solution
27. Add 20 microliters water to each sample during incubation
28. Add 20 microliters of water to each humidifying well in microarray hybridization chamber base
29. Add mixture of labeled RNA and reference DNA to slide
30. Use thin pipette tip to touch edge of lifter slip and apply suction to draw solution into space between slip and slide
31. Place hybridization chamber cover over base
32. Seal chamber with metal clips
33. Place entire chamber inside provided plastic bag
34. Place chamber cassette in container with wet paper towel

39. Wash slides three times in 0.4X SSC buffer for 1-2 minutes total

41. Scan slides with microarray scanner

45. Immerse slide in staining dish containing prewarmed 1 mM NaOH and 0.1X SSC at 62°C
46. Incubate slide in solution for 10-20 minutes
47. Repeat incubation in solution a second time
48. Wash slides several times in water for up to 60 minutes with gentle shaking at 22°C
49. Dry slides using centrifuge

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Thoroughly wash two LCMs with deionized water
2. Clean LCMs in ultrasound bath with deionized water
3. Dry LCMs in oven at 80 degrees Celsius for several minutes
4. Clean LCM holder, O-rings, and sample chamber with acetone and compressed air
5. Place both clean, dry LCMs on clean, dry LCM holder
6. Designate one LCM as reference position and one as sample position
7. Connect LCM holder to oscillator using high temperature resistant electric cables
8. Turn on oscillator
9. Check for presence of resonance frequencies for both LCMs using oscillator software
10. Close and insulate the sample chamber
11. Evacuate the sample chamber
12. Purge chamber with nitrogen gas multiple times
13. Set chamber pressure to lowest test pressure
14. Set chamber temperature to lowest test temperature
15. Measure resonance frequencies of both LCMs using Butterworth Van Dyke equivalent circuit model
16. Determine and record F-0 value
17. Slowly add nitrogen gas to pressurize sample chamber to next test pressure
18. Wait for pressure to stabilize
19. Obtain delta F-0 value
20. Pipette several drops of Zeolite mixture onto center gold electrode of sample LCM
21. Avoid contact between mixture and oscillator connection points on outer gold electrodes
22. Dry loaded LCM in oven at 80 degrees Celsius for 2 hours to form viscous gel
23. Place 10 milliliters of deionized water in PTFE-lined autoclave reactor
24. Place PTFE holder in autoclave reactor
25. Place loaded LCM on holder ensuring no contact with liquid water
26. Close autoclave reactor
27. Transfer autoclave reactor to oven
28. Heat reactor at 150 degrees Celsius for 48 hours for steam-assisted crystallization
29. Wash loaded LCM with deionized water
30. Dry washed LCM in oven
31. Cool LCM to room temperature
32. Place LCM in oven
33. Heat LCM at 3 Kelvin/minute to 450 degrees Celsius for calcination
34. Hold LCM at 450 degrees Celsius for 4 hours
35. Cool LCM at 3 Kelvin/minute to room temperature
36. Soak LCM in 1 millimolar ammonium chloride for ion exchange
37. Repeat ammonium chloride soaking for ion exchange
38. Calcine LCM using identical heating/holding/cooling protocol
39. Place reference and sample LCMs in holder
40. Ensure resonance frequencies are detectable
41. Close and evacuate sample chamber
42. Heat LCMs overnight under vacuum to activate Zeolite
43. Set sample chamber temperature to 50°C for absorption measurements
44. Wait for temperature stabilization
45. Connect sample LCM to oscillator
46. Measure resonance frequency of loaded sample LCM
47. Connect oscillator to reference LCM
48. Measure resonance frequency of reference LCM
49. Determine mass of H-ZSM-5 deposited on sample LCM using Sauerbrey equation and frequency differences
50. Slowly fill sample chamber with selected gas to first desired pressure
51. Wait for temperature stabilization
52. Measure and record resonance frequencies of reference and sample LCMs
53. Repeat frequency measurements at several different pressures
54. Calculate combined mass of H-ZSM-5 and absorbed gas using Sauerbrey equation
55. Subtract deposited mass of H-ZSM-5 to determine absorbed gas mass
56. Determine gas absorption isotherm at temperature using calculated absorbed gas masses

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Wrap desired amount of polylactic acid fibers (200 micron diameter) around lower three quarters of customized spindle
2. Reduce fiber overlap to maximize surface area exposure in sealable bottle
3. Mix 400 milliliters of deionized water with 40 milliliters of dysbaric
4. Close bottle
5. Shake bottle until homogeneous solution is obtained
6. Place 1000 milliliter beaker in water bath at 37 degrees Celsius
7. Pour 400 milliliters of tri fluoro ethanol into beaker
8. Add water disper solution to beaker
9. Stir mixture until uniform
10. Add one gram of malachite green dye to mixture
11. Stir mixture until dye dissolves
12. Attach spindle to digital mixer
13. Adjust spindle height
14. Position spindle 0.5 inches from container bottom
15. Set mixer speed to 400 RPM
16. Initiate slow mixing
17. Add 1.3 grams of tin oxalate catalyst to mixture
18. Adjust pH using sodium hydroxide
19. Calibrate pH to 6.8-7.2 range
20. Secure lid to beaker
21. Increase spindle rotation to 500 RPM
22. Maintain 500 RPM rotation for 24 hours
23. Manually break up agglomeration of tin oxalate during first two hours
24. Preheat oven to 35 degrees Celsius
25. Remove spindle from mixer
26. Place spindle in preheated oven
27. Leave spindle to dry overnight (≥8 hours)
28. Remove dried spindle from oven
29. Unwrap fibers from spindle
30. Remove excess catalyst from fibers
31. Obtain laser-cut brass patterning plates with desired microvascular pattern
32. Affix patterning plates on clip holders
33. Cut 10-inch length of catalyzed fiber per micro channel
34. Use plate cut to fiber diameter to remove remaining catalyst from fibers
35. Taper fiber edges using hot glue gun tip by slowly extruding fiber tips
36. Thread fibers through matching holes in brass patterning plate pairs
37. Screw plates onto molding box
38. Verify fibers are not twisted during plate attachment
39. String fiber tips through tuning pegs of custom tensioning board
40. Apply tension to PLA fibers until taut while avoiding over-tensioning
41. Remove excess particulates from fiber pattern using compressed air
42. Mix poly dimethyl siloxane base with curing agent in 10:1 volume ratio
43. Place mixture in desiccator jar
44. Degas mixture under vacuum for 10 minutes
45. Pour PDMS mixture into molding box (avoiding direct pour over fibers)
46. Remove bubbles in molding box and between fibers using 26 gauge needle
47. Cure assembly at 85°C for 30 minutes
48. Unfasten brass plates from molding box
49. Remove cured first stage from molding box
50. Select hypodermic needle with gauge ≥2× fiber outer diameter
51. Puncture holes in RTV end cap using selected needle
52. Thread fiber through needle-punctured hole
53. Remove needle from end cap
54. Fasten end caps to larger molding box
55. Pour second stage of PDMS
56. Remove remaining gas bubbles from PDMS
57. Cure PDMS at 85 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
58. Cut excess PLA fibers from sample
59. Place sample in vacuum oven at 210 degrees Celsius for 24 hours or until PLA fibers evacuate
60. Inject 1 milliliter of chloroform to dissolve residual PLA in micro channels (if needed)

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Install strobe assembly into centrifuge with power cord fed through left port
2. Adjust strobe flash lamp to align with centrifuge viewing window
3. Secure assembly by tightening two Phillips head screws at top of each bracket
4. Tighten thumb screws at bottom of bracket to complete assembly securing
5. Plug power cord into strobe power port at back of centrifuge
6. Set rotor straight down onto centrifuge drive hub
7. Firmly secure rotor using T-handle hex wrench
8. Place assembly containing elutriation chamber, counterbalance, rotating seal assembly, and mounting plate onto rotor
9. Push down evenly with one hand on elutriation chamber and other hand on counterbalance until rotor bolts snap into place
10. Place pin at cable end into hole in rotating seal assembly
11. Secure eyehole at opposite cable end with Phillips head screw onto bracket to connect anchoring cable
12. Connect size 16 tubing to variable speed pump
13. Insert free end of tubing through top left port of centrifuge chamber
14. Attach fitting to free end of tubing
15. Insert fitting into input hole of rotating seal assembly
16. Insert output tubing through port and attach to transfer tube at top of rotating seal assembly
17. Place free end of input tubing into 500 milliliter bottle filled with 5% bleach
18. Place output tubing into empty one liter waste bottle
19. Turn on pump to flow bleach through elutriation system into waste bottle
20. Turn on centrifuge to reach maximum designated speed
21. Stop centrifuge once set speed is reached
22. Open lid and inspect chamber for leaks
23. Run sterile water through system for 10 minutes
24. Replace water reservoir with sterile glass bottle
25. Fill reservoir bottle with 100 milliliters of elutriation buffer
26. Place input tubing at bottom of reservoir bottle
27. Start centrifuge
28. Allow buffer to flow through system
29. Move output tubing from waste bottle to reservoir bottle for buffer recycling
30. Switch on the strobe lamp
31. Turn elutriation knob to extreme left position
32. Adjust viewing window by slowly turning knob right until elutriation chamber is visible and strobe light synchronizes with rotor speed
33. Prepare cell sample according to text protocol
34. Add resuspended cells into reservoir bottle containing recycling elutriation buffer
35. Allow cells to enter system and reach equilibrium within elutriation chamber
36. Take drop of eluent onto glass slide
37. View eluent sample under microscope
38. Begin fraction collection after confirming cells entered chamber with distinct boundary at equilibrium
39. Move output tubing to pre-chilled 50 mL conical tube labeled W1
40. Collect 50 mL of elutriation buffer in W1 tube and store on ice
41. Lower centrifuge speed to 821 G after W1 collection
42. Move output tubing to conical tube labeled W2 and collect 50 mL at adjusted speed
43. Change centrifuge speed progressively
44. Collect fractions during speed changes
45. Verify medium level in reservoir tank
46. Store collected fractions on ice
47. Stop centrifuge after fraction collection
48. Collect 50 milliliters in conical tube
49. Label conical tube as 'stop'

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Take sterile flat surface for mouse placement
2. Place heating pad recovery station next to surgery setup
3. Prepare urokinase and Hamilton syringe
4. Draw urokinase solution into Hamilton syringe
5. Locate lumbar spinal segments L2-L3 below rib cage using two fingers
6. Verify injection site
7. Place tip of Hamilton syringe next to spine
8. Position syringe at 45-degree angle
9. Insert needle tip into ligament adjacent to bone
10. Slowly inject 5 microliters of solution
11. Remove needle gently and slowly
12. Position animal into light/dark place preference test box with inter-chamber passageway
13. Configure apparatus with one brightly lit chamber and opposite dark chamber
14. Monitor animal's light/dark occupancy times and transitions via computer during 10-minute test
15. Place rodents on zero maze apparatus
16. Assess time spent in closed/open maze areas using stopwatch
17. Spray 10-30% sucrose solution on dorsal coat
18. Score frequency, duration, and latency of grooming for 10 minutes
19. Acclimate mice individually to clear plastic cage with open top for one hour
20. Add two identical toy mini figures to opposite cage corners for five minutes
21. On testing day, acclimate animals for one hour and place identical figures in same positions for five minutes
22. Replace one original figure with distinctly different novel object
23. After four hours, return mice to test cage and record time spent exploring objects
24. Cut paper towel tube lengthwise on one edge
25. Spread open tunnel on clean printer paper
26. Hold mice gently until calm
27. Apply non-toxic India ink to mouse paws using ink well, stamp pad, or cotton swab
28. Release mice at tunnel entrance
29. Allow mice to run through tunnel
30. Capture mice at tunnel end
31. Score paw prints based on stride length, stride width, and toe spread

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place one liter centrifuge bottle into ice water bath
2. Pour cell culture into chilled centrifuge bottle after OD600 reaches 3.0
3. Add water to second centrifuge bottle using double beam balance until weights match
4. Centrifuge bottles at 5,000 g and 10°C for 10 minutes to pellet cells
5. Pour off and dispose of supernatant
6. Place cell pellet on ice
7. Scrape cell pellet from centrifuge bottle using sterile spatula and transfer to cold 50 milliliter conical tube
8. Add 30 milliliters of cold S30 buffer supplemented with 2 millimolar DTT
9. Resuspend pellet by vortexing in short bursts with rest periods on ice until fully resuspended with no chunks
10. Prepare balance tube: fill another 50 milliliter conical tube with water
11. Centrifuge sample at 5,000 g and 10 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes
12. Pour off and dispose of supernatant
13. Resuspend pellet with 20-25 ml cold S30 buffer
14. Centrifuge at 5,000 g and 10°C for 10 minutes
15. Pour out and dispose of supernatant
16. Add 30 ml S30 buffer
17. Resuspend pellet using vortex mixer
18. Set out three pre-weighed 50 milliliter conical tubes (cold)
19. Aliquot 10 milliliters of pellet suspension into each tube using serological pipette filler with sterile pipette
20. Centrifuge tubes at 5,000 g and 10 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes with appropriate balancing
21. Pour out and dispose of supernatants
22. Wipe inside of each tube and cap with clean tissue to remove excess buffer while avoiding pellet contact
23. Reweigh tubes using analytical balance and report final pellet weight for each tube
24. Add 1 milliliter of cold S30 buffer with 2 millimolar DTT per 1 gram of cell mass to pellets
25. Resuspend pellets using a vortex
26. Transfer 1.4 milliliters of resuspended pellet into 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tubes
27. Place microcentrifuge tube into ice water bath in beaker
28. Position sonicator probe without touching tube bottoms or sides
29. Sonicate tube at 50% amplitude for 45 seconds
30. Close and invert tubes during 59-second rest periods
31. Add 4.5 microliters of 1M DTT to lysate
32. Invert tube several times to mix
33. Place tube on ice
34. Repeat sonication and DTT addition process for all resuspended cell tubes
35. Centrifuge samples at 18,000 g and 4°C for 10 minutes
36. Pipette supernatant into new 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes while leaving residual supernatant to avoid pellet disturbance
37. Incubate supernatant tubes at 37°C with shaking at 250 RPM for 60 minutes
38. Centrifuge samples at 10,000 g and 4°C for 10 minutes
39. Transfer supernatant to new tubes without disturbing pellet
40. Thaw solution A, solution B, DNA template, BL21DE3 extract, T7 RNA Polymerase, and molecular grade water on ice
41. Label required quantity of microcentrifuge tubes for CFPS reaction
42. Mix each reagent by pipetting or vortexing
43. Add solution to labeled reaction tube
44. Invert tube to mix (avoid vortexing cell extract)
45. Ensure final reaction is well mixed and combined into single 15 microliter bead at tube bottom
46. Incubate reaction without shaking at 37°C for 4 hours or 30°C overnight
47. Obtain a 96-well plate
48. Load 48 microliters of 0.05M HEPES (pH 8) into each required well
49. Remove reaction tubes from incubator
50. Mix each reaction by pipetting up and down
51. Transfer 2 microliters of each reaction into HEPES-containing wells
52. Mix each well by pipetting up and down
53. Load plate into fluorometer
54. Measure SFGFP endpoint fluorescence at 485nm excitation and 510nm emission
55. Use standard curve to determine superfolded GFP concentration from fluorescence readings

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Collect bulk soil samples using ethanol-sterilized soil core collector at 23-30 cm depth from plant base
2. Transfer collected soil to plastic bag
3. Homogenize soil by gentle shaking of plastic bag
4. Transfer 600-milligram soil aliquot into two-milliliter tube
5. Place two-milliliter tube on dry ice for preservation until DNA extraction
6. Dig up plant using ethanol-sterilized shovel while preserving root mass
7. Gently shake excess soil from roots until approximately 2mm of soil adheres to root surface
8. For large plants, cut representative root subsection using sterile scissors
9. Place minimum 500mg root tissue into 50-milliliter conical vial
10. Add epiphyte removal buffer until roots are covered
11. Immediately place sample on dry ice
12. Thaw root sample on ice
13. Sonicate root samples
14. Transfer roots to chilled 50-milliliter tube using sterile forceps
15. Centrifuge tube containing buffer and rhizosphere fraction
16. Decant supernatant
17. Place rhizosphere fraction in dry ice
18. Add 20 milliliters of 4°C sterile water to root fraction
19. Transfer 250 milligrams of soil and rhizosphere sample into commercial DNA isolation kit collection tubes using sterile spatula
20. Store extracted DNA at -20°C after elution
21. Chill sterilized mortar and pestle with liquid nitrogen
22. Measure 600-700 mg of root tissue
23. Place measured root tissue into mortar
24. Add sufficient liquid nitrogen to cover roots in mortar
25. Grind roots into small pieces
26. Repeat liquid nitrogen addition and grinding process ≥2 times consistently
27. Grind until roots become fine powder
28. Transfer root powder using sterile spatula to pre-weighed 1.5-mL tubes on ice before thawing
29. Prepare sufficient PCR master mix for triplicate amplification of each DNA sample
30. Pour master mix into sterile 25-milliliter multichannel pipette reservoir
31. Distribute 66 microliters of master mix into each well of new 96-well PCR plate
32. Add 6 microliters of 5 nanograms/microliter DNA from normalized DNA plate to master mix plate
33. Add 1.5 microliters of 10 micromolar forward primer with column-specific barcodes to master plate
34. Add 1.5 microliters of 10 micromolar reverse primer with row-specific barcodes to master plate
35. Cover reaction plate with sealing film
36. Centrifuge plate briefly at 3,000 RCF
37. Gently mix plate contents using multichannel pipette
38. Divide mixture into three plates with 25 microliters each
39. Cover all plates with PCR sealing film
40. Amplify DNA in plates using thermocycler
41. Pool three replicate amplification plates into a single 96-well plate
42. Calculate volume for 100 nanograms of each sample and average volume using computer spreadsheet
43. Add calculated average volume of blank PCR product into 1.5-milliliter tube
44. Pool 100 nanograms of each successfully amplified sample into the same 1.5-milliliter tube
45. Measure concentration of pooled product using benchtop fluorometer
46. Elute 600 nanograms of DNA in molecular-grade water to final volume of 100 microliters in 1.5-milliliter tube
47. Prepare 600 microliters of fresh 70% ethanol
48. Shake tube of magnetic beads to resuspend settled beads
49. Add 100 microliters of bead solution to 600-nanogram DNA aliquot
50. Mix solution and beads by pipetting 10 times
51. Incubate mixture for five minutes at room temperature
52. Place tube on magnetic stand for two minutes or until solution clears
53. Keep tube in magnetic stand
54. Aspirate clear supernatant carefully without touching magnetic beads
55. Add 300 microliters of 70% ethanol to tube
56. Incubate beads at room temperature for 30 seconds
57. Aspirate out ethanol and discard
58. Remove tube from magnetic stand
59. Air-dry contents for five minutes
60. Add 30 microliters of molecular-grade water to dried beads
61. Mix sample by pipetting 10 times
62. Incubate at room temperature for two minutes
63. Place tube on magnetic stand for one minute to separate beads from solution
64. Transfer eluate to new tube
65. Measure DNA concentration using benchtop fluorometer
66. Dilute aliquot to 10 nanomolar in 30 microliters (or to sequencing facility specifications)

Complete step list: Cut skin from neck to nose using small scissors following sagittal suture (15-20 mm)
2. Insert scissor tips into foramen magnum parallel to skull
3. Cut skull from each side toward eyes
4. Cut between eyes with small scissors to detach skull and brain
5. Grab skull near olfactory bulbs with two forceps and carefully tear
6. Remove cerebellum and olfactory bulb using razor blade
7. Cut brain into two pieces
8. Place brain pieces in Petri dish containing 40 ml HBSS without calcium and magnesium
9. Prepare dissociation mixture according to specified table
10. Transfer 12 brain pieces into C tube (total weight: 1.2 grams per tube)
11. Place C tubes on dissociater with heating
12. Start optimized NTDK program on dissociater
13. Centrifuge samples for 20 seconds
14. Complete mechanical dissociation by pipetting three times
15. Transfer dissociated cells to four 15-mL tubes with attached strainers
16. Rinse strainers with 10 mL HBSS containing calcium and magnesium
17. Centrifuge sample for 10 minutes
18. Remove supernatant using 10-milliliter pipette
19. Add 10 milliliters of HBSS with calcium and magnesium
20. Re-suspend pellet
21. Centrifuge sample
22. Remove supernatant
23. Re-suspend pellet with 6 milliliters of sorting buffer
24. Centrifuge sample
25. Discard supernatant
26. Add 200 microliters of CD11b microbead solution
27. Incubate tubes at 4°C for 15-20 minutes
28. Re-suspend pellet with 6 ml sorting buffer
29. Centrifuge sample
30. Re-suspend pellet with 8 ml sorting buffer
31. Prepare 8 columns using Possel program on separator
32. Pass cells through column by adding 1 ml cell suspension aliquots
33. Elute CD11b-positive cells using 1 ml sorting buffer onto sterile elution plate
34. Pool cells in 50-ml tube
35. Centrifuge collected cells
36. Re-suspend pellet with 10 ml cold microglia medium
37. Count CD11b-positive cells
38. Re-suspend cells in cold microglia medium to achieve 650,000-700,000 cells per milliliter
39. Dispense cell suspension into cell culture plates
40. Incubate plates overnight at 37°C with 5% CO₂
41. Replace medium with preheated microglia medium
42. Incubate overnight
43. Calculate number of beads using table at 50 beads per cell ratio
44. Prepare beads mixture
45. Incubate tube in 37°C water bath for one hour
46. Vortex tube every 10 minutes during incubation
47. Add calculated volume of bead solution to each well
48. Incubate plate for three hours

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Connect silicone tubing between reservoir, pump, and bubble trap to establish fluid pathway
2. Fill reservoir with 70% ethanol
3. Turn pump on at 1 milliliter per minute flow speed
4. Turn pump on at 1 milliliter per minute flow speed
5. Let 70% ethanol circulate to sterilize tubing
6. Empty reservoir
7. Pump ethanol out of system
8. Fill reservoir with sterile distilled water
9. Fill reservoir with sterile distilled water
10. Pump water through system to remove residual alcohol
11. Pump water out of system
12. Fill reservoir with 45 milliliters of freshly prepared medium
13. Circulate medium to fill all tubing
14. Allow medium to adapt (equilibrate)
15. Inject mouse with 500 units of heparin intraperitoneally
16. Place mouse on dissection board after 10 minutes
17. Confirm appropriate anesthesia level by toe pinch
18. Sterilize fur with 70% ethanol
19. Pinch and sterilize fur with 70% ethanol
20. Use scissors to open abdominal cavity, exposing vena cava and diaphragm to visualize heart
21. Make lateral incisions from bottom to top of ribs
22. Reflect thoracic cage over animal's head
23. Observe heart beating
24. Insert 21-gauge needle attached to 5ml syringe containing sterile tyros buffer through diaphragm into inferior vena cava
25. Ensure blood exit pathway from vena cava at needle insertion site
26. Perfuse buffer gently with constant pressure into vena cava until heart partly loses red color
27. Switch syringe to new 5ml syringe of buffer
28. Transfer heart into 10 cm Petri dish within laminar flow hood
29. Add fresh PBS supplemented with antibiotics and antimycotic
30. Using dissecting microscope, micro scissors, and forceps, remove all non-cardiac tissue while preserving ascending aorta to bifurcation with brachiocephalic artery and pulmonary veins 2 mm proximal to heart
31. Cut 2 mm off tip of heart apex
32. Cut 2 mm off tip of heart apex to access left ventricular lumen
33. Place perfusion chamber in hood using silicone tubing
34. Attach 5 ml syringes filled with medium to needle #1 (mitral valve) and needle #2 (aortic valve)
35. Fill perfusion chamber with 20 ml of medium
36. Position heart on rotation stage in perfusion chamber between two blunted needles
37. Adjust height of rotation stage
38. Ligate aorta with silk 7.0 suture
39. Ligate left atrial appendage with silk 7.0 suture
40. Insert needle number one through pulmonary vein into left atrium
41. Place suture proximal to needle entry in left atrium
42. Insert needle number two into left ventricle
43. Transfer medium from perfusion chamber to 50 milliliter tube
44. Seal needle to myocardium using biocompatible glue
45. Inject medium into heart using needle number one syringe
46. Check heart for leakage
47. Confirm medium exits from needle number two and blood perfuses out

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Set up LabWare in fume hood
2. Connect round bottom flask to condenser
3. Immerse flask in silicone oil bath on hot plate
4. Combine reagents in metal flask (iron acetylate, oleic acid, oleylamine, 1,2-hexadecanediol, phenyl ether)
5. Stir mixture vigorously
6. Heat mixture to 250-260°C
7. Maintain temperature at 250-260°C for one hour under reflux
8. Cool reaction to room temperature
9. Add gold acetate, oleic acid, oleylamine, 1,2-hexadecanediol, and phenyl ether to new round bottom flask
10. Add 5 mL magnetic nanoparticle suspension to flask
11. Heat reaction mixture to 180°C under reflux conditions
12. Maintain reaction at 180°C under reflux for 1 hour
13. Cool solution to room temperature
14. Initiate precipitation of hybrid primary nanoparticles
15. Add 50 milliliters of ethanol to nanoparticle suspension
16. Transfer suspension to centrifugation tube
17. Centrifuge suspension
18. Resuspend pellet in 25 milliliters of hexane using sonication
19. Add 25 milliliters of ethanol to resuspended mixture
20. Centrifuge mixture again
21. Repeat washing process (resuspension in hexane/sonication + ethanol addition + centrifugation) three more times
22. Dry pellet of primary hybrid nanoparticles in vacuum desiccator overnight until completely dry
23. Resuspend dry primary nanoparticles in 1 milliliter of hexane
24. Sonicate suspension until no visible precipitate is present
25. Add nanoparticle suspension dropwise to 10 milliliters of sodium esal sulfate solution in 20 milliliter glass-capped vial
26. Cap vial and shake by hand
27. Sonicate two-phase solution for two hours
28. Position vial near center of sonication bath
29. Heat mixture in 80°C water bath for 10 minutes
30. Let mixture cool to room temperature
31. Centrifuge suspension at 100 G for 30 minutes
32. Centrifuge suspension at 100 G for 30 minutes
33. Save supernatant
34. Resuspend pellet in 0.1 millimolar sodium citrate using 10 minutes of sonication
35. Centrifuge saved supernatant at 400 G for 30 minutes
36. Save supernatant
37. Resuspend pellet in 0.1 millimolar sodium citrate using 10 minutes of sonication
38. Centrifuge saved supernatant at 1500 G for 30 minutes
39. Centrifuge saved supernatant at 1500 G for 30 minutes
40. Resuspend pellet for 90 nm nano clusters
41. Transfer 300 microliters of each suspension to individual wells in 96-well plate
42. Read UV-Vis-NIR absorption spectra of samples in 96-well plate
43. Transfer 10 microliters of suspension to carbon-coated copper TEM grids
44. Prepare TEM grids for imaging
45. Transfer 100 microliters of antibodies to a 10K molecular weight cutoff centrifuge filter
46. Add 3.9 milliliters of 4 millimolar HEPES pH 7.2 to the filter
47. Centrifuge at 3,250 G at 8°C for 20 minutes
48. Add 10 microliters of 100 millimolar sodium periodate to oxidize antibodies
49. Cover vial with foil
50. Incubate on room-temperature orbital shaker for 30 minutes
51. Add 0.5 milliliters to quench oxidation reaction
52. Quench oxidation reaction by adding 0.5 mL of PBS
53. Add 2 microliters of linker solution to oxidized antibodies
54. Shake reaction for 1 hour at room temperature
55. Filter reaction through 10K MWCO filter at 3,250g for 20 minutes at 8°C
56. Transfer antibody to new vial
57. Transfer antibody to new vial
58. Adjust volume to 100 microliters using 1X PBS
59. Collect 100 microliters of nano cluster suspension (OD ~1.0)
60. Add 1 microliter of modified antibodies to nano cluster suspension
61. Incubate mixture at room temperature for 2 hours with mild shaking
62. Add 10 microliters of reagent after incubation
63. Add 10 microliters of 1 millimolar 5 kilodalton thiol-PEG
64. Incubate reaction for 15 minutes at room temperature with shaking
65. Pellet antibody-conjugated nano clusters at 830 G for 3 minutes
66. Resuspend pellet in 100 microliters of 2% weight/volume 5 kilodalton PEG in PBS at pH 7.2
67. Measure absorbance of suspension compared to bare nano clusters
68. Increase thiol PEG concentration to 5 mM
69. Increase incubation time to 30 minutes
70. Decrease centrifugal speed incrementally by 200 G until nanoparticle aggregation ceases

question:
