Complete step list: Dissect experimentally induced tumor from mouse brain in 10 cm Petri dish
2. Visualize tumor through fluorescent dissecting microscope (if expressing fluorescent protein) at 0.3x magnification with appropriate channel
3. Use scalpel and forceps to separate tumor tissue from normal brain
4. Mince tumor into small pieces in Petri dish
5. Transfer dissected tumor to 1.5 ml tube with 300 microliters NSC medium
6. Gently homogenize tumor using disposable syringe
7. Add 1 milliliter of cell detachment medium
8. Incubate sample for 5 minutes at 37°C
9. Pass cell suspension through 70 micron cell strainer into 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
10. Wash strainer with 25 milliliters of NSC medium
11. Centrifuge suspension at 300 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
12. Resuspend cell pellet in 6 milliliters of NSC medium
13. Transfer tumor cell suspension into T25 culture flask
14. Culture cells at 37°C in tissue culture incubator with 95% air and 5% CO₂ atmosphere until neurospheres form
15. Centrifuge 1×10⁶ neurosphere cells at 300×g for 5 minutes
16. Decant supernatant after centrifugation
17. Resuspend neurosphere pellet in 1 ml balanced salt solution
18. Transfer suspension to low protein binding 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tubes
19. Centrifuge suspension as before (300×g for 5 minutes)
20. Decant supernatant from cell pellet
21. Re-suspend cell pellet in 95 microliters of digestion buffer
22. Supplement digestion buffer with protease inhibitor cocktail (1:100 ratio per 1×10⁶ cells)
23. Pipette cell suspension up and down to prevent clumping and avoid bubble formation
24. Prepare diluted micrococcal nuclease by mixing 5 microliters of 0.1X nuclease with 145 microliters of digestion buffer
25. Add 5 microliters of diluted micrococcal nuclease per 1×10⁶ cells
26. Flick tube to mix contents
27. Incubate tube in 37°C heat block for exactly 12 minutes
28. Add 10 microliters of 10X micrococcal nuclease stop buffer per 1×10⁶ cells
29. Place samples on ice for subsequent steps
30. Add 110 microliters of 2X RIPA buffer supplemented 1:100 with protease inhibitor cocktail per 1×10⁶ cells
31. Flick tube to mix samples
32. Centrifuge samples for 15 minutes at 4°C and 1700×g
33. Transfer chromatin-containing supernatant to 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube
34. Place transferred supernatant on ice
35. Dilute chromatin with RIPA buffer supplemented with protease inhibitors at 1:1000 ratio
36. Adjust diluted chromatin to 100-200 microliters per IP reaction
37. Remove aliquot equivalent to 10% of ChIP volume as input sample
38. Add 100 microliters of TE buffer supplemented with proteinase K to input sample
39. Incubate input sample at 55 degrees Celsius for one hour
40. Purify input sample using PCR purification kit
41. Measure concentration of purified input using micro volume spectrophotometer
42. Record concentration results in notebook
43. Add 10 microliters of washed protein A/G magnetic beads per IP sample
44. Incubate samples for one hour at 4°C with rotation at 20 RPM
45. Place samples on magnetic holder for bead separation
46. Transfer required volume to new 1.5 milliliter tubes
47. Add desired antibody to each tube
48. Wrap tube caps with plastic paraffin film to prevent evaporation
49. Incubate samples overnight at 4°C with rotation at 20 RPM
50. Spin tubes at 2000×g for 10 seconds
51. Add 10 microliters of beads to each IP
52. Incubate samples for 3 hours at 4°C with rotation at 20 RPM
53. Place samples on magnetic stand for bead separation
54. Remove supernatant using pipette
55. Add 150 microliters of RIPA buffer with protease inhibitors to each IP
56. Incubate samples at 4°C at 20 RPM for 5 minutes
57. Place samples on magnetic stand
58. Allow magnetic beads to separate
59. Remove supernatant using pipette
60. Add 150 microliters of lithium chloride buffer with low-concentration protease inhibitors to each IP
61. Incubate at 4°C with rotation at 20 RPM for five minutes
62. Place samples on magnetic stand
63. Allow magnetic beads to separate
64. Remove supernatant using pipette
65. Add 150 microliters of cold TE without protease inhibitors to beads
66. Incubate at 4 degrees Celsius with rotation at 20 RPM for five minutes
67. Re-suspend sample in 100 microliters of TE buffer supplemented with 0.5 milligrams per milliliter proteinase K
68. Incubate at 55 degrees Celsius for one hour
69. Purify amino precipitated DNA using PCR purification kit
70. Perform qPCR with primers targeted to positive and negative control regions

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Thaw and gently resuspend mitochondrial vesicles (mitoplasts) in 5 ml ice-cold Buffer A
2. Keep sample chilled
3. Determine protein concentration using BCA assay according to manufacturer's instructions
4. Perform BSA dilution series in ultra pure water to construct standard curve
5. Dilute sample 20-100 times with ultra pure water to fit BSA standard range
6. Adjust protein concentration to 16 mg/ml by diluting with additional Buffer A
7. Fragment mitoplasts via sonication: 7 pulses of 15 seconds each at 70-100 J/impulse using 3.9mm microtip
8. Incubate sample on ice during fragmentation
9. Sediment membrane fragments by ultracentrifugation at 54,000 g for 16 hours or 98,000 g for 5 hours at 4°C
10. Decant supernatant
11. Transfer frozen sediment from centrifuge tube to homogenizer
12. Resuspend mitochondrial membrane pellet in Buffer B using small Dounce homogenizer
13. Transfer suspension to 50 mL conical tube
14. Remove sample from ice
15. Equilibrate sample and solutions to room temperature
16. Add chloroform saturated with 2M Tris-HCl (pH 8.5) at 1:1 ratio
17. Tightly close tube cap
18. Vigorously shake sample for 20 seconds
19. Centrifuge mixture at 8,400 g
20. Centrifuge mixture at 8,400 g for 5 minutes at room temperature
21. Transfer upper cloudy aqueous phase to 1.6 milliliter microtubes
22. Add protease inhibitors to sample
23. Centrifuge samples at 13,000 g for 30 minutes at room temperature
24. Equilibrate 5 ml anion exchange Q column attached to fast protein liquid chromatography system with 50 ml of Q column buffer at 5 ml/min flow rate until absorbance at 280 nm and conductivity stabilize
25. Load supernatant on equilibrated column at 1 ml/min flow rate
26. Load supernatant onto equilibrated column at 1 mL/min flow rate
27. Monitor until absorbance at 280 nm stabilizes at background level
28. Apply 25 mL linear gradient of Q column elution buffer (0% to 100%) at 0.5 mL/min flow rate
29. Collect 1 mL fractions during gradient application
30. Equilibrate Superose 6 Increase column attached to liquid chromatography system with 48 milliliters of SEC buffer at 0.5 milliliters per minute flow rate
31. Apply sample to column
32. Run size-exclusion chromatography at 0.25 milliliters per minute flow rate
33. Collect 0.25 milliliter fractions during chromatography run
34. Run 10 microliters of peak fractions (identified by UV 280 nm absorbance) on SDS-PAGE
35. Stain SDS-PAGE samples with Coomassie Blue
36. Collect 0.25 milliliter fractions
37. Run 10 microliters of peak-corresponding fractions from UV 280 nm absorbance trace on SDS-PAGE
38. Stain samples with Coomassie Blue
39. Assay fractions corresponding to first major F1 peak for ATP hydrolytic activity and azide sensitivity using Pullman ATPase assay
40. Perform BCA assay to determine protein concentration
41. Keep purified F1-ATPase at room temperature
42. Store purified F1-ATPase at room temperature
43. Use purified F1-ATPase within three days after purification for downstream applications

Complete step list: Shave injection site on anesthetized mouse
2. Clean shaved site with alcohol swab two times
3. Load cold 1-mL syringe with 1-2×10⁷ cells/mL and 250 μL basement membrane matrix
4. Attach 25-gauge needle to syringe
5. Load needle with tumor cell suspension
6. Press and retract syringe plunger multiple times to remove air bubbles
7. Press and retract plunger to remove air bubbles
8. Insert needle subcutaneously at injection site at 30-degree angle
9. Slowly inject 50 microliters of tumor cells with needle parallel to body
10. Withdraw needle slowly after 10 seconds while maintaining parallel position without sideways movement
11. Clean injection site gently with alcohol swab
12. Return mouse to cage
13. Excise subcutaneous tumor using sterile forceps and micro scissors under BSL-2 hood conditions while avoiding peritoneal cavity puncture
14. Wash excised tumor twice in sterile PBS
15. Mince tumor into 1-2 mm³ pieces using sterile scalpels
16. Transfer tumor pieces into Petri dish containing cold sterile DMEM basal medium on ice
17. Confirm lack of response to toe pinch
18. Apply ointment to anesthetized recipient animal's eyes
19. Place mouse on right side to expose left abdomen
20. Remove abdominal hair with depilatory cream
21. Remove depilatory cream with gauze

23. Make 1.5 cm incision in skin 1 cm left of midline below rib cage
24. Gently externalize spleen with flat-tipped forceps

28. Pass fine 6-O sterile suturing needle through tumor piece
29. Insert needle containing tumor fragment into tail of pancreas
30. Pass suture slowly through pancreatic tissue
31. Completely remove needle from tissue after securing tumor fragment
32. Maintain organs externalized for 60 seconds while inspecting for hemorrhage
33. Return spleen, pancreas, and tumor to abdominal cavity using blunt forceps or cotton tip applicator

36. Elevate skin under tumor using forceps
37. Insert two needle array electrodes parallel to body through skin to bracket tumor
38. Set electroporator to deliver 100 microsecond pulses at 1 Hz frequency, 1500 volts per centimeter for 150 pulses

46. Tightly capture tumor with platinum electrodes of Tweezertrode
47. Deliver electroporation pulses

2. Remove culture medium when cells reach 80% confluency

14. Screw on sterile 22 gauge plastic tip

16. Turn on recirculating water bath
17. Manually set water bath temperature to 4 degrees Celsius
18. Configure printing parameters on correlating dispenser computers
19. Set dispense volume to 230 nanoliters and back steps to zero
20. Set dispensary rate to 10 microliters per second
21. Open design software and USB camera viewing program

23. Save program and send to robot

25. Close and lock chamber door

27. Open chamber after 90 seconds sterilization
28. Load syringe containing human adipose tissue stromal cell suspension into gun one

31. Wait 30 seconds for internal pressure equilibration
32. Run program containing geometrical pathway and printing parameters

36. Image stained constructs using confocal microscope

Complete step list: Pick 30 to 40 young adult worms into watch glass dish containing 500 microliters of 1x M9 and levamisole
2. Remove and discard most media to eliminate transferred bacteria
3. Add 500 microliters fresh 1x M9 and levamisole
4. Rinse worms by gently drawing and dispensing media with pipette
5. Remove and discard most media after rinsing
6. Repeat wash cycle (steps 3-5) two to three times
7. Leave worms in approximately 100 microliters media for ≤8 minutes
8. Transfer worms to 25-millimeter-by-75-millimeter microscope slide coated with 0.001% poly-L-lysine using glass or polyethylene pipette
9. Remove excess media until approximately 15 microliters remains on slide
10. Position two 26 1/2-gauge needles crossed in scissor formation under dissecting microscope
11. Cut worms behind pharynx using crossed needles to release germlines
12. Complete dissection of all worms within five minutes
13. Place 22-millimeter-by-40-millimeter coverslip perpendicularly over slide with ends hanging off
14. Freeze slides on pre-chilled aluminum block maintained on dry ice for at least 20 minutes
15. Place chilled pencil on top of coverslip to prevent displacement from ice expansion
16. Flick off coverslips with a pencil
17. Dip slide into jar of fresh ice-cold methanol for 1 minute
18. Gently wipe edges of slide surrounding sample
19. Apply 150 microliters of formaldehyde fixative at room temperature for 5 minutes
20. Touch slide to paper towel at 90-degree angle to drain fixative
21. Block slides twice for 15 minutes at room temperature
22. Absorb solution into paper towel
23. Block slides twice for 15 minutes at room temperature in Coplin jar with 50 ml PBT/BSA
24. Block slides with PBT/BSA solution containing 10% normal goat serum
25. Gently wipe edges surrounding slide
26. Apply 100 microliters of solution to each slide
27. Gently agitate slide to spread solution
28. Incubate for one hour at room temperature in humid chamber
29. Place slide on paper towel to let solution run off
30. Gently wipe slide edges
31. Apply one drop of blocking reagent to 14mm×14mm space
32. Incubate slides for one hour at 37°C in humid chamber
33. Gently wipe slide edges
34. Dilute primary antibodies using antibody diluent
35. Apply 40 microliters of diluted primary antibody solution per 14-by-14-millimeter space on slides
36. Place slides in humid chamber
37. Incubate slides overnight at 4°C
38. Wash slides twice for 5 minutes each with 50 ml of 1x wash buffer A at room temperature in Coplin jar
39. Set Coplin jar on orbital shaker at 60 rpm
40. Place slides on paper towel to drain wash buffer
41. Gently wipe slide edges
42. Prepare 40-microliter solution containing plus and minus probes
43. Add probe solution to each 14x14 mm space
44. Incubate slides in humid chamber for 1 hour at 37°C
45. Wash slides twice for 5 minutes each with 50 mL of 1x wash buffer A at room temperature in Coplin jar
46. Set Coplin jar on orbital shaker at 60 rpm
47. Dilute ligation buffer 1:5 with ultrapure water
48. Dilute ligase 1:40 using diluted buffer to prepare ligation solution
49. Place slide on paper towel to drain wash buffer
50. Gently wipe slide edges
51. Add 40 μL ligation solution to each 14x14 mm space
52. Incubate slides in humid chamber for 30 minutes at 37°C
53. Add 40 microliters of freshly prepared light-protected amplification solution to each 14-by-14-millimeter space
54. Incubate slides in humid dark chamber at 37°C for 1 hour 40 minutes
55. Wash slides twice for 10 minutes each with 50 milliliters of 1x wash buffer B
56. Wash slides once for 1 minute with 50 milliliters of 0.01x wash buffer B
57. Add 10 microliters of mounting medium to each sample
58. Gently lay coverslip on sample allowing mounting medium to spread
59. Paint nail polish around coverslip edge to seal while avoiding movement
60. Let nail polish harden for at least 20 minutes at room temperature protected from light
61. Ensure sufficient water is present in chamber
62. Ensure slides are level inside chamber
63. Image extruded gonads on confocal microscope
64. Quantify images using Fiji/ImageJ workflow

Complete step list: Pipette 40 microliters of liquid nitrocellulose onto edge of indium tin oxide microscope slide
2. Drag nitrocellulose across slide using regular glass microscope slide under surface tension to create thin-film coating
3. Allow nitrocellulose coating to dry at room temperature for 20 seconds and store at room temperature
4. Cut thick blotting paper to fit bottom half of standard plastic petri dish
5. Cut paper to create rectangular strip matching microscope slide size with excess for positional stability
6. Float mount FFPE tissue sections onto nitrocellulose pre-coated ITO slides
7. Submerge slides in fresh xylene for 2 minutes to remove residual paraffin
8. Submerge slides in 70% v/v ethanol-water solution for 30 seconds
9. Submerge slides in 100% ethanol for 30 seconds
10. Submerge slides in Carnoy's fluid for 2 minutes
11. Submerge slides sequentially in 100% ethanol, ultrapure water, and 100% ethanol for 30 seconds each
12. Load sample slides into plastic slide box filled with 20 millimolar tris HCO
13. Seal box and place in water bath containing 500 milliliters of water with box touching bath bottom
14. Heat box at 120 degrees celsius for 15 minutes in pressure cooker at 70 kilopascals operating pressure
15. Remove slides, allow cooling, and dry at ambient temperature for 15 minutes
16. Pipette 10 microliters of trypsin solution in ultrapure water onto edge of tissue section
17. Drag droplet across entire tissue surface using pipette tip under surface tension
18. Allow samples to dry at ambient temperature
19. Mount samples inside top of vapor chamber using autoclave tape on slide edges
20. Pipette 600 microliters of 1:1 v/v 100% acetonitrile and 50mM ammonium bicarbonate solution onto blotting paper in petri dish bottom
21. Assemble vapor chamber by placing top half on bottom half with aligned paper finger and sample slide
22. Seal vapor chamber equator with paraffin film
23. Incubate sealed chamber at 37°C overnight for complete digestion
24. Mount slide onto cooling finger of sublimation apparatus
25. Secure slide with copper tape
26. Place 300 milligrams of CHCA matrix into glass petri dish at chamber bottom
27. Spread matrix evenly to create thin layer of crystals
28. Assemble sublimator
29. Secure sublimator halves with horseshoe clamp
30. Suspend assembled unit 15-20 cm above 220°C sand bath using metal ring on retort stand
31. Connect chamber to vacuum source
32. Engage vacuum
33. Stabilize vacuum at 25 millitorr for 5 minutes
34. Pack cooling finger to top with ice
35. Add 50 milliliters of water to apparatus
36. Allow apparatus to settle for 5 minutes
37. Lower chamber onto stand surface ensuring sand contacts chamber bottom
38. Incubate for 45 minutes to form 0.22 mg/cm² coating
39. Remove chamber from sand bath by raising metal ring

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Open webpage shown in video
2. Input approximately 60 base pairs of sequence flanking desired edit site
3. Select C. Elegans genome and protospacer adjacent motif according to protocol
4. Click submit button
5. Choose top-ranked target sequence closest to edit site on results page
6. Centrifuge lyophilized sgRNA tube at room temperature at 12,000 g for 1 minute
7. Add 60 microliters of nuclease-free TE buffer to the tube
8. Resuspend sgRNA by pipetting up and down 15-20 times gently using p200 pipette
9. Centrifuge lyophilized ssODN tube
10. Resuspend ssODN in nuclease-free water to 100 µM concentration
11. Centrifuge reagents at maximum speed and 4°C for 2 minutes
12. Add reagents in specified order to nuclease-free PCR tube
13. Mix contents gently and thoroughly using p20 pipette
14. Incubate tube at room temperature for 10 minutes
15. Allow injected P0 animals to recover at room temperature for 1-2 hours on OP50-seeded 35 mm NGM plate
16. Transfer single injected P0 animals to individual OP50-seeded 35 mm NGM plates using platinum wire worm pick
17. Use fluorescent microscope to identify P0 plates containing F1 progeny expressing mCherry in pharynx
18. Select three P0 plates containing the most mCherry-positive F1 animals
19. Use worm pick to single 8-12 mCherry-positive F1 animals per selected P0 plate (total 24-36 animals)
20. Allow individual F1s to self-fertilize and lay eggs at room temperature for 1-2 days
21. Transfer F1 worms using worm pick into individual PCR strip tube caps containing 7 microliters of worm lysis buffer
22. Centrifuge PCR tubes at maximum speed and room temperature for 1 minute
23. Freeze PCR tubes at -80°C for 1 hour
24. Lyse frozen worms in thermocycler using specified program
25. Set up PCR master mix according to table specifications
26. Add 21 microliters of PCR master mix into clean PCR tubes
27. Add 4 microliters of worm lysis solution to PCR tubes
28. Mix reaction thoroughly by pipetting
29. Run PCR program following manufacturer's guidelines
30. Purify PCR reaction products
31. Purify PCR reactions using DNA clean and concentrate kit following manufacturer's instructions
32. Dilute DNA by adding 10 microliters of water to spin column
33. Set up restriction enzyme master mix
34. Add 10 microliters of master mix to each cleaned PCR reaction
35. Incubate tubes at 37 degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours
36. Separate digested PCR products on 1.5% agarose gel using 1x TAE buffer at 120 volts
37. Examine enzyme-digested samples for presence of bands indicating potential edited animals
38. Use remaining 3 microliters of worm lysis for PCR repetition
39. Load completed PCR reactions on 1% agarose gel
40. Separate and extract band using gel extraction kit
41. Perform Sanger sequencing using F1 primer
42. Select 8-12 F2 animals from verified heterozygous edited animals
43. Carry out genotyping and sequence verification according to text protocol

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Retrieve one vial of papain powder from 4°C storage
2. Incubate papain powder vial at room temperature
3. Set aside Complete Schneider's Medium (CSM) in Eppendorf tube at room temperature
4. Add room temperature CSM to papain vial through cap using syringe to achieve 100 units/mL concentration
5. Invert vial to capture powder from cap
6. Pipette vial contents up and down
7. Bring water to 37°C in beaker using water bath
8. Place vial in 37°C water bath for 30 minutes, ensuring it doesn't float
9. Retrieve proteolytic enzyme blend aliquot from freezer
10. Incubate enzyme blend at room temperature
11. Incubate activated papain at room temperature
12. Dissect staged pupal brains in cold CSM using clean forceps
13. Dissect maximum number of brains within 10 minutes
14. Pool dissected brains in fresh drop of cold CSM
15. Cut off optic lobes by pinching at junction between optic lobe and central brain
16. Add dissected optic lobes to microtube containing 500 microliters of 1x RS solution (experimental tissue)
17. Add dissected optic lobes to separate microtube containing 500 microliters of 1x RS solution (negative control tissue)
18. Keep microtubes on ice
19. Dissect maximum number of optic lobes within one hour
20. Pool all dissected optic lobes into single microtube to minimize tissue loss during transfers
21. Remove 1x RS solution from microtube containing dissected optic lobes using P200 pipette
22. Leave sufficient solution to cover optic lobes
23. Add 500 microliters of 1x RS to side of microtube
24. Allow optic lobes to settle at bottom of microtube
25. Aspirate 200 microliters of solution from microtube
26. Expel solution mixture and observe lobe movement
27. Allow optic lobes to resettle
28. Aliquot 600 microliters of activated room temperature papain into a microtube
29. Add 4.2 microliters of room temperature proteolytic enzyme blend to 600 microliters of papain solution to achieve 0.18 WU per milliliter final concentration
30. Mix solution by pipetting up and down
31. Remove 1x RS from each sample
32. Add 300 microliters of dissociation solution to optic lobes
33. Incubate samples at 25°C and 1,000 RPM for 15 minutes in microtube ThermoMixer
34. Stop ThermoMixer at 5-minute incubation time point
35. Allow lobes to sink to bottom of microtube
36. Pipette up 200 microliters of solution and mix
37. Wait for lobes to settle after 15-minute incubation
38. Remove dissociation solution from lobes without disturbing tissue
39. Add 500 microliters of 1x RS without disturbing lobes
40. Pipette up 200 microliters of solution and gently expel
41. Allow lobes to sink to bottom and repeat solution expulsion
42. Wash sample with 500 microliters of CSM (first wash)
43. Wash sample with 500 microliters of CSM (second wash)
44. Carefully remove CSM
45. Add 200 microliters of CSM to tube
46. Disrupt tissue by pipetting up and down with P200 pipette without foaming
47. Filter cell suspension through 30 micrometer mesh cap into 5 milliliter fax tube on ice using P200 pipette
48. Ensure entire suspension passes through mesh
49. Add 500 microliters of CSM to rinse dissociation microtube
50. Filter rinse solution into fax tube
51. Carefully remove cap of fax tube
52. Collect solution underneath mesh filter with P200 pipette
53. Add collected solution to suspension in fax tube

Complete step list: Grow a single Agrobacterium colony overnight in 5 ml of YEB medium supplemented with antibiotics in a 50 ml centrifuge tube at 28°C and 200 rpm

6. Place bacterial cells on ice
7. Transfer donor plant from 2MS medium culture to 120x120 mm square plate
8. Inject 3 microliters of A.rhizogenes culture into multiple stem internodes using surgical needle
9. Transfer entire plant to new square plate containing solid MS medium supplemented with 0.1 mM acetosyringone
10. Place second transformed plant onto same plate
11. Seal plate with surgical tape
12. Place sealed plate vertically in growth cabinet for 4 days
13. Transfer plant to square plate with MS medium supplemented with cefotaxime sodium
14. Place sealed plate vertically in growth cabinet for 4 days
15. Excise native roots of each plant
16. Transfer plants to new square plate with MS medium supplemented with cefotaxime sodium
17. Grow transgenic hairy roots in MS medium with cefotaxime sodium for 3-4 weeks
18. Cut leaf from three to four week old plant and place in petri dish, excluding petiole
19. Make 1-3 transverse cuts from leaf center to edges using scalpel without detaching pieces
20. Transfer leaf to petri dish containing 10 ml fresh 2MS liquid medium with abaxial side up
21. Cover petri dish
22. Add 80 microliters of A.tumefaciens culture to liquid medium
23. Gently stir medium for 1 minute to homogenously distribute bacterial solution
24. Seal and cover plate with aluminum foil
25. Incubate plate at 24°C for two days
26. Transfer leaves abaxial-side up to callus-inducing medium
27. Scrape medium with tweezers
28. Incubate in growth cabinet for one week
29. Transfer leaves abaxial-side up to tweezer-scraped shoot-inducing medium
30. Incubate in growth cabinet
31. Cut three 2-cm tall emerged chutes from each callus
32. Place up to five chute transformation events into MG medium flasks supplemented with cefotaxime sodium
33. Label flasks appropriately
34. Incubate in growth cabinet for 3-4 weeks until chutes are vigorous
35. Select most vigorous plant of each event after incubation
36. Cut apical segments with 3-4 internodes from each selected plant
37. Place cut segments into culture flask containing 2MS medium supplemented with cefotaxime sodium
38. Grow plants in culture flask until development of vigorous shoots and roots
39. Fix roots from 2-3 week in-vitro culture in 90% chilled acetone for 20 minutes on ice
40. Wash roots two times in distilled water
41. Treat roots with fresh GUS-staining solution under vacuum for 20 minutes
42. Incubate roots at 37°C in dark for approximately 4 hours
43. Wash roots two times with 70% ethanol after blue color appears
44. Visualize staining under bright field microscope

22. Check image displayed on monitor

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Culture HCT116 cell line in DMEM cell culture medium
2. Incubate cultures until six to twelve confluent plates are obtained
3. Wash cells gently with 10 milliliters of PBS to remove cell culture medium
4. Add three milliliters of ice-cold PBS supplemented with five-millimolar EDTA
5. Incubate plates for five minutes at room temperature
6. Detach cells from plates using a cell scraper
7. Collect cells in 50-milliliter tube placed on ice
8. Centrifuge at 250 G for 10 minutes
9. Measure volume of harvested cells using tube's volumetric scale
10. Resuspend harvested cell pellet in equal volume of lysis buffer
11. Lyse cells by sonication
12. Spin sample at 100,000 G for one hour at 4°C in ultracentrifuge rotor
13. Remove cleared lysate using syringe without disturbing pellet or floating layer
14. Add 2 microliters of cleared lysate into tube
15. Add 18 microliters of water to tube
16. Add 5 microliters of 5X SDS sample buffer to tube
17. Add 1 mM GTP and 20 µM paclitaxel to collected supernatant
18. Incubate mixture at 37°C for 30 minutes
19. Prepare cushion buffer by adding 600 µL glycerol to 400 µL lysis buffer
20. Supplement cushion buffer with 20 µM paclitaxel
21. Prewarm cushion buffer to 37°C
22. Add 800 microliters of cushion buffer to an ultracentrifuge tube
23. Pipette GTP-paclitaxel-supplemented lysate carefully on top of cushion buffer
24. Place tube in ultracentrifuge rotor
25. Centrifuge at 100,000 G at 30°C for 30 minutes
26. Mark outward-facing edge of tube to identify microtubule pellet location
27. Add lysis buffer next to pellet
28. Rotate tube several times to remove glycerol from pellet and tube walls
29. Aspirate and repeat glycerol washing step three times
30. Resuspend washed pellet gently using cut pipette tip in 50 microliters prewarmed resuspension buffer
31. Place tube at 37 degrees Celsius
32. Install blotting paper in plunge freezer device
33. Set plunge freezer temperature to 30 degrees Celsius
34. Set plunge freezer humidification to 100%
35. Allow device to equilibrate for 30 minutes
36. Configure plunge freezer for two applications
37. Set first application parameters: force=10, blot time=2 seconds, wait time=0 seconds
38. Place EM grids with carbon side up into metal glow discharger vehicle
39. Glow discharge EM grids at 30 milliamperes for 60 seconds
40. Cool polystyrene container assembly with liquid nitrogen
41. Prepare liquid ethane by condensing ethane gas into cold metal cup
42. Grab glow discharged grid with plunge tweezers
43. Click grid into plunge freezer
44. Position polystyrene container with liquid ethane in plunge freezer
45. Run through prepared program
46. Apply 3.5 microliters of microtubule solution to grid
47. Let plunge freezer blot grid
48. Apply 3.5 microliters of diluted protein
49. Let plunger blot and plunge freeze grid in liquid ethane

12. Electroporate mixture

20. Transfer 5 mL seed culture to 500 mL LB with ampicillin

24. Remove supernatant and resuspend pellet
25. Centrifuge cells for 20 minutes
26. Remove supernatant
27. Resuspend pellet in 5 milliliters of binding buffer
28. Sonicate cells on ice for 10 seconds
29. Cool cells for 10 seconds
30. Repeat sonication and cooling cycle five times
31. Centrifuge lysate for 30 minutes
32. Transfer supernatant to collection tube
33. Purify supernatant on nickel NTA affinity column
34. Fill 10,000 MWCO concentrator with 20 milliliters of purified sample

38. Store purified FRET biosensor at -80 degrees Celsius
39. Prepare 0.2 micromolar solution of CMY biosensor protein in 0.8% PBS as detection solution
40. Turn on handheld FRET analyzer
41. Set analysis temperature to 53 degrees Celsius
42. Set instrument to calibration mode

44. Preheat PBS sample to 53 degrees Celsius in analyzer and set background
45. Fill cuvette with detection solution
46. Place detection solution cuvette into analyzer and set baseline
47. Replace cuvette with one containing detection solution and one millimolar sugar
48. Set intensity at saturation to finish calibration
49. Transfer 1 milliliter of beverage to a 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube
50. Centrifuge beverage sample at specified parameters
51. Remove supernatant using a 1 milliliter syringe
52. Filter supernatant through 0.2 micrometer syringe filter
53. Combine 0.1 milliliters of filtrate and 0.9 milliliters of 0.8% PBS in 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube
54. Vortex mixture gently to ensure complete dilution
55. Transfer 5 microliters of diluted beverage sample and 495 microliters of detection solution to cuvette
56. Place cuvette in analyzer and preheat sample to 53 degrees Celsius
57. Press set button to measure sugar content

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Thaw bottle of basement membrane matrix on ice or at 4°C overnight
2. Transfer resected tissue to 10-centimeter sterile Petri dish
3. Examine tissue macroscopically; document morphological characteristics (fatty/vascularized/necrotic), record size/shape, and photograph with ruler
4. Mince tissue into small pieces using sterile number 10 scalpel
5. Transfer minced tissue to 50-milliliter conical tube
6. Add 10 milliliters of 2 mg/mL collagenase IV solution
7. Seal the tube
8. Place sealed tube on orbital shaker at 37°C, 140 RPM, 30° angle for 30-90 minutes
9. Place aliquot of complete medium in bead/water bath at 37°C to pre-warm
10. Resuspend tissue every 15 minutes by vigorous mixing using 5-mL sterile pre-coated serological pipette
11. Monitor dissociation under microscope at ≥5X magnification
12. Centrifuge sample at 400 G for five minutes
13. Aspirate supernatant
14. Add 10 milliliters of AdDF+++
15. Centrifuge sample and carefully aspirate supernatant
16. Add two milliliters of red blood cell lysis buffer (if tissue pellet is partially red)
17. Incubate for five minutes at room temperature (if tissue pellet is partially red)
18. Add 10 milliliters of AdDF+++ to tube
19. Centrifuge at 400 G for five minutes
20. Discard supernatant
21. Resuspend pellet in 50 to 300 microliters
22. Discard supernatant
23. Resuspend pellet in 50-300 microliters of undiluted cold basement membrane matrix
24. Mix suspension by pipetting carefully to avoid bubble formation
25. Plate 300 microliters of basement membrane matrix dome containing organoids per well in pre-warmed six-well tissue culture plate
26. Leave plate undisturbed in hood for 5 minutes
27. Incubate plate at 37°C for 20-30 minutes for matrix solidification
28. Add 3 ml of pre-warmed complete medium dropwise to each well
29. Incubate plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂
30. Capture organoid images using 5X objective on inverted bright-field microscope
31. Lift basement membrane matrix dome into well medium using cell scraper or 1-ml pipette tip
32. Transfer floating organoid dome with medium to 15-ml or 50-ml conical tube using pre-coated pipette tip
33. Add DPBS to increase volume to at least 5 milliliters
34. Centrifuge tubes at 400 G for 5 minutes to form basement membrane matrix-organoid layer
35. Aspirate supernatant
36. Add 5-20 ml DPBS (volume dependent on number of wells pooled)
37. Mix organoid-basement membrane matrix pellet in DPBS using pre-coated sterile disposable pipette
38. Centrifuge and discard supernatant
39. Add cell dissociation reagent using coated pipette tip at three times basement membrane matrix volume
40. Resuspend organoids in dissociation reagent
41. Place tube on orbital shaker at 37°C and 140 RPM in angled position for 8-15 minutes
42. Monitor dissociation progress under microscope every five minutes to ensure organoid breakdown
43. Add AdDF+++ at volume equal to or greater than cell dissociation reagent
44. Pipette to mix organoids
45. Centrifuge at 400 G for five minutes to obtain organoid pellet
46. Discard supernatant
47. Resuspend pellet in one milliliter of AdDF+++
48. Adjust volume to 10 milliliters with AdDF+++
49. Centrifuge tube for wash step
50. Discard supernatant after wash step
51. Add required amount of basement membrane matrix to digested organoids based on split ratio
52. Mix by gently pipetting up and down to avoid bubbles
53. Immediately place mixture on ice
54. Plate 300-microliter domes of organoid-matrix suspension in pre-warmed six-well plate
55. Leave plate undisturbed in hood for 5 minutes
56. Incubate plate at 37°C with 5% CO₂ for 20-30 minutes for dome solidification
57. Add 3 mL pre-warmed complete medium to each well
58. Place plate back in incubator

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Prepare yeast extract with supplements without adenine (YES), Pombe Glutamate Medium (PMG), and PMG without adenine
2. Mix media components according to specified table
3. Add G418 to media after autoclaving if necessary
4. Stir media for 5-10 minutes
5. Aliquot media into 90 milliliter Petri dishes
6. Store plates at 4 degrees Celsius
7. Use cloned rpt4 positive with 5' and 3' UTRs and silent mutation
8. Perform error-prone PCR with primers p5 and p6 and error-prone polymerase in 50 microliter total volume
9. Inoculate 10 milliliters of YES medium with yeast
10. Incubate culture for >16 hours to saturation
11. Dilute cells to OD600 0.2 using 200 milliliters of YES medium
12. Incubate culture at 30°C with shaking for 5-6 hours to OD600 0.6-0.8
13. Calculate volume of cells equivalent to OD600 of 30
14. Dispense cells into four 50ml conical tubes
15. Chill tubes on ice for 10 minutes
16. Harvest cells by centrifugation at 1050 G, 4°C for 3 minutes
17. Place microfuge tubes with cassette DNA and 10 electro cuvettes on ice
18. Discard supernatant while keeping tubes on ice
19. Add 15ml of 1.2M sorbitol
20. Gently shake tubes to resuspend cells
21. Centrifuge at 1050 G, 4°C for 3 minutes
22. Discard supernatant and perform second sorbitol wash
23. Discard supernatant after second centrifugation
24. Resuspend cells and transfer to one 15ml conical tube
25. Add 1.2 molar sorbitol up to 2.4 milliliters
26. Keep tube of cells on ice
27. Add 200 microliters of sorbitol-suspended cells to tube containing fusion PCR construct
28. Mix sample well
29. Transfer sample to electro cuvette
30. Add 600 microliters of 1.2-molar sorbitol to each electroporation cuvette
31. Spread transformed cells onto four YES plates
32. Incubate plates at 30 degrees Celsius for 24 hours
33. Perform replica plating on YES plates containing G418
34. Incubate replica plates for three additional days
35. Perform replica plating from YES plus G418 plates to YES without adenine plates
36. Perform replica plating from YES plus G418 plates to PMG without adenine plates
37. Incubate replica plates at 30°C for 1-2 days until colonies on YES without adenine plates show white coloration
38. Compare growth on YES without adenine and PMG without adenine plates
39. Select colonies showing pink or white coloration on YES without adenine plates with concurrent growth on PMG without adenine plates
40. Exclude colonies without growth on PMG without adenine plates (false positives)
41. Pick each colony and add to 10 microliters of SPZ solution containing 2.5 mg/ml zymolyase 100T
42. Incubate colonies at 37°C for at least 30 minutes
43. Use 1 microliter of incubated solution as template for colony PCR
44. Mark colonies with XhoI-cut PCR products and patch them to YES plus G418 plates
45. Isolate genomic DNA (gDNA) from fission yeast cells
46. Sequence PCR products
47. Compare obtained sequence with wild-type sequence
48. Identify mutations through sequence comparison
49. Re-introduce identified mutations into wild-type cells
50. Confirm phenotype using spotting assay in newly-made mutant cells

19. Attach 1-liter sidearm flask to vacuum pump

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 3 grams of sediment sample to pre-weighed sterile 50 milliliter tubes using ethanol-flamed metal scoops under UV-sterilized biosafety cabinet
2. Add 15 milliliters of sterile filtered 1% sodium hexametaphosphate (SHMP) solution to sample using sterile graduated burette
3. Homogenize sample at 17,500 RPM for one minute using liquid disperser/homogenizer with sterile dispersion rotor
4. Let sample rest for two minutes
5. Repeat homogenization process
6. Allow sample to rest for 10 minutes for particle sedimentation
7. Transfer supernatant containing cell biomass to clean 50 ml tube without disturbing pellet
8. Add 15 ml aliquot of SHMP to sedimented pellet
9. Repeat homogenization of pellet with SHMP
10. Collect supernatant from homogenized sample
11. Combine collected supernatant with first supernatant aliquot
12. Centrifuge combined sample at 20 × g for 1 minute
13. Transfer supernatant to fresh 50 ml tube
14. Discard pellet after supernatant transfer
15. Sterilize forceps using ethanol flame
16. Add sterile nitrocellulose membrane to filtration unit using sterilized forceps
17. Add half of supernatant sample onto membrane in filtration unit
18. Apply vacuum pump to filtration unit to collect cells on membrane
19. Stop vacuum pump after liquid fully passes through filter
20. Remove membrane from filtration unit using sterilized forceps
21. Place membrane into sterile petri dish
22. Repeat filtration process with fresh sterile membrane and remaining half of solution
23. Thaw membrane if frozen
24. Cut membrane into smaller pieces using ethanol-flamed sterilized scissors
25. Transfer membrane pieces into sterile 2 ml tube
26. Add 900 microliters of 1X TE buffer to tube
27. Vortex tube thoroughly to release biomass from membrane
28. Incubate tube at 65°C and 80 RPM for 10 minutes
29. Remove tube from incubator and cool for 15 minutes
30. Add 100 microliters of freshly prepared lysozyme (to final 2 mg/ml concentration)
31. Incubate sample at 37°C and 80 RPM for 60 minutes to lyse vegetative cells
32. Add 250 microliters of 3 normal sodium hydroxide and 250 microliters of 6% SDS solution to sample
33. Incubate sample at room temperature and 80 RPM for 60 minutes
34. Prepare sterile filtration unit holding 25 millimeter diameter membranes by autoclaving
35. Place 25 millimeter diameter 0.2 micrometer nitrocellulose membrane onto cooled filtration unit
36. Add sample onto membrane
37. Apply vacuum pump to filter liquid and capture endospores on membrane
38. Add 2 milliliters of sterile physiological solution to membrane
39. Apply vacuum pump to filter wash solution
40. Turn off vacuum pump
41. Leave membrane on filtration unit
42. Add 450 microliters of sterile water onto membrane
43. Add 50 microliters of 1X DNase reaction buffer onto membrane
44. Add 0.5 microliters of DNase enzyme onto membrane
45. Incubate membrane at 25°C for 15 minutes
46. Add 1 milliliter of physiological solution
47. Turn on pump to wash residual enzyme from sample
48. Transfer membrane containing endospores into sterile petri dish using sterile forceps

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Cut fresh citrus leaf with clean scissors and place in clean plastic sandwich bag
2. Chill mortar and pestle by adding small quantity of liquid nitrogen
3. Cut leaf tissue into small pieces (~1 square inch) using scissors
4. Place leaf pieces in chilled mortar to freeze immediately
5. Grind leaf tissue with mortar until liquid nitrogen evaporates and fine green powder forms
6. Chill metal spatula in liquid nitrogen for 10-15 seconds
7. Transfer ground tissue from mortar to 1.5mL micro-centrifuge tube using chilled spatula
8. Add 600 mL of nuclei lysis solution to sample using 1000 mL pipette
9. Vortex sample for 1-3 seconds
10. Incubate tube at 65°C in water bath for 15 minutes
11. Add 3 mL of RNA solution to lysate using 10 mL pipette
12. Mix tube contents by inverting 2-5 times
13. Incubate at 37°C for 15 minutes in cabinet incubator
14. Add 200 mL protein precipitation solution to sample
15. Vortex tube at high speed for 20 seconds
16. Centrifuge sample at 13000G for 3 minutes
17. Add 600 mL room-temperature isopropanol to new 1.5 mL micro-centrifuge tube
18. Remove supernatant from centrifuge tube
19. Transfer supernatant to tube containing isopropanol
20. Discard pellet
21. Invert tube to visualize DNA as thread-like strand
22. Centrifuge sample at 13000G for 1 minute at room temperature
23. Decant supernatant after centrifugation
24. Add 600 mL of 70% ethanol to pellet
25. Invert tube several times to wash pellet
26. Centrifuge tube at 13000G for one minute
27. Carefully aspirate supernatant
28. Invert tube with lid open on absorbent paper to air-dry for 15 minutes
29. Add 100 mL of DNA rehydration solution to the pellet
30. Incubate DNA suspension at 65°C in water bath for 1 hour
31. Tap tube periodically while mixing
32. Add 1 mL purified water to micro-cuvette
33. Read water as blank on spectrophotometer
34. Add 1 mL sample to micro-cuvette
35. Place micro-cuvette on spectrophotometer and obtain DNA concentration
36. Add all PCR reagents and 100 nanograms of genomic DNA to a 50mL PCR tube
37. Mix reagents by gently flicking the PCR tube
38. Perform quick centrifugation of PCR tube
39. Place PCR tube in thermocycler and run program
40. Weigh 0.4 grams of agarose powder
41. Add agarose powder to 200ml Erlenmeyer flask
42. Adjust volume to 50ml with 1X TAE buffer
43. Microwave mixture on high for 1.5 minutes
44. Shake mixture every 30 seconds until agarose dissolves completely
45. Add 2.5 mL of 10 mg/mL ethidium bromide to liquid agarose
46. Shake flask to mix ethidium bromide
47. Pour liquid agarose gel into level gel mold
48. Insert standard 12-tooth gel comb
49. Let gel cool at room temperature
50. Remove gel comb
51. Place solidified gel in electrophoresis unit filled with 1X TAE buffer
52. Load amplified DNA sample and 5μL DNA ladder in separate wells
53. Start electrophoresis unit at constant 90 volts for 35 minutes
54. Remove gel from electrophoresis unit
55. Place gel on UV transilluminator
56. Capture photographs of gel banding patterns at 302 nanometers

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Dissolve 30.29 grams of tris HCl powder in 100 milliliters of deionized water
2. Transfer solution to 250 milliliter volumetric flask
3. Fill flask to line with deionized water
4. Mix solution to obtain 1M tris HCl buffer
5. Prepare 20 milliliters of 0.1M tris HCl buffer by dilution from stock
6. Adjust pH to 8.5 using 1M hydrochloric acid while monitoring with pH meter
7. Dilute 0.02 milliliters of 1 mg/mL 100nm nanodiamond suspension to 1 milliliter with pH 8.5 tris buffer
8. Stir mixture for 10 minutes to obtain 0.02 mg/mL nanodiamond suspension
9. Dissolve 20 milligrams of dopamine hydrochloride in two milliliters of pH 8.5 tris buffer
10. Vortex mixture for 30 seconds
11. Add 5, 7.5, or 10 microliters of dopamine solution to nanodiamond solution to achieve final dopamine hydrochloride concentration of 50, 75, or 100 milligram per milliliter
12. Vigorously stir mixture at 25 degrees Celsius for 12 hours in the dark
13. Transfer suspension of polydopamine-coated nanodiamonds to 1.5 milliliter centrifuge tube
14. Centrifuge at 16,000 g for two hours
15. Remove supernatant
16. Wash nanodiamonds three times with 1 ml portions of deionized water at 16,000 g for one hour per wash
17. Add 200 microliters of deionized water to washed solids
18. Sonicate mixture for 30 seconds to redisperse polydopamine-coated nanodiamonds
19. Serially dilute 40 microliters of polydopamine-coated nanodiamonds suspension twice with deionized water
20. Dissolve 100 milligrams of silver nitrate in 10 milliliters of deionized water by vortexing
21. In fume hood, add one molar aqueous ammonia to silver nitrate solution dropwise until yellow precipitate forms, periodically shaking solution
22. Continue adding ammonia until precipitate disappears to obtain diamine silver hydroxide solution
23. Add 4.3 or 6.4 microliters of diamine silver solution to 40 microliters of diluted nanodiamond dispersion
24. Adjust mixture volume to 100 microliters with deionized water
25. Sonicate mixture for 10 minutes
26. Centrifuge dispersion at 16,000 g for 15 minutes to remove free silver ions
27. Discard supernatant
28. Wash silver nanoparticle-decorated polydopamine-coated nanodiamonds by centrifugation: 3 cycles in 100 microliter deionized water at 16,000 g for 5 minutes per cycle
29. Add 100 microliters deionized water to nanodiamonds
30. Redisperse nanodiamonds by sonication for 30 seconds
31. Redisperse samples by sonication for 30 seconds
32. Characterize nanodiamonds with UV-Vis spectroscopy scanning from 250 to 550 nanometers
33. Deposit 5 microliters of silver nanoparticle-decorated nanodiamonds on plasma-cleaned carbon-coated copper grids
34. Let grids sit for 3 minutes
35. Wick away excess solution with filter paper
36. Apply drop of deionized water to each grid
37. Let water sit on grid for 15 seconds
38. Wick away water with filter paper
39. Repeat washing cycle (steps 6-8) three times
40. Let grids air dry
41. Visualize samples with transmission electron microscopy

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Centrifuge cyanobacteria sample at 3,500 G for 10 minutes at 25°C
2. Discard supernatant into biological waste
3. Cover centrifuge tube with sealing film and pierce holes using sharp long-nose tweezers
4. Store tube upright at -80°C
5. Place tube upright into freeze dryer glass container
6. Place container inside -80°C freezer
7. Cool container in freezer for 5 minutes
8. Remove glass container from freezer
9. Attach rubber lid to container
10. Ensure rubber valve outlet handle points upward and is vented to atmosphere
11. Firmly attach glass container to freeze-dryer
12. Slowly turn rubber valve outlet handle to down position to expose jar to vacuum
13. Allow freeze-drying for up to 24 hours to sublimate all liquid
14. Weigh 15 to 50 milligrams of frozen sample pellets into 15 milliliter centrifuge tube using analytical balance
15. Add 300 to 600 microliters of 10% aqueous Trichloroacetic acid (TCA) to sample
16. Place sample tubes in container filled with crushed ice
17. Wipe sonicator probe with lint-free paper wipe sprayed with 70% ethanol
18. Fully immerse probe end into sample and start sonication
19. Place sample-containing centrifuge tube on ice for 1 minute
20. Repeat sonication process for cell lysis
21. Cool sample at 4°C for 24 hours
22. Centrifuge sample at 3,500 G for 15 minutes at 8°C
23. Transfer supernatant to 2 ml tube labeled 'free fraction' using micropipette
24. Add 400 microliters of 10% aqueous TCA into sample pellet
25. Break up pellet by vortex agitation or micro pipette tip
26. Centrifuge sample
27. Transfer supernatant to 2ml free fraction tube
28. Repeat pellet wash using 10% TCA acetone
29. Place open free fraction tube in centrifugal evaporator
30. Evaporate until volatile liquid is removed
31. Cover tube securely with ceiling film
32. Pierce film with holes using sharp long-nose tweezer
33. Reconstitute sample with 200 microliters of 20mM hydrochloric acid
34. Place sample in -80°C freezer
35. Add 400 microliters of 100% acetone to sample pellet using micro pipette
36. Break up pellet using vortex agitation or micro pipette tip
37. Quantitatively transfer washed/resuspended pellet to glass shell vial using 1mL micro pipette
38. Add 400 microliters acetone to sample tube to transfer remaining pellet
39. Centrifuge sample
40. Decant supernatant liquid into biological waste
41. Dry pellet in centrifugal evaporator until liquid is removed and pellet is dry
42. Add 1 mL of 6 M hydrochloric acid to bottom of hydrolysis vial
43. Insert labeled shell vials with dried samples into hydrolysis vial using tweezers
44. Ensure vials are in upright stable position
45. Attach lid to hydrolysis vial
46. Push red knob on lid to close valve
47. Attach vacuum tube to head of hydrolysis vial lid after turning on vacuum pump
48. Press green knob on lid to open valve
49. Allow vacuum pump to remove air from vial for 1 minute
50. Depress red knob on hydrolysis vial lid to close vial
51. Turn off vacuum pump and remove vacuum tube
52. Attach rubber tube to nitrogen gas tap on laboratory bench
53. Open nitrogen gas tap slightly
54. Seal tube end with thumb and monitor gas pressure buildup until escape
55. Attach rubber tube to hydrolysis vial lid head
56. Push green knob on hydrolysis vial lid
57. Time duration for gas escape
58. Push red knob on hydrolysis vial lid
59. Remove rubber tube from vial
60. Repeat entire vacuum treatment process twice
61. Place hydrolysis vial into preheated oven at 110°C
62. Hydrolyze samples at 110°C for 16-18 hours
63. Remove glass hydrolysis vial from oven using oven gloves
64. Cool vial in fume hood for 10 minutes
65. Push green knob while facing vial away to release pressure and gas

2. Place beaker with 10% bleach solution and sterile 10 microliter syringe in workspace

5. Ensure no air bubbles in syringe containing 4 microliters of AAV solution

7. Add additional 10% bleach to waste container
8. Allow waste to sit for 30 minutes

12. Shave fur from between eyes to behind ears
13. Place rat back in induction chamber for 3 minutes
14. Secure rat in stereotaxic apparatus
15. Cover eyes with ophthalm ointment
16. Clean incision site with Betadine
17. Make 2.4 centimeter midline incision over skull starting 2 mm caudal to eyes
18. Retract skin to expose skull
19. Clear membranous tissue from skull until bgma and lambda are visible
20. Ensure skull is flat
21. Position drill at bgma, zero coordinates, then move to desired medial/lateral/anterior-posterior coordinates
22. Drill hole using 0.9, 0.7, or 0.5 millimeter drill bit
23. Set infusion pump to deliver virus at 0.2 microliters per minute
24. Load syringe into infusion arm of stereotaxic device
25. Lower syringe to desired coordinate
26. Activate pump to deliver desired amount of virus
27. Leave syringe in place for 10 minutes to prevent backflow
28. Raise syringe at 5 millimeters per minute
29. Move to next coordinate to repeat infusion procedure
30. Administer postoperative analgesia according to approved protocol
31. Place rat in clean cage with bedding, lid, and proper signage
32. Monitor rat until fully recovered from anesthesia
33. Return rat to housing room
34. Prepare fresh 1 mg/ml solution of clozapine-N-oxide according to protocol instructions
35. Inject CNO solution intraperitoneally at 1 mg/kg dosage
36. Initiate computer code for behavioral session
37. Place rats in testing chambers
38. Begin first preconditioning session (64 minutes duration)
39. Present 12 intermixed auditory-visual stimulus trials
40. Present 10-second tone stimulus (6 trials)
41. Immediately present 5-second flashing light stimulus after tone
42. Present 10-second white noise stimulus alone (6 trials)
43. Present visual stimulus (flashing light) for 5 seconds
44. Deliver 2 food pellets (45 mg each) immediately after stimulus
45. Implement inter-trial intervals averaging 7 minutes
46. Perform 78-minute test session with 12 intermixed trials of auditory stimuli alone
47. Present tone stimulus (preconditioned stimulus) alone for 10 seconds on 6 trials
48. Present white noise stimulus (unpaired stimulus) alone for 10 seconds on 6 trials
49. Remove rats from chambers
50. Return rats to animal colony
51. Analyze behavioral data from conditioning and test phases according to protocol
52. Perform immunohistochemistry against tagged reporter or reporter protein
53. Examine stained sections by microscopy
54. Ascertain location and expression of designer receptor and/or reporter

23. Transfer S1 sarkosyl-soluble supernatants
24. Transfer S1 sarkosyl-soluble supernatants to 1.5-milliliter tubes
25. Store supernatants at -80°C

27. Dislodge pellets from tube bottoms using pipette tip
28. Transfer resuspended P1 pellets to new 500-microliter ultracentrifuge tubes
29. Pair-balance tubes and centrifuge at 180,000×g for 30 minutes at 4°C
30. Discard S2 supernatant
31. Add 50-75 microliters of urea buffer with protease and phosphatase inhibitor cocktail to sarkosyl-insoluble pellets

50. Rinse membrane three times in TBS with 0.1% Tween-20 (10 minutes per rinse) and twice in TBS (5 minutes per rinse)

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Thinly spread a small amount of epoxy on both sides of double-sided tape using a razor blade
2. Laser-cut epoxy-coated tape to create four channels
3. Sandwich epoxy tape between four-hole slide and PEG coverslip to create flow chip
4. Apply gentle pressure along channel length using pipette tip to create seal
5. Cure epoxy for minimum one hour
6. Align chip so channel openings are positioned at centers of adapters
7. Place two transparent acrylic spacers on top of chip
8. Apply firm pressure in middle of block
9. Screw at ends of each spacer
10. Screw inlets into threaded holes on other side of bracket
11. Monitor sealing condition through transparent acrylic block
12. Flow 200 microliters of wash buffer into chamber to check for leakage
13. Aggressively push additional 200 microliters of wash buffer to remove bubbles if present
14. Add 40 microliters of BSA to flow chamber to prevent nonspecific binding
15. Incubate chamber in humidity chamber for 10 minutes
16. Add 40 microliters of Tween 20 to flow chamber
17. Incubate for 10 minutes to reduce nonspecific binding
18. Wash channel with 200 microliters of wash buffer
19. Add 40 microliters of Streptavidin to flow chamber
20. Incubate flow chamber for 20 minutes
21. Wash flow chamber with 200 microliters of wash buffer
22. Add 40 microliters of hybridized protein G-TGT to flow chamber
23. Incubate for 20 minutes
24. Wash with wash buffer
25. Add 40 microliters of protein G-TGT top strand
26. Incubate for 20 minutes to complete unhybridized TGT bottom strand
27. Wash with wash buffer
28. Add 40 microliters of P-selectin-Fc to flow chamber
29. Incubate flow chamber for 60 minutes
30. Wash flow chamber with wash buffer
31. Fill 5 mL glass syringe with rolling buffer
32. Tap sides of syringe to dislodge bubbles
33. Push dislodged bubbles out as they float toward tip
34. Insert sterile needle into 200 mm polyethylene tubing
35. Connect needle to glass syringe
36. Fix syringe onto syringe pump
37. Tilt syringe pump to elevate plunger side
38. Insert end of tube into flow chamber inlet
39. Take 1-2 ml of cell suspension sample
40. Centrifuge sample to pellet cells
41. Remove supernatant medium
42. Gently resuspend cell pellet in 500 μl rolling buffer
43. Disconnect tubing from flow chamber inlets and outlet
44. Pipette 40 μl cell suspension into flow chamber
45. Reconnect tubing to flow chamber
46. Start syringe pump at desired flow rates
47. Observe cell rolling using dark field microscope with 10X objective
48. Add 40 microliters of DNA-PAINT imager strand prepared in DNA-PAINT buffer to the channel
49. Perform total internal reflection fluorescence microscopy using conditions specified in manuscript
50. Add permanent imager strand
51. Incubate for 120 seconds in T50M5 buffer
52. Wash the channel
53. Infuse 200 microliters of wash buffer
54. Program microscope to scan 400 × 50 image area
55. Split raw data into individual TIP files (max 10,000 images/file) using ImageJ

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Measure 150 milliliters of acetone using graduated cylinder
2. Transfer measured acetone to 250 milliliter Erlenmeyer flask
3. Add 30 grams of commercial gliadin powder to flask while stirring
4. Seal flask opening with aluminum foil
5. Stir solution continuously at room temperature overnight
6. Filter solution through filter paper
7. Wash retentate with approximately 50 milliliters of fresh acetone
8. Transfer retentate and filter paper to square cell culture dish
9. Cover dish with large filter paper to slow acetone evaporation
10. Transfer dried defatted gliadin to airtight container
11. Store container on lab bench
12. Measure 150 milliliters of deionized water using a graduated cylinder
13. Transfer measured deionized water to a 1,000 milliliter Erlenmeyer flask
14. Measure 350 milliliters of absolute ethanol using a mess cylinder
15. Add measured absolute ethanol to the Erlenmeyer flask containing deionized water
16. Stir solution vigorously until air bubbles are no longer observed
17. Add 20 grams of defatted gliadin powder to the stirring solution
18. Transfer entire solution to a 1,000 milliliter mess cylinder
19. Allow solution to sit for two days
20. Transfer supernatant to round-bottomed flask using flexible tubing
21. Remove ethanol from supernatant via rotary evaporation
22. Freeze solution containing gliadin aggregates by immersing flask in methanol dry ice mixture
23. Freeze-dry solution at -70 degrees Celsius under vacuum
24. Crush freeze-dried gliadin with mortar and pestle
25. Grind crushed gliadin with coffee grinder to obtain fine powder
26. Transfer powder into airtight container
27. Store powder at room temperature
28. Add 3.2 grams of distilled water and 6.8 grams of absolute ethanol to a tared scintillation vial
29. Stir solution vigorously until air bubbles are no longer observed
30. Add 40 microliters of four normal hydrochloric acid to vial while stirring
31. Add 20 milligrams of purified gliadin powder
32. Continue stirring until powder is fully dissolved
33. Slowly add 80-100 microliters of ethyl cyanoacrylate monomer while stirring
34. Reduce stirring speed to 500 rpm
35. Continue stirring solution at 500 rpm for one hour
36. Transfer solution into centrifuge tube
37. Balance weight of centrifuge tube
38. Centrifuge sample at 10,000 × g for 20 minutes
39. Transfer nanoparticle suspension to scintillation vial using pipette
40. Store nanoparticle suspension at room temperature
41. Prepare 68 weight percent aqueous ethanol solution in 20 milliliter scintillation vial
42. Stir solution until no air bubbles are observed
43. Add 40 microliters of 4N hydrochloric acid to solution while stirring
44. Add 50 microliters of nanoparticle suspension to solution while stirring
45. Measure nanoparticle size using dynamic light scattering following manufacturer's instructions
46. Wash glass plate surface with soap water
47. Spray nanoparticle suspension on half of glass plate
48. Rinse surface with water
49. Spray water on entire glass plate surface
50. Observe wettability difference between coated and uncoated surfaces

Complete step list: Weigh 5.92 grams of aluminum chloride hexahydrate using calibrated digital balance
2. Transfer weighed aluminum chloride hexahydrate to 250 milliliter beaker

6. Place covered beaker on magnetic stir plate
7. Stir at moderate speed until aluminum salt dissolves
8. Remove beaker from magnetic stir plate
9. Uncover beaker

11. Replace paraffin film on beaker
12. Place beaker on magnetic stir plate
13. Stir solution at moderate speed until gelled
14. Remove beaker from magnetic stir plate
15. Age gel at room temperature for 24 hours
16. Weigh 1.4 grams of copper nitrate trihydrate using calibrated digital balance
17. Add weighed copper nitrate trihydrate to 40 milliliters of absolute ethanol in beaker

19. Sonicate until copper salt dissolves

21. Remove stir bar from alumina gel
22. Break alumina gel into pieces using spatula
23. Transfer copper solution to 250 milliliter beaker containing gel pieces
24. Cover beaker with paraffin film
25. Age gel at room temperature for 24 hours
26. Remove excess solvent from aged gel
27. Add 40 milliliters of fresh absolute ethanol to beaker
28. Replace paraffin film on beaker
29. Age gel for another 24 hours at room temperature
30. Obtain stainless steel mold
31. Cut graphite gasket sheet (1.6 mm thick) to size for mold coverage
32. Cut stainless steel foil (0.012 mm thick) to size for mold coverage
33. Program hot press for ethanol extraction

35. Distribute wet sol-gels into mold wells
36. Center mold on hot press heating plate
37. Top off each mold well with absolute ethanol
38. Place stainless steel foil on top of mold
39. Place graphite sheet on top of mold
40. Seal mold with foil and graphite sheet
41. Begin hot press extraction program
42. Remove mold from hot press
43. Remove gasket material from mold
44. Transfer aerogels into sample containers
45. Place clean 250 milliliter beaker on calibrated digital balance
46. Pipette approximately 13 milliliters of TMOS into beaker

48. Pipette 32.63 grams of methanol and 3.90 grams deionized water into beaker
49. Shake 20 weight percent copper two oxide nanodispersion to resuspend nanoparticles
50. Pipette 1.5 grams nanodispersion into precursor solution beaker
51. Pipette 200 microliters of 1.5 molar ammonia solution into beaker
52. Cover beaker with paraffin film and sonicate mixture for 5-10 minutes until monophasic
53. Pour 20 milliliters of cooled aerogel material
54. Transfer material into clean, dry UCAT test section and insert end screen
55. Load test section into UCAT assembly using copper washers and clamps to seal
56. Securely close UCAT oven
57. Set desired oven temperature
58. Start the oven
59. Set bypass valve to deliver air through test cell
60. Adjust mass flow controllers to deliver correct quantities of air and simulated exhaust for warm-up and testing to maintain desired space velocity
61. Turn on warm-up air flow to purge test cell
62. Wait for flow through test cell to stabilize at desired test temperature (typically 30 minutes)

Complete step list: Identify distal stomach and pylorus
2. Hold pylorus with forceps while snipping away mesentery
3. Perform blunt dissection with back of scissors to scrape away adherent pancreas
4. Use scissors to remove seven centimeters of proximal intestine without tearing
5. Place intestinal segment into ice-cold DPBS
6. Place intestinal segment into ice cold DPBS without calcium or magnesium
7. Attach blunt end 18-20 gauge needle to 5-10 milliliter syringe
8. Flush fecal contents with ice cold DPBS using prepared syringe
9. Divide intestine into three centimeter segments
10. Remove mesentery and longitudinal muscle from segments
11. Take a cotton swab
12. Break off approximately 4 cm of cotton swab
13. Separate wooden stick from cotton tip
14. Soak wooden stick in DPBS
15. Slip intestinal segment onto wetted stick
16. Remove adherent mesentery using needle nose forceps
17. Make two longitudinal nicks along intestine attachment site using razor blade or scalpel
18. Wet cotton tip with DPBS
19. Rub wetted cotton tip longitudinally along muscle to loosen LMMP
20. Move cotton tip horizontally to tease LMMP from circular muscle
21. Discard LMMP unless harvesting EGCs
22. Cut intestines into 0.5 cm pieces using fine scissors
23. Collect tissue pieces in 30 ml ice-cold EDTA HEPES DPBS solution
24. Rock tissue solution at 4°C for 10 minutes at 60 tilts/minute
25. Pre-moisten 5 ml plastic pipette by pipetting EDTA HEPES DPBS buffer up and down once
26. Triturate tissue-buffer suspension 20 times using pre-moistened pipette
27. Pour mixture through 100 micron nylon cell strainer to collect tissue
28. Transfer strained tissue to new 50 ml tube containing 30 ml ice-cold EDTA HEPES DPBS using needle nose forceps
29. Rock tissue solution at 4°C for 10 minutes at 60 tilts/minute (second incubation)
30. Perform gentle trituration using pre-moistened 5 ml pipette
31. Pour mixture through 100 micron nylon cell strainer and discard flow-through
32. Transfer tissue from nylon strainer to 15 ml conical centrifuge tube containing 5 ml cell recovery solution
33. Rock tube for 25-30 minutes at 4°C
34. Gently triturate 10 times
35. Gently triturate tissue 10 times to dissociate enteric glial cells from lamina propria
36. Filter dissociated material through 40 micron filter and collect filtrate in clean 50 milliliter tube
37. Rinse tissue on nylon filter with 1 milliliter of DPBS
38. Discard tissue
39. Transfer 5-6 milliliters of filtrate to clean 15 milliliter conical centrifuge tube
40. Centrifuge filtrate at 2,000 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C in swinging bucket centrifuge
41. Resuspend glial cell pellet in ≥1 milliliter resuspension buffer by pipetting gently with 500 microliter pipette tip without introducing bubbles
42. Plate enteric glial cells
43. Plate enteric glial cells
44. Add 200 microliters of cell suspension to each well of laminin-coated six-well plate containing glial growth media
45. Add 100 microliters of cell suspension to each well of laminin-coated 12-well plate containing glial growth media

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Wash neonatal foreskin twice with 5 ml wash media in 5 ml conical tube
2. Transfer washed foreskin to sterile Petri dish
3. Scrape off adipose tissue and loose connective tissues from dermal layer using scalpel and forceps
4. Rewash foreskin with wash media
5. Place foreskin epidermis side up in six-well plate containing 2 ml Dispase enzyme diluted in wash media
6. Transfer plate to incubator for 4 hours
7. Transfer incubated foreskin to Petri dish
8. Separate epidermis from dermis layer using fine-tip forceps
9. Place epidermis into 15 ml conical tube containing 2 ml of 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA
10. Crush floating epidermis using 5 ml serological pipette
11. Incubate sample for 15 minutes at 37°C with periodic vortexing (5 seconds every 5 minutes)
12. Add 2 ml soybean trypsin inhibitor
13. Mix by pipetting to neutralize trypsin
14. Centrifuge cell suspension
15. Resuspend pellet in 12 milliliters of complete KSFM-SCM medium
16. Plate cells in 10 centimeter dish
17. Incubate dish overnight at 37 degrees Celsius and 5% carbon dioxide
18. Remove medium using aspirating pipette
19. Replace medium with 12 milliliters of complete KSFM-SCM
20. Repeat medium replacement procedure on days four and seven
21. Add 2.5 grams of agarose to 50 milliliters of PBS in a 100 milliliter glass bottle
22. Autoclave the bottle on liquid cycle
23. Allow agarose solution to cool to room temperature
24. Place cooled agarose bottle in a one liter beaker filled with 200 milliliters of deionized water
25. Melt agarose mixture in research-grade microwave for up to two minutes
26. Mix agarose every 60 seconds by gently tilting bottle side to side
27. Add 3 milliliters of melted 5% agarose to 12 milliliters of prewarmed KSFM-SCM at 42°C to achieve 1% final concentration
28. Dispense 200 microliters of 1% agarose mix into each well of 96-well plate using multi-channel pipetter
29. Aspirate media from spheroid-forming normal human keratinocytes
30. Wash cells with two milliliters of PBS
31. Aspirate PBS from cells
32. Add two milliliters of 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA to washed cells
33. Incubate cells for five minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
34. Add two milliliters of soybean trypsin inhibitor to plate
35. Wash cells from plate into 15 milliliter tube
36. Centrifuge at 250 times G for five minutes
37. Resuspend cell pellet in 1 mL PBS
38. Quantify cells using Trypan Blue staining
39. Aliquot 20,000 normal human keratinocytes in 100 μL KSFM-SCM
40. Seed cells in each well of prepared 96-well plate
41. Incubate plate overnight
42. Analyze seeded wells for epidermis sphere formation using inverted phase contrast microscope
43. Add 4 ml of prewarmed KSFM-SCM at 37°C to six-centimeter dish
44. Transfer single spheroid to plate using wide-bore 1 ml pipette tip
45. Incubate plate overnight
46. Analyze seeded spheroid for attaching/propagating cells using inverted phase contrast microscope
47. Remove media from plate
48. Add 2 ml of fresh KSFM-SCM media to plate
49. Quantify cell viability using Trypan Blue and automated cell counter
50. Aliquot 100 microliters containing 100,000 to 4 million keratinocytes into 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes
51. Place tubes on ice in dark environment by turning off laminar hood lights
52. Add 2 μL FITC-conjugated anti-integrin alpha six and 2 μL PE-conjugated anti-EGFR to tubes for 1:50 dilution
53. Prepare tube without antibodies for unstained control
54. Incubate tubes on ice in dark for 30 minutes
55. Perform flow cytometry analysis with appropriate lasers
56. Use negative and positive controls to establish gates
57. Sort epidermal stem cell fraction, proliferative progenitor fraction, and committed progenitor fraction
58. Transfer sorted cells from sorting tubes into 15 ml conical tubes containing 10x volume of PBS
59. Centrifuge tubes at 250 × g for 5 minutes
60. Remove supernatant from tubes
61. Resuspend pellet in 4 mL of KSFM-SCM
62. Transfer resuspended cells to 6 cm plate
63. Incubate plate overnight at 37°C in 5% CO₂ incubator with 95% humidity
64. Remove media from plates
65. Add 4 mL of prewarmed KSFM-SCM (37°C) to plates
66. Repeat media change with 4 mL prewarmed KSFM-SCM every 3 days until 70-80% confluency

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Grow cells to 70-80% confluence on 10 cm tissue culture plates
2. Stimulate cells according to experimental aims
3. Remove tissue culture medium
4. Add 5 mL fixation solution
5. Incubate cells for 10 minutes at room temperature on shaking platform
6. Remove fixation solution
7. Wash cells twice with 10 mL ice-cold 1X PBS
8. Remove 1X PBS
9. Add 5 mL glycine stop fix solution
10. Incubate cells for 5 minutes at room temperature on shaking platform
11. Remove glycine stop fix solution
12. Wash cells twice with 10 mL ice-cold 1X PBS
13. Add 2 ml of cell scraping solution to culture vessel
14. Harvest cells using cell scraper
15. Transfer harvested cells to conical tube on ice
16. Centrifuge samples at 600 × g and 4°C for 10 minutes
17. Remove supernatant from centrifuged samples
18. Resuspend pellet in 1 ml of 1X cell lysis buffer
19. Incubate resuspended cells on ice for 30 minutes
20. Transfer cell suspension to ice-cold Dounce homogenizer
21. Homogenize suspension with type B pestle for 10 strokes
22. Transfer homogenized suspension to microcentrifuge tube
23. Centrifuge sample at 2,400 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
24. Discard supernatant
25. Re-suspend pellet in 350 microliters of nuclei digestion buffer
26. Incubate samples at 37°C for 5 minutes
27. Add micrococcal nuclease and mix by vortexing
28. Incubate samples at 37°C for 5-20 minutes with mixing every 2 minutes by inversion
29. Add 7 microliters of ice-cold 0.5 M EDTA to stop micrococcal nuclease reaction
30. Centrifuge samples at 16,200 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
31. Transfer sheared DNA supernatant to fresh microcentrifuge tube
32. Aliquot 50 microliters of supernatant into separate microcentrifuge tube for quality control
33. Combine 10-25 μg sheared cross-linked chromatin with 25 μl chromatin immunoprecipitation grade protein G magnetic beads
34. Add 10 μl immunoprecipitation dilution buffer and 1 μl protease inhibitor cocktail
35. Incubate mixture for 4 hours
36. Add 1-10 μg antibody to 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube
37. Add distilled water to achieve 100 μl final volume in microcentrifuge tube
38. Incubate samples on end-to-end rotator for 4-12 hours at 4°C
39. Place incubated tubes on magnetic stand
40. Remove and discard supernatant after magnetic bead aggregation
41. Wash beads three times with 800 microliters of wash buffer one
42. Mix beads by inverting tube several times (per wash cycle)
43. Place tube on magnetic stand (per wash cycle)
44. Remove wash buffer after magnetic bead aggregation (per wash cycle)
45. Wash beads once with 800 microliters of wash buffer two
46. Re-suspend beads after washes
47. Add 50 microliters of elution buffer
48. Incubate samples on end-to-end rotator for 15 minutes at room temperature
49. Transfer supernatant to fresh tube using magnetic stand
50. Add six microliters of five molar sodium chloride
51. Add two microliters of proteinase K
52. Mix samples
53. Incubate samples for two hours at 65 degrees celsius

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Spray mouse skin with 70% ethanol
2. Open skin and expose leg muscle using sterile dissecting scissors
3. Cut muscle alongside leg to view bone clearly
4. Gently remove bone without damaging it
5. Place bone into tube containing 4% paraldehyde
6. Place tube on ice
7. Place bone sample tubes at 4°C in cold room on orbital shaker for minimum 24 hours
8. Retrieve fixed bone tissue from orbital shaker
9. Wash bone tissue with 1X PBS twice for 3 minutes each
10. Place bone specimen in new container with 20% EDTA at pH 8
11. Incubate container on orbital shaker at 140 RPM for 30 minutes at room temperature
12. Replace EDTA solution with fresh 20% EDTA
13. Agitate container on orbital shaker for 30 minutes
14. Add fresh EDTA to container
15. Incubate container on orbital shaker at 4°C overnight
16. Retrieve samples from EDTA container
17. Wash samples two times with 1X PBS to remove EDTA residues
18. Place bone in 70% ethanol
19. Incubate in 70% ethanol for 15 minutes at 140 RPM
20. Discard 70% ethanol solution
21. Add 90% ethanol solution
22. Incubate in 90% ethanol for 15 minutes at 140 RPM
23. Place dehydrated bone tissue in new container with xylene
24. Incubate in xylene for 20 minutes at 140 RPM
25. Replace used xylene with fresh xylene
26. Incubate in fresh xylene for 20 minutes
27. Place cleared bone sample in embedding cassette
28. Begin wax infiltration
29. Incubate sample at 60°C for 30 minutes
30. Discard used wax and add new wax
31. Incubate sample at 60°C for 30 minutes
32. Replace wax with fresh wax
33. Incubate sample at 60°C for 45 minutes
34. Position bone in mold with desired orientation
35. Solidify wax on cold surface
36. Store FFPE block at 4°C until sectioning
37. Spray all work surfaces and instruments with decontaminating solutions to remove RNases
38. Set up water bath with double distilled water at 42 degrees Celsius
39. Clean microtome blade with 70% ethanol to remove oil
40. Decontaminate microtome blade to remove RNases
41. Secure blade on microtome
42. Set microtome clearance angle to 10 degrees
43. Spray tweezers, brushes, and probes with decontaminating solutions
44. Store decontaminated tools in ice bucket
45. Prepare ice bath by adding double distilled water to ice bucket
46. Place FFPE block on microtome
47. Set microtome to trim thickness of 14 micrometers
48. Trim sample until tissue is visible
49. Hydrate FFPE block by placing on ice bath
50. Place FFPE block on ice bath for hydration
51. Secure hydrated sample in microtome specimen clamp and align with blade
52. Set microtome section thickness to five micrometers
53. Start tissue sectioning
54. Collect sectioned tissue using cold tweezers and brushes
55. Place tissue section in 42°C water bath
56. Place tissue on glass slide
57. Incubate slide at 42°C for three hours
58. Dry slide overnight in oven at room temperature
59. Store prepared slide in slide box at room temperature
