Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place anesthetized mouse in supine position on sterile-draped surgery table
2. Apply ocular lubricant to protect eyes from drying
3. Shave abdominal hair for operation and leg hair for postoperative perfusion measurements using pen trimmer
4. Clear shaved hair off surgical field
5. Fixate upper and lower extremities with rubber bands and tacks
6. Check anesthesia depth via toe pinch reflex and titrate anesthesia as necessary
7. Perform respiratory pattern evaluation every 3-5 minutes to calibrate anesthesia level
8. Perform midline laparotomy from lower sternal margin to pubis symphysis
9. Dissect pubis fat pad to widen operative field
10. Open celiotomy to access peritoneal contents
11. Cover bowels with saline-soaked non-woven sponge
12. Dissect perivascular fascia and adipose tissue from 1 cm proximal to aortic bifurcation to left iliac bifurcation using straight Dumont forceps and double-ended sharp cotton swabs
13. Pass tip of angled forceps under left common iliac vascular bundle
14. Gently spread forceps multiple times to mobilize vessels from retroperitoneal musculature
15. Place two 4-0 silk sutures around isolated left common iliac arteriovenous bundle as ligatures
16. Create single knot with each 4-0 silk tie
17. Apply knots sequentially (proximally then distally)
18. Rotate left iliac arteriovenous bundle clockwise using 4-0 silk suture strings as handles
19. Fine-tune position to temporarily locate vein anterior to artery
20. Make 1mm longitudinal venotomy using straight Vannas spring scissors
21. Flush residual blood from venous lumen with 0.9% saline
22. Place 10-0 nylon suture through posterior wall of vein
23. Grab suture ends and apply gentle tension to separate anterior and posterior vessel walls
24. Make elliptical incision (1.0 × 0.3 mm) using curved Vannas spring scissors
25. Remove adherent walls of iliac artery and vein
26. Flush exposed arterial lumen with 0.9% saline and heparinized saline
27. Repair initial anterior wall venotomy with 2-3 10-0 nylon sutures in interrupted fashion
28. Restore vascular bundle to original anatomic orientation
29. Place saline-soaked absorbable gelatin sponge adjacent to repaired venotomy
30. Loosen 4-0 single knot cross clamp ligatures sequentially from distal to proximal
31. Monitor venotomy site for excessive bleeding
32. Gently rub vascular bundle with double-ended hard sharp-pointed cotton swabs
33. Visualize pulsatile bright red oxygenated blood entering iliac vein and mixing with dark venous blood to confirm fistula success
34. Inject heparinized saline into inferior vena cava for systemic anticoagulation
35. Reinspect surgical site for hemostasis after heparinized saline injection
36. Close midline fascia with absorbable 5-0 PGA sutures in running fashion if no bleeding concerns
37. Close skin incision with absorbable 5-0 PGA sutures in running fashion

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Remove culture media from 10 cm tissue culture dish containing HeLa cells
2. Rinse cells twice with 6 ml ice-cold PBS
3. Add 1 ml ice-cold PBS to 10 cm tissue culture dish
4. Harvest cells using cell scraper
5. Transfer harvested cells to 2 ml siliconized plastic tubes
6. Remove supernatant after centrifugation
7. Add 1 ml methanol to each cell pellet
8. Vortex tubes briefly
9. Place samples into bath-type sonicator
10. Sonicate samples at 200 watts for five minutes
11. Transfer whole cell homogenate
12. Transfer homogenate into Teflon-lined screw cap test tubes
13. Add reagents to each test tube: 1 ml methanol, 1 ml chloroform, 0.8 ml double distilled water, 50 μl of 10 μM internal standard
14. Cap sample tubes
15. Vortex tubes at 2,500 rpm for 5 minutes at room temperature
16. Add 1 milliliter of chloroform to each tube
17. Add 1 milliliter of double distilled water to each tube
18. Vortex tubes at 2,500 rpm for 5 minutes at room temperature
19. Centrifuge samples to separate aqueous and organic phases
20. Transfer lower phase into disposable glass tubes
21. Add 2 milliliters chloroform to test tubes
22. Vortex tubes at 2,500 rpm for 5 minutes at room temperature
23. Transfer modified lower phase into glass tubes with initial lower phase
24. Place glass tubes under nitrogen stream
25. Completely remove organic solvents from collected lower phase
26. Reconstitute samples with 500 microliters of methanol or ethanol
27. Filter resulting mixture with 0.02 micron filter into glass vials
28. Perform serial dilutions of standard compound and add 50 microliters of each concentration into separate test tubes
29. Prepare three quality control samples: first with concentration within three times lower boundary of standard curve, second near center of curve, third near upper boundary of curve
30. Add one milliliter of methanol containing cell homogenate to each test tube
31. Extract total lipid fraction from each sample using previously demonstrated method
32. Prepare aqueous mobile phase by mixing acetonitrile, methanol, and double distilled water at 2:2:1 ratio
33. Prepare organic mobile phase using isopropanol in glass bottles with Teflon lined screw caps
34. Sonicate both mobile phases for 5 minutes in bath-type sonicator
35. Add formic acid to final concentration of 26.4 mM and ammonium hydroxide to 14.9 mM to each mobile phase
36. Activate high performance liquid chromatography system
37. Place inlet tubes into mobile phase glass bottles
38. Purge HPLC lines
39. Connect C18 HPLC column to HPLC system
40. Set column oven temperature to 50°C
41. Condition column with aqueous mobile phase at 100 microliters per minute

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Transfer eggs laid by adult moths to two liter boxes
2. Add wax moth substrate to boxes containing eggs
3. Incubate boxes at 30 degrees Celsius in the dark
4. Transfer 25 grams of larva-containing substrate to fresh wax moth substrate after 1-2 weeks when larvae become visible
5. Synchronize larvae by size into groups of 30-40 larvae after two weeks
6. Transfer synchronized larvae groups to new two liter containers with wax moth substrate
7. Select larvae for experiment by weight (180-200mg) after two weeks, choosing only pale, fast-moving specimens
8. Load insulin syringe with blunt-ended needle containing 1 × 10⁷ bacteria per 10 microliters of DPBS per larva
9. Load syringe into microsyringe pump
10. Wait for larva to open mouth parts
11. Insert syringe carefully between larval mandibles
12. Deliver bacterial suspension
13. Incubate fed larvae in dark at 37°C for 1-24 hours
14. Clean safety hood with ethanol
15. Spray reagent to prevent RNase contamination
16. Snap-freeze living larva in liquid nitrogen
17. Homogenize individual frozen larva using mortar and pestle with small volume of liquid nitrogen
18. Transfer powdered homogenate to disposable weigh boat
19. Wait for liquid nitrogen to evaporate
20. Mix powdered homogenates with 1 ml TRIzol in 2 ml tube
21. Incubate mixture at room temperature for 1 hour
22. Pellet homogenate by centrifugation
23. Transfer supernatant to new tube
24. Mix supernatant with 200 μl BCP (1-Bromo-3-chloropropane)
25. Vortex mixture and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature
26. Incubate mixture on ice for 10 minutes
27. Centrifuge BCP-added reaction mixture
28. Transfer upper transparent aqueous layer to new 2 ml tube
29. Add 500 microliters isopropanol to precipitate RNA
30. Mix by inverting tube for 5 minutes
31. Collect precipitated RNA via centrifugation
32. Wash RNA pellet with 500 microliters of 75% ethanol
33. Air-dry RNA pellet for 5-10 minutes at room temperature
34. Add 100 microliters of ribonuclease inhibitor in nuclease-free water to dried RNA pellet
35. Vortex tube until RNA pellet is completely dissolved
36. Measure RNA quality and quantity by spectrophotometry (260/280≈2, 260/230=2-2.2)
37. Prepare 5 micrograms isolated RNA for DNase digestion and gene expression analysis

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Shave the surgical knee with electric clippers
2. Vacuum the fur
3. Disinfect the shaved knee
4. Cover the animal with sterile dressing
5. Palpate the patella
6. Perform medial skin incision of the patella
7. Open knee joint by medial para patella arthrotomy under sterile conditions
8. Displace the patella
9. Displace patella laterally
10. Use sterile scalpel to peel 2-3 mm cartilage pieces from trochlea ossis femoris
11. Place cartilage biopsies into 50 ml tube containing complete medium
12. Rinse defects with sterile saline
13. Use forceps to remove residual cartilage pieces
14. Reposition patella within trochlea groove
15. Close wound in layers with single button sutures and continuing absorbable cutaneous suture
16. Seal wound with water vapor-permeable spray dressing
17. Wash sterile harvested cartilage twice in PBS
18. Centrifuge washed cartilage at 500 × g for 3 minutes at room temperature
19. Cut cartilage into 1 cubic millimeter pieces
20. Transfer cartilage pieces to new 50 mL conical tube
21. Digest cartilage pieces with 10 mL trypsin-EDTA and 10 mL PBS on shaker
22. Stop trypsinization by adding 10 mL complete medium after 30 minutes
23. Remove supernatant after centrifugation
24. Add serum-free medium with collagenase A
25. Shake mixture for 12 hours
26. Centrifuge resulting solution
27. Resuspend cell pellet in 5 mL complete medium
28. Seed cells onto 25 cm² tissue culture flasks in 5 mL complete medium
29. Grow cells in humidified incubator at 37°C with 5% CO₂
30. Wash chondrocytes twice in PBS
31. Expose chondrocytes to 0.05% trypsin EDTA for 3 minutes
32. Add 5 milliliters of complete medium to stop trypsinization after cell detachment
33. Spin down cells
34. Resuspend cell pellet in complete medium
35. Seed cells in 75 square centimeter tissue culture flasks
36. Count harvested cells from in vitro culture
37. Transfer 5×10⁴ cells into 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube
38. Centrifuge cell suspension
39. Resuspend pellet in 15 microliters of complete medium
40. Cut bilayer collagen scaffold to exact dimensions of trochlear defect
41. Place collagen matrix into 24-well plate
42. Seed chondrocytes onto porous cell adhesive side of matrix
43. Incubate cell-seeded matrices without cell culture medium for one hour
44. Create two isolated 3.6 mm diameter chondral defects in trochlear groove using sterile air operating power drill
45. Rinse defects with sterile saline while avoiding opening marrow cavity
46. Implant chondrocyte-seeded matrices with porous side face down
47. Apply fibrin glue to seal implanted matrices for secure fixation
48. Relocate patella within trochlear groove
49. Apply full range of motion to knee joint multiple times
50. Displace patella laterally while holding matrices to check fixation
51. Replace patella in position
52. Perform wound closure in layers
53. Apply spray dressing to wound

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 25 mm magnetic stir bar and 113 ml deionized water to 500 ml beaker
2. Stir deionized water at highest rate without vortex formation
3. Slowly add 13.05 g anhydrous Zirconium Chloride to water in small increments
4. Add 53.29 g glucose to solution
5. Add 3.73 g Yttrium nitrate hexahydrate to solution
6. Increase stirring rate to 700 RPM until all solids dissolve
7. Add 42 ml propylene oxide to solution
8. Decrease stirring to 150 RPM after sufficient mixing
9. Continue stirring until magnetic stir bar stops due to gel formation
10. Remove parafilm from beaker
11. Decant liquid on top of gel
12. Add 300 milliliters of absolute ethanol to beaker containing gel
13. Tightly cover beaker with parafilm
14. Leave covered beaker at room temperature for 24 hours
15. Remove gel from beaker
16. Transfer gel to two-liter porcelain evaporating dish using laboratory spatula
17. Break gel into pieces with spatula
18. Spread gel pieces over surface of evaporating dish
19. Allow gel pieces to dry under ambient conditions
20. Grind dried xerogel into fine powder using agate mortar and pestle
21. Place one gram of xerogel powder into 13mm cylindrical pellet press die
22. Apply 22 kilo Newtons of force using hydraulic press for 90 seconds to form pellet
23. Slowly release force from hydraulic press
24. Carefully remove pellet from pellet die
25. Place xerogel pellet onto YSZ plate and load into center of tube furnace
26. Blow Argon at 1/3 working tube volume per minute for ≥15 minutes while venting outlet to fume hood
27. Program tube furnace temperature controller to heating schedule while maintaining constant Argon flow
28. Start heating program and verify furnace follows schedule
29. Remove pellet from tube furnace after program completion
30. Cut 50 milligram piece from centered xerogel pellet using utility knife
31. Grind xerogel into fine powder using agate mortar and pestle
32. Place approximately 50 milligrams of fine powder into Alumina sample cup
33. Heat sample in thermogravimetric analyzer at 10°C/min from ambient to 1200°C with 100 mL/min air flow
34. Place centered xerogel pellet in alumina crucible
35. Place crucible into box furnace at 700°C for 2 hours
36. Remove hot crucible from furnace using stainless steel crucible tongs
37. Cool crucible to room temperature for 1 hour
38. Remove porous white YSZ scaffold from crucible

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Prepare a 22-gauge syringe needle
2. Blunt needle tip using honing stone
3. Plicate needle at right angle using pliers
4. Prepare required surgical materials including recovery cage
5. Autoclave surgical instruments
6. Place rat in supine position on surgery platform with heating pad
7. Completely shave surgical area
8. Scrub cleanly shaven abdomen with betadine or cleansing reagent
9. Make 2 cm midline abdominal incision using scalpel
10. Moisten abdominal organs with normal saline and maintain moisture during surgery
11. Displace digestive organs to the side using cotton balls
12. Expose inferior vena cava and posterior peritoneal region
13. Identify abdominal aorta juxtaposed to left of inferior vena cava
14. Pierce peritoneum with forceps to uncover vessels
15. Gently isolate abdominal aorta adjacent to renal arteries
16. Pass 8cm 4/0 silk suture underneath abdominal aorta between renal artery origins
17. Make loose double knot with suture and leave 3 mm diameter loop
18. Place blunted/bent 22-gauge needle inside suture loop
19. Tighten suture knot around aorta and needle
20. Remove needle to create 0.7 mm diameter aortic constriction
21. Close abdominal cavity with 6/0 sutures
22. Close abdominal cavity with 6/0 sutures
23. Close muscle and skin incisions using simple interrupted sutures
24. Scrub surgical site with iodine tincture
25. Treat animal with acetaminophen for postsurgical pain
26. Observe animal until regaining consciousness indicated by free movement, food intake, and sternal recumbency
27. Weigh rat at 10 weeks postsurgery
28. Anesthetize rat and confirm anesthesia depth
29. Place anesthetized rat on metal tray
30. Make 5 cm thoracic incision at midline of xiphoid process
31. Pierce diaphragm using sharp forceps
32. Cut and remove rib cage along midclavicular lines on both sides to expose heart
33. Excise heart along cardiac and vascular borders without grabbing tissue
34. Mount heart on modified Langendorff perfusion apparatus
35. Tie aortic trunk to perfusion needle
36. Perfuse heart with Krebs buffer to wash out blood
37. Weigh heart and calculate heart weight to body weight ratio
38. Place organ in 4% paraformaldehyde while wearing mask
39. Fix heart on ice overnight
40. Place fixed heart tissue on tissue sectioning device
41. Cut tissue into two millimeter thick slices
42. Place tissue sections in embedding cassette
43. Deparaffinize slides in xylene tank for 30 minutes
44. Rehydrate tissue in sequentially diluted alcohol series followed by distilled water
45. Apply picrosirius red solution to completely cover tissue sections
46. Incubate sections in picrosirius red solution for one hour
47. Rinse slides two times with 0.5% acetic acid solution
48. Rinse samples twice in absolute alcohol
49. Air-dry samples
50. Mount samples in synthetic resin with cover slip
51. Image slides under microscope using visible light at 200X magnification
52. Calculate percentage of picrosirius red positive zone relative to total area

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 25 milliliters of sterile 0.1% gelatin solution to 275 milligrams of purified sodium alginate in a sterile 50 milliliter tube
2. Vortex tube to partially dissolve alginate powder
3. Place tube on orbital mixer at 10 times G overnight
4. Add 9% sterile sodium chloride to alginate solution
5. Vortex mixture for 30 seconds
6. Centrifuge at 95 times G for five minutes
7. Store alginate solution at four degrees Celsius in dark environment
8. Pretreat human embryonic stem cells with culture media supplemented with 10 micromolar rock inhibitor in dark environment
9. Incubate at 37 degrees Celsius for two hours
10. Wash cells twice with DPBS to dislodge cells enzymatically
11. Add accutane and incubate for 10 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
12. Gently harvest cells with cell scraper and transfer to 15 milliliter tube
13. Harvest cells at 37°C using cell scraper
14. Transfer harvested cells to 15 milliliter tube
15. Neutralize Accutase with equal volume of SL medium
16. Filter neutralized cells through 40 micron filter into fresh 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
17. Enumerate cells using hemocytometer
18. Pellet cells by centrifugation
19. Discard supernatant
20. Wash cells with pre-warmed 0.9% sodium chloride
21. Centrifuge washed cells
22. Discard supernatant after washing
23. Resuspend cells with prewarmed alginate solution
24. Attach 20 milliliter syringe to soft plastic tubing
25. Aspirate alginate into syringe
26. Gently mix cell suspension using syringe without creating bubbles
27. Aspirate cells into syringe
28. Discard plastic tubing
29. Aspirate cells into syringe
30. Discard plastic tubing
31. Attach syringe to encapsulation machine
32. Set up bead generator, syringe pump, airflow meter, and calcium chloride precipitation bath
33. Verify 10 centimeter gap between encapsulation machine end and Petri dish collection point
34. Set syringe pump to 10 milliliters per hour
35. Set airflow rate to eight liters per minute
36. Set pressure to 100 kilo pascals
37. Collect encapsulated cells into prewarmed precipitation bath
38. Maintain in precipitation bath for 7 minutes
39. Transfer capsules via gentle aspiration to 50 mL centrifuge tube containing 20 mL of 0.9% sodium chloride
40. Allow capsules to settle at tube bottom
41. Gently discard supernatant
42. Wash capsules once with 0.9% sodium chloride
43. Resuspend encapsulated cells in prewarmed culture medium supplemented with rock inhibitor
44. Transfer suspension to culture flask
45. Incubate for 3 days
46. Seed mouse stromal line PA6 cells in 0.1% gelatin-coated T75 flask
47. Condition dopaminergic neural differentiation medium for 24 hours
48. Transfer capsules to 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
49. Allow capsules to settle to bottom of tube
50. Discard supernatant
51. Resuspend capsules in PA6 cell-conditioned dopaminergic neural differentiation medium
52. Co-culture encapsulated human embryonic stem cells with PA6 cell monolayer for 21 days
53. Replenish media by changing only half of the medium each time
54. Aspirate capsules and transfer to 15 ml centrifuge tube
55. Allow capsules to settle at tube bottom
56. Discard supernatant
57. Wash capsules twice with DPBS (discarding supernatant after each wash)
58. Add 5 ml of decapsulation solution
59. Thoroughly mix suspension
60. Add 5 milliliters of decapsulation solution
61. Mix suspension thoroughly
62. Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature
63. Centrifuge to pellet decapsulated cells
64. Wash pellet twice with DPBS
65. Proceed with decapsulated cells for monolayer culture or immediate downstream analysis

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Use fertilized quail eggs (fresh or stored at 10-15°C for ≤5 days)
2. Select only clean and undamaged eggs
3. Incubate eggs in forced-draught incubator at 50-60% humidity, 37.5°C for ~54 hours (horizontal position, rotation off)
4. Disinfect egg surface with 70% ethanol without rotating egg
5. Open eggshell using sterile surgical scissors in laminar flow cabinet while wearing gloves
6. Transfer egg content into 6-well culture plate
7. Disinfect scissors with 70% ethanol after processing each egg
8. Add approximately 5 milliliters of sterile water to gaps in 6-well plate to maintain humidity
9. Aspirate improperly-tipped embryos or unfertilized eggs using vacuum aspirator
10. Place embryos in incubator at 37°C with 80-90% humidity
11. Place sterilized silicone ring on CAM surface along small capillaries while avoiding major blood vessels
12. Apply 30 microliters of hypericin solution to silicone ring under sterile conditions
13. Keep embryos in incubator at 37 degrees Celsius in 80-90% humidity
14. Illuminate CAM with violet excitation light
15. Record hypericin and CAM tissue fluorescence with digital camera at timed intervals
16. Record CAM in white light before hypericin administration (if required)
17. Record CAM in white light before tissue fixation (if required)
18. Place CAM under optical fiber to cover entire silicone ring area with laser beam
19. Perform in vivo irradiation with 405nm laser at 285 mW/cm² fluence rate
20. Record CAM images using white light and/or fluorescent light before and after treatment
21. Fix CAM tissue in cultivation plate with 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS
22. Remove paraformaldehyde solution
23. Cut out target tissue from CAM within silicone ring
24. Wash excised CAM tissue in water for 10 minutes
25. Dehydrate tissue in 70% ethanol for 3 minutes
26. Stain tissue in eosin solution for 2 minutes
27. Dehydrate tissue in 96% ethanol for 5 minutes
28. Dehydrate tissue in 100% ethanol for 5 minutes
29. Treat tissue with xylene for 10 minutes (first treatment)
30. Treat tissue with xylene for 10 minutes (second treatment)
31. Incubate samples twice in xylene for 10 minutes in a new Petri dish
32. Transfer samples to dissolved paraffin in Petri dishes using spatula or thin brush
33. Place tissue in histological mold after 24 hours
34. Fill histological mold with paraffin embedding medium
35. Allow paraffin to solidify in refrigerator
36. Cut solidified CAM from embedding medium
37. Turn sample in tray by 90 degrees
38. Refill tray with embedding medium
39. Allow embedding medium to solidify
40. Prepare 5-10 micrometer sections using microtome
41. Mount native or 4% paraformaldehyde fixed CAM on glass slide
42. Fill embedding mold halfway with OCT medium
43. Freeze mold in liquid nitrogen or dry ice/ethanol mixture
44. Transfer CAM from glass slide onto frozen OCT medium
45. Place CAM in mold, cover with OCT medium, and freeze
46. Overflow CAM with prewarmed fixation solution (4% paraformaldehyde and 2% glutaraldehyde in PBS)
47. Remove fixation solution after 48 hours
48. Carefully separate CAM from embryo using micro scissors and fine brush
49. Wash separated CAM in PBS
50. Mount washed CAM on glass slide
51. Let mounted CAM slowly dry
52. Photograph slide using digital camera with transilluminator as homogenous white light source

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Clean trachea and larynx from excess surrounding tissues (lateral muscles, excessive tongue) using dissecting scalpel, Adson forceps, and Metzenbaum scissors by cutting and dissecting
2. Remove hyoid bone and surrounding soft tissues by finding eminence of greater horn, following horn with blade, and passing through contralateral side
3. Cut trachea at distal side approximately 15 centimeters from larynx using dissecting knife
4. Reserve removed mediastinal organs for next procedural step
5. Take previously discarded mediastinal organs
6. Search for thymus in mediastinal organs
7. Detach thymus from surrounding tissue using dissecting forceps
8. Cut thymus into butterfly shape using dissecting knife to create 1.5 cm thick slice
9. Shape thymus slice with Metzenbaum scissors into butterfly form (two 3x2 cm lobes connected by isthmus)
10. Suture thyroid to tracheal wall
11. Place simulated thyroid between first and third tracheal rings
12. Take needle holder
13. Take surgical forceps
14. Grab two zero silk suture
15. Suture thyroid with two lateral horizontal mattress stitches in each lobe to lateral portion of trachea
16. Pass needle only in superficial part of trachea
17. Remove esophagus from larynx connection using scalpel or surgical scissors
18. Cut and open esophagus longitudinally with surgical scissors
19. Hold muscle and mucosa with tooth forceps during cutting
20. Suture esophagus to trachea
21. Place muscle layer on trachea and larynx with mucous membrane face up
22. Suture opened esophagus with six simple stitches
23. Place proximal suture on thyroid cartilage summit
24. Place distal suture on frontal partition of last tracheal ring
25. Mark a line on esophagus mucosa with white India ink to simulate linea alba using insulin syringe
26. Withdraw India ink into insulin syringe
27. Track line on simulated fascia by scratching mucosa with needle while dispensing ink drops
28. Remove excess ink gently using small swab
29. Cut foam square to dimensions 3 cm × 10 cm × 15 cm
30. Shape central furrow measuring 2.5 cm wide × 10 cm long
31. Fold foam square in half to create longitudinal hump
32. Cut 1 cm off entire length of hump using scissors
33. Unfold foam and trim furrow edges smoothly
34. Take wooden tablet matching foam dimensions
35. Place foam square on wooden tablet
36. Insert model into foam furrow
37. Staple tracheal end with wood stapler
38. Staple thyroid cartilage lateral muscles
39. Staple epiglottis
40. Take pig skin collected from butcher shop
41. Cut skin into 25x20 cm square shape to cover model
42. Cover model with skin using dissecting knife
43. Staple skin to wooden tablet with 10-15 clips using stapler
44. Trim excess skin on each side of tablet using knife
45. Use Kocher forceps to firmly hold skin for safer cutting

2. Incubate sample at 4°C for 48 hours

5. Position flask flat in bucket

8. Add 20 milliliters of 0.1 molar PBS
9. Add 20 milliliters of ice-cold 40% acrylamide solution
10. Add 12.2 milliliters of 16% paraformaldehyde solution

13. Place 15 milliliter conical tube in ice next to flask containing hydrogel solution

18. Place sample on ice

20. Pierce cap of conical tube containing sample with 18 gauge hypodermic needle
21. Insert needle into tube until submerged under surface of liquid monomer solution
22. Puncture opposite side of cap with 18-gauge hypodermic needle
23. Ensure needle remains unsubmerged to act as vent
24. Connect nitrogen tank tubing to submerged hypodermic needle beneath hydrogel
25. Slowly turn on nitrogen until steady bubbling through liquid
26. Remove both needles after deoxygenation
27. Cover cap with paraffin film to prevent gas exchange

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Dry two 20-milliliter measuring flasks and two 20-milliliter beakers equipped with magnetic stir bars
2. Transfer 4.13 grams of DMAPAAQ, 0.31 grams of N,N'-methylenebisacrylamide, 0.5 grams of sodium sulfite, and 3.36 grams of sodium hydroxide to one 20-milliliter beaker
3. Dissolve the mixture in distilled water as solvent
4. Stir the solution for 30 minutes using a magnetic stir bar
5. Transfer the mixture from the beaker to one 20-milliliter measuring flask
6. Add distilled water to the measuring flask to generate a 20-milliliter solution
7. Label the solution as the monomer solution
8. Weigh 0.54 grams of ammonium peroxodisulfate
9. Weigh 7.57 grams of iron chloride
10. Transfer both compounds to a 20 milliliter beaker
11. Dissolve compounds completely in distilled water
12. Stir solution for 30 minutes using magnetic stir bar
13. Transfer mixture to 20 milliliter measuring flask
14. Add distilled water to compose 20 milliliter solution
15. Label solution as initiator solution
16. Purge solutions with nitrogen gas for 30 minutes
17. Mix solutions together in a 50 milliliter test tube
18. Stir mixture with electric stirrer
19. Place mixture in chiller maintained at 10°C for four hours
20. Take gel block out from test tube
21. Place gel block on flat cutting board
22. Shape gel block into cubic form
23. Soak gel slices in deionized water for 24 hours to remove impurities
24. Spread gel slices onto Petri dish and dry at room temperature for 24 hours
25. Place Petri dish with gel slices in oven at 50°C for 24 hours
26. Dry five 40 milliliter plastic containers
27. Measure 20 milligrams of dried gel
28. Place measured gel into each 40 milliliter plastic container
29. Add 40 milliliters of disodium hydrogen arsenate heptahydrate solution to each container at different concentrations
30. Incubate containers in stirrer at 20 degrees Celsius and 120 RPM for 24 hours
31. Collect 5 milliliter sample from each container
32. Measure equilibrium arsenic levels using HPLC
33. Configure HPLC with 4 × 200 mm analytical column, 4 × 50 mm guard column, and 4 mm suppressor
34. Add 20 mg of dried gel to dried 40 ml plastic container
35. Add 40 ml of 0.2 millimolar solution
36. Place container in stirrer at 20°C and 120 RPM
37. Collect 5 ml sample in plastic tube using micropipette
38. Analyze equilibrium arsenic level in solution using HPLC
39. Collect fragile gel pieces one at a time to prevent breakage
40. Place collected gel pieces in mesh sieve
41. Wash gel pieces minimum five times with deionized water to remove surface arsenic
42. Transfer washed fragile gel pieces into dried 40 milliliter plastic container for desorption analysis
43. Place container in stirrer at 20°C and 120 RPM for 24 hours
44. Collect 5 mL sample into plastic tube using micropipette
45. Evaluate equilibrium arsenic concentration in solution using HPLC

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Filter medium with 0.2 micrometer filter
2. Autoclave filtered solution
3. Prepare 10 millimolar chemoattractant solution in 1 milliliter sterile medium
4. Filter chemoattractant solution with 0.2 micrometer syringe filter to remove particles and contaminants
5. Dilute filtered chemoattractant stock solution in series according to experimental protocol
6. Attach 27 gauge needle to fill an ISCA
7. Attach 27-gauge needle to 1 milliliter syringe
8. Load one syringe per concentration of filtered chemoattractant solution
9. Hold ISCA device with port facing upwards and slowly inject each chemoattractant concentration into five wells of one row until droplet appears on each port
10. Transfer 1.5 milliliter of marine or freshwater bacteria culture into 150 milliliters of appropriate bacterial culture medium
11. Place two small pieces of double-sided adhesive tape on flat surface of 200 milliliter capacity tray
12. Secure one ISCA device onto each piece of adhesive tape
13. Slowly fill deployment tray with bacterial solution using 50 milliliter serological pipette
14. Allow bacteria to respond to chemoattractant of interest for one hour
15. Remove medium from ISCA tray using a new 50 milliliter pipette
16. Position ISCA with port facing downward
17. Draw solution from each well using sterile 1 milliliter syringe with 27 gauge needle
18. Pool samples from each row of same concentration into single tube
19. Analyze samples by flow cytometry
20. Collect 5 milliliters of water per ISCA from field site
21. Filter water through 0.2 micrometer syringe filter into 50 milliliter conical tube
22. Pass syringe-filtered water through hydrophilic GP filter cartridge (first pass)
23. Collect filtrate in new 50 milliliter conical centrifuge tube after first rinse
24. Pass syringe-filtered water through hydrophilic GP filter cartridge (second pass)
25. Collect filtrate in new 50 milliliter conical centrifuge tube after second rinse
26. Filter water through 0.02 micrometer syringe filter into new 50 milliliter tube
27. Resuspend chemoattractants to desired concentrations using filtrate aliquots in individual 15 milliliter conical tubes
28. Filter resuspended chemoattractants using 10 milliliter syringes through 0.2 micrometer syringe filters into sterile 15 milliliter conical centrifuge tubes
29. Screw each ISCA onto apparatus for field deployment
30. Screw each ISCA onto piece 9 of the device enclosure
31. Close the device enclosure
32. Seal enclosure with adhesive tape
33. Secure enclosure to deployment arm and structure using bungee cords
34. Submerge enclosure completely while holding firmly to prevent excessive water movement
35. Verify no air trapped when water reaches top of enclosure
36. Seal bottom and top holes with two plugs when enclosure is full
37. Leave ISCA in place for 1 to 3 hours
38. Remove enclosure at end of sampling period
39. Remove enclosure from water
40. Place enclosure over container to drain water
41. Remove upper part of adhesive tape from front holes
42. Remove bottom plug and drain residual water after water line passes below ISCA top
43. Use 1 milliliter pipette or syringe to remove water trapped on top of each ISCA
44. Remove ISCAs from enclosure without touching upper surface
45. Use disposable wipe to remove remaining liquid on surface
46. Retrieve samples as demonstrated for laboratory sample retrieval

9. Aspirate supernatant

18. Trypsinize plate of mesothelial cells

21. Resuspend cells in 10% base medium

11. Gently agitate tubes to resuspend contents

20. Aspirate supernatant using P1000 pipette without disturbing bead clusters
21. Resuspend beads in 1 mL complete medium per tube

30. Transfer plate to 37 degree Celsius incubator
31. Incubate plate for 1.5 to 2 hours at 37 degrees Celsius
32. Re-suspend normal human lung fibroblasts at 2×10⁴ cells/mL concentration in complete medium
33. Add 1 mL of fibroblast suspension to each well
34. Return plate to cell culture incubator

12. Fit flask with three-neck vacuum adapter connected to vacuum line and argon balloon

18. Transfer entire content from 500-milliliter flask to two-liter flask

21. Place celite pad in fritted funnel positioned over flask
22. Filter reaction mixture through celite funnel
23. Wash filtration setup with approximately 100 mL distilled dichloromethane

31. Add 0.25 millimoles of dicyclohexyl isobutylamine to vial

37. Evacuate vial under vacuum for 30 seconds
38. Backfill vial with argon (3 cycles)
39. Add 5 milliliters of freshly distilled degassed acetonitrile through septum
40. Add 890 microliters of n-methylpyrrole through septum
41. Replace rubber septum with screw cap
42. Filter reaction mixture through pad of celite
43. Wash celite pad with approximately 5 mL diethyl ether
44. Concentrate filtrate under reduced pressure using rotary evaporator
45. Dissolve concentrate in minimum solvent volume

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Adjust CV-1 cell concentration to 100,000 cells per milliliter
2. Seed 2 milliliters of cell suspension into each well of six-well plate
3. Transfect cells with 0.5 ng gRNA-N2 plasmid and 0.5 ng pSTCas9 plasmid
4. Incubate transfected cells for 24 hours
5. Replace cell culture medium
6. Dilute backbone VVL15 virus with DMEM to 200,000 PFU per milliliter
7. Add 100 microliters of diluted VVL15 virus into each well of transfected CV-1 cells
8. Transfect cells with 1 microgram of shuttle donor vector for HR expression
9. Incubate transfected cells for 24 hours
10. Detach transfected CV-1 cells using cell scraper
11. Collect cell suspension into cryovial
12. Store cryovial at -80°C for future use
13. Seed 15 six-well plates with 300,000 unmodified CV-1 cells per well
14. Thaw frozen suspension of transfected CV-1 cells at 37 degrees Celsius for 3 minutes
15. Vigorously vortex cell suspension for 30 seconds to lyse cells
16. Dilute 1 microliter of lysate with 3 milliliters of DMEM
17. Add 0.5 milliliter of dilution to each plate well containing unmodified CV-1 cells
18. Return plates to incubator for 2 days
19. Identify RFP-positive plaques using fluorescence microscope with 10X objective
20. Label identified plaques underneath plate using marker pen
21. Prepare cryovial containing 200 microliters of serum-free DMEM for each positive plaque
22. Aspirate all medium from wells with labeled plaques
23. Set 200 microliter pipette to aspirate 30 microliters
24. Partially load pipette with 10 microliters of medium from cryovial
25. Scrape RFP-positive plaque and draw up cells with remaining aspiration
26. Eject harvested material into cryovial
27. Repeat scraping and ejection procedure twice more targeting same plaque
28. Prepare six-well plate with 500,000 CV-1 cells per well
29. Thaw frozen purified plaque at 37°C for 3 minutes
30. Vortex plaque vigorously for 30 seconds to make lysate
31. Add all lysate including cell debris into one well of CV-1 cells
32. Incubate plate for three days
33. Inspect cells daily under microscope
34. Harvest infected cells when >50% show cytopathic effect
35. Collect cells in 15-ml tube with culture medium using cell scraper
36. Pellet cells at 300g for three minutes
37. Discard supernatant
38. Use PCR to verify mutant VV modification
39. Freeze cell pellet at -80°C for later processing

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Grind 200 milligrams of plant tissue sample to powder in liquid nitrogen
2. Add 1 milliliter of RNA extraction reagent (containing guanidine thiocyanate and acid phenol) to ground tissue
3. Mix sample well by inversion until all tissue is wet
4. Centrifuge samples at room temperature for 10 minutes
5. Transfer supernatant to new 1.5 milliliter tube
6. Add 200 microliters of 1-bromo-3-chloropropane to supernatant
7. Vigorously vortex sample
8. Centrifuge sample to separate phases
9. Transfer approximately 500 microliters of upper aqueous phase to new 1.5 milliliter tube
10. Add 500 microliters of isopropanol and 50 microliters of three molar sodium acetate to sample
11. Mix sample well by inversion
12. Incubate sample at -20°C for 10 minutes
13. Precipitate RNA by centrifugation
14. Discard supernatant
15. Wash pellet twice with 500 microliters of 80% ethanol
16. Wash pellet once with 500 microliters of 99% ethanol
17. Dry pellet for 5-10 minutes
18. Dissolve pellet in 30 microliters of RNase-free water
19. Measure RNA concentration using spectrophotometer
20. Digest 20 micrograms of RNA sample in DNase water and buffer solution
21. Incubate sample for 30 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
22. Add appropriate volume of DNase inactivation reagent to remove enzyme
23. Check quality and purity of isolated RNA by capillary electrophoresis according to standard protocols
24. Add at least 12 micrograms of each RNA sample to new tubes
25. Spike in 0.1 nanogram of methylated and non-methylated in vitro transcripts per 3 micrograms of RNA sample
26. Adjust final volume to 80 microliters per tube using RNase-free water
27. Sonicate entire volume of each RNA sample in individual sonication tubes
28. Confirm sonication efficiency and RNA concentration by capillary electrophoresis
29. Add 9 µg of each sonicated RNA sample and up to 60 µL RNase-free water into new low binding tubes
30. Heat RNA to 70°C in water bath for 10 minutes
31. Cool samples for 10 minutes in ice water bath
32. Transfer 20 µL of each sample into new low binding tube for -80°C storage as input control
33. Add 50 microliters of 10X MeRIP buffer to each sample
34. Add 10 microliters of RNase inhibitor to each sample
35. Add 10 microliters of anti-M5C antibody to immunoprecipitation tubes
36. Add 10 microliters of water to mock tubes
37. Seal tubes with parafilm
38. Incubate samples at 4°C with overhead rotation for 12-14 hours
39. Add 40 microliters of beads to one 15 milliliter tube per sample
40. Perform three 5-minute washes with 800 microliters of MeRIP buffer using overhead rotation per sample
41. Collect beads on magnet after each wash
42. Discard buffer after each wash
43. Resuspend beads in 40 microliters of fresh MeRIP buffer per sample
44. Add 40 microliters of resuspended beads to immunoprecipitation and mock sample tubes
45. Incubate tubes for two hours at four degrees Celsius with overhead rotation
46. Place tubes on magnetic rack for one minute
47. Carefully discard supernatant without disturbing beads
48. Wash beads five times in 700 microliters of MeRIP buffer supplemented with 0.01% Tween-20 for 10 minutes per wash at room temperature with overhead rotation
49. Resuspend beads in 200 microliters of proteinase K digestion buffer and 3.5 microliters of proteinase K
50. Incubate suspension for three hours at 50 degrees Celsius with shaking at 800 revolutions per minute
51. Extract RNA using 800 microliters of RNA extraction reagent via acid guanidinium thiocyanate phenol-chloroform protocol
52. Resuspend extracted RNA pellet in 20 microliters of RNase-free water

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place euthanized mouse torso in 35 mm Petri dish
2. Keep Petri dish with torso on ice
3. Open skin of torso using scissors
4. Remove vertebra using small scissors
5. Extract spinal cord
6. Place spinal cord in dissection buffer on ice
7. Position sample under stereotactic dissection microscope at 2x magnification
8. Remove meninges from spinal cord using forceps
9. Transfer dissected and cleaned spinal cord tissue to C tube
10. Add 50 microliters of enzyme P and 30 microliters of previously prepared enzyme mix to each C tube
11. Invert C tubes and place on heated dissociator, ensuring tissue collects in tube lid
12. Elute cells by removing column from separator, adding 800 microliters of astrocyte culture medium, and pushing cells out with plunger
13. Plate cells in prepared culture flasks by adding 4.2 milliliters of astrocyte culture medium supplemented with 10 ng/mL EGF and bFGF
14. Culture cells at 37 degrees Celsius and 5% carbon dioxide
15. Perform astrocyte seating under biological safety cabinet (safety level 1)
16. Prepare 24-well plates with Poly-D-Lysine coated glass cover slips
17. Isolate six spinal cords from P2 mice (yield ~1 million cells)
18. Aspirate media from T-12.5 culture flasks containing cultured astrocytes
19. Wash astrocytes once with 1x PBS
20. Add 0.5 milliliters of 0.05% trypsin-EDTA to culture flask
21. Incubate flask at 37 degrees Celsius for five minutes
22. Gently tap side of flask to release cells
23. Verify cell detachment under brightfield microscope at 10× magnification
24. Stop trypsinization by adding 2.5 milliliters of astrocyte culture medium
25. Collect cell suspension in 15 milliliter tubes
26. Centrifuge suspension at 300 RCF for five minutes at 4°C
27. Aspirate supernatant
28. Resuspend cell pellet in 1 milliliter of astrocyte culture medium
29. Calculate cell concentration using hemocytometer or automated cell counting system
30. Dilute cell suspension with fresh astrocyte medium supplemented with EGF and bFGF (10 ng/mL per factor)
31. Add 500 microliters of cell suspension to each well of 24-well plates
32. Culture cells at 37°C and 5% CO₂
33. Prepare neuronal differentiation medium by adding 1 mL B27 supplement to 49 mL basic culture medium
34. Replace astrocyte medium with 1 mL neuronal differentiation medium per well after 24-48 hours
35. Culture cells at 37°C with 9% CO₂
36. Supplement neuronal differentiation medium with forskolin (30 µM) and dorsomorphin (1 µM) during medium replacement
37. Add second dose of dorsomorphin directly to culture medium 2 days after initial treatment

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Select worms unfed for at least one week and 5-7 mm long
2. Transfer 15-30 worms to 100 mm Petri dish
3. Fill Petri dish two-thirds full with worm water
4. Replace Petri dish cover immediately
5. Observe worms for missing/unpigmented tissues (e.g., white heads/tails)
6. Verify worms are undamaged and not recently regenerating
7. Wipe workspace and dissecting microscope base with 70% ethanol solution
8. Allow workspace to dry completely
9. Place dish with selected worms on left side of microscope
10. Place equipment on right side of microscope: number five forceps, 31-gauge 5/16-inch insulin needle with 1-mL syringe, clean transfer pipette, and labeled 12-well plate for collecting ablated worms
11. Place box of lint-free tissue wipes, plastic wash bottle with fresh worm water, and additional transfer pipettes within easy reach
12. Position Peltier plate at center of dissecting microscope base
13. Set DC power source output to cool Peltier plate surface sufficiently for worm immobilization
14. Cut 5 cm × 10 cm piece of plastic paraffin film
15. Place paraffin film over center of Peltier plate
16. Fold lint-free tissue wipe into ~2 cm square
17. Place folded wipe on top of paraffin film
18. Hold folded wipe in place
19. Moisten wipe with worm water
20. Roll wipe flat with transfer pipette
21. Place worm dorsal side up on filter paper using transfer pipette
22. Turn light on and focus beam on worm
23. Adjust dissecting microscope focus and magnification to visualize worm eyes clearly
24. Rotate paraffin film to position worm with head toward researcher at 30-40 degrees right angle
25. Reposition ventral-side-up worm to dorsal-side-up position using dull side of forceps (conditional)
26. Readjust microscope settings to bring eyes into clear focus
27. Ensure both eyes and surrounding head tissues are within microscope field of view
28. Hold clean syringe in right hand between thumb and index finger
29. Support bottom of syringe on left thumb braced against Peltier plate
30. Verify needle bevel visibility through microscope
31. Place left index finger on surgery surface at 40-degree angle to left thumb
32. Stabilize surgery surface using finger placement
33. Position needle at 90-degree angle to eye
34. Position needle at 90-degree angle to eye with bevel facing upward
35. Penetrate tissue overlying optic cup (white unpigmented region) with needle tip
36. Remove pigmented and unpigmented tissues within optic cup by gentle right-to-left scooping
37. Ablate second eye (conditional for double-eye procedures)
38. Backload transfer pipette with small amount of worm water
39. Release worm water onto worm to lift it off filter paper
40. Draw worm into transfer pipette
41. Transfer worm to labeled 12-well plate containing fresh worm water (two-thirds full)
42. Wash worms by replacing well water with fresh worm water
43. Incubate worms in 20°C incubator protected from light while monitoring regeneration

Complete step list: Take suspension of HepG2/C3A and THLE-3 cells
2. Count number of cells and dilute suspension in complete growth media to obtain 1 million cells in maximum 1.5 milliliters volume
3. Wash wells of ultra-low attachment 24-well round bottom plate with 0.5 milliliters of growth media
4. Centrifuge plate at 3,000g for five minutes
5. Transfer cell suspension to plate
6. Centrifuge plate at 120g for three minutes
7. Incubate plate to initiate spheroid formation
8. Fill humidity chamber with 25 milliliters of sterile water using 10-milliliter syringe with long needle
9. Fill cell chamber with 9 milliliters of growth media using 10-milliliter syringe with long needle
10. Place bioreactor in 3D incubator at 37°C with 5% CO₂ for 24 hours
11. Pipette spheroids up and down gently using 1-mL wide-bore tips to detach them from ultra-low attachment plate
12. Transfer detached spheroids to tissue culture dish
13. Wash plate with 0.5 mL prewarmed growth media to capture remaining spheroids
14. Transfer washed spheroids to culture dish
15. Evaluate spheroid size, compactness, and roundness using light microscope
16. Select adequately formed spheroids
17. Transfer selected spheroid to equilibrating bioreactor containing 5 mL fresh growth media
18. Fill entire bioreactor with fresh growth media
19. Position bioreactor in 3D incubator
20. Adjust rotation speed using control unit
21. Replace 10 milliliters of old growth media with 10 milliliters of fresh media
22. Culture spheroids for 15 days
23. Divide cultured spheroids between two fresh bioreactors
24. Open 3D app installed on tablet
25. Select bioreactor in app
26. Capture image of bioreactor
27. Place black background behind bioreactor (alternative method)
28. Ensure no light reflects on cell chamber
29. Capture image close to bioreactor
30. Remove 5 mL media from bioreactor using syringe with long needle via top port
31. Ensure spheroids descend to bottom center of bioreactor
32. Open front port of bioreactor
33. Collect spheroids using 1mL wide-bore tip
34. Transfer spheroids to microcentrifuge tubes
35. Transfer spheroids into microcentrifuge tubes using 1-mL wide-bore tip
36. Centrifuge at 500g for 5 minutes
37. Dispose of media
38. Add 200 microliters of HBSS to wash spheroid and remove FBS
39. Centrifuge at 500g for 5 minutes
40. Discard supernatant
41. Immerse spheroid pellet rapidly in liquid nitrogen to freeze
42. Store frozen pellet at -80°C until further processing
43. Resuspend collected spheroids in 25 microliters of 5% SDS
44. Homogenize pellet by pipetting up and down
45. Dissolve 1 microgram of samples in 10 microliters of 0.1% trichloroacetic acid
46. Set up MS acquisition method to generate MS/MS spectra
47. Transfer plate into nanoliter liquid chromatography autosampler
48. Set full MS scan to 300-1100 mass-to-charge ratio in Orbitrap
49. Adjust resolution to 120000
50. Set automatic gain control target to 125
51. Set MS/MS in Orbitrap with sequential isolation window of 50 mass-to-charge ratio
52. Set automatic gain control target to 400 for MS/MS
53. Set higher energy collisional dissociation energy to 30

Complete step list: Sterilize surgical instruments, gauze sponges, and cognitive applicators using autoclave
2. Store surgical tools in 70% ethanol during surgery
3. Air dry surgical tools on sterile gauze immediately before use
4. Spray work area with 70% ethanol two hours before surgery
5. Inject mouse with 0.05 milligrams per kilogram of buprenorphine
6. Anesthetize mouse using anesthetic vaporizer with 3% isoflurane and 20% oxygen gas mixture

8. Test anesthesia depth via toe or tail pinch
9. Maintain anesthesia at 2% isoflurane
10. Apply artificial tears to mouse's eyes with caution to avoid eye damage during surgical procedure
11. Place mouse on right side in lateral position
12. Shave left side area between eye and ear base using animal clippers
13. Cleanse area by alternating Betadine solution and 70% ethanol using cotton tip applicators with light rubbing
14. Place mouse onto heating pad connected to rectal probe
15. Insert rectal probe using mineral oil to maintain 37°C body temperature
16. Position mouse on right side under microscope and secure with tape
17. Cut window in gauze sponge and cover surgical area
18. Make vertical skin incision between left eye and left ear base using fine straight scissors
19. Retract surgical area using curved hemostats
20. Apply sterile PBS to tissue using cotton tip applicators if excessive drying occurs
21. Make horizontal incision on temporal muscle using spring scissors
22. Separate temporal muscle from skull by slow pulling with forceps
23. Perform subtemporal craniotomy at zygomatic-squamosal junction using 18-gauge needle while holding jawbone with curved forceps
24. Remove skull fragments with bone rongeurs to expose middle cerebral artery (MCA)
25. Ligate distal MCA using small vessel cauterizer
26. Reposition temporal muscle to original anatomical position
27. Close incision site using 5-0 nylon sutures
28. Discontinue anesthesia
29. Remove rectal probe
30. Inject mouse subcutaneously with second dose of buprenorphine
31. Return mouse to cage maintained at 37°C with heating panel
32. Provide recovery gel to mouse
33. Wait 2-3 hours after surgery
34. Inject mouse subcutaneously with buprenorphine every 3-5 hours for 24 hours
35. Deeply anesthetize the mouse
36. Perfuse mouse with 4% TTC solution
37. Remove brain and place into 4% paraformaldehyde solution for overnight fixation
38. Position brain in sectioning block
39. Slice brain into 1mm thick sections using razor blades
40. Place brain slices next to millimeter scale ruler and photograph using digital camera connected to dissecting microscope
41. Open desired photo in ImageJ software
42. Select straight line tool
43. Draw straight line between ruler margins
44. Open 'Set Scale' in Analyze menu
45. Set known distance to 1 millimeter
46. Select freehand circle tool
47. Outline contralateral hemisphere with circle
48. Measure outlined area via Analyze menu
49. Outline ipsilateral hemisphere excluding stroke area
50. Measure outlined ipsilateral hemisphere area
51. Repeat measurement steps for 1mm slices
52. Sum stroke areas across slices to calculate volume

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Make incision in abdominal skin using surgical scissors and tweezers
2. Cut muscle and peritoneal membrane from bladder to left lower rib edge
3. Moisten two cotton swabs with PBS
4. Pull intestines aside using moistened cotton swabs
5. Place moistened swabs to identify left kidney and ureter
6. Close peritoneal membrane with 4-0 nylon suture
7. Close abdominal incision with 4-0 nylon suture
8. Make abdominal incision and cut muscle/peritoneal membrane for UUO
9. Place 2.5-ml syringe underneath mouse
10. Moisten two cotton swabs with PBS for UUO
11. Pull intestines aside using tweezers and moistened swabs
12. Place swabs to identify left ureter
13. Lift peritoneal membrane with tweezers and make sideways incision at upper edge using surgical scissors
14. Continue incision along lowest edge of ribs using surgical scissors
15. Identify left kidney
16. Reflux kidney with PBS until it turns yellow-white
17. Cut left renal artery and vein with surgical scissors to remove kidney
18. Place kidney in Petri dish
19. Wash kidney carefully with PBS
20. Cut kidney into approximately 10-mg samples using surgical scissors and tweezers
21. Transfer each kidney sample to individual 1.5-ml microcentrifuge tube
22. Close microcentrifuge tube cap
23. Place kidney sample in silicone homogenizer
24. Add 700 microliters of phenyl/guanidine-based lysis reagent to homogenizer
25. Homogenize sample by slowly pressing and twisting pestle against kidney tissue
26. Transfer homogenized lysate to biopolymer spin column
27. Centrifuge spin column at 14,000 × g for 3 minutes
28. Transfer precipitated lysate to unused 1.5-ml microcentrifuge tube
29. Add 140 microliters of chloroform to lysate and cap tube tightly
30. Invert tube 15 times to mix lysate and chloroform
31. Incubate mixture at room temperature for 2-3 minutes
32. Centrifuge samples at 12,000 × g for 15 minutes at 4°C
33. Transfer supernatant to new 1.5-milliliter microcentrifuge tube without disturbing precipitate
34. Add 1.5 times volume of 100% ethanol to supernatant
35. Vortex mixture for five seconds
36. Load 700 microliters of sample onto membrane-anchored spin column in two-milliliter collection tube
37. Close column cap and spin at 15,000 × g for 15 seconds
38. Discard precipitated lysate in collection tube
39. Add 700 microliters of wash buffer one to membrane-anchored spin column in two-milliliter collection tube
40. Centrifuge sample
41. Discard used collection tube
42. Repeat wash twice with 500 microliters of wash buffer two per wash
43. Centrifuge membrane-anchored spin column in two-milliliter collection tube at 15,000 × g for one minute
44. Discard precipitated lysate
45. Transfer membrane-anchored spin column to new 1.5-milliliter tube
46. Add 30 microliters of RNase-free water to column to dissolve total RNA
47. Close column cap
48. Incubate column for five minutes at room temperature
49. Centrifuge column at 15,000 × g for one minute
50. Transfer total RNA sample to new microcentrifuge tube on ice
51. Measure concentration of total RNA by spectrophotometry
52. Prepare master mix solution according to manuscript directions
53. Add 8 microliters of master mix to each tube of eight-well tube strip
54. Place 12-microliter aliquot of adjusted total RNA into each tube and close caps
55. Centrifuge tubes for 15 seconds
56. Place tubes in thermocycler and incubate for 60 minutes at 37°C
57. Incubate tubes for 5 minutes at 95°C for cDNA synthesis
58. Transfer cDNA to new 1.5-milliliter microcentrifuge tube and dilute 10 times
59. Create unilateral urethral obstruction mouse model via left ureteral ligation
60. Completely obstruct ureters using double ligation with 4-0 silk sutures
61. Collect kidneys at eight days post-surgery

Complete step list: Weigh 1000 grams of cement and 350 grams of water using electronic balance
2. Wipe five liter mixing pot with wet towel to moisten
3. Add weighed water and cement to mixing pot
4. Place pot on mixer and raise to stirring position
5. Mix at 65 rpm for 90 seconds
6. Let mixture sit still for 30 seconds
7. Scrape paste from inner wall of pot
8. Mix at 130 rpm for 60 seconds
9. Remove pot from mixer
10. Put ceramic particles into cement paste
11. Thoroughly mix ceramic particles with cement paste by hand
12. Half-fill mold with fresh cement paste
13. Place ceramic particle on top of paste in mold
14. Fill remainder of mold with cement paste
15. Wipe off excess cement paste with scraper knife
16. Vibrate mold on vibrating table for one minute
17. Seal mold surface with cling film
18. Cure specimen in curing room for 24 hours
19. Remove specimen from mold
20. Cure specimen for 28 days under same environmental conditions
21. Cut specimen through geometric center of ceramic particle
22. Immerse both specimen parts in isopropyl alcohol for 3 days to remove unbound water and terminate hydration
23. Replace isopropyl alcohol solution every 24 hours during immersion period
24. Dry both specimen parts in vacuum drying oven for 7 days at 40°C
25. Smear demolding paste on inner surface of two cylindrical plastic molds using finger
26. Place sample piece into each mold with examination surface facing downward
27. Weigh 50 grams of low-viscosity epoxy resin in paper cup
28. Add five grams of hardener to epoxy resin
29. Manually stir epoxy-hardener mixture for two minutes
30. Place mold and paper cup into cold mounting machine
31. Start vacuum on machine
32. Pour epoxy resin into mold until merged with samples
33. Maintain mold in machine for 24 hours to harden epoxy resin
34. Remove bottom of each mold
35. Squeeze out sample from mold
36. Store sample in vacuum drying oven
37. Grind sample with silicon carbide paper and alcohol using automatic polishing machine
38. Attach flannelette to turn table of polishing machine
39. Polish sample with diamond paste (3, 1, 0.25 micrometers) at 150 rpm
40. Remove debris using ultrasonic cleaner with alcohol
41. Place sample in plastic box with examination surface facing up
42. Store plastic boxes in vacuum dry oven
43. Spray thin layer of gold foil on examination surface using automatic sputter coater in vacuum environment
44. Place adhesive tape strip on sample side to connect testing surface and opposite surface
45. Position sample on test bench with testing surface facing upward
46. Move sample to focus on region one
47. Vacuum SEM chamber
48. Switch to backscattered electron mode
49. Set magnification to 1000X
50. Adjust brightness and contrast
51. Move SEM lens along aggregate boundary direction
52. Position SEM lens at new location
53. Capture SEM image
54. Acquire minimum 15 images for statistical analysis
55. Repeat SEM imaging process on region two
56. Repeat SEM imaging process on region three
57. Apply ImageJ pretreatment with best fit filter
58. Apply 3x3 median filter three times to reduce noise
59. Manually capture ceramic particle boundaries
60. Manually capture the boundary of the ceramic particle
61. Cut the ceramic particle boundary region from the original image
62. Set different threshold values to roughly determine the upper threshold of pore phases
63. Segment the image and compare with the original image for threshold evaluation
64. Obtain the gray-scale distribution of the remaining image region
65. Choose two approximately linear parts of the curve above the predetermined upper threshold
66. Fit the two linear curve parts with linear regression
67. Set the intersection point of fitted curves as the exact upper threshold value
68. Use the determined threshold value to perform image segmentation
69. Compare the binary segmented image with the original gray-scale image for final threshold validation
70. Convert the gray-scale image to a binary image with white representing pore phase and black representing solid phase

2. House mice in conventional husbandry room cages equipped with filters

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Cut a five millimeter section of tubing
2. Incise at midpoint and remove anterior half of tubing
3. Crush flat portion of tubing using hemostat
4. Melt end of angiocatheter using lighter to create lip
5. Cut injection port from 27 gauge angiocatheter
6. Connect catheter to 10 cm section of 27 gauge cannula tubing
7. Position anesthetized rat with nose in anesthesia nose cone and extremities immobilized
8. Attach vital signs monitor to left hind extremity
9. Perform toe pinch to confirm depth of anesthesia
10. Spray abdomen with 70% isopropyl alcohol
11. Allow abdomen to dry
12. Place sterile drape over animal
13. Make midline incision from xiphoid to pubis using sharp scissors
14. Enter peritoneum and incise muscle
15. Extend incision laterally to left and right to form cross at inferior liver border
16. Turn isoflurane anesthesia down to 2%
17. Retract xiphoid process using curved mosquito clamp
18. Retract ribs using rib retractors
19. Cut falciform, phrenic, and gastrohepatic ligaments using sharp scissors
20. Tie off phrenic vein with 7-0 suture near its origin
21. Eviscerate rat using sterile moistened cotton-tip applicator
22. Wrap bowel in gauze moistened with 0.9% normal saline
23. Dissect over inferior vena cava to remove excess tissue
24. Dissect behind IVC superior to bifurcation
25. Pass loop of 4-0 silk suture behind IVC for later use
26. Retract right kidney to expose right adrenal vein
27. Retract right liver lobe superiorly with gauze
28. Tie off right adrenal vein with 7-0 silk suture near IVC
29. Cauterize across right adrenal vein distal to suture tie
30. Dissect out the splenic vein
31. Tie off splenic vein with two 7-0 silk sutures
32. Cut across splenic vein between sutures
33. Dissect around gastroduodenal artery
34. Tie off gastroduodenal artery with 7-0 silk suture
35. Ligate gastroduodenal artery
36. Dissect around hepatic artery
37. Loosely place 7-0 silk suture tie around hepatic artery
38. Dissect out the bile duct
39. Check length of bile duct
40. Tie off bile duct at distal end
41. Place suture loop around bile duct proximally
42. Cut hole using small scissors (half diameter of duct)
43. Place 27-gauge catheter into bile duct
44. Advance catheter proximally in bile duct
45. Secure catheter using roman sandal tie suture
46. Inject 0.5 milliliters of heparin into IVC using 27-gauge needle
47. Clamp and tie off IVC using pre-placed 4-0 silk suture
48. Tie off hepatic artery using pre-placed 7-0 silk suture
49. Clamp portal vein using microsurgical clip
50. Cannulate portal vein using 22-gauge angiocatheter
51. Flush with 60 milliliters of cold 0.9% normal saline containing 100 units heparin until liver blanches
52. Expose the suprahepatic IVC
53. Cut across the suprahepatic IVC as high in the chest as possible
54. Perform hepatectomy by cutting around the diaphragm, hepatic artery, IVC, portal vein, and additional ligaments
55. Lift out the liver and place in ice-cold 0.9% normal saline
56. Place 16-gauge vascular cuff in the portal vein and connect liver to ex vivo normothermic liver perfusion circuit
57. Flow perfusate at 2 milliliters per minute
58. Monitor portal vein pressure for spikes indicating occlusion
59. Insert portal vein cannula into cuffed portal vein for perfusate return flow
60. Suture cannula using 7-0 silk suture
61. Increase perfusate flow by 1 milliliter per minute until physiological pressure of 10-16 cm H₂O is reached
62. Remove 1 milliliter samples from pre-port and post-port during perfusion
63. Divide each 1 milliliter sample into two 0.5 milliliter aliquots
64. Snap freeze one 0.5 milliliter aliquot in cryogenic tubes using liquid nitrogen
65. Perform arterial blood gas analysis using remaining 0.5 milliliter perfusate
66. Measure pH and buffer perfusate as needed to maintain pH 7.4
67. Disconnect liver from perfusion circuit after 4 hours
68. Divide liver into 0.5 gram segments
69. Transfer segments to cryogenic tubes and snap freeze in liquid nitrogen

Complete step list: Clean 50 ml tungsten carbide milling jar and 10×10 mm tungsten carbide balls, then dry in 100°C oven for 1 hour
2. Wipe clean inside of cooled milling jar to remove residue
3. Purge glove box antechamber with argon 3 times (10 minutes per purge)
4. Transfer milling jar, balls, and clean spatula into argon-filled glove box
5. Weigh vanadium and boron powders in 1:2 molar ratio into milling jar
6. Add tungsten carbide balls to milling jar and seal jar
7. Remove sealed milling jar from glove box and place in planetary ball mill
8. Set ball mill to 600 RPM and mill for 4 hours
9. Allow milling jar to cool to room temperature in ball mill before removal
10. Purge glove box anti-chamber with argon three times for 10 minutes each
11. Transfer milling jar, round bottom flask, paraffin film, spatula, and magnetic stir bar into glove box
12. Scrape walls of milling jar with spatula to recover bulk starting mass into whey boat
13. Weigh collected nano vanadium diboride
14. Weigh collected nano vanadium diboride
15. Transfer weighed vanadium diboride to round bottom flask
16. Weigh 3.5% by weight zirconium chloride relative to vanadium diboride
17. Add zirconium chloride powder to flask
18. Place magnetic stir bar in flask and seal with paraffin film
19. Remove sealed flask from glove box
20. Transfer 10 milliliters of diethyl ether into flask using 10mL syringe
21. Cover syringe hole with additional paraffin film
22. Place flask on stir plate and mix for one hour at medium setting
23. Evaporate remaining diethyl ether using rotary evaporator until nano vanadium diboride appears dry
24. Obtain freshly prepared 8M KOH/NaOH electrolyte (4M KOH + 4M NaOH)
25. Use diagonal cutting pliers to create a cut in the lip of zinc air coin cell casing
26. Crimp the outside edges of the lip outward
27. Repeat crimping process completely around the cell twice
28. Use razor blade to slowly push up on edges of the cap
29. Gently force open the cell while avoiding damage or cracks
30. Use razor blade to gently remove zinc anode material from cap and separator parts
31. Use cotton swab to carefully wipe remaining zinc residue from cap bottom and separator
32. Clean cap and outside of cell with isopropyl alcohol
33. Weigh 1.2 milligrams of zirconium-coated vanadium diboride per electrode
34. Transfer weighed zirconium-coated vanadium diboride to mortar and pestle
35. Add IC carbon black equal to 30% of total anodic material mass to mortar and pestle
36. Grind mixture in mortar and pestle for 30 minutes
37. Verify absence of large visible material clusters after grinding
38. Transfer approximately 1.7 milligrams of 70:30 powder mixture to each clean electrode cap using a spatula
39. Add a single drop of isopropyl alcohol to each cap
40. Swirl powder with small pointed tip until no clumps remain and suspension is evenly distributed across cap surface
41. Allow electrodes to dry for 30 minutes
42. Inspect each cap to ensure anode material is evenly spread and not cracked
43. Add 27 microliters of electrolyte mixture to each bottom
44. Use cotton swab to dab once in bottoms to remove excess electrolyte
45. Turn over each bottom and place on cap to contact anode material with electrolyte
46. Apply pressure and seal assembly using fast-drying epoxy
47. Place cells on discharge rack and rest for 10 minutes for equilibration
48. Measure open circuit potential
49. Measure cell discharge at constant load of 3000 ohms using battery tester
50. Measure discharge of cell at constant load of 3000 ohms using battery tester

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Warm chemically competent E. coli cells on ice
2. Add 25 microliters of competent cells per construct to chilled 1.5 milliliter microtube
3. Add 1 microliter of 100 ng/μL solution of each co-expression construct
4. Incubate mixtures on ice for 30 minutes with gentle mixing every 10 minutes by scraping tubes across microtube rack
5. Heat shock cell mixtures at 42°C for 1 minute
6. Place cells back on ice for at least 2 minutes
7. Add 200 microliters of 37 degrees Celsius SOC medium to tubes
8. Incubate cultures for one hour at 37 degrees Celsius and 200 rotations per minute
9. Add approximately 10 autoclaved glass beads and 100 microliters of cells to one 37 degrees Celsius warmed lysogeny broth agar plate with appropriate antibiotics
10. Shake plate horizontally with lid in place to evenly distribute cells
11. Gently tap glass beads into waste container
12. Incubate plate at 37 degrees Celsius overnight with coated surface side down
13. Remove plate from incubator
14. Add 5 milliliters of lysogeny broth medium with antibiotics to 15 milliliter sterile glass tubes
15. Pick individual transformed E. coli colonies from agar plate using pipet tip
16. Add one picked colony to each prepared 15 milliliter tube
17. Cap each tube with breathable plastic cap
18. Place capped E. coli small cultures in 37°C shaking incubator
19. Add 100 mL of 10x PBS to 900 mL terrific broth in 1L flask with antibiotics
20. Place flasks in 37°C shaking incubator at 140 RPM for 30 minutes
21. Add 5 mL inoculation culture to each 1L culture flask
22. Place flasks in shaking incubator at 140 RPM for 3 hours
23. Reduce incubator temperature to 16°C when OD600 reaches 0.6
24. Add 1 mL of 1 M IPTG, 1 mL of 4 g/L riboflavin, and 1 mL of 150 g/L aminolevulinic acid to each culture
25. Add 25 mL of 1 M sodium pyruvate to each culture for diterpenoid production
26. Incubate cultures at 16°C and 140 rpm for 72 hours
27. Add 25 mL of sodium pyruvate daily to appropriate cultures during incubation
28. Secure separatory funnel onto ring stand in fume hood
29. Place waste container under separatory funnel
30. Pour 500 milliliters of 50/50 ethyl acetate/hexanes solution into separatory funnel
31. Add 500 milliliters of transformed E. coli co-expression culture into separatory funnel
32. Place glass stopper on separatory funnel
33. Shake separatory funnel 5-10 times to mix culture with extraction solvent
34. Degas funnel by opening spigot while inverted and pointing into fume hood
35. Place funnel upright in ring stand
36. Allow layers to separate for approximately one minute
37. Remove stopper from funnel
38. Drain aqueous E. coli layer into waste beaker
39. Retain solvent layer within funnel
40. Repeat extraction with remaining 500 milliliters of culture using same 500 milliliters of solvent
41. Drain solvent layer containing extracted metabolites into clean flask

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Pour molten agarose into reagent trough
2. Dispense 80 microliters of molten agarose into each well of 96-well microplate using multi-channel pipette
3. Refrigerate leftover agarose in sealed bag for up to one week
4. Coil barrier strips around finger to add curvature for secure channel placement
5. Insert barrier strips into barrier strip channels
6. Affix coupler to starvation plate without using coupler to manipulate plate
7. Align angled corner of coupler with angled corner of microplate for correct orientation
8. Sort three- to five-day-old flies under CO₂ anesthesia
9. Load individual flies by column into starvation plate
10. Close barrier strip per column as filled
11. Record sample layout within microplate
12. Remove CO₂ from flies
13. Allow flies to recover spontaneously
14. Initiate six-hour starvation period from anesthesia time
15. Dissolve 0.4 grams of sucrose and 0.1 gram of yeast extract in 10 milliliters of distilled water in a 15 milliliter conical tube
16. Vortex the tube until solids fully dissolve
17. Add 40 microliters of dye stock solution to the liquid food
18. Transfer liquid food into a 10 milliliter syringe tipped with 0.45 micrometer filter
19. Filter approximately 1.5 milliliters of solution into a 1.7 milliliter microcentrifuge tube
20. Set syringe containing solution aside for later filtration during feeder plate preparation
21. Seal bottom of 1536-well microplate with sealing film
22. Adhere film thoroughly using sealing paddle
23. Trim excess film from left and right edges with razor blade
24. Dispense 10 microliters of filtered liquid food column-wise into upper left-hand well of each 4-well cluster
25. Apply sealing film to top of plate
26. Adhere top film using same sealing steps as bottom
27. Centrifuge plates at 200×g for 10 seconds to settle fluid
28. Perforate wells on top plate surface using 0.25mm needle probe in solution dispensing order
29. Wipe needle probe between solutions to prevent cross-contamination
30. Flip plate and perforate wells on bottom surface
31. Read plate absorbance at 630 nm without lid
32. Place internal lid on top sealing film to position condensation rings around perforated wells
33. Place external lid on plate
34. Position feeder plate face up on coupler, aligning guide holes with starvation plate
35. Adjust barrier strips on coupler to open wells for plates
36. Place coupler and plate assemblies in secondary container
37. Place lower half of pipette box containing soaked paper towels into each secondary container for humidity
38. Close lid of secondary container
39. Transfer secondary container to controlled environment
40. Allow flies to consume for 22 hours
41. Check each plate for dead flies
42. Update plate layout according to mortality observations
43. Anesthetize flies en masse by pumping CO₂ inside secondary container
44. Verify all flies are immobilized after 60 seconds of CO₂ exposure
45. Gently tap anesthetized flies into starvation plate
46. Replace plastic barrier strips
47. Remove feeder plates for reading
48. Reread plate absorbance at 630 nm
49. Repeat absorbance reading process for all plates
50. Quantify evaporation for every well
51. Calculate Pearson correlation coefficients for evaporation versus rows and columns
