question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Wash gradient pourer and PVC peristaltic tubing with deionized water
2. Position two 50 mL centrifuge tubes on ice with peristaltic tubing outlet touching tube wall
3. Turn black lever down to close connection between inner and outer chambers
4. Place gradient pourer with magnetic stir bar in inner chamber on magnetic stirrer
5. Pour 35 mL of heavy gradient solution into inner chamber
6. Dispense half of heavy gradient solution through peristaltic pump at 300 mL/hour into two ice-chilled centrifuge tubes
7. Pour 35 mL of light gradient solution into outer chamber
8. Turn black lever up halfway to reconnect inner and outer chambers
9. Expel entire gradient mixture from chambers at 60 mL/hour
10. Keep gradients on ice before use
11. Cut whole rosette tissue from at least 80 four-week-old Arabidopsis plants grown on soil
12. Add half of cut plant tissues to cooled large mortar and pestle containing 75 mL of grinding medium
13. Moisten four layers of 20-25 μm pore size filtration material with grinding medium
14. Filter homogenate through all four layers of filtration material using plastic funnel into 500 mL conical flask
15. Grind other half of tissue with 75 mL grinding medium
16. Combine entire homogenate in filtration material for maximum filtration
17. Grind remaining tissue on filtration material with 150 mL grinding medium and filter into 500 mL conical flask
18. Centrifuge homogenate at 2,500 g at 4°C in fixed angle rotor for 5 minutes
19. Pour supernatant into clean centrifuge tubes
20. Centrifuge supernatant at 17,500 g at 4°C in fixed angle rotor for 20 minutes
21. Leave 3 mL of supernatant in tube
22. Resuspend pellet in remaining supernatant using fine paintbrush
23. Add 10 mL of single strength wash buffer to each tube
24. Pool contents of all four tubes into one centrifuge tube (resulting in two tubes total)
25. Adjust volume to 50 mL with single strength wash buffer
26. Repeat centrifugation steps
27. Disperse pellet using fine paintbrush
28. Pool crude mitochondrial pellet into tube using dropper
29. Layer mitochondrial suspension on 0% to 4% PVP gradients using dropper
30. Balance tube weight using single strength wash buffer
31. Centrifuge at 40,000 g at 4°C for 40 minutes
32. Turn off centrifuge brake
33. Aspirate upper 5 cm of solution above mitochondrial band
34. Distribute mitochondrial solution into two clean tubes
35. Fill tubes with single strength wash buffer
36. Cover tube mouth with plastic paraffin film
37. Invert tube several times to distribute colloidal density gradient and mitochondria
38. Centrifuge at 31,000 g for 15 minutes at 4°C with slow brake
39. Aspirate supernatant and adjust tube volume to 50 mL with single strength wash buffer
40. Invert tube several times for mixing
41. Centrifuge solution at 31,000 g for 15 minutes at 4°C with slow brake
42. Aspirate supernatant and resuspend mitochondrial pellet in 500 µL single strength wash buffer using pipette with modified 200 µL tip
43. Add 970 µl of air-saturated respiration medium to reaction chamber with plunger open
44. Add 150 µg of total mitochondrial protein to reaction chamber using modified pipette
45. Add 500 µM Adenosine Triphosphate to reaction chamber
46. Continuously stir mitochondrial suspension using magnetic stir bar
47. Close plunger
48. Start recording oxygen consumption
49. Wait for stable linear oxygen consumption trace (1-2 minutes)
50. Add 5 mM succinate
51. Record oxygen consumption for approximately 2 minutes
52. Add 1 mM Adenosine Diphosphate (ADP)
53. Record oxygen consumption for 2 minutes
54. Add 1 mM Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide Hydrogen (NADH)
55. Record oxygen consumption for 4-8 minutes
56. Add cytochrome C pathway inhibitors
57. Record oxygen consumption via alternative oxidase for 2 minutes
58. Add 5 mM dithiothreitol and 10 mM pyruvate
59. Record oxygen consumption for 5-7 minutes
60. Add 500 mM N-propyl gallate
61. Record oxygen consumption for 2 minutes

Complete step list: Layer 3 milliliters of 0.05% Tween 20 on Trichoderma mycelium culture
2. Use rake to release conidia from conidiophores without pressing on mycelium to prevent hyphae damage
3. Recover conidia solution using micropipette and transfer to 15 milliliter tube for counting

6. Gently shake plate forward and backward
7. Spin and rotate plate in 90° segments until fully rotated to distribute spores across agar surface
8. Incubate plate at 30°C until growth inhibition analysis
9. Weigh desired quantity of garlic powder into individual 50 milliliter tubes using sterile spatula
10. Add 10 milliliters of 45°C PDA to each tube and invert several times to distribute powder evenly
11. Pour homogenized suspensions into 5cm diameter Petri dishes and allow solidification at room temperature
12. Use 5 mm diameter sterile stainless steel tube to plot circles in center of agar in control and antifungal-treated dishes
13. Use sterile toothpick to dispose of agar cylinders
14. Use new 5 mm diameter sterile stainless steel tube to plot 15-20 random circles in fungal plates
15. Use sterile toothpicks to transfer spore/hyphae/mycelium-covered cylinders into empty regions of PDA plates
16. Pour 10 milliliters of PDA medium into lid of 5 cm diameter Petri dish containing antifungal compounds or PDA medium alone
17. Allow agar to solidify at room temperature
18. Use 50 milliliter centrifuge tube as calibration tool to make PDA circle in center of agar
19. Use sterile spatula to remove agar around circle
20. Use 5 mm diameter sterile stainless steel tube to plot circle in center of medium in lid
21. Use sterile toothpick to discard agar cylinder
22. Use 5 mm diameter sterile stainless steel tube to extract plugs from prepared fungal plates
23. Transfer spore/hyphae/mycelium-coated plugs into assay plate lids using sterile toothpick

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Clear organic matter from soil surface
2. Sieving soil samples
3. Cleaning mesh with water after sieving each sample
4. Placing three individual 100 gram soil samples into beakers
5. Covering beakers with laboratory film
6. Pre-incubating covered beakers at 25 degrees Celsius for 10 days
7. Remove film from soil sample
8. Place 1 gram of soil in pre-weighed aluminum weigh boat
9. Record combined weight of moist soil and weigh boat
10. Dry samples in weigh boats at 105°C for 48 hours
11. Check sample weight every 8 hours for first 24 hours
12. Check sample weight every 6 hours until no weight loss occurs
13. Determine dry soil weight using known weigh boat weights
14. Calculate dry to moist soil weight ratio
15. Place damp paper towel at bottom of two glass vacuum desiccators with porcelain plates
16. Designate one desiccator for fumigation
17. Prepare three narrow glass vials per sample, each containing 30 grams of soil
18. Label each vial in pencil on laboratory tape
19. Place two vials per set in fumigation desiccator and third vial in other desiccator
20. Place fumigation desiccator in fume hood
21. Cover bottom of 100 milliliter beaker with boiling stones
22. Pour 50 milliliters of ethanol-free chloroform into beaker in fume hood
23. Place beaker in center of fumigation desiccator and close desiccator
24. Connect fumigation desiccator to vacuum pump
25. Turn on vacuum pump and boil chloroform for 30 seconds
26. Turn off and disconnect vacuum pump to allow air into desiccator
27. Boil chloroform for two minutes (fourth boiling)
28. Close off desiccator to maintain vacuum
29. Turn off and disconnect vacuum pump
30. Close and seal other desiccator
31. Close and seal desiccator
32. Store desiccators in dark area at room temperature for 24 hours
33. Place fumigation desiccator in fume hood
34. Vent desiccator
35. Remove paper towel from desiccator
36. Place soil-filled vials into desiccator
37. Close desiccator
38. Connect vacuum pump to desiccator
39. Open vacuum to remove chloroform gas from soil samples
40. Run vacuum for five minutes
41. Vent desiccator
42. Repeat vacuum and vent cycle four additional times
43. Remove soil-filled vials from desiccator
44. Label wide-mouth polypropylene bottles for fumigated and non-fumigated subsamples
45. Label three bottles as no soil controls for titration
46. Drill hole in center of size 10 rubber stopper
47. Insert 15 centimeter glass rod into stopper hole
48. Affix 40 milliliter glass vial to glass rod with rubber band
49. Add one gram of pre-incubated soil to each of three subsample bottles
50. Place one milliliter deionized water into bottle labeled for fumigated soil
51. Place vial of fumigated soil into bottle
52. Pipette one milliliter of two molar sodium hydroxide into glass vial
53. Stopper bottle
54. Seal bottle with laboratory film
55. Repeat assembly process for remaining soil samples
56. Prepare no soil control containers without soil samples
57. Store incubation chambers in dark at room temperature for 10 days
58. Carefully remove vial after incubation
59. Remove vial of sodium hydroxide from incubation container
60. Pipette 2 milliliters of 1 molar barium chloride into vial
61. Add one drop of phenolphthalein to vial
62. Add magnetic stir bar to vial
63. Begin stirring mixture on stir plate
64. Slowly add 0.1 normal hydrochloric acid from burette until solution changes from red to colorless
65. Record volume of hydrochloric acid added
66. Repeat titration for all incubation chambers, noting no soil values separately
67. Calculate carbon dioxide mineralized during soil incubation using titration data and dry-moist soil weight ratio
68. Calculate microbial biomass carbon for each sample set
69. Perform seven additional rounds of fumigation and incubation on fumigated samples
70. Calculate mineralized carbon dioxide after each fumigation-incubation cycle
71. Determine labile organic carbon and potential turnover time for fumigated samples using non-linear regression

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Fast mouse for 8 to 12 hours prior to surgery
2. Prepare 5% sodium taurocholate solution
3. Filter sodium taurocholate solution through 0.22 microfilter
4. Autoclave surgical instruments and materials (forceps, scissors, blade holder, needle holder, hemostatic clip, vascular clamps, sterile drapes)
5. Sterilize micro-syringe through irradiation
6. Induce general anesthesia via intraperitoneal injection of 1% pentobarbital sodium solution at 50 mg/kg dosage
7. Fix mouse in supine position on irradiated foam flat plate using tape
8. Remove fur from mouse abdomen using depilation cream within boundaries: xiphoid process to bilateral anterior superior iliac spine line, parallel to front axillary lines
9. Wipe skin 2-3 times with 0.5% iodophor
10. Let skin dry for 1-2 minutes after each iodophor application
11. Cover mouse with sterile drapes
12. Cut open skin layer and subcutaneous layer
13. Make 2-3 cm median abdominal incision
14. Locate duodenum from left to right along stomach
15. Gently pull duodenum to left side using surgical forceps and disinfectant-soaked cotton swab
16. Expose pancreas, biliopancreatic duct, and duodenal papilla
17. Use 8-0 sutures to pass through duodenum-pancreas connection around papilla
18. Pull suture to left side of mouse body to fix duodenum
19. Gently pull skin and lower edge of liver aside
20. Clamp common hepatic duct
21. Pierce common hepatic duct with microinjector
22. Clamp the common hepatic duct
23. Pierce microinjector into distal biliopancreatic duct retrogradely
24. Fix needle tip near duodenal papilla with clamp
25. Infuse 10 microliters of 5% sodium taurocholate into biliopancreatic duct at 5 microliters/minute
26. Maintain biliopancreatic duct closure for 5 minutes
27. Remove clamps
28. Remove the clamps
29. Pull out the microinjector
30. Remove the other clamp
31. Fix the suture
32. Restore the duodenum to original anatomical position
33. Check for bleeding
34. Close abdominal incision with 6-0 sutures
35. Remove sterile drape

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Create R mix by dissolving 0.88 grams of polyethyleneimine in 25.00 grams of water using a planetary mixer
2. Create F mix by dissolving 0.88 grams of polyethyleneimine in 16.83 grams of water using a planetary mixer
3. Dissolve 12.60 grams of resorcinol powder into R mix until solution turns clear
4. Add 17.03 grams of formaldehyde solution to F mix and ensure complete mixing
5. Add 5.25 grams of boron carbide powder incrementally into R mix and F mix separately
6. Add 6.50 grams of acetic acid to R mix and F mix
7. Ensure complete mixing of both R mix and F mix
8. Print molds using fused deposition modeling 3D printer with ABS filaments
9. Combine R mix and F mix to obtain final suspension
10. Mix final suspension using stirring plate at 200-300 RPM
11. Apply vacuum to final suspension for 10 minutes using vacuum jar to remove air bubbles (without boiling water)
12. Pour de-aired suspension into 3D printed mold
13. Seal glass container to prevent moisture loss
14. Place sealed container with mold into 60-80°C oven
15. Cure cast for at least eight hours
16. Remove sealed container with mold from oven
17. Allow container to cool to room temperature
18. Transfer mold to new container
19. Add acetone until mold is fully submerged
20. Extract free green body from acetone bath after ABS dissolution
21. Place green body in oven at 80 degrees Celsius for drying
22. Place dried green bodies in two-inch quartz tubes lined with graphite foil
23. Load quartz tubes into furnace
24. Establish gas flow at 250 SCCM with 4 wt% H₂ and 96 wt% Ar to create reducing atmosphere
25. Heat green bodies at 5°C/min to 1050°C and hold for 3 hours
26. Verify uniform dark coloration indicating carbon formation from pyrolysis
27. Place carbonized parts in graphite furnace
28. Backfill furnace with flowing helium gas under vacuum
29. Heat furnace to 2,290 degrees Celsius
30. Hold temperature at 2,290°C for one hour
31. Remove parts from furnace

11. Stir mixture with magnetic stirrer for 20 minutes at room temperature

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Cover 2/3 of conductive side of FTO coated glass slide with semi-transparent acrylic adhesive tape
2. Coat uncovered areas of slide with zinc powder
3. Pour 2M hydrochloric acid solution in distilled water over slide to etch substrate
4. Wipe residue from exposed slide sections using cotton swab
5. Rinse etched FTO substrate with distilled water
6. Remove tape from substrate
7. Wash etched surface in 2% w/v alkaline liquid detergent concentrate solution
8. Sonicate FTO substrate for 10 minutes in acetone bath
9. Sonicate FTO substrate for 10 minutes in isopropanol bath
10. Treat FTO substrate in oxygen plasma cleaner for 15 minutes
11. Place etched and cleaned FTO substrate on hot plate at 450°C
12. Cover contact area with precut glass slide
13. Allow substrate to heat to 450°C
14. Mix 0.6 mL TAA with 7 mL isopropanol
15. Apply TAA solution to substrate via spray pyrolysis using air carrier gas at 450°C
16. Maintain sample at 450°C for 30 minutes
17. Cool sample to room temperature
18. Remove glass cover after substrate cooling
19. Dilute 30 nanomolar titanium oxide paste with ethanol to 2:7 weight ratio
20. Sonicate suspension for 30 minutes
21. Spin coat sample with suspension for 30 seconds at 5,000 RPM with 2,000 RPM/s ramp rate
22. Anneal titanium film at 500°C for 30 minutes to form mesoporous titanium oxide film
23. Immerse sample in 40 millimolar titanium chloride solution in distilled water
24. Treat sample at 70°C for 20 minutes
25. Anneal titanium chloride-treated sample at 450°C for 30 minutes
26. Transfer sample to nitrogen-filled glove box with <1% humidity
27. Add 1 milliliter of DMF to 553 milligrams of lead iodide
28. Heat mixture to 80 degrees Celsius while stirring continuously
29. Continue heating until lead iodide dissolves
30. Prepare 0.02 molar solution of monovalent cation halide in lead iodide solution
31. Apply 80 microliters of monovalent cation halide in lead iodide solution to substrate
32. Spin coat substrate for 30 seconds at 6,500 RPM with 4,000 RPM/s ramp rate
33. Bake thin film at 80 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
34. Dissolve 40 milligrams of methylammonium iodide in 5 milliliters of isopropanol
35. Place lead iodide coated substrate in spin coater
36. Apply 120 microliters of MAI solution to substrate
37. Allow MAI solution to sit on substrate for 45 seconds
38. Spin coat substrate with MAI for 20 seconds at 4,000 RPM
39. Anneal perovskite film at 100 degrees Celsius for 45 minutes
40. Add 1 milliliter of chlorobenzene to 72.3 milligrams of spiro-methoxyTAD
41. Shake mixture until solution appears transparent
42. Prepare stock solution of 520 milligrams of lithium TFSI in 1 milliliter of acetonitrile
43. Mix 17.5 microliters of lithium TFSI solution with 28.8 microliters of 4-tert-butylpyridine
44. Combine mixture with spiro-methoxyTAD solution
45. Spin coat sample with mixture for 30 seconds at 4,000 RPM with 2,000 RPM/s ramp rate
46. Cover sample with mask pattern for solar cell top contacts
47. Deposit 80 nanometer gold layer onto sample at 0.01 nm/s rate

Complete step list: Grow bacteria in 5 ml LB broth with 10 mM calcium chloride and optionally 25 μg/ml kanamycin to OD600=1.0
2. Prepare dilution series of P1 phage stock in LB medium
3. Mix 200 μl bacterial suspension with 100 μl of each phage dilution in 15 ml centrifuge tubes
4. Incubate tubes statically for 20 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
5. Add approximately three milliliters of molten top agar supplemented with ten millimolar calcium chloride to tubes
6. Mix tube contents by vortexing
7. Pour mixtures onto pre-warmed LB plates to make even layers
8. Incubate plates overnight at 37 degrees Celsius
9. Select plate with semi-confluent growth of phage plaques
10. Scrape top agar layer using inoculation loop
11. Transfer scraped agar to centrifuge tube
12. Add 1-2 mL LB to tube under fume hood
13. Add one drop chloroform to tube
14. Vortex tube vigorously for one minute
15. Centrifuge to pellet agar and bacterial cells
16. Transfer supernatant to microcentrifuge tube while avoiding pellet debris
17. Add two drops chloroform to supernatant
18. Store lysate at 4-10 degrees Celsius
19. Grow recipient strain in LB to optical density of 1 at 600 nm
20. Add calcium chloride to recipient strain culture to 10 millimolar concentration with mixing
21. Add appropriate volume of phage lysate (taken from sample top to avoid chloroform transfer) to 1 ml recipient culture to achieve multiplicity of infection of 0.5
22. Statically incubate mixture for 20 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
23. Add sodium citrate to reach 100 millimoles per liter concentration to stop infection
24. Centrifuge transduced bacteria
25. Resuspend pellet in 1 milliliter of fresh LB supplemented with 100 millimolar sodium citrate
26. Wash cells twice with LB supplemented with 100 millimolar sodium citrate to remove free phages and calcium
27. Resuspend washed bacteria in 1 mL fresh LB supplemented with 100 mM sodium citrate
28. Incubate resuspended bacteria at 30°C with 100 RPM shaking for 1 hour
29. Collect bacteria by centrifugation
30. Resuspend pellet in approximately 100 μL LB supplemented with 100 mM sodium citrate
31. Spread bacteria on LB plate supplemented with 25 μg/mL kanamycin and 10 mM sodium citrate
32. Incubate plate at 30°C until colonies appear
33. Restreak colonies onto new LB agar plates supplemented with kanamycin
34. Incubate plates at 30°C until single colonies appear
35. Prepare electrocompetent cells from kanamycin-resistant recipient strain
36. Add 1 picogram of pCP20 plasmid DNA to cell suspension
37. Gently mix DNA-cell suspension
38. Transfer mixture to pre-cooled 1 mm electroporation cuvette
39. Set electroporator to 1.8 kilovolts
40. Perform electroporation
41. Rescue transformed cells with 1 ml SOC medium
42. Incubate rescued cells at 30°C for 1 hour
43. Plate 100 microliters of cells on LB plus ampicillin plates
44. Incubate plates overnight at 30 degrees Celsius
45. Pick single colonies and inoculate in fresh LB without antibiotics
46. Grow inoculated cells overnight at 43 degrees Celsius to induce flippase recombinase expression
47. Plate 50 microliter serial dilutions on non-selective plates
48. Incubate serial dilution plates overnight at 30 degrees Celsius

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Use plaster to make floors in nest boxes
2. Press foam block into wet plaster (few centimeters deep, few centimeters from box back) to designate lower nest region
3. Cover designated nest region after plaster drying
4. Feed colonies with live crickets
5. Apply water to plastic nest box flooring using wash bottle
6. Remove trash and dead individuals during feeding
7. Freeze all waste and dead ants overnight at -30°C
8. Dispose of frozen waste materials as regular garbage
9. Add pinch of dried sawdust to colonies
10. Use micro pipette puller to pull glass microinjection needles
11. Set first-step parameters: heat=575, filament=3, velocity=35
12. Set first-step parameters: delay=145, pull=75
13. Set second-step parameters: heat=425, filament=0, velocity=15
14. Set second-step parameters: delay=128, pull=200
15. Verify needle dimensions: 2mm taper and 0.5μm tip
16. Store pulled needles in dust-free clean environment
17. Prepare microinjection mixture of casein proteins and in-vitro synthesized small guide RNAs
18. Keep mixture on ice until microinjection needle loading time
19. Store microinjection mixture at -80°C when not in use
20. Set injection parameters to 140 hPa injection pressure, 70 hPa constant pressure, and 0.4 seconds duration
21. Load microinjection needle with two microliters of mixture
22. Load microinjection needle with 2 microliters of mixture using microloader pipette tips slowly to avoid bubble formation
23. Break tip of needle along edge of tape to open tip while maintaining narrow taper
24. Mount needle to micro manipulator
25. Select syncytial-stage embryos for microinjection
26. Line embryos vertically on double-sided tape attached to glass slide, ensuring secure placement at tape edge to prevent movement
27. Place slide with embryos onto microscope stage at microinjection workstation
28. Align injection needle with first embryo
29. Align needle with first embryo using micromanipulator
30. Laterally puncture first embryo along dorsal/ventral axis under microscope
31. Inject microinjection mixture
32. Monitor for slight embryo movement indicating internal pressure increase
33. Observe formation of small droplet with tissue/lipid traces on embryo membrane
34. Gently remove needle from embryo
35. Adjust microscope slide position to align with next embryo
36. Repeat injection process for all embryos on slide
37. Transfer slide with injected embryos to humid box
38. Incubate embryos in humid box for 1 hour for recovery
39. Wash slide with ethanol
40. Remove injected embryos from tape using featherweight forceps
41. Transfer embryos to tube containing small amount of 70% ethanol
42. Invert tube several times
43. Transfer embryos to 1% agar plates with 2% antibiotic antimycotic using small soft paintbrush
44. Incubate plates at 25°C for approximately four weeks while checking regularly for hatching
45. Transfer hatched larvae and remaining embryos to nest box with young nurse workers

Complete step list: Weigh out 2-Chlorotrityl chloride resin
2. Place resin in 50 mL fritted medium porosity synthesis vessel
3. Affix synthesis vessel to variable speed mechanical shaker
4. Turn vessel to 45-degree angle to maximize agitation
5. Add 15 milliliters of DCM to resin
6. Allow resin beads to swell for 15 minutes
7. Add eight equivalents of desired polyamine to resin
8. Allow reaction to proceed for five hours
9. Perform Kaiser test to confirm polyamine attachment to resin
10. Add four equivalents of DDE in anhydrous methanol to protect primary amine group
11. Shake reaction mixture overnight
12. Perform Kaiser test to confirm protection
13. Drain DCM from reaction mixture
14. Wash resin twice with 2:1 DCM:DMF mixture
15. Add 20 equivalents of Boc Anhydride in DCM to the resin
16. Allow reaction to proceed for three hours
17. Perform chloranil test to confirm secondary amine protection
18. Drain solvent mixture and wash resin twice with 2:1 DCM:DMF mixture
19. Add 10 milliliters of 2% hydrazine solution in DMF to the resin
20. Shake mixture for one hour
21. Perform Kaiser test to confirm primary amine deprotection
22. Mix 4 equivalents Fmoc-amino acid, 3.95 equivalents HBTU, and 15 equivalents DIPEA
23. Add one-to-one mixture of DCM and DMF to cocktail
24. Sonicate cocktail until complete dissolution
25. Sonicate cocktail until complete dissolution
26. Verify complete mixing and activation of amino acid, coupling agent, and DIPEA
27. Wait 3-5 minutes to ensure carboxylic acid activation
28. Add activated reaction mixture to vessel containing resin
29. Allow coupling reaction to proceed for 2-4 hours at ambient temperature
30. Perform Kaiser or chloranil test to confirm successful coupling
31. Deprotect Fmoc group from amino acid after coupling confirmation
32. Add 10 milliliters of 20% 4-Methylpyridine solution in DMF to deprotect Fmoc group
33. Perform Kaiser test to confirm successful deprotection
34. Wash resin with 10 milliliters of DMF for 5 minutes
35. Wash resin again with 10 milliliters of DMF for 5 minutes
36. Wash resin with 10 milliliters of DCM for 10 minutes
37. Add 10 equivalents of desired carboxylic acid functionality
38. Add 9.5 equivalents of HBTU
39. Add 12 equivalents of DIPEA
40. Sonicate cocktail until complete dissolution
41. Add cocktail to vessel
42. Carry out reaction for at least five hours
43. Drain solvent from vessel
44. Wash resin with 8 mL DMF for 2 minutes
45. Wash resin twice with 8 mL DCM for 5 minutes each
46. Drain solvent from vessel
47. Dry resin under vacuum for 15 minutes
48. Add 14 milliliters of TFA to 0.5 milliliters of water
49. Add 0.5 milliliters of Triisopropylsilane to mixture
50. Add cleavage cocktail to resin
51. Shake mixture for two to four hours at room temperature
52. Collect solution in 50 milliliter round bottom flask
53. Concentrate TFA in vacuo to one to two milliliters
54. Collect solution in 50 milliliter round bottom flask
55. Concentrate TFA to 1-2 milliliters using rotary evaporator at reduced pressure and 40°C
56. Add concentrated TFA solution dropwise to 15 milliliters anhydrous cold ether to precipitate PPA
57. Add 5 milliliters anhydrous cold ether to original flask containing concentrated TFA solution
58. Sonicate flask to recover additional solids
59. Combine sonicated mixture with ether solution from precipitation step
60. Cover flask and refrigerate overnight to maximize precipitation
61. Collect precipitated material by vacuum filtration
62. Use fine or medium pour size center disk filter funnel for filtration
63. Wash precipitate twice with 5-10 milliliters of cold ether to remove residual organics

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Assemble 12-well plate in biological safety cabinet
2. Unpack plate and inserts using sterilized forceps
3. Grab inserts at base with forceps and place into wells
4. Coat porous membrane of each insert with 90 microliters of 10 μg/μL collagen IV/fibronectin mixture
5. Incubate plate at 37°C for 24 hours in cell culture incubator
6. Pipette 1 mL of PBS into each collagen-coated insert
7. Aspirate PBS from inserts using vacuum pump
8. Pipette prewarmed DMEM F12 medium into upper and lower chambers
9. Incubate plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂
10. Aspirate medium from cell culture flask
11. Wash cells with 10 milliliters of PBS
12. Cover cells completely with 5 milliliters of trypsin EDTA
13. Incubate flask for 3-5 minutes at 37°C
14. Transfer 5 milliliters of cells into 15 milliliter tube
15. Add 5 milliliters of FCS-containing medium to stop enzymatic reaction
16. Centrifuge suspension for 3 minutes at 210 × g
17. Remove supernatant
18. Resuspend cell pellet in 5 mL medium
19. Add 10 μL cell suspension to 10 μL 0.4% trypan blue stain
20. Add 10 μL mixture to counting chamber
21. Insert counting chamber into cell counter and start program with focus verification
22. Seed 200,000 cells per upper chamber in 12-well plate
23. Incubate plate at 37°C for 14 days with media changes in upper/lower chambers every 2-3 days
24. Inoculate one colony of E. coli strain GM2163 into three milliliters of LB medium
25. Culture inoculated cells at 37 degrees Celsius for 24 hours in an incubation shaker
26. Dilute compound of interest to final concentration in DMEM F12 medium
27. Add diluted compound mixture into upper and lower chambers of 12-well plate
28. Incubate plate in cell culture incubator
29. Exchange complete medium with antibiotic-free medium after incubation
30. Measure optical density at 600 nm of overnight bacterial culture
31. Dilute culture to OD600 0.5 in 50 milliliter Falcon tube
32. Work in biological safety cabinet
33. Add 450 microliters of bacterial solution to each upper chamber of 12-well plate
34. Incubate plate for six hours at 37 degrees Celsius
35. Remove insert with forceps
36. Sample 50 microliters of medium from each lower chamber
37. Drop each sample onto separate agar plate
38. Streak solution on plates using cell spreader
39. Incubate plates at 37 degrees Celsius for 24 hours
40. Count colonies on plates
41. Treat cells with 0.5 mM and 0.15 mM glyoxal solution for 1 hour
42. Perform immunoblotting with anti-AGE antibodies to detect glycation
43. Count bacterial colonies and normalize counts to untreated control
44. Cultivate THBMECs in normal glucose medium and high glucose medium

8. Incubate sample overnight at 37°C and 5% CO₂
9. Verify cell attachment and viability using inverted phase-contrast microscope
10. Irradiate dish with 6 Gy X-ray radiation

18. Aspirate fixation solution from dish
19. Add 2 milliliters of PBS along dish walls
20. Aspirate PBS from dish
21. Add 5 microliters of DAPI staining reagent onto glass slide
22. Remove cover slip from dish using scalpel
23. Drain excess PBS by touching edge of cover slip with paper towel
24. Mount cover slip upside down on DAPI staining reagent drop on glass slide
25. Examine stained cells under fluorescence microscope with DAPI filter

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place clean paper (5 by 35 millimeters) on center of double-sided tape
2. Excise paper using clean blade
3. Place glass cover slip on top of double-sided tape
4. Rub cover slip using pipet tip to form microfluidic chamber
5. Place assembled flow cell between two microscope slides
6. Clip microscope slides together
7. Connect lure lock syringe containing 3 mL deionized water
8. Wash chamber with 3 mL deionized water
9. Wash chamber again with 3 mL lipid buffer via drop-by-drop connection
10. Inject 1 mL of 0.04x biotinylated lipid in lipid buffer into chamber
11. Deliver injection in two shots
12. Incubate for 5 minutes per shot
13. Inject 1 mL of 0.025 mg/mL streptavidin in BSA into chamber
14. Deliver streptavidin injection in two shots
15. Incubate streptavidin for 10 minutes per shot
16. Inject 1 mL of ~30 pM biotinylated lambda phage DNA in BSA buffer into chamber
17. Deliver DNA injection in two shots with 10-minute incubations per shot
18. Connect syringe containing 10 milliliters of imaging buffer to syringe pump
19. Remove bubbles in all tubing lines of microfluidic system
20. Couple prepared flow cell with microfluidic system via drop-to-drop connection to avoid bubble injection
21. Assemble flow cell and flow cell holders
22. Mount assembly on custom-built total internal reflection fluorescence microscope
23. Inject 200 microliters of YOYO-1 dye in imaging buffer through 100 microliters sample loop
24. Run imaging software
25. Turn on 488-nanometer laser to check DNA curtain formation
26. Inject pre-incubated protein sample after YOYO-1 dye removal
27. Turn off syringe pump when proteins reach DNA curtain
28. Switch off shutoff valve
29. Incubate for 15 minutes for histone loading by Abo1
30. Wash out unbound Abo1 and histone proteins for 5 minutes
31. Transiently turn off buffer flow to check histone protein loading

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Cut soft sponge into 5 cm² section (20-25 mm tall)
2. Cut groove into sponge (5-10 mm deep, width matching fish)
3. Cut 0.3 mm thick polyvinyl chloride to fit over sponge
4. Add valley fold to PVC
5. Position PVC into central groove of sponge
6. Transfer assembly to 160 mm dish with 30 mm walls
7. Fill dish with recovery tank water until PVC is fully immersed
8. Choose injection site and tag color for fish tagging
9. Prepare VIE tags using tagging kit
10. Mix elastomer materials
11. Load mixed elastomer into 3mm syringe with 29-gauge needle
12. Prepare two water tanks for anesthesia and recovery
13. Place air stone in recovery tank to mildly circulate water
14. Prepare 1:1 mixture stock of 2-methylquinoline and 99.5% ethanol
15. Prepare sufficient stock for 18 ppm 2-methylquinoline concentration in tank
16. Thoroughly mix stock solution into tank water
17. Transfer individual fish to anesthesia tank
18. Cover anesthesia tank to prevent escape
19. Wait until fish shows no reaction to water vibrations or touch
20. Remove anesthetized fish from tank by hand
21. Use cold water acclimated hand for handling
22. Lift fish from anesthesia tank and take physical measurements
23. Transfer fish back to anesthesia tank if recovery signs appear
24. Transfer small fish to surgical table positioned ventral side down in PVC groove
25. Use binocular microscope for injection if fish is very small
26. Hold large fish by hand for injection
27. Position needle with beveled side outwards and pointed side toward fish
28. Insert needle subcutaneously parallel to body at shallow depth under skin
29. Adjust needle position to ensure tag visibility
30. Inject VIE tag while withdrawing needle, stopping injection before needle bevel reaches entry point to create wide deposition area
31. Transfer injected fish to recovery tank
32. Gently circulate water by hand if recovery exceeds two minutes
33. Return recovered fish to rearing aquarium using net
34. Administer 10 ppm Elbagin in rearing aquarium for three days to prevent infection

5. Place tubes in preheated hot-water bath for 15 minutes
6. Centrifuge tubes at 15,000 G for 10 minutes
7. Pipette supernatant into new labeled microcentrifuge tubes using fresh pipette tips for each sample

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place one embryo in dish containing dissection media coded with silicon
2. Hold embryo with abdomen against silicon using forceps under microscope
3. Insert tip of second forceps into central canal of rostral spinal cord
4. Close forceps to slightly tear dorsal tissue
5. Repeat tearing operation towards caudal side until entire spinal cord is opened
6. Position embryo with open spinal cord against silicon
7. Detach rostrad part of spinal cord
8. Separate spinal cord from body
9. Pinch rostrad part of spinal cord
10. Pull embryo by head to extract spinal cord
11. Pull away remaining meninges and DRGs attached to spinal cord
12. Flatten spinal cord on silicon
13. Remove dorsal half of cord using scalpel by cutting along middle of each side
14. Cut middle part of spinal cord into ten pieces
15. Transfer spinal cord pieces to new tube
16. Collect spinal cord pieces at bottom of tube
17. Replace HBSS with 1 ml Ham F-10 medium
18. Add 10 µl trypsin
19. Incubate tube for 10 minutes at 37°C
20. Transfer fragments to new 15 ml tube containing 800 µl complete L-15 medium
21. Add 100 µl of 4% BSA to tube
22. Add 100 µl DNAs and titrate mixture twice
23. Let fragments settle for 2 minutes
24. Collect supernatant in fresh 15 ml tube
25. Add 2 ml of 4% BSA to bottom of supernatant tube
26. Centrifuge at 470 × g for 5 minutes
27. Remove supernatant after centrifugation
28. Resuspend cell pellet with 2 ml complete L-15 medium
29. Split cell suspension into two 15 milliliter tubes
30. Add 2 milliliters of L-15 PH medium to each tube
31. Slowly add 2 milliliters of density gradient solution to bottom of each tube to create interface
32. Centrifuge tubes at 830 x gravity for 15 minutes at room temperature
33. Aspirate 1-2 milliliters of cells from density gradient/medium interface
34. Aspirate 1-2 milliliters of cells at the density gradient solution/medium interface
35. Transfer aspirated cells to a fresh 15 milliliter tube
36. Adjust volume to ten milliliters with L-15 PH medium
37. Add two milliliters of 4% BSA cushion to bottom of tubes
38. Centrifuge at 470 x gravity for 5 minutes at room temperature
39. Remove supernatant after centrifugation
40. Resuspend pellet in 3 milliliters of complete L-15 medium
41. Repeat centrifugation at 470 x gravity for 5 minutes at room temperature
42. Remove supernatant after second centrifugation
43. Resuspend cell pellet in two milliliters of complete culture medium
44. Dilute motor neurons to 5,000-10,000 cells in 500 microliters of culture medium per well in 24-well plate
45. Remove laminae solution using P-1000 pipet tip
46. Immediately transfer diluted motor neurons in culture medium to coated plates to avoid drying
47. Re-suspend 1 microgram of DNA in 50 microliters of neuronal culture medium
48. Vortex DNA solution for 5 seconds
49. Re-suspend 1.5 microliters of magnetic beads in 50 microliters of neuronal culture medium
50. Add 50 microliters of bead solution to 50 microliters of DNA solution
51. Incubate DNA-bead mixture for 20 minutes at room temperature
52. Withdraw 100 microliters of culture medium from target well
53. Transfer 100 microliters of DNA-bead mixture to each well
54. Incubate 24-well plate for 20-30 minutes on magnetic plate at 37°C

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Lay surgical drape
2. Arrange instruments for gross dissection
3. Set up bio safety cabinet for eye visualization on dissecting microscope
4. Set up bio safety cabinet for dish assembly
5. Set up open globe injury induction workspace inside bio safety cabinet
6. Position cross tracking vice on lab jack in front of puncture platform
7. Wash eyes with antibiotic antimycotic PBS
8. Place eye on antibiotic antimycotic PBS-soaked gauze
9. Create incision near eye equator using sterile razorblade or scalpel
10. Hemisect eye using curved surgical scissors to isolate anterior segment
11. Scoop vitreous humor from anterior segment using micro scissors as shovel
12. Remove lens from anterior segment using micro scissors
13. Cut iris radially to iris root until trabecular meshwork is visible
14. Extend cut 360 degrees around iris to expose entire trabecular meshwork region
15. Clean residual iris covering trabecular meshwork if necessary
16. Trim ciliary body remnants posterior to trabecular meshwork region
17. Leave one millimeter thin band of tissue
18. Wet cotton swab in media and gently dab center of cornea to remove pigment
19. Hold eyes with forceps and wipe extra pigment around sclera
20. Place inverted anterior segment over elevated region of bottom dish with cornea centered
21. Position clamping ring on top of anterior segment
22. Tighten screws gently with L-key
23. Attach both fluidic connectors with O-rings to threaded ports on dish bottom
24. Attach 18 gauge 90-degree bent needle hub to first fluidic connector
25. Attach length of tubing to first fluidic connector
26. Attach 18 gauge needle hub to first fluidic connector
27. Attach nylon syringe filter to first fluidic connector
28. Attach three-way valve to first fluidic connector
29. Attach 20 millimeter media-filled syringe to first fluidic connector
30. Attach 18-gauge 90-degree bent needle hub, tubing, 18-gauge needle hub, three-way valve, and 10ml syringe barrel to second fluidic connector
31. Keep three-way valves open to syringes
32. Gently push media through system via first fluidics connector port
33. Remove bubbles by gently pushing media into dish
34. Invert dish to push out bubbles
35. Place anterior segment organ culture dish into cell culture incubator
36. Direct tubing lines out through bottom of incubator door
37. Position tubing lines with reservoir at pressure transducer instrument
38. Connect side three-way valve to pressure transducer setup while PBS flows through line
39. Set pressure regulator on puncture platform to 50 PSI
40. Position cross-tracking vice on lab jack in front of puncture platform
41. Extend piston arm to maximal distance
42. Position corneal apex within 1 mm region of puncturing object
43. Retract piston arm
44. Bring anterior segment 1 cm closer to puncturing object
45. Turn on puncture device
46. Open solenoid valve with second switch
47. Press second switch to retract device from eye
48. Verify injury induction via visual inspection and media leakage from injury site
49. Assess corneal epithelium integrity using optical coherence tomography
50. Image injured anterior segment tissue immediately after injury
51. Image injured anterior segment tissue 72 hours post-injury

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Seed fibroblasts into 24-well plates at 50,000 cells per well in 1 mL medium
2. Incubate plate overnight at 37°C and 5% CO₂
3. Prepare MMC media by mixing Ficoll 70, Ficoll 400, and ascorbic acid into 10% FCS DMEM
4. Incubate media in water bath to disperse crowders
5. Sterilize media using 0.2 micrometer filter
6. Aspirate spent media from fibroblasts
7. Replace aspirated media with freshly made MMC media
8. Incubate cells at 37°C for 6 days with MMC media changes every 3 days
9. Prepare Sirius Red solution by dissolving 0.2 grams of Direct Red 80 powder in 200 milliliters of distilled deionized water with one milliliter of acetic acid
10. Aspirate MMC media from cells
11. Add 300 microliters of Sirius Red solution to each well
12. Return cells to incubator for 90 minutes
13. Gently rinse Sirius Red solution with tap water
14. Allow plate to air dry overnight
15. Add 200 microliters of 0.1 molar sodium hydroxide to each well to extract Sirius Red
16. Shake plate for 5-10 minutes
17. Transfer 100 microliters of extracted stain to transparent 96-well plate
18. Measure absorbance at 620 nm using microplate reader
19. Wash each well with 200 microliters of PBS
20. Fix cells with methanol at 4°C for 10 minutes
21. Add 3% BSA to wells
22. Incubate plate for 30 minutes at room temperature
23. Aspirate blocking solution
24. Add 200 microliters of anti-collagen one antibody to each well
25. Incubate plate for 90 minutes
26. Aspirate antibody solution
27. Wash wells three times with PBS (5 minutes per wash)
28. Add 200 microliters of Goat Anti-Rabbit-FITC Secondary Antibody and DAPI to each well
29. Cover plate with aluminum foil and incubate for 30 minutes at room temperature
30. Discard secondary antibody and DAPI
31. Repeat PBS washes
32. Visualize fluorescence under microscope
33. Wash cells twice with PBS
34. Add 40 microliters of lysis buffer into each well
35. Scrape cell layer with pipette tip
36. Transfer protein lysate into microcentrifuge tubes
37. Load 10 micrograms of protein into each well of 4-12% Bis-Tris protein gel
38. Perform SDS-PAGE electrophoresis at 200 volts for 30 minutes
39. Transfer proteins to nitrocellulose membrane using western blot at 90 volts for 90 minutes while avoiding bubble formation between gel and membrane
40. Block membrane with 10 milliliters of blocking buffer
41. Incubate membrane with primary antibodies at 4°C overnight
42. Wash membrane five times with 0.1% TBS Tween 20 for 5 minutes per wash
43. Incubate membrane with species-appropriate secondary antibody for 1 hour at room temperature
44. Repeat washing procedure with 0.1% TBS Tween 20
45. Visualize fluorescence using imaging system
46. Measure RNA concentration using micro volume spectrophotometer
47. Prepare cDNA synthesis kit
48. Perform first strand DNA synthesis
49. Mix cDNA with 10 microliters of SYBR Green Supermix
50. Transfer mixture to custom 96-well plate pre-coated with oligonucleotide primers
51. Adjust total volume to 20 microliters per well with water
52. Run RT-PCR according to manuscript directions

Complete step list: Cut and gently pull back outer skin of peritoneum to expose thoracic cavity
2. Open diaphragm by cutting rib cage to expose heart and lungs
3. Fill 5 milliliter syringe with EDTA-HBSS solution
4. Connect 25 gauge needle to syringe
5. Insert needle into right ventricle
6. Use forceps to secure myocardial tissue around needle
7. Gently inject solution while maintaining constant pressure
8. Remove and discard draining lymph nodes
9. Collect lung tissue and transfer to Petri dish on ice
10. Fill GentleMACS tube with 5 ml of lung collagenase digestion solution
11. Chop lung tissue into small pieces
12. Transfer chopped tissue into tube using razor blade
13. Run GentleMACS dissociation program M_lung_01
14. Incubate sample at 37°C for 30 minutes
15. Shake tube every 5 minutes during incubation to resuspend tissue fragments
16. Run GentleMACS dissociation program M_lung_02
17. Transfer sample to 15 milliliter conical tube
18. Centrifuge cells at 335 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
19. Discard supernatant
20. Add 2 milliliters of CK lysing buffer to pellet
21. Incubate for 1 minute at room temperature to lyse red blood cells
22. Wash cells with 13 milliliters of cold PBS supplemented with 0.5% BSA
23. Discard supernatant
24. Resuspend pellet in 5 milliliters of cold PBS with BSA
25. Pass cell solution through 100 micrometer nylon mesh
26. Determine total cell number by trypan blue exclusion
27. Fill 15 ml conical tube with 5 ml collagenase solution per lung
28. Add chopped lung tissue to collagenase solution in tube
29. Incubate lung tissue at 37°C for 1 hour
30. Vortex tissue every 15 minutes during incubation to re-suspend fragments
31. Disrupt digested tissue by passing sample 6-8 times through 3 ml syringe with 20-gauge needle while avoiding bubble formation
32. Wash single cell suspension for 10 minutes at 200 × g at 4°C
33. Discard supernatant
34. Resuspend cells in 400 microliters of separation buffer
35. Block Fc-mediated nonspecific binding with 0.5 µg anti-CD16/32 antibody per 10^6 cells
36. Incubate cells with antibody for 30 minutes at 4°C
37. Wash cells with 10 milliliters of cold separation buffer
38. Resuspend cells in 400 microliters of cold separation buffer
39. Add 100 microliters of CD11C microbeads to cell suspension
40. Mix cell-bead solution thoroughly
41. Incubate cell-bead solution for 15 minutes in refrigerator
42. Remove unbound beads by washing with 10 milliliters of cold separation buffer
43. Discard supernatant after washing
44. Resuspend cell pellet in 1 milliliter of cold separation buffer
45. Load samples into AutoMACS Pro separator
46. Run POSSELD program on AutoMACS Pro separator
47. Incubate enriched CD11C-positive cell suspension with anti-CD11C PEI7 and MHC Class II antibodies
48. Wash cells with cold PBS plus BSA
49. Resuspend cells in 0.5 milliliters of PBS plus BSA
50. Pass cell suspension through 40 micrometer nylon mesh filter
51. Add purified CD11C-positive cells to FACS tube for quantitation
52. Add 100 microliters of PBS plus BSA to five milliliter collection tubes
53. Sort filtered cell suspension by FACS while maintaining refrigerated temperature

2. Tighten nose clamp while maintaining mouse head on level plane
3. Adjust ear bar height to reach caudal portion of ear canal for head immobilization
4. Disinfect head with alternating wipes of topical antiseptic and 70% ethanol
5. Make 0.75 centimeter horizontal incision in mid scalp using sterile scalpel
6. Drill two symmetrical holes using 0.45 millimeter burr: positioned above right/left cortical hemispheres, 2 mm from sagittal suture and lambdoid suture at middle of parietal bone

9. Advance needle perpendicularly into first burr hole

12. Withdraw needle 0.5 millimeters to 2 millimeters depth

18. Monitor mouse until full recovery
19. Secure mouse on dissecting pad
20. Make lateral incision through integument and abdominal wall beneath ribcage using scissors and forceps
21. Separate liver from diaphragm
22. Cut diaphragm along entire ribcage length to expose pleural cavity
23. Make incision to posterior end of left ventricle using scissors
24. Perfuse right heart chamber with 15 ml PBS over 2 minutes
25. Perfuse heart with 10 ml of 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS over 2 minutes

27. Place scissors tip into foramen magnum to create lateral skull incision toward eye
28. Make similar lateral incision on opposite skull side keeping scissors superficial
29. Cut skull region between eyes and above nose
30. Use forceps to gently peel cranial bones from brain hemispheres
31. Use spatula to elevate brain
32. Dissect cranial nerve fibers fixing brain to skull
33. Place brain into plastic dish
34. Excise cerebellum and olfactory bulbs
35. Fix brain in 15 mL tube with 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS at 4°C overnight
36. Rinse brain tissue three times for 5 minutes each at 4°C in fresh PBS
37. Remove front of each hemisphere with scissors and forceps
38. Place samples into labeled cryo-molds front side down
39. Submerge each tissue in optimal cutting temperature medium
40. Snap freeze samples in liquid nitrogen in 10 mm plastic Petri dish for -80°C storage
41. Immerse 5-6 micrometer brain tissue sections in antigen retrieval solution
42. Incubate slides in antigen retrieval solution for 5 minutes on 100°C heat block
43. Wash slides with four 5-minute washes using fresh PBS at room temperature
44. Immerse slides in blocking solution
45. Wash slides three times for 5 minutes each in fresh wash buffer at room temperature
46. Stain tissue samples with appropriate primary antibodies
47. Incubate with primary antibodies at 4°C overnight
48. Image slides on fluorescence microscope
49. Adjust microscope contrast and brightness for GFP-positive cell quantification

Complete step list: Place female rat on dissection table
2. Hold ankle firmly between thumb, index finger, and middle finger
3. Sever calcaneal tendon using 12-centimeter straight blunt scissors

11. Dissect overlying muscle tissue while exposing nerve moving up leg

14. Severe nerve close to spine using fine scissors
15. Place dissected nerve in 15-mL centrifuge tube filled with MKHB
16. Place tube on ice until cleaning procedure
17. Fill coated Petri dish halfway with chilled oxygenated MKHB
18. Place dissected sciatic nerve in Petri dish
19. Pin both ends of nerve to dish ensuring straight alignment without kinks/twists
20. Tie double knot with 6-0 silk sutures around each nerve end to prevent cytosol leakage
21. Position knots adjacent to insect pins near nerve center to prevent leakage
22. Under microscope, remove fat/blood vessels/muscle tissue from nerve using 2mm angled spring scissors and fine forceps
23. Prune unused nerve branches
24. Replace buffer with fresh chilled oxygenated MKHB every 5 minutes during cleaning
25. Transfer cleaned nerve to transport tube filled with buffer
26. Place transport tube on ice
27. Prepare clean dual-chamber nerve bath
28. Position nerve bath below level of two-liter bottle on heating stirrer
29. Connect bath drain to peristaltic pump inlet
30. Connect peristaltic pump outlet to tube leading to buffer bottle
31. Connect bath inlet to tube with adjustable flow valve
32. Insert tube into buffer bottle
33. Connect three-way valve with syringe to middle outlet for siphon priming
34. Prime siphon by drawing syringe until buffer flows
35. Configure valve to establish 5-6 mm/min buffer flow rate into bath
36. Increase flow rate initially to fill bath
37. Place nerves in bath after buffer level reaches drain
38. Secure one end of nerve at corner of buffer-filled bath chamber using insect pin
39. Thread nerve through partition hole between bath chambers using 45-degree angled fine forceps while pinching only nerve ends
40. Secure other end of nerve in oil chamber using insect pin while ensuring nerve is straight without stretching and free of kinks/twists
41. Apply silicone grease to seal buffer-oil chamber interface to prevent leakage
42. Fill oil chamber with silicone or mineral oil
43. Place silver/silver chloride recording electrode hooks in oil bath chamber
44. Secure recording electrode hooks using boss heads and grippers
45. Drape nerve portion in oil bath over hooks without pulling taut
46. Adjust or repair silicone grease seal if leakage observed after nerve movement
47. Connect reference silver/silver chloride electrode to amplifier ground
48. Place reference electrode in buffer-filled bath chamber
49. Secure reference electrode using laboratory gripper
50. Place nerve cuff electrode in buffer-filled bath chamber
51. Open electrode in bath using forceps or blunt/angled tweezers to wet cuff interior
52. Remove bubbles by drawing bath buffer with fine syringe and forcing bubbles out of cuff
53. Gently open cuff with tweezers
54. Slide cuff under nerve
55. Close cuff around nerve while avoiding nerve kinking or twisting
56. Connect stimulation electrode and current return electrode to stimulator
57. Position square platinum sheet current return electrode away from nerve in bath
58. Secure electrodes' leads with tape
59. Connect stimulator TTL signal output to oscilloscope channel 4
60. Press Trigger channel tab on oscilloscope screen
61. Specify channel 4 as triggering channel
62. Set trigger level to 1 volt using level knob
63. Set time resolution to 1 millisecond per division
64. Set voltage resolution to 10 millivolts per division
65. Center trigger reference in time
66. Set trigger level to 1 volt
67. Connect stimulator to computer
68. Turn on stimulator
69. Connect battery power supply to stimulator power input
70. Start MATLAB software on computer
71. Execute custom MATLAB script
72. Open indicated MATLAB script
73. Edit script parameters: set pulse amplitude to -300 μA, pulse width to 300 μs, pulse count to 10, inter-pulse interval to 1 second
74. Start stimulation protocol by clicking Run in software

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Wash cells with 10 milliliters of PBS
2. Centrifuge sample at 1,450 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C
3. Discard supernatant
4. Lyse cells using 1 ml ice-cold Lysis Buffer A per 10^6 cells via gentle pipetting
5. Sonicate sample in 1.5-ml tube at 25% amplification for 10 seconds
6. Centrifuge sample at 20,400 × g for 15 minutes at 4°C
7. Collect supernatant
8. Transfer 1 ml supernatant into 1.5-ml microcentrifuge tubes
9. Add 40 μl prewashed Protein A-agarose beads per 1 ml lysate
10. Mix by inverting tube several times
11. Rotate sample for 30 minutes at 4°C
12. Centrifuge sample at 13,000 × g
13. Recover supernatant
14. Add 40 μl prewashed Protein A-agarose beads to pre-cleared lysate
15. Add 10 μg anti-human TERT antibody to pre-cleared lysate
16. Mix sample by inverting tube several times
17. Rotate sample at 4°C overnight
18. Centrifuge sample at 3,300 × g for 30 seconds at 4°C
19. Remove supernatant
20. Add 1 ml Wash Buffer One to beads
21. Mix by inverting tube
22. Rotate sample for 5 minutes at 4°C
23. Centrifuge sample at 3,300 × g for 30 seconds at 4°C
24. Remove supernatant
25. Repeat wash procedure (steps 3-7) three additional times
26. Resuspend beads in 60 microliters of micrococcal nuclease reaction mixture by gentle pipetting
27. Shake sample gently on turntable shaker in Peltier incubator at 25°C for 15 minutes
28. Centrifuge sample at 3,300 × g for 30 seconds at 4°C
29. Remove supernatant
30. Wash beads twice with AGC solution containing 3 mM EGTA
31. Wash beads once with Wash Buffer Two
32. Add six microliters of P-32 radiolabeled uridine triphosphate to reaction mixture
33. Mix reaction mixture well by pipetting
34. Add one microliter of template RNA to mixture
35. Resuspend beads with 20 microliters of RdRP reaction mixture
36. Shake sample gently on turntable shaker in Peltier incubator at 32°C for two hours
37. Add 5.4 microliters of 20 mg/mL Proteinase K to reaction mixture
38. Add 45.4 microliters of 2X Proteinase K Buffer to reaction mixture
39. Mix reaction mixture by pipetting
40. Incubate sample in Peltier-type incubator at 37°C for 30 minutes
41. Add 109.2 microliters of RNase-free water to sample
42. Add 200 microliters of acid phenol-chloroform to sample
43. Mix sample by vortex
44. Centrifuge sample at 21,100 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
45. Transfer aqueous phase to new tube
46. Add 20 microliters of three molar sodium acetate (pH 5.2) to aqueous phase
47. Add four microliters of precipitation carrier to aqueous phase
48. Add 250 microliters of ethanol to aqueous phase
49. Shake tube vigorously to mix contents
50. Centrifuge sample at 21,100 × g for 20 minutes at 4°C
51. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
52. Add 300 microliters of cold 70% (v/v) ethanol (stored at -20°C) to pellet
53. Centrifuge sample at 21,100 × g for 15 minutes at 4°C
54. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
55. Air-dry pellet after supernatant removal
56. Resuspend pellet in 20 microliters of RNase-free water
57. Prepare RNase reaction mixture in new tube
58. Add 180 microliters of RNase reaction mixture to sample
59. Incubate tube for 2 hours at 37°C
60. Add 2.3 microliters of 10% SDS to sample
61. Incubate for 15 minutes at 37°C
62. Add 2 microliters of 20 mg/ml Proteinase K to sample
63. Incubate for 15 minutes at 37°C

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Draw 30 milliliters of whole blood from forearm vein
2. Split blood evenly into two non-polystyrene 50 milliliter conical tubes
3. Dilute blood in each tube with 10 milliliters of PBS
4. Add 25 milliliters of histopaque to each tube containing blood and PBS, avoiding mixing
5. Centrifuge tubes for 30 minutes at 400 Gs, room temperature, with no brake
6. Aspirate plasma layer after centrifugation
7. Transfer opaque interface layer to fresh 50 milliliter tube
8. Wash cells in 20 milliliters PBS plus CDTA for 5 minutes at 250 Gs and 4°C
9. Discard supernatant after washing
10. Resuspend pellet in 10 milliliters PBS plus CDTA
11. Vortex cell suspension for 10 seconds to mix well
12. Dilute 200 microliters cell suspension in 300 microliters PBS plus EDTA
13. Add 500 microliters trypan blue to diluted cell suspension
14. Spin down cells three times for 10 minutes at 120 Gs
15. Suspend pellet in nine milliliters of sterile water for three seconds
16. Add one milliliter of 10X PBS
17. Wash and count cells
18. Dilute cells in easy set buffer at 5×10⁷ cells per milliliter
19. Incubate cells with 50 microliters per milliliter of monocyte enrichment cocktail for 10 minutes at 4°C
20. Vortex cept monocyte enrichment beads for 30 seconds during last minute of incubation
21. Add 50 microliters per milliliter of beads to cells
22. Incubate bead-cell suspension for 5 minutes
23. Adjust cell solution volume to 2.5 milliliters using eep buffer
24. Place tube in eep magnet for 2.5 minutes at room temperature
25. Pour buffer containing non-binding monocytes into fresh sterile tube
26. Wash unbound cells once with PBS plus EDTA
27. Transfer washed cells to multi-well plate
28. Add 1 ml culture media per 1×10⁶ cells to culture plate
29. Culture cells under experimental conditions for 6 days to induce macrophage differentiation
30. Replace half of media with fresh media after 3 days
31. Harvest cells for experimental analysis after 6 days

2. Vortex sample for 15 seconds
3. Wash ticks with 70% ethanol

5. Transfer ticks to new PCR tube
6. Allow ticks to air dry
7. Place ticks into a new PCR tube
8. Allow ticks to air dry in PCR tube
9. Mechanically disrupt tick tissues by crushing with mortar and pestle
10. Purify DNA from individual ticks using commercial DNA extraction kit following manufacturer instructions
11. Elute purified DNA to final volume of 100 microliters
12. Set up amplicon PCR in 27.5 microliter reaction volume
13. Add 5 microliters of each primer at 1 micromolar concentration
14. Add 12.5 microliters of commercially available PCR mix
15. Add 5 microliters of DNA extracted from individual ticks at 5 nanograms per microliter concentration
16. Move tubes to thermocycler
17. Run thermocycler program listed in text protocol
18. Prepare new PCR tube for each sample
19. Combine PCR product with double-distilled water to achieve total volume of 60 microliters
20. Bring paramagnetic beads to room temperature
21. Vortex paramagnetic beads thoroughly
22. Add 48 microliters of paramagnetic beads to 60 microliters of sample
23. Incubate bead-sample mixture for five minutes
24. Place tubes on magnetic rack for five minutes
25. Wait until solution becomes clear
26. Remove supernatant
27. Place tubes on magnetic rack
28. Add 500 microliters of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to tubes
29. Pipette out liquid from tubes while retaining beads
30. Air dry samples by leaving tubes open on magnetic rack for five minutes or until small cracks appear in beads
31. Add 20 microliters of TE buffer to tubes
32. Incubate samples off magnet at room temperature for five to ten minutes
33. Place tubes back on magnet
34. Transfer supernatant to fresh tube after liquid-bead separation
35. Add 2.5 microliters of each primer to PCR reaction
36. Add 12.5 microliters of commercially available PCR Master Mix
37. Add 5 microliters of double-distilled water
38. Add 2.5 microliters of cleaned amplicon product
39. Move PCR tubes to thermocycler
40. Run thermocycler program as detailed in protocol
41. Load 4-6 microliters of PCR product per sample onto 1.5% agarose gel

43. Move qPCR plate to real-time PCR instrument
44. Perform qPCR run using pre-programmed protocol
45. Create combined library following quantification

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Sort male and female mosquito pupae by size differences
2. Transfer 100 male pupae into pre-labeled cage
3. Transfer female pupae in batches of 40-50 per cage
4. Check cages for presence of male mosquitoes after adult emergence
5. Dissolve 200 milligrams of Rhodamine B powder in 100 milliliters of 10% sucrose solution
6. Mix solution thoroughly to ensure complete dissolution of powder
7. Prepare 20 sugar feeder bottles with wicks
8. Add 10 milliliters of 10% sucrose solution to 10 feeder bottles
9. Add 10 milliliters of 0.2% Rhodamine B sucrose solution to the remaining 10 feeder bottles
10. Place five feeder bottles into each prepared male cage
11. Allow male mosquitoes to feed from bottles for three days
12. Turn on mercury burner lamp
13. Turn on stereo microscope
14. Allow mercury burner lamp to stabilize for 10 minutes
15. Set fluorescence filters to red fluorescence protein one (RFP1)
16. Aspirate small number of mosquitoes into oral aspirator glass tube
17. Observe male mosquito bodies through glass tube under fluorescent stereo microscope to verify Rhodamine B marking
18. Transfer set A male mosquitoes: Place 10 sucrose-fed males per cup into six net-secured paper cups
19. Transfer set A male mosquitoes: Place 10 Rhodamine B sucrose-fed males per cup into six net-secured paper cups
20. Repeat transfer and grouping procedure for set B male mosquitoes
21. Set up 12 mating cages
22. Add mosquitoes to cages: For 6 cages - 10 Rhodamine B marked set A males, 10 unmarked set B males, 10 virgin wild-type females. For other 6 cages - 10 unmarked set A males, 10 Rhodamine B marked set B males, 10 virgin wild-type females
23. Label cages to distinguish mating combinations
24. Place respective male cups in mating cages according to labels
25. Remove netting and gently tap cup to release males
26. Allow male mosquitoes to acclimate in mating cage for at least 1 hour
27. Transfer virgin wild-type female mosquitoes into 12 paper cups using an oral aspirator
28. Transfer one cup of females into every mating cage and remove netting
29. Gently jostle cup to encourage remaining female mosquitoes out
30. Remove paper cup and netting from cage to prevent mosquito escapes
31. Allow mating to take place for three hours
32. Terminate mating experiment by removing all mosquitoes from each cage with mechanical aspirator
33. Cold-anesthetize mosquitoes on ice for at least five minutes
34. Gently pick up female mosquitoes and house them in separate netting-secured paper cup
35. Label paper cup by transferring respective label from mating cage
36. Cold-anesthetize female mosquitoes on ice for at least five minutes prior to dissection
37. Dissect female mosquito under stereo microscope
38. Examine spermathecae under microscope
39. Determine presence of Rhodamine B marked seminal fluid in spermathecae using fluorescent stereo microscope with RFP1 filter

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Collect up to 15 larvae in a 2 ml tube using glass pipette and pipette pump
2. Wash larvae twice with 1 ml cold 1X PBS on ice
3. Aspirate PBS from tube using glass pipette and pipette pump
4. Add 1 ml cold 4% PFA in PBS to tube
5. Incubate at 4°C overnight with gentle rocking
6. Aspirate PFA from tube using glass pipette and pipette pump
7. Rinse three times with 1 ml cold PBS
8. Hold larva on its back using fine forceps
9. Grip skin on either side of larva's head gently with forceps
10. Grab skin overlying heart with very fine forceps in other hand
11. Pull skin down diagonally toward tail on one side of fish in dish
12. Repeat skin pulling procedure on opposite side of fish
13. Continue grabbing skin flaps and pulling down until all skin/melanophores near liver are removed
14. Hold 5-6 DPF larvae on their back using fine forceps
15. Gently prod yolk of 5-6 DPF larvae using very fine forceps to lift in one piece
16. Hold 3-4 DPF larvae on their back using fine forceps
17. Stroke yolk of 3-4 DPF larvae from ventral side using very fine forceps
18. Scrape yolk off 3-4 DPF larvae in pieces
19. Transfer dissected larvae to fresh cold PBS in 2ml tube using glass pipette and pipette pump
20. Pour few milliliters of 3% methylcellulose onto lid of clean plastic Petri dish
21. Transfer larvae to methylcellulose using glass pipette with pipette pump, minimizing PBS transfer
22. Orient fish laterally under dissecting microscope at low magnification using fine forceps (right side facing left)
23. Verify alignment with one eye directly above the other eye
24. Remove bent tail by forceful pinching with forceps to ensure flat positioning
25. Zoom microscope to high magnification and focus on liver specimen
26. Press Capture Image button to acquire and save liver image
27. Repeat imaging process for all fish specimens at identical magnification
28. Acquire image of micrometer at same magnification for calibration
29. Rename files randomly and create randomization file listing original and blinded file names
30. Open randomized files in order starting with file one
31. Choose Freehand Selections tool
32. Outline each liver using Freehand Selections tool
33. Press Control-M to measure outlined liver area
34. Identify livers with residual skin/yolk, out-of-focus, or non-intact for placeholder measurement
35. Insert placeholder measurement for inadequate livers
36. Save all measurements in text file
37. Open measurements file and randomization file in spreadsheet program
38. Insert new column in measurements file
39. Add original file names for blinded files using randomization file
40. Save file as Unblinded Measurements file
41. Sort data by original file name
42. Exclude liver measurements with inadequate pictures
43. Convert measurement values to square millimeters if necessary
44. Perform procedure using small and controlled movements to prevent liver damage
45. Wear gloves when handling PFA
46. Handle concentrated PFA solutions in chemical fume hood

22. Resuspend larval pellet in 1 milliliter of water

25. Count worms under stereo microscope
26. Repeat counting to obtain average worm count

30. Pick 8-10 late L4 stage worms using eyelash pick under stereoscope and submerge pick in solution well until worms detach and swim into solution

Complete step list: Place CryoTubes containing frozen cells in 37°C water bath for two minutes
2. Transfer entire thawed cell suspension to a fresh 50 milliliter conical tube

4. Add washed medium to 50 ml conical tube containing thawed cells
5. Add 8 ml of warm plating medium to tube
6. Carefully mix the suspension
7. Count live cells using trypan blue exclusion method and hemocytometer
8. Adjust cell concentration to 50,000 cells/ml by adding warm plating medium
9. Distribute cell suspension into two 384-well cell culture plates
10. Add 25 microliters of cell suspension to each well
11. Incubate plates at 37°C in humidified atmosphere with 5% CO₂
12. Replace plating medium with 50 microliters of warm maintenance medium
13. Replace half of maintenance medium every two to three days
14. Replace maintenance medium entirely on days seven, 14, and 21
15. Turn on plate reader and set heating system to 37 degrees Celsius
16. Warm 40 milliliters of maintenance medium supplemented with 1% penicillin streptomycin to room temperature
17. Distribute test compounds and controls into two 384-well plates by adding 1.5 microliters of stock solution per well
18. Centrifuge compound plates at 100 G for one minute
19. Add 37 microliters of maintenance medium to each well of compound plates
20. Centrifuge plates at 100 G for one minute
21. Warm 100 milliliters of maintenance medium supplemented with 1% volume-by-volume penicillin streptomycin in a 37 degree Celsius water bath
22. Add 10 milliliters of warmed medium to calcium-sensitive fluorescent dye and quencher mix to reconstitute as stock fluorescence medium
23. Start plate reader software
24. Configure software to record two-minute calcium wave segment with acquisition frequency of at least 10 hertz
25. Dilute three milliliters of stock fluorescence medium with 12 milliliters of warm maintenance medium with antibiotics to prepare fluorescence medium
26. Replace 20 microliters of medium in each well with 30 microliters of fluorescence medium
27. Mix contents by pipetting up and down once
28. Place plate into plate reader
29. Equilibrate HIPSCCMs to reader temperature for 60 minutes
30. Start plate reader software
31. Record two-minute segment of calcium waves with ≥10 Hz acquisition frequency to establish baseline beating rate
32. Use plate reader robot to pipette 5 microliters of test and reference compounds well-to-well from compound plate to cell plate

Complete step list: Attach custom silicone isolator grid to functionalized slide to create well maker
2. Press down on silicone grid to prevent air bubble formation between glass surface and silicone
3. Prepare primer mix by adding 5 micromolar 5' amino primer in 1× print buffer
4. Add 4 microliters of primer mixture to each well
5. Incubate well maker in hybridization chamber with saturated sodium chloride
6. Add 2 microliters of 5 micromolar topoisomerase 1 specific substrate, 2 microliters of recombinant topoisomerase 1 enzyme or prepared cell extract to 16 microliters of one-time topoisomerase reaction buffer
7. Incubate circle mixture at 37 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
8. Immerse well maker in tray filled with Buffer 1 preheated at 50°C
9. Pipette Buffer 1 into wells to remove air bubbles
10. Incubate tray at 50°C for 30 minutes
11. Remove Buffer 1 from tray
12. Wash twice with distilled water with 1-minute vigorous shaking between washes
13. Remove distilled water from tray
14. Add Buffer 2 preheated at 50°C to tray
15. Incubate and wash well maker using Buffer 1 procedure
16. Wash well maker with 70% ethanol
17. Shake well maker vigorously for 1 minute
18. Dry setup using compressed air
19. Add 4 microliters of prepared circle mixture to each corresponding well
20. Place well maker in humidity chamber with distilled water
21. Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour
22. Wash well maker sequentially for 1 minute each in Buffer 3, Buffer 4, and 70% ethanol
23. Air dry well maker
24. Prepare and add 4 microliters of RCA mixture to each well of well maker
25. Incubate at 37°C for 2 hours in humidity chamber
26. Wash slide with buffer 3, buffer 4, and 70% ethanol
27. Dry slide with compressed air
28. Glue slide onto microscope slide
29. Mount slide with 2 microliters of mounting medium without DAPI
30. Add cover glass to slide
31. Analyze slide using fluorescence microscope with 60x oil immersion objective lens and camera
32. Import microscope pictures to ImageJ
33. Stack images using Image > Stacks > Images to Stack menu
34. Change image type to 8-bit
35. Set threshold using Image > Adjust > Threshold menu
36. Set lower threshold bar to 255 and adjust upper bar to make signals red and background black
37. Sweep through individual images to verify correspondence with pre-threshold images
38. Set threshold as low as possible without causing real signals to disappear
39. Click Analyze menu and select Analyze Particles function
40. Verify that 'summarized' field is checked in ImageJ settings
41. Add 4 microliters of diluted horseradish peroxidase-conjugated anti-biotin antibody to each well
42. Incubate at 15-25 degrees Celsius for 50 minutes in humidity chamber
43. Wash well maker three times with Buffer 5 for three minutes each
44. Let well maker dry
45. Add 2 microliters of freshly prepared ECL luminol and hydrogen peroxide mix to wells
46. Visualize slide using CCD camera or X-ray films
47. Remove silicone grid after buffer 5 wash
48. Add 400 microliters of TMB substrate to entire slide
49. Place slide in humidity chamber
50. Incubate for 5-10 minutes for color development
51. Wash slide with 70% ethanol
52. Photograph slide using camera or mobile phone
53. Import picture to ImageJ
54. Change image type to 8-bit via Image Type menu
55. Select rectangle drawing tool from toolbar
56. Draw rectangular area around first band for measurement
57. Open Analyze menu and select Measure
58. Move rectangular selection to next band
59. Measure intensity of new band position

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 5 milliliters of methanol to 50 milliliter vial
2. Dissolve 2 milligrams of conjugated polymers in 2 milliliters of chloroform in 5 milliliter vial
3. Place 5 milliliter vial inside 50 milliliter vial
4. Cap 50 milliliter vial
5. Incubate at 25 degrees Celsius for 3 days to precipitate polymer microspheres
6. Dissolve 5 mg of conjugated polymers in 1 ml chloroform
7. Dissolve 30 mg of SDS in 2 ml deionized water
8. Add 100 μl of polymer mixture to SDS solution
9. Homogenize mixture at 30,000 RPM for 2 minutes
10. Allow emulsion to rest uncapped for 1 day to evaporate chloroform
11. Centrifuge dispersion in 1.5 ml tube at 2,200 × g for 5 minutes
12. Remove supernatant
13. Add 1 ml deionized water and shake vigorously
14. Repeat centrifugation, supernatant removal, and water wash three times
15. Dissolve 20 mg polystyrene and 1 mg fluorescent dye in 20 ml tetrahydrofuran
16. Add 1 ml deionized water to a 1.5 ml microtube
17. Gently pour 0.2 ml THF solution onto water layer while preventing mixing
18. Leave uncapped vial undisturbed for 6 hours to precipitate polymer microspheres
19. Dilute suspension of prepared microspheres by 10 times with poor solvent (methanol or deionized water)
20. Spin cast one drop of diluted suspension onto quartz substrate using spin coater
21. Air dry casted film until solvents evaporate completely
22. Transfer quartz substrate to optical microscope sample stage
23. Identify isolated and well-defined microsphere for micro-photoluminescence measurement
24. Set laser conditions as per text protocol
25. Irradiate microsphere with focused laser beam
26. Record photoluminescence spectrum using spectrometer
27. Take fluorescent image of irradiated microsphere
28. Place quartz substrate onto optical microscope sample stage
29. Identify single well-defined microsphere
30. Set tungsten microneedle on micromanipulation apparatus
31. Move microneedle using touch panel controller
32. Pick up identified microsphere with microneedle
33. Connect collected microsphere to another single well-defined microsphere
34. Irradiate microsphere with focused laser beam
35. Record photoluminescence spectrum using spectrometer
36. Take fluorescent image of irradiated microspheres
