Complete step list: Crush dry ice block into fine flakes using hammer and hard flat object in 5L plastic container
2. Sieve fine dry ice flakes from non-crushed material using large strainer into another 5L plastic container
3. Pour 200 cubic centimeters of dry ice flakes into upright 1L glass beaker
4. Add 10g frozen cannabis sativa inflorescences onto dry ice layer and cover with additional 200cc finely crushed dry ice
5. Cover beaker opening with 2-3 layers of 1mm screen door mosquito net secured with rubber bands
6. Pour liquid nitrogen into large round-bottom stainless steel container
7. Insert 350 micrometer mesh in flour sifter to cover mesh from below
8. Place flour sifter above stainless steel container filled with liquid nitrogen
9. Shake one liter glass beaker with opening downward toward flour sifter to separate trichomes
10. Set aside beaker every 2-3 minutes to allow sifting of accumulated dry ice and plant material on flour sieve
11. Sift flour sieve horizontally to pass plant material into liquid nitrogen in stainless steel container below
12. Add liquid nitrogen to stainless steel container when level runs low
13. Add crushed dry ice to one liter glass beaker when level runs low
14. Refill glass beaker containing used plant material with 10 grams of fresh plant material
15. Repeat sifting process until sufficient enriched trichomes accumulate in container
16. Verify presence of white powder-like substance at bottom of stainless steel container submerged in liquid nitrogen
17. Add small amount of liquid nitrogen
18. Add liquid nitrogen to clean small round-bottom stainless steel container
19. Fold 40x40 cm microsieve (150 μm mesh) twice to obtain 20x20 cm fold
20. Open folded microsieve into cone-like shape
21. Secure mesh cone to container edge with clothespins, positioning cone upright with pointed end submerged in liquid nitrogen
22. Pour liquid nitrogen containing white powder-like substance into microsieve cone
23. Gather and transfer residual plant material from first container into mesh cone using wide brush
24. Detach clothespin from container lid
25. Open microsieve cone
26. Locate trichomes in middle of opened microsieve
27. Hold all four corners of microsieve together
28. Submerge middle part containing trichomes in liquid nitrogen
29. Gently dip and shake microsieve in liquid nitrogen for one minute
30. Puncture cap of 50 milliliter test tube with sterile needle to create vent holes
31. Transfer non-sifted plant debris, dry ice, and larger trichomes from microsieve center into 50 milliliter test tube
32. Store filled test tube at -80 degrees Celsius
33. Transfer sifted trichomes submerged in liquid nitrogen sequentially through 105μm, 80μm, and 65μm microsieves
34. Transfer sample through 50μm microsieve
35. Collect retained trichomes from each microsieve separately
36. Label each collected trichome size fraction
37. Make tiny holes in cap of tube using sterile needle
38. Transfer trichomes (submerged in liquid nitrogen and wrapped in 50 micrometer microsieve) to clean plate using pre-chilled spoon
39. Transfer powder-like trichomes into labeled pre-chilled 1.5 milliliter tube using pre-chilled spatula
40. Immediately store tubes at -80°C
41. Carefully perform T bag-like infusion motion of 150 to 50 micro microsieved isolation

3. Pour 200 cubic centimeters of dry ice flakes into upright 1L glass beaker
4. Add 10g frozen cannabis sativa inflorescences onto dry ice layer and cover with additional 200cc finely crushed dry ice
5. Cover beaker opening with 2-3 layers of 1mm screen door mosquito net secured with rubber bands
6. Pour liquid nitrogen into large round-bottom stainless steel container
7. Insert 350 micrometer mesh in flour sifter to cover mesh from below
8. Place flour sifter above stainless steel container filled with liquid nitrogen
9. Shake one liter glass beaker with opening downward toward flour sifter to separate trichomes
10. Set aside beaker every 2-3 minutes to allow sifting of accumulated dry ice and plant material on flour sieve
11. Sift flour sieve horizontally to pass plant material into liquid nitrogen in stainless steel container below
12. Add liquid nitrogen to stainless steel container when level runs low
13. Add crushed dry ice to one liter glass beaker when level runs low
14. Refill glass beaker containing used plant material with 10 grams of fresh plant material
15. Repeat sifting process until sufficient enriched trichomes accumulate in container
16. Verify presence of white powder-like substance at bottom of stainless steel container submerged in liquid nitrogen
17. Add small amount of liquid nitrogen
18. Add liquid nitrogen to clean small round-bottom stainless steel container
19. Fold 40x40 cm microsieve (150 μm mesh) twice to obtain 20x20 cm fold
20. Open folded microsieve into cone-like shape
21. Secure mesh cone to container edge with clothespins, positioning cone upright with pointed end submerged in liquid nitrogen
22. Pour liquid nitrogen containing white powder-like substance into microsieve cone
23. Gather and transfer residual plant material from first container into mesh cone using wide brush
24. Detach clothespin from container lid
25. Open microsieve cone
26. Locate trichomes in middle of opened microsieve
27. Hold all four corners of microsieve together
28. Submerge middle part containing trichomes in liquid nitrogen
29. Gently dip and shake microsieve in liquid nitrogen for one minute
30. Puncture cap of 50 milliliter test tube with sterile needle to create vent holes
31. Transfer non-sifted plant debris, dry ice, and larger trichomes from microsieve center into 50 milliliter test tube
32. Store filled test tube at -80 degrees Celsius

35. Collect retained trichomes from each microsieve separately

Complete step list: Load 1 mL syringe with 27-gauge needle with 0.1 mL LPS in PBS per mouse
2. Manually restrain 6-8 week old male C57BL/6J mouse
3. Deliver LPS intraperitoneally

6. Use blunt forceps to gently separate muscles and expose trachea
7. Insert sterile 27-gauge needle attached to 10-mL syringe into trachea and inject 1 mL sterile PBS
8. Replace 10-mL syringe with 3-mL syringe equipped with new 27-gauge needle
9. Aspirate bronchoalveolar lavage fluid (BOLF) and transfer to labeled 15-mL conical tube on ice
10. Centrifuge BALF samples
11. Transfer supernatants to new 15 milliliter tubes
12. Centrifuge samples again
13. Transfer supernatants to ultra-centrifuge tubes
14. Transfer supernatants to new conical tubes
15. Load samples into 0.2 micron syringe filters
16. Add filtered samples to new ultra-centrifuge tubes
17. Discard supernatants
18. Re-suspend pellets in 100 microliters of PBS per tube
19. Transfer exosome samples to individual 1.5 milliliter tubes
20. Add 0.4 microliters of appropriate exosome dye to a tube containing 100 microliters of suspension solution per sample
21. Add one thawed exosome sample to each dye-containing tube
22. Thoroughly mix exosomes with dye
23. Incubate samples at room temperature for 5 minutes protected from light
24. Place one exosome spin column per sample into a 1.5 milliliter tube
25. Transfer exosome solutions to the center of each spin column
26. Centrifuge samples
27. Discard spin columns
28. Store purified samples at -20°C
29. Seed 1×10⁴ mouse lung epithelial 12 cells in 0.5 mL medium per well of eight-well chamber slide
30. Culture seeded cells for 16 hours at 37°C in 5% CO₂
31. Add 10 μL thawed fluorescent labeled exosomes to each well
32. Incubate cell cultures for 1 hour
33. Wash cells with PBS
34. Fix co-cultures with 1% paraformaldehyde for 10 minutes at room temperature
35. Remove medium from slide
36. Mount slide with DAPI-supplemented medium
37. Image samples by fluorescence microscopy at 40× magnification
38. Seed 2 × 10⁵ mouse lung epithelial 12 cells in 2 ml medium per well in 12-well culture plate
39. Incubate seeded plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 2 hours
40. Add 20 μg exosomes to three wells of epithelial cells per experimental group
41. Return culture plate to incubator
42. Remove medium after 24-hour incubation
43. Wash cells with PBS
44. Add 600 μl lysis buffer to each well with gentle shaking
45. Transfer entire well contents to individual 1.5 ml tubes

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Seed 2 × 10⁵ B16F10 melanoma cells in 100-millimeter dish with 10 ml DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS
2. Incubate seeded cells at 37°C with 5% CO₂ for 24 hours
3. Wash approximately 80%-confluent cell culture with 5 ml PBS
4. Repeat PBS wash with another 5 ml PBS
5. Add 10 ml serum-free culture medium to starve cells
6. Incubate starved cells for 18-24 hours in cell culture incubator
7. Transfer conditioned supernatant into 50-milliliter conical tube using pipette
8. Load medium into syringe equipped with 0.45 micron-pore filter
9. Strain solution into sterile container to remove cell debris
10. Store conditioned medium at -20°C for up to one month
11. Culture mouse F14 1.1 bone marrow stromal cells in 60-millimeter dish with complete DMEM to 80% confluency
12. Harvest cells with 1.5 milliliters of 0.25% trypsin solution for 1-2 minutes at 37°C
13. Arrest enzymatic reaction with 5 milliliters of medium supplemented with 10% FBS
14. Adjust cells to 1-2×10⁵ cells/mL concentration with fresh serum-supplemented culture medium
15. Seed 5×10⁴ cells in 0.5 ml adipogenic induction medium per well in 24-well plate
16. Incubate cells in culture incubator for 2 days until 90% confluency
17. Replace supernatant with 1 ml adipogenic maintenance medium per well
18. Culture cells in maintenance medium until adipocyte maturation
19. Wash mature adipocytes two times with PBS
20. Fix cells in 0.2 milliliters of 3.7% formaldehyde for one hour
21. Rinse fixed cells in 60% isopropanol
22. Allow wells to dry completely
23. Add 0.2 milliliters of Oil Red O working solution for 30-120 minute incubation at room temperature
24. Wash cells with water to remove unbound dye
25. Image adipocytes by light microscopy
26. Add 0.5 milliliters of ready-to-use RNA isolation reagent to each adipocyte culture well
27. Use appropriate primers for adipocyte-specific gene signature confirmation
28. Run reaction in thermal cycler using indicated settings
29. Add 150 microliters of serum-free DMEM to inner chambers of 0.4 micron-pore membrane inserts
30. Place each membrane insert into one well of a 24-well plate containing 500 microliters of serum-free medium per well
31. Dilute B16F10 melanoma cells from 80% confluent culture to experimental concentration in fresh serum-free medium
32. Replace medium in inner chamber of each insert with 150 microliters of diluted melanoma cell suspension
33. Replace adipogenic maintenance medium supernatant in each well of 90%-confluent mature bone marrow adipocyte culture with 600 microliters of complete DMEM
34. Transfer melanoma cell-containing inserts to each well of mature adipocyte culture plate
35. Place plate in cell culture incubator after all inserts are transferred
36. Place plate in cell culture incubator
37. Remove inserts after two-day incubation
38. Wash bottom of each well twice with 500 microliters PBS per wash
39. Stain adipocytes with Oil Red O working solution
40. Analyze adipocyte gene expression by quantitative PCR

2. Wash tissue sample three times with 1X PBS, discarding solution between each wash

4. Add fungi zone to final concentration of 0.25 micrograms per milliliter and incubate for 30 minutes

9. Separate endometrium from tissue

12. Scratch tissue fragments onto cell culture dish

15. Separate incubator from other cell lines to prevent contamination
16. Monitor cells daily using light microscope
17. Gently remove old media from cultures
18. Wash cultures with 1X PBS
19. Add 2 milliliters of fresh growth media
20. Add 4 milliliters of growth media
21. Aspirate remaining tissue pieces during wash steps
22. Passage cells using 0.05% trypsin after one week of growth
23. Monitor cells reaching 75-80% confluency
24. Place endometrial tissue piece in conical tube with 2 ml of 0.25% trypsin supplemented with 0.03 mg/ml gentamicin
25. Incubate tissue on rotating platform at 37°C for 30 minutes
26. Vortex tissue briefly
27. Centrifuge at 200-400 × g for 2 minutes
28. Discard supernatant
29. Add fresh trypsin and gentamicin solution
30. Incubate tissue at 37°C for 1 hour while rotating
31. Vortex tissue for 5-10 seconds after incubation
32. Add 2 ml growth media containing 10% FBS and 1% penicillin streptomycin
33. Centrifuge cells at 200-400 × g for 2-3 minutes
34. Discard supernatant
35. Resuspend cell pellet in 2 ml growth media
36. Plate cell suspension onto 6 cm culture dish
37. Scrape plate to enhance primary cell attachment
38. Monitor cell growth daily using light microscope
39. Change growth media every two to three days
40. Use 0.05% or 0.25% trypsin to passage cells

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Drill one 4 mm diameter hole 20 mm from each end of 1200 × 30 × 4.5 mm acrylic diffuser bar
2. Drill and tap threaded holes 20 mm from each end of aluminum u-bar section
3. Drill and tap threaded holes into base of aluminum u-bar to suit mounting hardware
4. Straighten one 1.25m length of 1.25mm bare copper wire by securing one end to vise
5. Secure opposite end of wire within grips of hand drill
6. Turn on hand drill at low speed
7. Repeat wire straightening process for second wire
8. Use fine tip permanent marker to mark photodiode positions along diffuser edge
9. Mark first photodiode position at 13.5cm from one diffuser end
10. Mark subsequent photodiode positions every 2cm toward far diffuser end
11. Center photodiode on diffuser bar with electrical tabs pointing sideways
12. Place wire under one tab to mark first copper wire position
13. Mark wire position at center and opposite end of diffuser bar
14. Secure first straightened copper wire to diffuser along marks using cyanoacrylate glue
15. Glue 50 photodiodes facedown at 20mm intervals ensuring center alignment and uniform orientation (large tab on wire, small tab opposite)
16. Place second copper wire under smaller tabs of photodiodes
17. Secure wire to diffuser with cyanoacrylate glue
18. Apply solder flux to both tabs of one photodiode, adjacent wires, and underlying wires
19. Solder each diode tab to underlying copper wires using fine-tipped soldering iron at 350-400°C
20. Shine light onto each photodiode to test solder connections
21. Check voltage signal for each diode across wires using multimeter
22. Solder 1.5 ohm low-temperature coefficient precision resistor in parallel across copper wires (optional)
23. Solder male end of waterproof DC connector to ends of copper wires
24. Seal connections using glue-lined heat shrink tubing
25. Apply bead of silicone sealant to surface of diffuser near edge to create continuous barrier around circuitry
26. Inspect silicone bead closely to ensure no air gaps remain between silicone and diffuser bar
27. Fill well with epoxy resin after sealant curing
28. Allow epoxy resin to harden overnight
29. Remove silicone sealant using razor blade
30. Bolt diffuser to pre-threaded aluminum u-bar using M4 bolts
31. Secure diffuser to aluminum along its whole length using masking tape
32. Fill void inside ceptometer with polyurethane foam filler
33. Allow filler to set overnight
34. Remove masking tape
35. Solder female end of direct current connector to two-conductor cable
36. Seal connections with glue-lined heat shrink
37. Connect PARbar and quantum sensor to data logger
38. Place both sensors outside in full sun on level plane
39. Record sensor outputs during period of widely varying solar radiation
40. Calculate PARbar calibration factor as slope of linear regression (quantum sensor PAR vs. raw voltage output)
41. Install one PARbar above canopy ensuring no shading by light-absorbing elements
42. Install another PARbar below all light-absorbing elements
43. Align both PARbars at 45° angle to planting rows
44. Confirm upper PARbar does not shade lower PARbar
45. Level both PARbars using spirit level
46. Connect PARbars to data logger
47. Convert differential voltage output to PAR using previously determined calibration factors

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Boil 0.4-0.6 mm thick viscose non-woven fibrous material
2. Dry boiled viscose material
3. Iron dried viscose material
4. Cut material into 11 mm circles using metal hole punch
5. Place cut fibrous material between two slides
6. Wrap slides in foil
7. Autoclave foil-wrapped slides
8. Place inserts into wells of sterile 12-well plate
9. Place each sterilized scaffold into corresponding insert
10. Mix 2.7 × 10⁵ fibroblasts in 100 microliters fetal calf serum with 100 microliters freshly diluted thrombin per scaffold
11. Dispense thrombin-fibroblast mixture onto each scaffold
12. Add 200 microliters freshly diluted fibrinogen to each scaffold
13. Mix cell mixture within each well using gentle pipetting to distribute solution evenly over scaffold
14. Prepare test gel in control well without scaffold to track clot formation time
15. Assess clot formation using pipette tip or by inverting plate
16. Submerge organ cultures in medium supplemented with ascorbic acid and TGF-beta 1
17. Replace medium with RFAD medium containing 500 units/mL aprotinin
18. Seed 2.5 × 10⁵ mTEC suspended in 100 μL RFAD medium onto each scaffold
19. Incubate co-cultures for 24 hours
20. Replace medium with fresh medium containing 250 units per milliliter of aprotinin
21. Use forceps to separate scaffold dermal equivalent from filter
22. Embed OTCs in OCT compound
23. Freeze OTCs in gaseous phase over liquid nitrogen
24. Separate scaffold tissue from filters
25. Cut OTCs into pieces using scalpel
26. Transfer OTC pieces into 2 ml RNA-free tube containing 1 ml denaturing solution
27. Mechanically shred OTCs using fast prep instrument at 6.0 speed for 30 seconds
28. Perform second shredding cycle at 6.0 speed for 30 seconds after 2-minute rest on ice
29. Isolate RNA using acid guanidinium thiocyanate-phenol-chloroform extraction per manufacturer protocol
30. Remove membrane and separate scaffold from fibrin fibroblast mtech gel
31. Cut gel with scalpel
32. Transfer tissue into fax tube
33. Add two milliliters of collagenase dis paste
34. Place tube in 37°C water bath with magnetic stirring for 20 minutes
35. Agitate mixture by pipetting once every five minutes
36. Filter cell suspension through 70 micrometer strainer
37. Stain cells using appropriate antibodies

Complete step list: Flush fixative line with buffer using 50 milliliter syringe repeatedly
2. Clear fixative line until all air bubbles are removed
3. Place outlet valve into beaker filled with buffer
4. Turn exit valve off
5. Remove tube from syringe while squeezing it
6. Ensure drop of buffer hangs from tube end
7. Place filled fixative inlet tubing into bottle containing 200 milliliters of paraldehyde fixative per animal
8. Prevent introduction of air bubbles during tubing placement
9. Tighten cap while ensuring seal is properly seated
10. Place bottle into perfusion rig
11. Close outlet port
12. Turn buffer valve to same position as fixative valve
13. Open outlet port and clear buffer line
14. Place tube into buffer bottle
15. Pump rubber manometer bulb to test pressure resistance
16. Fill perfusion needle with buffer to prevent air introduction
17. Place fully anesthetized rat on shallow tray filled with crushed ice
18. Make 5-6 cm lateral incision through integument and abdominal wall beneath rib cage
19. Separate liver from diaphragm
20. Make small incision in diaphragm using curved blunt scissors
21. Continue diaphragm incision along entire length of rib cage to expose pleural cavity
22. Place curved blunt scissors along one side of ribs while carefully displacing lungs
23. Cut through rib cage up to collarbone
24. Make cut on contralateral side
25. Lift sternum away
26. Trim tissue connecting sternum to heart
27. Clamp tip of sternum with hemostat
28. Place hemostat over head
29. Make small incision to posterior end of left ventricle using iris scissors
30. Pass 15 gauge blunt or olive-tipped perfusion needle through incised ventricle into ascending aorta
31. Clamp heart with hemostat to secure needle and prevent leakage
32. Attach perfusion needle in right atrium to outlook port without introducing air bubbles
33. Open outlook port
34. Pump manometer bulb evenly to 80 millimeters of mercury pressure
35. Maintain 80 mmHg pressure throughout buffer infusion period
36. Start timer
37. Adjust needle angle to position of strongest flow
38. Switch buffer valve when buffer is almost finished
39. Note time of liver clearing as perfusion indicator
40. Gradually increase pressure to maximum 130 mmHg to maintain flow
41. Close outlet valve when fixative is nearly finished
42. Record completion time
43. Begin dissection of fixed rat
44. Remove head using guillotine
45. Make midline incision along integument from neck to nose
46. Expose skull
47. Trim remaining neck muscle with scissors
48. Expose base of skull
49. Insert sharp end of iris scissors into foramen magnum on one side
50. Slide scissors along inner surface of skull
51. Make cut extending to distal edge of posterior skull surface
52. Make identical cut on contralateral side
53. Use rongeurs to clear skull around cerebellum
54. Slide scissors tip along inner skull surface from dorsal distal posterior corner to distal frontal ledge
55. Lift blade tip during cutting to avoid brain damage
56. Repeat cutting procedure on opposite side
57. Peel dorsal surface of skull away from brain using rongeurs
58. Trim sides of skull using rongeurs
59. Sever olfactory bulbs and ventral nervous connections using spatula
60. Cut dura connecting brain to skull using iris scissors
61. Place brain in vial containing fixative (≥10x brain volume)
62. Store vial at 4°C for 24 hours
63. Wash brain with PBS 3 times with gentle swirling
64. Store brain in PBS or Hanks solution with sodium azide at 4°C

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Drip one drop of 20% PVP PBS on cutting mat using pipette
2. Immerse dissected nervus tibialis in the PBS droplet
3. Cut two millimeter length of sample using scalpel
4. Place sample in 0.2 mm deep type A metal carrier using fine forceps
5. Transfer carrier into middle plate of cartridge
6. Place hexadecane-coated type B carrier on top as lid
7. Close assembly by lowering upper half of cartridge
8. Close three-piece cartridge in high-pressure freezer loading station
9. Press process button on high-pressure freezer
10. Place sample carriers into cryo vials containing 2 ml of frozen 0.1% tannic acid and acetone in liquid nitrogen bath
11. Close cryo vial caps
12. Place cryo vials into freeze substitution unit set at -90°C
13. Start freeze substitution program and maintain samples at -90°C for 100 hours
14. Wash samples with 2 ml of acetone three times for 30 minutes each in freeze substitution unit
15. Place 2% osmium tetroxide in 0.1% uranyl acetate into freeze substitution unit for cooling
16. Add staining solution to samples for seven-hour staining at -90°C
17. Maintain samples at -20°C for 16 hours in freeze substitution unit
18. Discard liquid in vials and fill with pure acetone at 4°C
19. Remove cryo vials from freeze substitution unit
20. Remove metal carriers from cryo vials using fine forceps in a fume hood
21. Transfer samples from carriers to a 150 mm deep watch glass dish filled with acetone
22. Transfer samples into 2 ml reaction tubes filled with acetone using fine forceps
23. Set microwave to 250 watts for 40 seconds and start to wash samples (repeat four times)
24. Pipette 1 milliliter of 1% thiocarbohydrazide solution
25. Transfer solution to reaction tube
26. Place reaction tube in microwave
27. Configure microwave vacuum function: 2 minutes on, 2 minutes off for 14 minutes
28. Set microwave power to 150 watts
29. Start microwave operation
30. Wash sample four times in acetone
31. Pipette 1 milliliter of 2% osmium tetroxide solution into reaction tube
32. Place sample in microwave
33. Set microwave to same parameters as thiocarbohydrazide treatment
34. Wash sample four times in acetone
35. Pipette 1 milliliter of 25% resin in acetone
36. Wash sample four times in acetone
37. Pipette 1 milliliter of 25% resin in acetone into reaction tube
38. Place reaction tube in microwave
39. Turn on vacuum function for 3 minutes
40. Set microwave power to 250 watts
41. Start microwave
42. Remove nervus tibialis from reaction tube using toothpick
43. Place nervus tibialis on plastic film
44. Place halogen lamp close to samples to heat resin
45. Manipulate sample with toothpick on plastic film until no resin remains
46. Transfer plastic film with sample to oven
47. Set oven temperature to 60 degrees Celsius
48. Polymerize samples for at least 48 hours
49. Cut polymerized samples with razor blade including plastic film to SEM stub size
50. Apply conductive silver resin to SEM stubs using toothpick
51. Attach cut samples to resin-coated SEM stubs
52. Add additional resin around mounted samples
53. Place SEM stubs in oven for polymerization at 60°C for ≥4 hours/overnight
54. Position SEM stubs after polymerization
55. Place SEM stubs in sputter coater
56. Set sputter coater to 35 milliamperes
57. Apply 10 nanometer thick gold coating for one minute
58. Place coated SEM stubs in focused ion beam scanning electron microscope

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Dissect 6-8 hippocampi from postnatal day 1 pups using HBSS medium in Petri dish
2. Chop hippocampi into smaller pieces using dissection scissors
3. Transfer chopped hippocampi from Petri dish to 15 mL tube
4. Wash hippocampi twice with 5 mL HBSS
5. Leave hippocampi in 4.5 mL HBSS after washing
6. Add 0.5 mL of 2.5% trypsin to 4.5 mL HBSS containing hippocampi
7. Incubate tube in 37°C water bath for 15 minutes with inversion every 5 minutes
8. Wash hippocampi with HBSS three times for five minutes per wash
9. Add two milliliters of HBSS to hippocampi
10. Pipette hippocampi up and down 15 times with Pasteur pipette
11. Triturate tissue 10 times with fire-polished Pasteur pipette
12. Rest tube for five minutes until chunks settle to bottom
13. Repeat trituration with remaining chunks until most disappear
14. Transfer supernatant containing dissociated neurons to plating dishes using one milliliter pipette tip
15. Add culture directly to pre-incubated plating medium
16. Shake plate gently
17. Check plating dishes with light microscope 2-4 hours after seeding
18. Use fine tip forceps to flip cover slips
19. Transfer cover slips to glial cell feeder dishes containing preconditioned neuronal culture medium
20. Position cover slips with wax dots side facing downwards
21. Feed neurons with 1 mL fresh neuronal culture medium every 7 days
22. Flip cover slips on third day in vitro
23. Position cover slips with wax dot side facing up in glial cell feeder dish with conditioned medium
24. Mix 0.25 micrograms of CRE-BFP DNA with 0.5 micrograms of ankyrin-G GFP DNA in a 1.7 milliliter tube
25. Add 100 microliters of optimum to the DNA mixture tube
26. Mix the DNA-optimum tube and rest it on a rack
27. Mix 3 microliters of transfection reagent with 100 microliters of optimum in a new 1.7 milliliter tube
28. Incubate transfection reagent mixture for 5 minutes at room temperature
29. Mix 100 microliters of DNA mixture with 100 microliters of transfection reagent mixture
30. Leave mixture for 5-10 minutes on a rack
31. Add 50 microliters of DNA mix on top of each cover slip by inserting pipette tip below medium without touching cover slips while pipetting slowly
32. Transfer dish slowly to incubator
33. Incubate dish for 30-45 minutes
34. Flip cover slips onto home glial feeder dish with wax dot side facing down
35. Return dish to incubator
36. Open Z series images in Fiji
37. Generate maximum projection of images
38. Subtract empty cover slip background signal from image
39. Draw line along AIS with width fully covering the structure, starting before signal rise and stopping after signal drop
40. Measure mean pixel intensity along the line
41. Export intensity data to spreadsheet
42. Run MATLAB script to generate final figure

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Anesthetize rat via IP injection of urethane
2. Test toe pinch withdrawal reflex to confirm anesthesia
3. Administer supplementary doses of urethane if anesthesia is insufficient
4. Shave head of rat
5. Place rat in prone position on homeothermic plate
6. Insert rectal probe to maintain homeothermic plate at 37.5°C
7. Place one ear bar into approximate position within stereotaxic frame
8. Adjust animal to position ear bar in external acoustic meatus
9. Align and place second ear bar into contralateral external acoustic meatus
10. Position animal to align ear bars with tooth holder
11. Open animal's jaw using rat tooth forceps
12. Hook upper incisors over tooth holder
13. Clamp nose in place
14. Make incision in head skin 1mm right of midline, extending 10mm rostral
15. Retract skin and muscle laterally from incision
16. Expose parietal and interparietal bones
17. Scrub exposed bone surface with 20% hydrogen peroxide solution using gauze pad under microscope
18. Drill ~3mm² hole in interparietal bone near Lambda and midline
19. Flush hole with sterile saline to remove bone dust/fragments
20. Dissect below the scruff of the neck with blunt scissors
21. Wrap silver chloride wire with cotton wool
22. Saturate wrapped wire with saline
23. Insert reference electrode into neck cavity
24. Make incision in dura mater on sagittal plane
25. Attach electrode array to manipulator using needle tip
26. Adjust electrode array position over brain opening at 19-degree Ros coddle angle
27. Manually insert electrode 2mm into brain targeting inferior colus
28. Attach speaker to left hollow ear bar
29. Ensure amplifier is turned on
30. Close door of recording chamber
31. Deliver white noise bursts at maximum rate of one burst every 200 milliseconds
32. Monitor activity on each electrode using motorized micro drive
33. Slowly insert electrode array until acoustically driven activity is recorded on three most distal electrodes per shank
34. Perform acoustic stimulation protocol (300 repetitions of 50ms white noise bursts at 1s repetition rate, 70dB SPL) while recording multi-unit activity at 24.4 kHz sampling rate
35. Slowly insert electrode array 200μm into inferior colliculus to reposition electrodes at consistent recording depth
36. Repeat acoustic stimulation and neural recording protocol
37. Insert electrode array in 100 micron steps while performing acoustic stimulation and neural recording protocol until all electrodes record acoustically driven activity from three positions
38. Retract electrode array in 200 micron steps while performing acoustic stimulation and neural recording protocol until initial position is achieved
39. Manually retract electrode array
40. Rinse electrode array with distilled water to remove contamination
41. Connect electrode array to potentiostat
42. Place electrode array into testing solution
43. Clamp electrode array into place
44. Connect counter and reference electrodes to potentiostat
45. Perform sequential electrochemical impedance spectroscopy and cyclic voltammetry on all electrodes
46. Remove electrode array from testing solution
47. Rinse electrode array gently with deionized water
48. Place electrode array into enzymatic cleaning solution
49. Immerse in enzymatic cleaning solution for 24 hours
50. Repeat cleaning procedures
51. Store probe in dry protective container

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Harvest virus-producing cells and culture medium
2. Obtain 25 T175 plates for purification
3. Detach cells by tapping culture dish or using long scraper (if attached)
4. Collect cells and medium
5. Wash flasks with PBS and collect wash in Falcon tubes
6. Pellet cells by centrifugation
7. Retain cell pellet and medium for adenovirus purification
8. Resuspend cell pellet in hypertonic Tris buffer
9. Pass cell homogenate through 23 gauge syringe needle
10. Centrifuge cell lysate at 9,600 x g for 12 minutes
11. Transfer supernatant to new tubes
12. Discard pellets
13. Store supernatant on ice
14. Weigh ammonium sulfate
15. Add ammonium sulfate to bottle containing collected culture medium
16. Shake bottle vigorously until cesium chloride crystals are completely dissolved
17. Incubate mixture at room temperature for 2.5 hours
18. Divide suspension of precipitate into Falcon tubes
19. Centrifuge tubes at 1,600 x g for 15 minutes
20. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
21. Resuspend pellet in 10 millimolar Tris buffer
22. Introduce suspension into previously wetted cassette using syringe
23. Dialyze samples against 10 millimolar Tris buffer overnight at 4°C
24. Pipette 3 mL of high-density cesium chloride solution
25. Gently pour 3 mL of low-density cesium chloride solution on top of first layer
26. Overlay adenoviral particle suspension on top of gradient

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Combine 10 male and 15 female adult drosophila flies in vial with rich fly food supplemented with yeast
2. Incubate vial at room temperature for 3-4 days to allow egg development into larvae
3. Use moist fine-bristle brush to roll along vial wall and collect wandering third instar larvae
4. Transfer collected larvae to glass well filled with 1X PBS
5. Wash larvae by transferring to another well filled with 1X PBS to remove food debris
6. Separate male and female larvae using forceps
7. Collect ≥10 female larvae and transfer to separate well filled with 1X PBS using forceps
8. Transfer female larvae into new vial containing fresh fly food supplemented with yeast using forceps
9. Place vial with female larvae in dark location to facilitate pupariation
10. Monitor larvae for pupariation throughout the day; circle each pre-pupa against vial and record approximate formation time
11. Melt glass tip of pester pipette
12. Melt glass tip of Pasteur pipette over Bunsen burner
13. Use forceps to pull molten tip horizontally away from pipette
14. Break off small portion of cooled pipette tip to form circular opening
15. Attach bulb to opposite end of pipette
16. Load pipette with 1X PBS solution
17. Apply small drop of water along contact zone between pupa and vial wall
18. Allow protein glue to dissolve for 1-2 minutes
19. Gently lift pupa off vial wall using moist fine brush
20. Transfer single pupa to well filled with 1X PBS
21. Grasp posterior end of pupa with one pair of forceps
22. Tear anterior portion of pupal case with another pair of forceps until pupa head is visible
23. Grip anterior tip of pupal head with forceps
24. Gently pull pupa out of pupal case
25. Separate and discard anterior half of pupa
26. Separate and discard anterior half of pupa to isolate abdominal sac
27. Grasp abdominal sac against well bottom with forceps
28. Hold thin glass pipette filled with 1X PBS
29. Aim pipette tip toward abdominal sac opening
30. Slowly pipette 1X PBS into abdomen to wash fat body cells from ovaries
31. Continue washing until ≥2/3 fat body removed or ovaries visible at sac opening
32. Transfer abdomen into glass chamber filled with fixation buffer
33. Place lid on glass chamber

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Clean 3x3 cm glass plates with soap and hot water
2. Place glass plates in beaker with ethanol
3. Place beaker in ultrasonic bath for 10 minutes
4. Dry glass plates with tissue and air blower
5. Place glass plates in UV ozone photoreactor
6. Treat glass plates in UV ozone photoreactor for 20 minutes to remove organic residues
7. Blow air on one glass plate
8. Place glass plate on spin coater
9. Deposit 0.5 milliliters of polyamide solution onto glass plate to cover entire surface
10. Spin coat alignment layer
11. Mark glass plates on non-coated side with distinctive signs to recognize homeotropic and planar alignment layers
12. Place coated glass plate on hot plate at 110°C for 10 minutes to remove majority of solvent
13. Place all coated glass plates in oven at 180°C for one hour to cure polyamide layer
14. Place coated glass plate face-down on velvet cloth
15. Apply uniform soft pressure with two fingers
16. Drag glass plate straight along velvet surface
17. Blow air on glass plate
18. Mix UV curing glue with 20μm diameter glass beads
19. Place two glue drops at adjacent corners of planar-aligned glass plate
20. Place two additional glue drops 5mm from other corners
21. Assemble homeotropic-aligned plate atop first plate with 4mm edge gap
22. UV cure assembly for 2 minutes
23. Place cell on hot plate with homeotropic side upwards
24. Set hot plate temperature to 110 degrees Celsius
25. Place LC mixture on edge of cell to melt solid and initiate capillary flow
26. Add more LC mixture at edge until cell is fully filled
27. Slowly cool cell to 90 degrees Celsius to reach nematic phase
28. Polymerize mixture under UV light at 90 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
29. Place polymerized cell on hot plate at 130 degrees Celsius
30. Allow cell to cool to room temperature
31. Place razor blade at one edge of cell
32. Push razor blade between glass plates to open cell
33. Lift film corners using razor blade
34. Gently peel film from glass plate
35. Cut a strip along the molecular director of the film
36. Clamp film sample using self-closing tweezers
37. Position clamped sample to allow 1.7 centimeters of film free to move
38. Hold sample vertically
39. Direct LED beam perpendicular to sample for self-oscillation observation
40. Observe film attached to glass substrate between crossed polarizers above diffused white light source to verify splay alignment

5. Select single mouse

7. Weigh mouse

9. Place mouse in treadmill with motor turned off

11. Remove sedentary group mice from treadmill
12. Return mice to cage or conduct post-exercise procedures per experimental design

14. Gently tap or lift mouse hindquarters 1-3 times with tongue depressor to encourage running
15. Start laboratory timer once mouse sustains 10 m/min running speed
16. Increase treadmill speed gradually by 1 meter per minute approximately every 2 minutes when mouse is at top of belt

18. Use tongue depressor to gently tap or lift rear of animal to encourage running resumption

22. Record total running time at all speeds
23. Return mouse to cage (if required by experimental design)
24. Conduct immediate post-exercise procedures (blood/tissue collection if required)
25. Wipe treadmill belt clean with germicidal towelette after testing each mouse
26. Test next mouse

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 7.625 millimoles of 1,3-propane sultone to a cooled 50 milliliter round bottom flask
2. Seal flask with rubber septum
3. Using 1 milliliter syringe, slowly add 7.625 moles of 1-methylimidazole to flask over 10 minutes at 0°C
4. Add 15 milliliters of dry toluene to reaction mixture
5. Remove ice water bath and reflux reaction mixture at 120°C for 16 hours
6. Separate zwitterion from toluene using vacuum filtration
7. Wash zwitterion with 40 milliliters of toluene
8. Set oven temperature to 80 degrees Celsius
9. Transfer zwitterion to Petri dish
10. Place Petri dish in oven to dry for four hours
11. Transfer dried zwitterion to round bottom flask and add sulfuric acid using 1,000 microliter micropipet
12. Connect round bottom flask to reflux condenser
13. Heat mixture under stirring at 110 degrees Celsius for 12 hours
14. Add 10 milligrams of para-nitroaniline indicator to 1 liter volumetric flask
15. Add distilled water to 1 liter volumetric flask
16. Manually shake blank solution thoroughly for two minutes
17. Set aside blank solution for one hour to fully mix para-nitroaniline
18. Add 1.59 millimoles of H+ ion acid catalyst to 50 milliliters of para-nitroaniline indicator solution
19. Manually shake catalyst-containing mixture thoroughly for two minutes
20. Perform UV measurement of blank and sample solutions
21. Determine maximum absorbance of para-nitroaniline
22. Add 1 g dried jute biomass and 15 ml of 72 wt% sulfuric acid to 50 ml vial
23. Stir mixture on hot plate at 30°C for 2 hours
24. Add 150 ml distilled water to 1 L round bottom flask
25. Transfer digested biomass sample to the flask
26. Wash vial with 195 ml water
27. Transfer wash water to flask containing digested biomass
28. Reflux solution for 4 hours
29. Cool round bottom flask to room temperature
30. After 12 hours, filter solution using G2 crucible to obtain insoluble lignin/ash
31. Wash insoluble solid with 150 ml hot water to remove residual acid
32. Dry lignin and ash in oven at 60°C for 16 hours
33. Transfer solid to Petri dish
34. Further dry sample in oven at 105°C for 1 hour
35. Place dried sample in desiccator to cool
36. Weigh cooled sample
37. Heat sample at 650°C for 5 hours in air for ash correction
38. Add 2 grams of oven-dried jute biomass to 160 milliliter high-pressure high-temperature batch reactor
39. Add 60 milliliters of water to reactor
40. Add 0.24 grams of acidic ionic liquid to reactor
41. Close reactor
42. Increase reactor temperature to 160 degrees Celsius
43. Set stirring speed to 200 RPM during heating phase
44. Increase stirring speed to 600 RPM after reaching 160 degrees Celsius
45. Decrease stirring speed to 200 RPM
46. Stop heating
47. Stop stirring
48. Open the reactor
49. Separate solid from reaction mixture
50. Analyze reaction mixture using HPLC

Complete step list: Dissolve succinyl glutarate modified PEG in 4 milliliters of Tris buffered saline
2. Sterilize PEG solution using 0.22 micron filter
3. Mix 500 microliters of fibrinogen stock and 250 microliters of PEG stock in culture well of six-well plate

25. Layer pegylated fibrinogen thrombin solution over ASC-CSM-collagen layers

27. Place 1 milliliter of medium in upper chamber over construct

Complete step list: Test pneumococcal isolate against negative control latex reagent
2. Test pneumococcal isolate with latex pool reagents until positive reaction is observed
3. Test isolate with groups and types present in positive pool using manufacturer's key
4. Test isolate with required factor latex reagents and interpret results using manufacturer's key
5. Label a 2 ml round-bottom microcentrifuge tube
6. Add 975 μl glycine buffered saline using 1000 μl pipette
7. Add 25 μl pneumococcal antiserum
8. Invert tube 5 times to mix
9. Dilute 120 microliters of polystyrene latex particles in 1080 microliters of sterile physiological saline
10. Invert mixture five times to mix
11. Pipette 1000 microliters of latex suspension into 1000 microliters of antiserum solution
12. Invert combined solution five times to mix
13. Transfer mixture to 37°C incubator and load onto slowly rotating wheel
14. Centrifuge particles at 1,100 × g for 15 minutes
15. Remove supernatant using 1000 microliter pipette
16. Discard supernatant
17. Resuspend pellet in 2 milliliters of sterile saline
18. Centrifuge mixture again at 1,100 × g for 15 minutes
19. Discard supernatant
20. Resuspend washed pellet in 1 milliliter of glycine buffered saline
21. Transfer suspension to 5 mL screw-cap tube
22. Add 1 mL glycine buffered saline
23. Add 2 mL glycine buffered saline with 0.2% BSA
24. Add 40 μL of 10% sodium azide preservative
25. Test reagent using positive and negative control serotype isolates
26. Store quality control-passed reagents at 4 degrees Celsius
27. Select single colony using sterile loop
28. Inoculate one third of non-selective blood agar plate
29. Streak plate to obtain single colonies
30. Incubate plate overnight at 37°C in 5% CO₂ atmosphere
31. Remove plate from incubator
32. Invert negative control latex reagent several times
33. Pipette 15 microliters of reagent onto labeled microscope slide
34. Collect bacterial culture using sterile 1 microliter loop until loop is half full
35. Smear inoculum from loop onto slide near negative control reagent drop
36. Mix inoculum into latex reagent drop using loop
37. Lift slide at both ends and gently rock back and forth
38. Continue rocking slide for one minute while preventing drop spreading
39. Read results against dark background
40. Use a new sterile loop for each reagent
41. Rock slide for one minute before reaction assessment
42. Use manufacturer's key to determine groups/types in positive pool mixture
43. Test groups/types comprising the pool with isolate until positive reaction is observed
44. Refer to manufacturer's key to identify required factor tests when group is identified
45. Refer to key to determine final serotype after factor test results are obtained

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 1 mg of fluorescently labeled compound to 100 microliters of deionized water
2. Dissolve 65 mg of PLGA in 750 microliters of dichloromethane in scintillation vial
3. Ultrasonicate mixture for 10-30 seconds at 160 watts to dissolve PLGA
4. Add entire first water phase to oil phase dropwise
5. Emulsify solution using handheld homogenizer at 20,000 rpm for 30 seconds
6. Add entire first water-oil phase dropwise into 15 ml of 1% PVA solution
7. Emulsify mixture as previously demonstrated
8. Transfer entire emulsion volume into 200 milliliter round-bottom flask
9. Remove solvent using rotary evaporator at 635 mmHg vacuum for 1 hour
10. Aliquot 1 milliliter of aqueous phase into each of 14 microcentrifuge tubes
11. Pellet microspheres by centrifugation
12. Remove 900 microliters of aqueous solution from each tube using micropipette without disturbing pellets
13. Wash microspheres with 1 milliliter de-ionized water
14. Remove 900 microliters of supernatant carefully
15. Seed 40 visually size-matched islets into one well of a 96-well tissue culture plate
16. Add 200 microliters of islet culture medium to the seeded well
17. Resuspend microspheres in pre-assay medium to achieve 10 nanograms per microliter compound concentration
18. Sonicate microspheres in ice water bath for 10 minutes at 160 watts with 10-second pulses at 4°C
19. Remove 100 microliters of islet culture medium from each well without dislodging islets
20. Add 100 microliters of microsphere-containing assay medium to each well
21. Carefully discard assay medium from all wells without disturbing islets
22. Gently wash islets with 200 microliters of PBS (two times)
23. Immerse islets in 100 microliters of trypsin EDTA solution
24. Incubate islets in trypsin EDTA for 3 minutes at room temperature
25. Perform gentle pipetting after 2 minutes of incubation
26. Transfer entire well volume into individual microcentrifuge tubes
27. Add 400 microliters of islet culture medium to each tube to stop dissociation
28. Centrifuge tubes to collect microsphere-treated islets
29. Remove supernatants gently using micropipette
30. Resuspend pellets in 200 microliters of fresh islet culture medium
31. Spin dissociated islets onto charged microscope slides using cytocentrifuge
32. Draw boundary around islets with hydrophobic marking pen
33. Fix cells with 75 microliters of 4% paraformaldehyde at room temperature for 10 minutes
34. Perform first wash with 75 microliters of PBS
35. Perform second wash with 75 microliters of PBS
36. Permeabilize cells with 75 microliters of 0.2% Triton X-100 in PBS for 10 minutes
37. Perform final wash with 75 microliters of PBS
38. Place slides cell side up in humid chamber
39. Remove PBS from slides
40. Apply 75 microliters normal donkey serum to block non-specific binding
41. Replace serum with 75 microliters primary antibody of interest
42. Incubate with primary antibody for 1 hour at room temperature
43. Aspirate primary antibody solution
44. Wash samples with 375 microliters PBS
45. Incubate islets with 75 microliters of immunofluorescent tagged secondary antibody for one hour at room temperature protected from light
46. Replace secondary antibody solution with 75 microliters of DAPI for three minutes
47. Rinse sample in deionized water for five minutes
48. Spot 100 microliters of fast-drying mounting solution containing anti-fade reagent onto glass cover slip
49. Invert microscope slide (sample side down) onto mounting solution

2. Top off volume with distilled water to 7.5 microliters
3. Add 2.5 microliters of fragmentation mix from kit

5. Centrifuge tube briefly to spin down contents

22. Open sequencer lid

24. Remove few microliters of buffer including any bubble
25. Prepare sufficient buffer to fill sensor array
26. Prepare 1 ml of freshly made thoroughly mixed priming buffer
27. Add 800 microliters of priming buffer
28. Wait 5 minutes
29. Add remaining 200 microliters of priming buffer

38. Perform pulse spin centrifugation to pool sample

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Thaw cryo vial containing tumor fragments in 37°C water bath with occasional gentle flicking
2. Mix fragments with gentle inversion and dry outside of vial
3. Spray vial with 70% ethanol and place in vial-safety hood
4. Transfer cryo vial contents to 15 milliliter conical tube
5. Add 10 milliliters of cold DMEM to conical tube
6. Invert tube to mix contents
7. Replace supernatant twice with 10 milliliter aliquots of fresh cold DMEM after fragment settling
8. Place tube on ice
9. Spray tumor region of tumor-bearing mouse with 70% ethanol
10. Use serrated forceps to pinch and lift skin surrounding tumor
11. Use scissors to make small incision
12. Separate tumor from skin while avoiding hair contamination
13. Trim remaining mouse fat pad tissue from outer surface of tumor
14. Place tumor in 15 milliliter conical tube containing 5 milliliters of cold DMEM
15. Transfer dissected tumor into sterile 10 cm Petri dish containing DMEM to prevent drying
16. Cut tumor into 1 mm³ fragments using UV-sterilized blade under aseptic conditions
17. Transfer tumor fragments to 15 ml tube containing cold DMEM on ice
18. Confirm lack of response to pedal reflex
19. Apply ointment to anesthetized recipient mouse's eyes
20. Shave abdomen around fourth mammary gland
21. Disinfect exposed skin using circular motion from center to outer edges with povidone-iodine surgical scrub
22. Remove surgical scrub using 70% isopropyl alcohol pad
23. Cover animal with sterile fenestrated surgical drape
24. Use serrated forceps to pinch and lift skin at fourth nipple
25. Make 1 cm parasagittal incision from fourth nipple toward head using scissors (blunt tips down)
26. Separate skin from peritoneum on medial incision side using cotton tip applicator
27. Gently peel mammary fat pad from skin
28. Secure skin near animal's body using sterile 27-gauge needle
29. Use serrated forceps to gently extend fat pad away from body
30. Locate inguinal lymph node at major vessel intersection and cauterize medial vessels/adjoining fat
31. Cut each cauterized vessel one at a time using micro dissecting scissors
32. Remove the section of fat pad containing the lymph node
33. Grasp the fat pad using blunt forceps
34. Insert closed tips of angled fine forceps into middle of fat pad above remaining vessel near peritenoneum using curved motion
35. Retract forceps tips and reinsert into original hole
36. Open forceps to stretch hole into small pocket
37. Remove forceps from pocket
38. Insert tumor fragment into pocket using forceps
39. Slowly open forceps tips to release tumor fragment into fat pad pocket
40. Carefully withdraw forceps from fat pad pocket
41. Collect skin edges on each side of incision with serrated forceps
42. Uncurl incision edges with claw forceps
43. Pinch incision edges together to form continuous surface
44. Hold incision sides with serrated forceps
45. Place wound clip in center of incision using applicator
46. Transfer animal to clean cage on warming surface

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Thaw matrigel aliquot on ice at 4°C
2. Prepare gel mixture with PBS sterile water, sodium hydroxide, matrigel, and rat tail type 1 collagen
3. Mix gel components on ice in specified sequence
4. Incubate final gel solution at 4°C for 1 hour
5. Place 12mm diameter 8μm cell culture inserts into 12-well plate using sterilized forceps
6. Count cells
7. Resuspend cells in serum-free media at 5×10⁶ cells/mL concentration
8. Add 100μL cell suspension to 900μL gel solution
9. Mix cell-gel solution thoroughly by pipetting gently up and down
10. Add 150μL final mixture to each insert
11. Transfer inserts to 37°C 5% CO₂ incubator for 30 minutes polymerization
12. Add 100 microliters of serum-free media on top of gel for static condition
13. Add 1,200 microliters of serum-free media under insert for static condition
14. Add 100 microliters of serum-free media under insert for flow condition
15. Add 650 microliters of serum-free media above gel for flow condition
16. Avoid creating air bubbles beneath insert during flow condition setup
17. Place plate in 37°C 5% CO₂ incubator for 24 hours
18. Add 500 microliters of 1x PBS per well into a new 24-well plate
19. Remove remaining medium from upper portion of flow transwell wells
20. Remove gel from inserts using cotton swabs by wiping membrane surface to eliminate non-VA cells
21. Wash inserts by placing them in 1x PBS-filled 24-well plate for 15 seconds
22. Remove PBS from wells
23. Add 500 microliters of 4% paraformaldehyde underneath each insert
24. Incubate inserts in paraformaldehyde for 30 minutes at room temperature to fix transmigrated cells
25. Remove PFA (paraformaldehyde fixative)
26. Rinse once with 500 microliters of 1× PBS to remove residual fixative
27. Add 500 microliters of 0.5% Triton X-100 solution beneath inserts
28. Incubate with Triton X-100 solution for 10 minutes at room temperature
29. Cut membranes out of insert using razor blade
30. Place membranes into 100 microliters of 2 μg/mL DAPI in 1× PBS solution with underside facing down
31. Wrap plate in aluminum foil
32. Place plate on shaker at 150 RPM for 30 minutes at room temperature
33. Wash membranes in 500 microliters of PBS
34. Place membranes back on shaker for 10 minutes
35. Repeat PBS wash two additional times to remove free dye
36. Place membranes on glass slides with transmigrated cells facing up
37. Add mounting solution to membranes
38. Cover membranes with cover slips
39. Count DAPI-stained nuclei and calculate number of invaded cells according to manuscript protocol

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Transfer L3 larvae using brush to borosilicate glass dish containing PBS
2. Stir larvae in PBS to detach fly food
3. Transfer larvae to well containing culture medium
4. Grasp larva across entire diameter at mid-body using forceps
5. Transfer larva to another well containing 200 microliters of fresh culture medium
6. Maintain grasp on larva without release
7. Perform transverse bisection by grinding grasped area with lateral forceps movement
8. Alternative bisection method: Slide tip of second forceps between grasping forceps tips
9. Hold larva by cuticle using forceps
10. Peel apart cuticle using second forceps
11. Repeat peeling until brain nerves are visible and mouthpart organs are accessible
12. Hold brain nerves with forceps
13. Cut connection between brain and mouthparts using second forceps
14. Hold brain by ventral nerve cord axons
15. Pluck surrounding organs by gentle pulling
16. Grasp eye imaginal disc-brain connection with forceps
17. Cut eye imaginal disc-brain connection
18. Visually inspect brain to confirm clearance of extraneous tissues and absence of damage
19. Discard brain if damage is observed
20. Pipette coating solution in and out using P20 pipette to coat tip interior
21. Aspirate brain with coated P20 pipette tip along with 3 microliters of medium
22. Transfer aspirated brain and medium to new well containing 200 microliters clean culture medium
23. Periodically replace culture medium in dissection well during repeated processing
24. Transfer dissected brains into borosilicate plate well containing 200 microliters of culture medium and fibrinogen using P20 pipette with coated tip
25. Aspirate one brain with nine microliters of culture medium and fibrinogen
26. Position pipette tip near cover glass bottom of cell culture dish
27. Pipette out contents ensuring culture medium contacts cover slip immediately after exiting tip
28. Use forceps to position brain within culture medium and fibrinogen drop
29. Push brain to one side of fibrinogen drop
30. Add 1 microliter of thrombin to opposite edge of drop using pipette tip
31. Wait 1-2 minutes for fibrinogen polymerization initiation
32. Push brain into fibrin clot while orienting ventral side toward cover slip without deformation
33. Pipette 1 microliter of thrombin solution near the side of the brain (avoiding fibrin clot)
34. Wait 2-3 minutes for secondary fibrin clot to set
35. Press edges of clot to enhance adhesion to cover slip while avoiding brain deformation
36. Adjust brain proximity to cover slip by pressing fibrin clot closer to brain (if needed)
37. Position brain in center of drop with dorsal part facing cover slip
38. Touch edge of drop opposite brain with P2 pipette tip
39. Pipette out 1 microliter of thrombin
40. Wait 2-3 minutes for fibrinogen polymerization
41. Press edges of clot to adhere to cover slip without deforming brain
42. Position pipette tip 0.5 cm above clot
43. Gently pipette 390 microliters of fibrinogen-free culture medium dropwise onto clot surface
44. Avoid adding medium to sides of clot to prevent detachment from cover slip
45. Remove 300 microliters of solution from dish to wash out excess thrombin
46. Add 300 microliters of clean culture medium to dish
47. Ensure clots remain fully immersed during medium exchange
48. Remove lid of culture dish without displacing dish
49. Place lid of 35mm dish upside down on top of dish before imaging
50. Position P1000 pipette tip close to surface of medium in culture dish
51. Gently release adequate volume of reagent solution onto medium surface without generating strong fluxes
52. Use P200 pipette to slowly pipette small amount of solution in and out five times to homogenize
53. Replace lid without displacing culture dish

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Rinse cell culture with PBS
2. Detach cells using 0.25% Trypsin and HBSS
3. Neutralize trypsin reaction with Complete RPMI Medium after 5 minutes
4. Transfer cells to conical tube for centrifugation
5. Resuspend cell pellet in fresh HBSS
6. Adjust cell concentration to 2×10⁶ cells/mL in HBSS after counting
7. Draw cell suspension into 1 mL syringe equipped with 18-gauge needle
8. Place sterile surgical drape over heating pad
9. Confirm sedation level by toe pinch
10. Remove hair from left flank of mouse
11. Apply vet ointment to eyes
12. Swab implantation site with 5% povidone iodine antiseptic
13. Make 1-1.5 cm incision on left flank using sterile scalpel without penetrating peritoneum
14. Separate dermis from peritoneum using scissors
15. Dab incision site with sterile cotton gauze to remove blood
16. Grasp mouse head with left hand
17. Support mouse flank with right hand
18. Locate spleen through peritoneum
19. Palpate mouse from underneath using one finger
20. Maintain firm hold on animal to provide tension across peritoneum and kidney
21. Have assistant deliver 0.1 milliliters of Renca cells through intact peritoneum into kidney center
22. Hold needle in place for 5-10 seconds to reduce cell backflow
23. Close incision with thin layer of cyanoacrylate tissue adhesive
24. Make midline incision with scissors starting at mid-abdomen through ribcage, neck, and salivary glands
25. Carefully remove ribs using scissors and forceps without compromising lung tissue
26. Remove connective tissue to expose trachea
27. Fill 3 ml syringe with 18-gauge needle using 10% India Ink in PBS
28. Thread silk suture under trachea using forceps
29. Tie loose suture knot around trachea
30. Insert needle into trachea and inflate lungs with 1-1.5 ml India Ink solution
31. Tighten suture knot to prevent ink backflow
32. Remove needle and excise connective tissue to extract inflated lungs
33. Fix lungs in 15 ml conical tube containing 5 ml Fekete's solution in chemical fume hood at room temperature
34. Remove de-stained lungs after 24-48 hours and carefully dissect lobes in 35×10 mm petri dish
35. Count white tumor nodules under dissecting microscope

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Transfer brains from 5-10 neonatal mice to Petri dish containing buffer A
2. Remove cerebellum, brainstem, meninges, and capillary fragments from each brain
3. Transfer cerebrals to second Petri dish containing buffer A
4. Cut cerebrums into small pieces using sterile scalpel
5. Homogenize tissue by aspirating and expelling with 10 ml pipette until no clumps appear
6. Transfer suspension to 50 ml centrifuge tube
7. Centrifuge brain tissue at 250 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
8. Discard supernatant
9. Dissolve pellet in 4.5 ml buffer A with 1.5 ml 0.75% w/v solution
10. Dissolve pellet in 4.5 milliliters of buffer A with 1.5 milliliters of 0.75% weight per volume collagenase dispase
11. Incubate tissue and collagenase solution for 45 minutes at 37°C in water bath with occasional agitation
12. Coat four wells of 12-well plate with 0.1 milligrams per milliliter collagen in 50 millimolar acetic acid
13. Allow collagen to adhere to wells at room temperature for one hour
14. Add 15 milliliters of buffer A to stop digestion
15. Thoroughly resuspend pellet in buffer A solution
16. Centrifuge at 250 × g for 10 minutes
17. Discard supernatant
18. Add 10 milliliters of sterile 25% w/v BSA in PBS to tissue
19. Centrifuge at 1000 × g for 20 minutes at 4°C
20. Carefully discard supernatant
21. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
22. Dissolve pellet in 15 milliliters of buffer A
23. Transfer solution to fresh 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
24. Centrifuge at 250 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
25. Wash collagen-coated plate twice with PBS
26. Leave two wells empty after washing
27. Add 2 milliliters of growth media to two other wells
28. Discard supernatant and resuspend cell pellet
29. Resuspend pellet in 4 milliliters of growth media containing 4 μg/mL puromycin
30. Plate 2 milliliters of cell suspension into each of two empty collagen-coated wells
31. Incubate at 37°C for 45 minutes
32. Cultivate GPE neo fibroblasts in gelatin-coated flasks containing GPE neomed medium
33. Grow GPE Neo fibroblasts for 24 hours
34. Remove 10 milliliters of media from GPE Neo Fibroblast culture
35. Add sterile polybrene solution to achieve final concentration of 8 micrograms per milliliter
36. Pass supernatant mixture through 0.45 micron filter to remove cellular fragments
37. Aspirate media from endothelial cell cultures
38. Add 2 milliliters of supernatant-polybrene mixture to wells
39. Remove supernatant-polybrene mixture from wells
40. Wash cells twice with PBS
41. Add fresh growth medium to cells
42. Passage confluent cells at 1:2 ratio onto collagen-coated plates
43. Transfer stable cell lines to collagen-coated tissue culture flasks
44. Add 3 milliliters of trypsin-EDTA to flasks
45. Incubate at 37°C for 5-15 minutes until cell layer disperses
46. Add 5 milliliters of medium to flask
47. Seed cells into new flasks at ≤1:4 split ratio
48. Harvest cells using trypsin
49. Centrifuge cell suspension at 250 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
50. Resuspend cell pellet in 6 ml of freezing medium
51. Pipette 1.5 ml of cell suspension into each of four cryo vials
52. Place cryo vials into Nalgene freezing container filled with isopropanol
53. Store cryo vials at -80°C for one day
54. Transfer cryo vials to long-term storage under liquid nitrogen vapor

2. Place one glass coverslip into well of 24-well plate

4. Remove coating solution after incubation
5. Wash coverslips with one milliliter of PBS
6. Incubate RPE-1 cells in 1 ml of 0.05% trypsin at 37°C
7. Resuspend RPE-1 cells in 6 ml of DMEM medium to inactivate trypsin
8. Centrifuge cells at 150 G at room temperature for 5 minutes
9. Resuspend cells in 1 ml of culturing medium
10. Seed 500 microliters of cell suspension onto coated coverslip

13. Remove medium containing EdU

18. Fix cells in 0.5 ml of 3.6% paraformaldehyde solution for 10 minutes at room temperature
19. Wash cells with 1 ml PBS containing 0.05% Triton X-100 to remove paraformaldehyde
20. Remove solution and wash cells twice with 1 ml blocking buffer
21. Add 1 ml blocking buffer
22. Gently rock plate for 10 minutes at room temperature
23. Remove blocking buffer
24. Add 500 microliters of Click reaction cocktail containing picolyl-azide 647
25. Wash cells two times with PBS containing 0.05% Triton X-100 for 10 minutes at room temperature
26. Incubate washed cells in 1 milliliter of blocking buffer for 30 minutes at room temperature

31. Incubate secondary antibody at room temperature for two hours with gentle rocking
32. Wash cells in PBS and blocking buffer

38. Store stained slides at four degrees Celsius

42. Select Process > Enhance Contrast
43. Set saturated pixels to 0.35%
44. Select Process > Binary > Convert to Mask
45. Select Process > Binary > Fill Holes
46. Select Analyze > Analyze Particles with size 10-infinity
47. Click Show All in ROI Manager

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place single drop of collodion into large beaker of water using glass pipette
2. Remove and replace dusty collodion film with another drop
3. Place 50-100 EM grids onto floating plastic film (shiny side down)
4. Select 400 mesh grids for routine negative stain
5. Select 200 mesh grids for collecting tilt pair images
6. Select slow-absorbency unwrinkled paper slightly larger than grid area
7. Place paper on top of grids
8. Wait until paper becomes completely wet
9. Use forceps to transfer paper-grid assembly to Petri dish
10. Allow assembly to slowly air dry in Petri dish
11. Sharpen carbon rods
12. Load carbon rods into carbon coating machine
13. Coat glass slide with vacuum grease leaving half of frosted area uncoated
14. Place paper with grids on stage under bell jar of carbon coating machine
15. Position greased glass slide next to grids
16. Pump vacuum chamber to at least 10 microtours
17. Increase current very slowly
18. Cut current at desired carbon thickness
19. Vent vacuum chamber
20. Remove coated grids
21. Soak grids in chloroform for several hours (optional plastic removal)
22. Glow-discharge carbon coated grids using a commercially available system
23. Place two 20 microliter drops of distilled water and two 25 microliter drops of fresh uranyl formate solution onto parafilm
24. Hold a glow-discharged grid carbon-coated side up using reverse force anti-capillary forceps
25. Hold glow-discharged carbon-coated grid (carbon side up) using reverse force anti-capillary forceps
26. Apply 2 microliters of protein sample to the grid
27. Wait for proteins to absorb into the grid (approximately 30 seconds)
28. Blot off protein sample using filter paper
29. Wash grid twice by briefly touching surface to drop of water and blotting dry within seconds
30. Briefly touch grid to drop of stain and quickly blot dry
31. Allow grid to contact second drop of stain for 20 seconds
32. Air dry grid on bench fume hood or preferably in vacuum
33. Check alignment of EM microscope
34. Load perfected alignment file if available
35. Load EM grid onto single tilt standard specimen holder
36. Insert specimen holder into microscope stage
37. Wait for pumping cycle to complete
38. Insert holder into microscope column
39. Search for sample area with good stain
40. Manually focus using minimum contrast method or CC/D camera with live power spectrum computation
41. Set defocus to -1.5 microns
42. Search for proteins exhibiting white contrast
43. Avoid areas with uneven stain or positive stain showing black protein contrast

Complete step list: Supplement DMEM with 5% dextran/charcoal coated FBS, 1X penicillin streptomycin, 2mM L-glutamine, and 100mM sodium pyruvate
2. Prewarm supplemented DMEM to 37°C
3. Wash HEK-293T cells in six-well tissue culture dish with PBS
4. Replace PBS with labeling media
5. Incubate cells at 37°C with 5% CO₂ for 45 minutes
6. Pipette at least 2 microliters of alkynyl fatty acid analog into bottom of 3ml conical reaction vial
7. Pipette equal volume of diluted potassium hydroxide near vial bottom edge for mixing
8. Close reaction vial lid
9. Tap vial gently to mix solutions
10. Heat reaction vial at 65°C until solution clears (≈5 min), monitoring evaporation
11. Pipette pre-warmed 20% fatty acid-free BSA
12. Pipette pre-warmed 20% fatty acid-free BSA at volume ratio of fatty acids to potassium hydroxide to fatty acid-free BSA of 1:1:50 to form 20X BSA-bound alkynyl fatty acids
13. Mix solution by pipetting up and down
14. Incubate solution for 15 minutes at 37°C
15. Label HEK-293 T-cells
16. Add 20X fatty acid BSA conjugate to starvation media
17. Adjust concentration to achieve 1% BSA with 25 µM alkynyl myristate or 100 µM alkynyl palmitate/stearate
18. Pipette 2 microliters of unlabeled fatty acid into starvation media
19. Place cells in incubator for 3-6 hours
20. Wash cells with PBS gently at room temperature
21. Harvest and lyse cells
22. Add 500 microliters EDTA-free modified RIPA buffer
23. Rotate lysates for 15 minutes at 4°C
24. Centrifuge lysates at 16,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
25. Collect supernatant
26. Transfer supernatant to 1.7 milliliter microcentrifuge tubes
27. Store supernatants at -20°C
28. Adjust protein lysates (50-100 μg) to equal volume using EDTA-free modified RIPA buffer in 1.7 ml microcentrifuge tubes
29. Add STS to samples to achieve 1% final concentration
30. Prepare master mix of click reagents
31. Add master mix to lysates
32. Mix lysates by pipetting up and down
33. Incubate lysates in dark at 37°C for 30 minutes in water bath with occasional mixing
34. Mix lysates with rabbit anti-GFP antibody
35. Incubate mixture overnight with rotation at 4°C
36. Add 15-20 μl magnetic beads pre-equilibrated with 0.1% SDS RIPA
37. React mixture end-over-end at 4°C for 3 hours
38. Wash beads with lysis buffer
39. Resuspend beads in 45 μl of 50 mM HEPES buffer with 1% SDS
40. Heat beads at 80°C for 15 minutes
41. Agitate tubes every 5 minutes during heating
42. Centrifuge tubes briefly
43. Collect supernatant while warm
44. Combine 43 μl supernatant with 7 μl click reagents master mix
45. React mixture in dark at 37°C for 30 minutes

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Prepare inoculum in 50 microliters of saline
2. Fill 1 mL syringe fitted with sterile bent gavage needle with inoculum
3. Load syringe with 100 microliter air pocket behind inoculum
4. Place anesthetized mouse on angled wooden platform hanging by incisors on wire
5. Gently restrain mouse in place with ribbon
6. Turn on laryngoscope with one hand
7. Grab blunt-tipped forceps with other hand
8. Use laryngoscope tip and forceps to gently pry open mouth
9. Pull tongue out and hold it to the side using forceps
10. Guide laryngoscope blade toward back of mouth
11. Maintain gentle 90-degree downward pressure with laryngoscope until trachea opening is visible
12. Take 1 mL syringe fitted with bent gavage needle containing inoculum
13. Insert needle into trachea until bend reaches front incisors
14. Push plunger evenly to deliver inoculum without bubbling
15. Withdraw needle from trachea immediately after delivery
16. Hold mouse upright for several seconds to facilitate lung inhalation
17. Place mouse near heating lamp for recovery until awake
18. Pin arms and legs of euthanized mouse to dissection board
19. Make midline incision on ventral side with scissors
20. Gently pull skin to expose peritoneum
21. Make incisions on peritoneum to expose abdominal organs
22. Hold tip of sternum with forceps
23. Puncture diaphragm with scissors
24. Cut diaphragm along rib cage sides with scissors parallel to dissection board
25. Cut through thoracic cavity along dorsoventral line
26. Lift ribcage on right side of heart
27. Identify lymph nodes under thymus and near tracheal blood vessel
28. Harvest lymph nodes into 1 ml digestion mix
29. Place lymph node sample on ice
30. Harvest lungs into six-well plate
31. Aspirate PBS from six-well plate kept on ice
32. Chop lung lobes into small pieces using forceps and scissors
33. Hold lymph node down with 26-gauge needle
34. Tease lymph node apart with second 26-gauge needle
35. Add 3 milliliters of digestion mix to lungs
36. Add 1 milliliter of digestion mix to lymph nodes
37. Incubate lymph nodes for 25-30 minutes at 37°C
38. Incubate lungs for 30 minutes at 37°C
39. Add EDTA to final concentration of 10 millimolar to digestion mixtures
40. Place digestion mixtures on ice
41. Pass pieces of lungs through 18-gauge needle using 3ml syringe
42. Mash lung and lymph node pieces through 70-100 micron cell strainers three times
43. Wash cell strainers with 10ml HBSS without calcium and magnesium using back end of 1ml syringe plunger
44. Centrifuge cell samples at 500 × g
45. Resuspend cell pellets in 5 milliliters of red blood cell lysis buffer
46. Incubate suspension for 3 minutes at room temperature
47. Add 10 milliliters of HBSS without calcium and magnesium to quench lysis
48. Centrifuge samples at 500 × g for 5 minutes
49. Bring lung single-cell suspension to 10 milliliters and lymph node suspension to 5 milliliters

Complete step list: Culture HeLa cells in supplemented DMEM at 37°C in 5% CO₂ atmosphere
2. Replace culture medium with 15 mL prewarmed fresh medium when cells reach 80% confluence
3. Add 15 μL of 100 mM EU to each dish to achieve 1 mM final concentration
4. Add 7.5 μL of 100 mM 4SU to experimental and no UV control sample dishes
5. Cover dishes with foil
6. Culture cells for 16 hours at 37°C in 5% CO₂ atmosphere
7. Remove culture medium from dishes
8. Wash each dish three times with 5 ml PBS per wash
9. Remove residual PBS completely from dishes
10. Place experimental and no four SU control sample dishes on ice
11. Remove lids from dishes
12. Irradiate cells with UV cross-linker at 365 nm and 2 J/cm²
13. Add 1 milliliter of prelysis buffer to each dish
14. Scrape cells using rubber cell lifter
15. Collect prelysis suspension in 15 milliliter tube
16. Adjust total volume of suspension to 6 milliliters with prelysis buffer
17. Add equal volume of R lysis buffer to suspension
18. Pass cell lysate through narrow-needle syringe repeatedly until clear and homogeneous
19. Incubate lysate at 4°C with gentle rotation for 1 hour
20. Dilute lysate in 20 volumes of dilution buffer and divide into 15 mL fractions in 15 mL ultrafiltration tubes (10 kDa cutoff)
21. Centrifuge tubes at 4000 × g and 4°C for ~15 minutes using swinging bucket rotator until concentrated to <1 mL
22. Add 14 mL dilution buffer to each concentrated lysate fraction
23. Repeat concentration process (centrifugation at 4000 × g, 4°C)
24. Combine fractions and concentrate to 6 mL volume using described centrifugation process
25. Add reaction mix to 6 mL of pre-cleared lysate
26. Mix lysate and reaction mixture thoroughly
27. Add 162.5 microliters of reducing reagent
28. Mix solution with reducing reagent thoroughly
29. Incubate mixture at room temperature on orbital shaker at 800 rpm for 2 hours
30. Quench reaction by adding 5 mM EDTA
31. Incubate quenched mixture on orbital shaker at room temperature for 5 minutes
32. Split reaction mixture between two 50 milliliter tubes containing four volumes of pre-chilled methanol
33. Incubate mixture at -30 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
34. Centrifuge at 4000 × g and 4 degrees Celsius for 15 minutes
35. Discard supernatant and add 1-2 milliliters of pre-chilled methanol to pellet
36. Pipet up and down to completely suspend pellet with no visible chunks
37. Repeat centrifugation and resuspension process twice
38. Carefully remove residual methanol without disturbing pellet
39. Transfer cleaned up and reconstituted sample to prepared streptavidin agarose beads
40. Incubate sample with beads at 4°C with gentle rotation overnight
41. Centrifuge beads at 4000 × g for 5 minutes
42. Transfer supernatant to a new tube
43. Collect 20 microliters of sample for quality control
44. Wash beads with 10 mL of wash buffer A
45. Incubate beads with wash buffer A at 12 rpm and room temperature for 10 minutes
46. Centrifuge beads at 4000 × g for 5 minutes
47. Remove supernatant
48. Repeat wash and centrifugation process once more (Steps 6-9)
49. Repeat wash process using wash buffer B
50. Repeat wash process using wash buffer C
51. Add 400 microliters of prepared biotin elution buffer to 400 microliters of washed, settled beads
52. Incubate mixture on orbital shaker at 1500 rpm and room temperature for 20 minutes
53. Incubate mixture on orbital shaker with heat block at 1500 rpm and 65°C for 10 minutes
54. Centrifuge beads at 7800 × g for 1 minute and collect eluted RNP
55. Add 400 microliters of fresh biotin elution buffer to beads
56. Repeat incubation and centrifugation process to collect second eluate
57. Combine both eluates in a single 15 milliliter tube
58. Add three volumes of dilution buffer to combined RNP eluates
59. Concentrate sample to approximately 40 microliters using 0.5 mL ultrafiltration tube
60. Add 0.5 μg/μL RNase A to sample
61. Incubate sample at 37°C for 2 hours

13. Suspend bacterial colony in 100 microliters of sterile double distilled water in microcentrifuge tube
14. Boil suspension in boiling water bath or dry bath for 10 minutes at 100°C
15. Centrifuge suspension at 10,000 g for two minutes to pellet debris
16. Transfer supernatant to fresh sterile microcentrifuge tube
17. Prepare 40 microliters of PCR reaction mixture in a PCR tube
18. Place PCR tube in thermal block
19. Run PCR program in thermal cycler
