question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Seed HEK293T cells in poly-L-lysine coated culture dish
2. Culture seeded HEK293T cells at 37°C in tissue culture incubator for one day
3. Transfect HEK293T cells with mt-Keima lentiviral DNA and packaging DNase using transfection reagent according to manufacturer's instructions
4. Remove transfection media
5. Add 8 milliliters of fresh media
6. Collect media containing viral particles
7. Remove cellular debris by centrifugation
8. Filter virus-containing media using 0.45 micrometer syringe filter
9. Plate HeLa-Parkin cells
10. Plate HeLa-Parkin cells in 60 millimeter culture dish
11. Culture cells with 4 milliliters of growth media at 37°C in incubator for one day
12. Remove growth medium
13. Add 1 milliliter of mt-Keima virus media
14. Add 2 milliliters of growth medium containing 3 microliters polybrene stock solution
15. Incubate cells for one day in CO₂ incubator
16. Remove virus-containing media
17. Wash cells twice with PBS
18. Add four milliliters of growth media
19. Treat cells with two micrograms per microliter puromycin for two days
20. Prepare fresh growth media containing 10 micromolar CCCP
21. Remove cultured media from cells
22. Add 4 milliliters of fresh CCCP-containing growth media to cells
23. Incubate cells at 37°C in CO₂ incubator for 6 hours
24. Remove growth medium
25. Wash cells once with PBS
26. Detach cells
27. Treat cells with trypsin EDTA solution
28. Collect cells by centrifugation
29. Re-suspend cells in 1 milliliter of PBS
30. Transfer cells to FACS tube
31. Place FACS tube on ice
32. Turn on flow cytometer
33. Start flow cytometry analysis software
34. Generate new experiment in software
35. Configure parameter window: select BV605 for violet laser and PE-CF594 for yellow-green laser
36. Run control sample of uninfected mt-Keima lentivirus cells
37. Draw polygon gate around live cells in forward scatter versus side scatter plot to exclude dead cells and debris
38. Run HeLa-Parkin cells infected with mt-Keima lentivirus
39. In BV605 vs side scatter dot plot, adjust voltage to distinguish mt-Keima-expressing cells from control cells
40. In PE-CF594 vs side scatter dot plot, adjust voltage to distinguish mt-Keima-expressing cells from control cells
41. Select mt-Keima-positive cell population in BV605 vs PE-CF594 dot plot using polygon gate tool
42. Create new BV605 vs PE-CF594 dot plot showing mt-Keima-gated cells
43. Draw gate around untreated mt-Keima-positive cells in new dot plot (designated as low gate)
44. Draw gate above low gate region (designated as high gate for high mitophagy activity cells)
45. Select Show Population Hierarchy menu
46. Run each sample
47. Record at least 10,000 events in mt-Keima stopping gate

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Check surgical plane of anesthesia via absence of reaction to toe pinch
2. Shave dorsal area of rat from lower back to neck
3. Position rat dorsal side up
4. Place rat on 40°C heating pad to maintain body temperature
5. Sterilize shaved area with iodine-soaked cotton balls followed by 75% alcohol wipes
6. Make skin incision over laminectomy site
7. Make skin incision over laminectomy site covering thoracic vertebrae T7 to T11
8. Cut attached muscles on both sides from T8 to T10 to expose spinous processes, laminae, and facet joints
9. Use scalpel to make incisions disconnecting T10-T11 junction
10. Dissect muscle layer away from T10-T11 junction to expose bone
11. Use scissors to clear muscle from lamina and around pedicle with small snips
12. Insert hemostatic forceps into T10-T11 gap and break pedicle
13. Repeat pedicle breaking procedure on contralateral side
14. Lift and break off T10 lamina to expose spinal cord without leaving bone fragments
15. Repeat lamina removal process for T9 and T8 vertebrae
16. Prepare to use impactor for concussion injury induction
17. Place rat dorsal side up on black non-reflective background
18. Click Measure button to enter measurement graphical user interface
19. Click Scanner Setup button to open Scanner Setup interface
20. Click Scan Size and Display options and select High Resolution mode
21. Click Image Scan option to check scanning parameters
22. Click Video and Distance option to check live video image
23. Position scanner 10-13 cm above surgical window
24. Move background with animal to center exposed spinal cord in scanning window
25. Select Auto Distance function to fine adjust scanning height
26. Apply non-reflective cover with window to expose surgical area and mark animal's direction
27. Attach probe perpendicular to stereotaxic instrument
28. Place rat on stereotaxic apparatus dorsal side up
29. Underlay rat with small Styrofoam piece to level exposed spinal cord
30. Remove excessive liquid or blood from incision using cotton pad
31. Position probe 2mm rostral to exposed spinal cord/lesion point using x- and y-axes
32. Avoid central vein during probe positioning
33. Lower probe to spinal cord surface level using z-axis
34. Position probe to touch spinal cord surface without pressure or light leakage
35. Click 'New Experiment' button in software
36. Check system configuration under General option
37. Click 'Next' after configuration check
38. Select blood flow and oxygen saturation channels in Display setup
39. Input file information
40. Click 'Next' to enter recording interface
41. Click green triangle button to start probe data recording
42. Record data for 8 minutes after signal stabilization
43. Lift probe from subject
44. Remove animal from stereotaxic apparatus
45. Place rat on its side in clean cage on heating pad, avoiding contact between surgery site and cage bottom
46. Monitor animal until anesthesia recovery to ensure no post-surgery bleeding and suture integrity

Complete step list: Put on personal protective equipment
2. Take proper precautions while making RTX stock solution
3. Dilute one 600 microliter aliquot of RTX in saline for experiment
4. Divide remaining RTX solution into 12 microliter aliquots for storage
5. Administer single dose of RTX solution intraperitoneally to test mice using micro-injection syringe
6. Inject equal volume of vehicle intraperitoneally to control group mice
7. House injected mice in home cages
8. Provide ad libitum access to food and water
9. Set metal hot plate to 52 degrees Celsius
10. Place animal into transparent plexiglass cage on hot plate
11. Start timer when animal's hind paws touch hot cage surface
12. Monitor for shaking, licking hind paws, or jumping behavior
13. Remove animal upon response behavior and record latency to nearest 0.1 second
14. Terminate trial after 25 seconds if no behavioral response occurs
15. Conduct three trials per animal with 30-minute inter-trial rest intervals
16. Place animal into transparent round cage with metal mesh floor
17. Allow animal to acclimate to cage environment
18. Apply von Frey hair filaments to plantar region of hind paw using up-down method
19. Apply middle force filament to hind paw for 5-8 seconds
20. Wait 2 minutes after stimulation, then apply next filament force based on animal's response
21. Use next lower caliber filament if hind paw withdrew
22. Use next larger filament if hind paw did not withdraw
23. Repeat stimulation process until mouse is poked six times
24. Calculate mechanical threshold using established formula
25. Perform three trials on each hind paw per testing day
26. Calculate mean mechanical threshold per animal from six thresholds
27. Euthanize subject using 5 percent isoflurane
28. Perform intracardiac perfusion
29. Remove first foot pads from two hind paws
30. Post-fix foot pads in 4 percent PFA for six hours
31. Transfer foot pads to 0.1 Molar phosphate buffer at 4 degrees Celsius
32. Soak foot pads in 30% sucrose in PBS overnight
33. Section foot pads into 30 micron slices using cryostat (vertical to plantar surface)
34. Label foot pad sections sequentially
35. Store sections in anti-freeze solution at -20 degrees Celsius
36. Select every third section for sampling
37. Transfer selected sections to coated glass slide for air drying
38. Cover each sample side with cover plate
39. Quench foot pad sections with 1% hydrogen peroxide in methanol for 30 minutes
40. Wash tissues three times with room temperature 0.5 Molar Tris buffer
41. Incubate tissues in block solution for one hour
42. Incubate sections with anti-sera against pan-axonal marker overnight at 4°C
43. Wash sections with Tris buffer three times
44. Incubate sections in biotinylated anti-rabbit IgG at room temperature for 1 hour
45. Wash sections with Tris buffer three times to remove unbound antibodies
46. Incubate tissues with avidin biotin complex at room temperature for 45 minutes
47. Apply DAB solution for 45 seconds to stain reaction product
48. Wash foot pad sections with distilled water
49. Air dry foot pad sections before mounting
50. Dissect fourth and fifth lumbar DRG tissues
51. Post-fix dissected DRG tissues in 4% PFA for 2 hours at room temperature
52. Cryoprotect DRG tissues in 30% sucrose/PBS solution overnight
53. Cut cryoprotected DRG tissues into 8-micron sections
54. Place tissue sections on slides
55. Label slides containing tissue sections
56. Store labeled sections at -80°C
57. Prepare primary antisera mixture containing ATF-3 injury marker and peripherin neuronal marker
58. Incubate sections with primary antisera mixture overnight
59. Wash sections after primary antibody incubation
60. Incubate sections with appropriate secondary antisera
61. Perform series of washes after secondary antibody incubation
62. Mount sections for quantification

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Streak cells from -80 °C glycerol stock onto YPD agar plate to isolate single colonies
2. Incubate streaked YPD agar plate at 30 °C for 2-3 days
3. Inoculate 2-3 colonies from YPD agar plate into 100 ml Erlenmeyer flask containing 10-30 ml YPGal medium
4. Incubate starter culture flask at 30 °C with 180-200 rpm shaking for 24 hours
5. Dilute starter culture into 1 L fresh YPGal medium to OD600 < 0.1
6. Cultivate diluted culture at 30 °C with 180-200 rpm shaking for ~12 hours until OD600 reaches 1-1.5
7. Isolate highly purified mitochondria as described in referenced text
8. Determine protein concentration of mitochondrial preparation using Bradford assay following manufacturer's instructions
9. Adjust protein concentration to 10 mg/ml with ice-cold SEM buffer
10. Transfer 40 μl of highly purified mitochondria into four 1.5 ml pre-chilled labeled microcentrifuge tubes
11. Add 360 μl of SEM buffer to first and second tubes
12. Add 360 μl of EM buffer to third and fourth tubes
13. Add 4 µL of freshly prepared proteinase K to second and fourth tubes
14. Mix all tubes gently and incubate on ice for 30 minutes with occasional mixing
15. Add 4 µL of 200 mM PMSF to all four tubes
16. Centrifuge tubes at 20,000 x g for 30 minutes at 4 °C
17. Collect supernatant into new pre-chilled labeled 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube without disturbing pellet
18. Resuspend pellet in 400 µl ice-cold SEM buffer
19. Precipitate collected supernatant
20. Resuspend pellet with trichloroacetic acid to 10% (w/v) final concentration
21. Incubate tubes on ice for 10 minutes
22. Centrifuge trichloroacetic acid-treated samples for 10 minutes at 12,000 x g at 4°C
23. Remove supernatant from centrifuged samples
24. Resuspend pellet in 200 µl of sample buffer
25. Check bromophenol blue color in sample buffer
26. Add 1 to 5 µl of 1 M Tris base if bromophenol blue is yellow
27. Continue adding Tris base until bromophenol blue turns blue
28. Transfer 200 µl of highly purified mitochondria into 1.5 ml pre-chilled microcentrifuge tube
29. Dilute mitochondria one-fold with ice-cold SEM buffer
30. Sonicate mitochondria 3 times for 30 seconds on ice using small-volume compatible sonicator
31. Centrifuge sample for 30 minutes at 100,000 x g at 4 °C
32. Collect supernatant in new 1.5 ml pre-chilled microcentrifuge tube
33. Label tube as 'S'
34. Prepare new 1.5 ml pre-chilled microcentrifuge tube
35. Label tube as 'S' for soluble protein fraction
36. Keep labeled 'S' tube on ice
37. Resuspend pellet in 400 µl ice-cold SEM buffer
38. Transfer 100 µl resuspended pellet to new pre-chilled microcentrifuge tube
39. Label new tube as 'SMP' for submitochondrial particles fraction
40. Keep labeled 'SMP' tube on ice
41. Dilute 300 µl of resuspended pellet one-fold with freshly prepared 200 mM sodium carbonate
42. Incubate diluted sample on ice for 30 minutes
43. Centrifuge sample for 30 minutes at 100,000 x g at 4 °C
44. Collect supernatant into new pre-chilled 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube
45. Label tube as 'CS' (carbonate supernatant fraction) and keep on ice
46. Resuspend pellet from previous step in 400 µl ice-cold SEM buffer
47. Label sample as 'CP' (carbonate precipitated fraction)
48. Precipitate samples with trichloroacetic acid to 10% (w/v) final concentration
49. Incubate tubes on ice for 10 minutes
50. Centrifuge samples for 10 minutes at 12,000 x g at 4°C
51. Remove supernatant
52. Resuspend each pellet in sample buffer
53. Add 1-5 µl of 1 M Tris base to sample buffer if yellow until blue (pH adjustment)
54. Add 1 µl of 200 mM PMSF to all tubes

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Prewarm one bottle of washing solution
2. Prewarm one bottle of collagenase solution
3. Confirm sedation by skin pinch test
4. Place anesthetized adult male rat in stainless steel pan
5. Spray 70% ethanol onto rat's abdomen and thorax
6. Make small cut in middle of abdomen with dissecting scissors
7. Peel back abdominal skin
8. Open abdomen with dissecting scissors
9. Confirm location of portal vein
10. Pass surgical thread under portal vein
11. Pass surgical thread under portal vein and loosely tighten thread
12. Clamp distal inferior vena cava and mesenteric vein with mosquito clamp to prevent blood reflux
13. Open thoracic cavity by cutting diaphragm with scissors and expose heart
14. Make small incision in portal vein with ophthalmic scissors
15. Insert 2mm plastic catheter into portal vein
16. Tighten surgical thread firmly around catheter
17. Tighten thread firmly around catheter
18. Load perfusion system with prewarmed washing solution
19. Connect perfusion system to catheter
20. Begin perfusion at 10 milliliters per minute
21. Make cut in right atrium wall with dissecting scissors
22. Continue perfusion for 10 minutes
23. Switch perfusion solution to collagenase solution
24. Perfuse with collagenase solution at 10 milliliters per minute for 10-20 minutes
25. Fill sterile beaker with 25 milliliters of collagenase solution
26. Place perfused liver into beaker
27. Mince liver into small pieces with scissors
28. Add 75 milliliters of cold MEM to cell suspension
29. Filter suspension through 100 micrometer cell strainer after gentle pipetting
30. Collect filtrate into 50 milliliter conical tubes to remove tissue fragments
31. Spin down cells at 50 G for 1 minute at 4°C (brake off)
32. Discard supernatant carefully
33. Resuspend pellet in cold MEM
34. Spin down cells again after final wash
35. Resuspend liver cells in growth medium
36. Seed cells in 5-10 T75 flasks at 6.7×10⁴ cells/cm² density
37. Place culture flasks in incubator
38. Suspend macrophages in culture medium by reciprocal shaking of culture flasks for 30 minutes
39. Plate 100mm non-tissue culture grade plastic dishes with medium from two T-75 flasks
40. Refill culture flasks with growth medium and return to incubator
41. Incubate plastic dishes for 30 minutes
42. Wash dishes three times by aspirating medium and gently rinsing with PBS
43. Add 1 ml of TrypLE Express solution to washed cells
44. Place dishes in incubator for 10 minutes
45. Add 9 ml of growth medium after incubation
46. Gently scrape attached macrophages with cell scraper
47. Transfer scraped cells into 15 ml conical tube
48. Centrifuge conical tube
49. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
50. Add 1 ml growth medium to cell pellet
51. Dissociate cell clumps into single cells by vigorous pipetting
52. Enumerate cell number using hemocytometer

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Set up control and experimental genetic crosses in egg-lay dishes
2. Keep flies in darkness for at least two days prior to timed egg collections
3. Transfer adults to new grape agar plates decorated with fresh yeast paste
4. Allow adults to lay eggs on plates for four hours
5. Incubate four-hour collection plates overnight at 25 degrees Celsius
6. Add 16 grams of fly agar and 700 milliliters of deionized water by heating
7. Prepare 17.5 milliliters Nipagen in 95% ethanol
8. Heat agar solution until almost completely dissolved
9. Add Nipagen solution to agar solution
10. Heat mixture until agar completely dissolves
11. Cool agar solution briefly
12. Load 10 cm plates with 15 ml agar solution
13. Allow agar to gel
14. Put lids on plates and dry at room temperature for hours/overnight
15. Verify agar is firm and resists crumbling when cut
16. Use 1.5 cm cork borer to make central hole in agar gel per plate
17. Fill holes neatly with fresh yeast paste
18. Bend a curve into the tip of a plastic microspatula
19. Dip microspatula tip in yeast paste to make it adhesive to larvae
20. Pick up first instar larvae individually with microspatula
21. Place larvae on agar plate close to food mound
22. Check assay plate to verify one larva is transferred per microspatula delivery
23. Label assay plate after loading larvae
24. Cover assay plate after loading larvae
25. Deliver larva using microspatula tip
26. Label assay plate
27. Cover assay plate
28. Place covered plate in darkness with lid side up at room temperature
29. Examine each plate daily until all larvae die or pupate
30. Remove and discard yeast food from central well of each plate
31. Flood each plate with tap water
32. Use soft paintbrush to rub debris off agar surface
33. Replace water and brush debris repeatedly until plate is clean
34. Rinse plate with deionized water
35. Cover plate
36. Let plate dry at room temperature overnight
37. Prepare TIFF file image of plate using black background to visualize tunnels as bright white lines
38. Quantitate tunneling in ImageJ using Oval tool to define agar gel area (excluding plastic edge of petri plate)
39. Apply automatic threshold to produce reverse image where tunnels appear as black lines
40. Use Color picker and Paintbrush tools to white out dark regions containing no tunnels
41. Select 'Set scale' under Analyze pull-down menu and click to remove scale
42. Go to 'Set measurements' window under Analyze menu and check 'Area' and 'Limit to threshold' options
43. Press Control+M keys to measure black area (representing tunneled area) in pixels
44. Uncheck 'Limit to threshold' option
45. Use Wand tool to define central hole
46. Press Control+M to measure central hole pixel value
47. Subtract 1.5cm diameter hole pixel value from measured central hole value
48. Add difference value to previously obtained tunneling pixel value
49. Use Color picker tool to prepare area limitation
50. Use Paintbrush tool to add black bar limiting quantified area

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Dissect E14.5 mouse embryos from uterus
2. Store embryos in L15 medium on ice
3. Transfer embryo to microscope
4. Isolate brain from embryo
5. Remove cephalon by cutting along medial part of cephalic vesicles
6. Remove meningeal sheath
7. Make dorso-ventral cut rostral to mesencephalic flexure
8. Make dorso-ventral cut caudal to mesencephalic flexure
9. Make rostral-caudal cut along dorsal midline using microdissection knife
10. Expose underlying ventral midbrain tissue
11. Make first rostral-caudal cut lateral and parallel to ventral midline
12. Make second rostral-caudal cut lateral and parallel to ventral midline
13. Remove dorsal midbrain tissue
14. Divide ventral midbrain tissue into explants using microdissection knife
15. Store explants in L-15 medium containing 5% FBS on ice
16. Transfer telencephalic vesicles to microscope
17. Remove thalamus by cutting between thalamus and striatum using forceps
18. Make medial-lateral cut rostral to striatum
19. Remove rostral structures including olfactory bulb
20. Repeat cutting procedure for tissue caudal to striatum
21. Position remaining tissue slice for coronal view of striatum
22. Identify striatum by its slightly more transparent tissue characteristic
23. Isolate striatum tissue
24. Cut striatum into explants using microdissection knife while avoiding darker tissue near midline containing ganglionic eminence neurons
25. Add a drop of prepared collagen to a cover slip in a four-well dish
26. Incubate at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 30 minutes to gelatinize collagen
27. Transfer dopaminergic or sal explants to gelatinized collagen using pipette with 200 microliter tip
28. Position explants in close proximity using needle, maintaining distance of approximately one explant diameter
29. Remove excess medium
30. Add 20 microliters of prepared collagen on top of explant
31. Reposition displaced explants using needle
32. Solidify collagen at room temperature for 15 minutes
33. Transfer to 37°C incubator with 5% CO₂ for 30 minutes
34. Add 400 microliters of explant medium and incubate at 37°C for 2-3 days
35. Add 4% paraformaldehyde to fix explants
36. Incubate fixed explants at room temperature for 1 hour
37. Wash fixed explants in PBS at room temperature three times (15 minutes per wash)
38. Incubate explants in blocking buffer for 2 hours
39. Incubate explants overnight with primary antibody
40. Incubate samples overnight with primary antibody in blocking buffer at 4°C
41. Wash samples in PBS five times at room temperature for 1 hour each
42. Incubate samples with secondary antibody conjugated to fluorophore in blocking buffer overnight at 4°C
43. Cover samples with aluminum foil to minimize light exposure
44. Wash samples overnight in PBS at 4°C
45. Perform several PBS washes the next day
46. Mount explants on microscope slide using prolonged anti-fade reagent mounting medium with explant side facing down
47. Place cover slip gently on mounting medium to avoid trapping air
48. Acquire digital images of explants using epi-fluorescence microscope
49. Measure length of 20 longest neurites emerging from explant in proximal and distal quadrants
50. Calculate proximal-distal ratio using average length of 20 longest neurites per explant

Complete step list: Add 0.7 milliliters of prewarmed culture media to each well of sterile 24-well plate
2. Remove tissue packaging
3. Transfer tissue culture inserts to freshly prepared 24-well plate
4. Maintain tissue culture in incubator
5. Replace media every two days during media change

8. Prewarm climatic chamber and cultivation base module to 37 degrees Celsius
9. Fill cultivation base module with 17.5 milliliters of media per row

12. Cover cultivation base module with glass lid

15. Accumulate and release mainstream cigarette smoke to in vitro exposure system
16. Expose tissues to cigarette smoke for 6-7 minutes
17. Transfer tissues back to culture plate
18. Place culture plate in incubator for 1 hour
19. Remove tissue culture plates from incubator
20. Leave plates under hood at room temperature for 30 minutes to stabilize ciliary beating
21. Observe tissues under light microscope connected to cliometrics software
22. Record ciliary beating frequency every three seconds for one minute
23. Add 200 microliters of culture media to apical side of human bronchial epithelial tissues
24. Measure tissue resistance by inserting chopstick electrode into basal media and apical media without touching tissue
25. Remove media from apical side using Pasteur pipette connected to vacuum pump
26. Return tissues to incubator
27. Prepare new plate with Lucifer and CEE substrate
28. Transfer tissue inserts into prepared plate
29. Incubate tissue inserts overnight
30. Transfer 50 microliters of culture media from each insert to 96-well opaque white luminometer plate
31. Add 50 microliters of Lucifer detection reagent to each well
32. Incubate plate at room temperature for 20 minutes
33. Measure luminescence using luminometer
34. Collect plate of 3D organotypic tissue inserts from incubator
35. Wash each insert three times with cold PBS
36. Aspirate PBS with pipette between washes
37. Aspirate PBS after last wash
38. Cover plate to prevent insert drying
39. Cut out insert membrane for each insert until membrane lies flat on blade
40. Transfer tissue from blade to homogenizing tube
41. Screw lid on homogenizing tube
42. Shake and vortex homogenizing tube
43. Screw lid onto tube
44. Shake tube
45. Vortex tube
46. Place tube on dry ice
47. Homogenize samples at 6,000 RPM for 45 seconds
48. Incubate samples at room temperature for 5 minutes
49. Add 140 microliters of chloroform to sample
50. Vortex sample for 15 seconds
51. Transfer sample from homogenizing tube to phase lock tube
52. Transfer sample from homogenizing tube to phase lock tube
53. Place phase lock tube on thermo shaker
54. Shake tube at 1400 RPM for 2 minutes at room temperature
55. Incubate tube for 2 minutes at room temperature
56. Centrifuge tube at 12,000 G for 15 minutes at 4°C
57. Transfer supernatant to new 2 ml tube
58. Perform RNA precipitation wash, suspension, and purification
59. Prepare liquid handling system and target synthesis
60. Place extracted RNA, plates, reagents, and labware in robot
61. Normalize RNA concentrations
62. Change robot deck configuration for amplification, labeling, purification, and fragmentation steps
63. Add hybridization mix to 33 microliters of fragmented and labeled RNA
64. Denature samples
65. Pre-wet array with 200 microliters of pre-hybridization mix from hybridization wash and stain kit
66. Place chip in hybridization oven at 45°C and 60 RPM for 10 minutes
67. Remove pre-hybridization buffer from chip
68. Load 200 microliters of sample onto chip
69. Incubate array for 16 hours at 45°C and 60 RPM in hybridization oven
70. Aspirate cocktail from chip
71. Aspirate cocktail from chip
72. Fill microarray with 250 microliters of wash buffer A
73. Open fluidics software
74. Run fluidics protocol from software menu bar
75. Select target module in software
76. Select Prime_450 program for each module
77. Scan chip barcode for module assignment
78. Place chips in fluidic system
79. Verify scanner warm-up status
80. Load chip into scanner auto-loader
81. Initiate microarray scan

48. Wash plate three times as previously described
49. Add 150 microliters of 1X Tris base Gold Read Buffer with surfactant (containing tripropylamine) to each well using reverse pipetting technique
50. Place 96-well plate on microplate platform of electrochemiluminescence detection system

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Anesthetize cockroaches on ice until immobile for three minutes
2. Pick up insect using thumb and index finger to access ventral side
3. Use dissecting scissors to cut tip of hind leg coxa between coxa and trochanter
4. Position 10-microliter micropipette at incision site
5. Squeeze abdomen gently while drawing up bleeding hemolymph
6. Collect hemolymph from five cockroaches into single microcentrifuge tube
7. Centrifuge pooled hemolymph for 10 seconds
8. Combine 10 microliters of hemolymph with 50 microliters of 1x insect saline buffer
9. Centrifuge diluted hemolymph for 10 minutes to separate hemocytes
10. Transfer supernatant to new tube
11. Quantify total protein concentration using microvolume UV-Vis spectrophotometer and adjust to 6 mg/μL
12. Anesthetize cockroaches on ice
13. Transfer one cockroach to dissection plate loaded with cold 1x insect saline buffer
14. Secure cockroach ventral side up using insect pins
15. Dissect abdomen using fine tweezers
16. Remove entire gut
17. Transfer gut to fresh dish containing 1x insect saline buffer
18. Combine all diluted reagent with diluted double-stranded RNA simultaneously
19. Vortex and incubate mixture immediately after combination
20. Mix 4 microliters of double-stranded RNA solution with 10 microliters of insect saline buffer (control)
21. Mix 4 microliters of double-stranded RNA solution with 10 microliters of extracted enzymes from hemolymph or midgut juice
22. Add 2 microliters of EGTA to create enzyme-inhibited control
23. Add 2 microliters of RNase-free water (alternative to EGTA addition)
24. Incubate samples at 25°C for ≥1 hour
25. Add 200 microliters of extraction reagent
26. Add 40 microliters of chloroform
27. Vortex samples
28. Centrifuge samples for 10 minutes
29. Transfer 150 microliters of supernatant to new tube
30. Add 150 microliters of isopropanol to precipitate RNA
31. Incubate samples on ice for 15 minutes
32. Centrifuge samples
33. Discard supernatant
34. Wash RNA pellets twice by adding 200 microliters of 70% ethanol to each pellet and centrifuging
35. Dry double-stranded RNA pellets using centrifugal vacuum concentrator for three minutes
36. Resuspend pellets in 10 microliters of RNase-free water
37. Check integrity of treated double-stranded RNA using 1.5% agarose gel
38. Prepare bolus with either 4 microliters of double-stranded RNA lipoplexes or 250 nanograms of naked double-stranded RNA
39. Grab cockroach by wings using flexible forceps
40. Slowly pipette RNA solution droplet close to cockroach mouthparts
41. Observe cockroach ingesting droplet
42. Provide water bottles to cockroaches after final feeding
43. Assess insects daily for mortality and remove non-moving cockroaches

Complete step list: Wash rootletin-eGFP and rootletin-mScarlet cells two times with 10 milliliters of PBS per wash
2. Dilute Violet cell dye to 500 nanomolar in PBS and Far Red cell dye to 200 nanomolar in PBS
3. Label rootletin-eGFP cells with diluted Violet cell dye and rootletin-mScarlet cells with diluted Far Red cell dye for one minute at room temperature
4. Stop labeling reactions by adding 10 milliliters of DMEM
5. Incubate reactions with DMEM for five minutes
6. Wash labeled cell cultures and unlabeled parental cell culture with 10 milliliters of fresh PBS
7. Incubate all cultures with one milliliter of prewarmed trypsin
8. Transfer detached cell solutions to individual 15 milliliter conical tubes
9. Centrifuge cell solutions (implied by tube transfer purpose)
10. Gently resuspend dye-labeled pellets in one milliliter of PBS
11. Resuspend unlabeled cell pellets in one milliliter of DMEM
12. Return all cell samples to cell culture incubator
13. Mix 500 microliters of Violet dye labeled cells with 500 microliters of Far Red dye labeled cells in a new 15 milliliter tube
14. Sediment unmixed labeled cells by centrifugation
15. Resuspend pellets in one milliliter of fresh DMEM
16. Place cells back into incubator
17. Collect mixed cells by centrifugation
18. Add 700 microliters of 50% 1450 PEG to pellet in drop-wise manner over 30 seconds
19. Add 10 milliliters of serum-free DMEM drop-wise over 30 seconds
20. Place tubes into incubator for 10 minutes
21. Sediment mixed cells by centrifugation
22. Gently resuspend pellet in one milliliter of complete medium
23. Filter all cells through a 70 micrometer strainer into individual FACS tubes
24. Create forward scatter versus side scatter plot in cytometer software
25. Create doublet discrimination plot in cytometer software
26. Run unlabeled cells to record fluorescence intensity
27. Create gates to identify fused cells
28. Run single positive Violet dye labeled cells
29. Run single positive Violet dye labeled cells to confirm no spillover into Far Red channel
30. Run single positive Far Red dye labeled cells to confirm no signal in Violet channel
31. Run fusion sample to confirm fused cells are visible with current gates
32. Align sorting stream centrally into eight-well imaging dish containing 100 microliters of growth medium
33. Sort double positive fused cells directly into dish
34. Place dish into cell culture incubator
35. Incubate dish for 2 to 16 hours
36. Replace cell culture supernatant with 200 microliters of 4% paraformaldehyde
37. Incubate in paraformaldehyde for 15 minutes at room temperature
38. Wash cells three times with 200 microliters of PBS per wash
39. Permeabilize cells in 200 microliters of 0.1% nonionic surfactant
40. Incubate in permeabilization agent for 10 minutes at room temperature
41. Block nonspecific binding with 200 microliters of 3% bovine serum albumin in PBS
42. Incubate in blocking solution for 30 minutes at room temperature
43. Label with antibodies of interest in 150 microliters of staining buffer
44. Wash cells two times with 300 microliters of PBS for five minutes per wash
45. Replace wash solution with 200 microliters of fresh PBS

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Suspend 20 mg of high surface area carbon black particles in 1 mL ultrapure water
2. Sonicate suspension at lower power for at least 15 minutes
3. Load 15 μL of ~7 mg/mL E. coli hydrogenase one solution into 50 kDa centrifugal filter unit
4. Dilute solution with 450 μL low ionic strength exchange buffer (pH near hydrogenase isoelectric point)
5. Concentrate mixture to 50 μL via centrifugation at 27,000 × g
6. Reconcentrate mixture four times to complete buffer exchange
7. Combine 5 microliters of 20 mg/mL carbon black dispersion with buffer-exchanged hydrogenase
8. Store mixture at 0 degrees Celsius overnight for hydrogenase absorption to carbon black particles
9. Centrifuge modified particles at 27,000 × g
10. Verify supernatant is nearly colorless to confirm hydrogenase absorption
11. Wash particles with 3-5 cycles of centrifugation and resuspension in fresh exchange buffer
12. Concentrate particle mixture to approximately 5 microliters for 20 mg/mL particle loading
13. Clean silicone internal reflection element by low power sonication in sulfuric acid for 15 minutes
14. Continue cleaning by sonication in nitric acid for one hour
15. Rinse element in ultrapure water
16. Dry element under stream of dry nitrogen gas
17. Apply electrical grade silicone sealant to fix IRE into baseplate of five reflection ATR accessory, keeping sealant to edges
18. Allow sealant to dry completely
19. Transfer baseplate into dry anaerobic glove box
20. Mount baseplate on ATR accessory
21. Acquire background infrared spectrum in rapid scan mode
22. Detach ATR accessory baseplate
23. Transfer baseplate to wet anaerobic glove box
24. Dropcast 1 microliter of enzyme-modified carbon black particles evenly over IRE surface without allowing particles to dry
25. Gently place carbon paper soaked in ultrapure water onto IRE surface to cover particle film without contacting silicone sealant
26. Mount custom spectroelectrochemical cell over IRE
27. Add 200 microliters of experiment buffer via solution inlet
28. Connect solution inlet and outlet to experiment buffer vial via peristaltic pump tubing
29. Transfer assembled cell to dry glove box
30. Mount cell assembly on ATR accessory
31. Connect tubing to peristaltic pump
32. Acquire absorption spectrum using previously acquired spectrum as background
33. Verify MI2 bands are strongly visible at 1,540 cm⁻¹
34. Verify hydrogenase active site peaks are detectable in 1,850-2,150 cm⁻¹ region
35. Apply reducing potential of -0.8 V vs. saturated calomel reference electrode to particle film
36. Saturate experiment buffer with anaerobic hydrogen gas
37. Initiate flow of hydrogen-saturated buffer through spectroelectrochemical cell at 12 mL/min
38. Maintain sample under flowing hydrogen-saturated buffer overnight for hydrogenase activation
39. Acquire absorption spectrum of activated sample
40. Saturate experiment buffer with anaerobic nitrogen gas
41. Flow nitrogen-saturated buffer through the cell
42. Apply oxidizing potential of 0 V vs. saturated calomel reference electrode
43. Maintain oxidizing potential for 30 minutes
44. Acquire absorption spectrum during oxidation
45. Apply reducing potential for 30 minutes
46. Acquire absorption spectrum during reduction
47. Verify complete electrochemical cycling (oxidation and reduction) of enzyme states
48. Check electrical connections of the cell
49. Acquire cyclic voltammograms using anaerobic hydrogen saturated buffer at increasing flow rates
50. Determine optimal flow rate for experiment
51. Acquire spectra at determined flow rate across range of potentials and solution conditions

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 15 milliliters of density gradient cell separation solution into each of two 50 milliliter centrifugation tubes
2. Allow density gradient solution to warm to room temperature
3. Transfer LRSC into a 50 milliliter centrifugation tube
4. Add 1x PBS to LRSC tube up to 50 milliliters
5. Mix LRSC and PBS solution
6. Slowly layer 25 milliliters of LRSC/PBS mix on top of 15 milliliters of equilibrated density gradient solution
7. Centrifuge tubes at 700 G for 25 minutes without breaks
8. Insert pipette through plasma phase without aspirating it
9. Collect PBMC layer into new 50 ml centrifugation tube
10. Add up to 50 ml of 1x PBS to PBMCs and centrifuge for 10 minutes at 300 G
11. Aspirate supernatant from cell pellet
12. Re-suspend pellet in 40 milliliters of macrophage medium
13. Count PBMCs using Malassez chamber
14. Transfer required amount of PBMCs to centrifugation tube
15. Centrifuge cells at 300 G for 10 minutes
16. Aspirate supernatant from centrifuged cells
17. Re-suspend pellet in sorting buffer (80 μl per 10 million PBMCs)
18. Add CD14 microbeads (20 μl per 10 million PBMCs)
19. Mix sample thoroughly
20. Incubate mixture at 4°C for 15 minutes under constant agitation
21. Add 1 ml of sorting buffer per 10 million PBMCs
22. Centrifuge sample according to previous protocol
23. Aspirate supernatant from centrifuged sample
24. Re-suspend pellet in 500 μl of sorting buffer per 100 million PBMCs
25. Place magnetic column in separator's magnetic field
26. Rinse column with three milliliters of sorting buffer
27. Apply cell suspension onto column
28. Collect flow-through containing unlabeled cells for subsequent staining
29. Wash column three times with three milliliters of sorting buffer
30. Place collection tube under column
31. Remove column from separator
32. Maintain monocytes and macrophages in oxygen-controlled environment using hypoxia workstation
33. Prepare media and washing buffers under hypoxia station
34. Wait for liquid to reach correct oxygen partial pressure
35. Perform cell lysis in buffer under adapted hood
36. Load protein equivalent of 300,000 cells per sample onto 4-12% bis-tris acrylamide gels
37. Control electrophoresis duration to separate protein samples into six gel bands
38. Excise protein bands using a clean scalpel
39. Dice each excised protein band
40. Place diced bands into 500 microliter tubes
41. Wash gel slices three times in 200 microliters of 25 millimolar ammonium bicarbonate at 37 degrees Celsius for 20 minutes per wash
42. Wash gel slices once in 25 millimolar ammonium bicarbonate and acetonitrile
43. Dehydrate gel pieces with 200 microliters of 100% acetonitrile for 10 minutes
44. Incubate each gel piece with 10 millimolar DTT and 25 millimolar ammonium bicarbonate for 45 minutes at 56 degrees Celsius
45. Discard DTT solution
46. Incubate gel piece with 55 millimolar iodoacetamide in 25 millimolar ammonium bicarbonate for 35 minutes in the dark at room temperature
47. Digest proteins overnight at 37°C with Trypsin/Lys-C Mix according to manufacturer's instructions
48. Add 50 microliters of 50% acetonitrile to gel pieces for 15 minutes in low-absorption tubes
49. Add 50 microliters of 5% formic acid for 15 minutes
50. Add 50 microliters of 100% acetonitrile for 15 minutes
51. Pull and dry each fraction in low-absorption tubes to limit peptide absorption and sample loss
52. Store samples at -80°C until further analysis

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 400 microliters of ice-cold methanol to 200 microliters of liquid sample
2. Incubate mixture for 30 minutes at -20°C
3. Centrifuge sample at 10,000 G for 30 minutes at 4°C
4. Transfer supernatant and collect pellet in 1.5 milliliter tubes
5. Add 600 microliters of 66% methanol water solution to pulping tubes containing 30-60 mg tissue/cell pellets
6. Homogenize soft tissue once for 20 seconds
7. Homogenize stiff tissue twice for 20 seconds with 5-minute interval between homogenizations
8. Place sample on ice
9. Transfer all lysate to new 1.5 milliliter tube
10. Incubate sample
11. Centrifuge sample at 10,000 G for 30 minutes at 4°C
12. Select supernatant in 1.5 milliliter tubes
13. Truncate caps of each tube
14. Add 500 microliters of nine molar hydrochloric acid to each sample
15. Transfer tubes into glass tubes
16. Close tubes tightly with caps ensuring proper sealing
17. Place sealed tubes into glass beaker partly filled with sand
18. Hydrolyze samples at 110 degrees Celsius for 16 hours in drying chamber
19. Cool samples and lyophilize overnight using high vacuum centrifuge
20. Dissolve completely dried pellets in 1 mL of 0.1 M hydrochloric acid
21. Add 50 μL of chloroform to each tube
22. Thoroughly dissolve pellet using 1,000 μL pipette
23. Transfer complete volume to new tube
24. Centrifuge samples for 10 minutes
25. Carefully collect upper aqueous phase of biphasic liquid in new tube
26. Select plastic tubes for manual solid phase extraction or glass tubes for robotic solid phase extraction
27. Pre-condition cartridges followed by centrifugation for one minute
28. Load sample onto SPE cartridges and centrifuge at 600 G for two minutes at room temperature
29. Wash cartridges with 1 mL water (3 repetitions)
30. Centrifuge cartridges for 1 minute after each water wash (3 repetitions)
31. Wash cartridges with 1 mL of 0.1 M hydrochloric acid (5 repetitions)
32. Centrifuge cartridges for 1 minute at 800 G and room temperature after each acid wash (5 repetitions)
33. Wash cartridges with 1 mL methanol (2 repetitions)
34. Centrifuge cartridges for 1 minute after each methanol wash (2 repetitions)
35. Add 1 mL of 3X replacement solution to elute arginine derivatives into 15 mL tube
36. Centrifuge for elution (2 repetitions of 1 minute each)
37. Place tubes for collection of eluates and glass tubes containing the upper phase of the biphasic liquid into respective robot positions
38. Use software application or method based on manual SPE protocol
39. Lyophilize samples overnight to completely dry and remove ammonia and water
40. Dissolve each sample in 500 microliters of NMR buffer until homogenous
41. Transfer equal amount of homogenous sample to each NMR tube

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place sterile disposable drape over surgical platform
2. Confirm lack of response to toe pinch in anesthetized donor mouse
3. Shave hair from entire abdominal area
4. Place mouse onto sterile surgical platform
5. Position mouse under operating microscope at 6-10X magnification
6. Sterilize exposed skin with alcohol prep pad
7. Sterilize exposed skin with alcohol prep pad
8. Secure limbs with surgical tape
9. Make 3-4 cm midline vertical skin incision from pubis to xyphoid process
10. Cauterize skin vessels
11. Extend incision laterally on both sides for better exposure
12. Move intestines to right abdominal flank using sterile cotton swab to expose spleen
13. Cauterize short gastric vein attached to spleen using sterile low-temperature cautery
14. Use sterile low temperature cautery to cauterize short gastric vein attached to spleen
15. Place sterile gauze soaked with 37°C saline
16. Position saline-soaked gauze over spleen to maintain moisture
17. Separate and mobilize portal vein from pancreatic tissue
18. Ligate branches of portal vein
19. Place suture around portal vein distal from splenic vein
20. Flip spleen to right side to expose aorta and splenic artery on celiac trunk
21. Inject 100 international units of heparin into inferior vena cava (IVC)
22. Ligate hepatic and gastric arteries in aorta proximal to celiac artery
23. Transect portal vein
24. Perfuse whole body with 10 milliliters of 4°C heparinized saline via abdominal aorta distal to celiac trunk
25. Collect spleen graft with aortic celiac splenic segment, portal vein segment, splenic vein segment, and small portion of pancreatic tissue
26. Preserve graft in 5 ml of 4°C saline solution
27. Enter recipient abdomen via midline incision
28. Remove recipient native spleen by ligating splenic vein and artery
29. Cover intestine tissues with 37°C soaked gauze
30. Move intestine carefully to left side
31. Dissect and ligate lumbar branches of infrarenal aorta and IVC
32. Cross-clamp infrarenal aorta and IVC with two 4ml microvascular clamps
33. Place 11-0 nylon suture through infrarenal aorta
34. Retract vessel to facilitate aortotomy creation
35. Create elliptical aortotomy with microscissors
36. Pierce IVC with 30-gauge needle to initiate venotomy
37. Extend venotomy opening to match donor portal vein length
38. Identify donor aortic cuff and portal vein
39. Confirm vessels are not twisted
40. Cover spleen graft with 4°C saline-soaked gauze
41. Place two 11-0 stay sutures to connect donor aortic cuff to proximal and distal apex of recipient aortotomy
42. Perform anastomosis with 2-3 continuous 11-0 nylon sutures between donor aortic cuff and anterior wall of recipient aortotomy
43. Turn spleen graft to recipient's left side
44. Anastomose donor aortic cuff to posterior wall of recipient aortotomy
45. Initiate venous anastomosis between donor portal vein and recipient IVC
46. Apply four to five bites of continuous sutures per side for venous anastomosis
47. Suture posterior wall within vessel lumen
48. Close anterior wall using same continuous suture
49. Suture posterior wall within vessel lumen
50. Close anterior wall using same suture
51. Complete anastomosis with knot placed on lower corner outside IVC
52. Release vessel clamps
53. Tamponade bleeding with sterile cotton swab until spleen color recovers
54. Close abdomen with 5-0 synthetic absorbable Vicryl suture in continuous pattern
55. Close skin layer with 5-0 nylon suture in interrupted pattern
56. Inject 250 microliters of warm saline subcutaneously into four separate locations
57. Place mouse in 30°C temperature-controlled incubator for postoperative recovery
58. Monitor mouse until sufficient consciousness is regained for transfer to home cage

Complete step list: Record weight of each mouse
2. Label each mouse tail with marking pen
3. Place mice in experimental room for ≥30 minutes acclimation
4. Set up 25x25x40 cm hanging box with rotatable mesh lid on lab bench
5. Place one mouse onto center of mesh lid
6. Turn mesh lid upside-down carefully
7. Measure fall latency of mouse from mesh lid
8. Return mouse to home cage
9. Place 9.9 by 42 cm white paper onto lab bench
10. Place dark goal box at distal end of paper
11. Place barrier boxes along both sides of runway to prevent escape
12. Add black and red ink to separate 35 mL Petri dishes
13. Place mouse at proximal end of paper facing goal box
14. Allow mouse to walk from proximal end to goal box
15. Gently scruff mouse upon reaching goal box
16. Tuck back and tail between thumb and fingers to limit hind limb movement
17. Immerse bottoms of forelimbs in red ink
18. Immerse bottoms of hind limbs in black ink
19. Place mouse back at proximal end of paper facing goal box
20. Allow mouse to walk from proximal end to goal box
21. Measure stride length three times using ruler by determining distances between same parts of paw prints
22. Draw line between consecutive same-side front footprints for front base-width measurement
23. Measure vertical line length from front footprint pad to drawn line for front base-width
24. Draw line between consecutive right hind footprints for hind base-width measurement
25. Measure vertical line length from left hind footprint pad to drawn line for hind base-width
26. Measure distance between pads of front and hind footprints for overlap
27. Use square drill to make 16 two-millimeter holes in 50-milliliter tube along scale marks
28. Make holes on backside of each scale mark
29. Cut off tube tip to create five-millimeter breathing hole
30. Make four-millimeter hole in tube cap for tail
31. Scruff mouse
32. Place mouse head-first into restraining tube with forelimbs confined
33. Pass tail through hole in cap
34. Cap the tube
35. Place mouse on bench top for two hours at room temperature
36. Remove mouse from tube after stress-loading period
37. Return mouse to home cage
38. Place mouse onto paper immediately after inking limb bottoms to prevent ink drying

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Mount clean 2 cm × 2 cm C-plane sapphire substrate on RF sputtering system sample stage with polished side facing molybdenum target
2. Pump down sample chamber to 3 × 10⁻⁶ Torr
3. Inject argon gas into system at 40 mL/min
4. Stabilize chamber pressure at 5 × 10⁻² Torr
5. Ignite plasma and set power output to 40 W
6. Reduce chamber pressure to 5 × 10⁻³ Torr
7. Manually open molybdenum target shutter
8. Deposit metal for 30 seconds while maintaining constant power output
9. Place substrate in quartz sample holder with metal film facing up
10. Position substrate at center of heating zone in calibrated tube furnace
11. Measure and place 1.5 grams of sulfur powder into alumina heating boat
12. Position sulfur boat 2 cm upstream of heating zone to achieve 120°C at 800°C substrate temperature
13. Close furnace and pump down to 5 × 10⁻³ torr
14. Flow argon gas through furnace at 130 mL/min
15. Stabilize furnace pressure at 0.7 torr
16. Ramp furnace temperature from room temperature to 800°C at 20°C/min
17. Hold furnace at 800°C until sulfur fully evaporates
18. Turn off furnace heat and cool substrate to room temperature under argon flow
19. Mount substrate in RF sputtering system with molybdenum disulfide film facing tungsten target
20. Sputter tungsten on substrate for 30 seconds using same settings as for molybdenum
21. Place tungsten-coated substrate in center of furnace heating zone
22. Place one gram of sulfur powder two centimeters upstream of heating zone
23. Sulfurize tungsten film using same parameters as for molybdenum film
24. Spin coat three drops of polymethyl methacrylate on prepared transition metal dichalcogenide film
25. Perform spin coating at 500 and 800 rotations per minute for 10 seconds each
26. Cure PMMA at 120 degrees Celsius for five minutes
27. Place PMMA-coated substrate in Petri dish filled with deionized water
28. Prepare tweezers with round, flat tips
29. Carefully peel one corner of PMMA with attached TMD film from substrate
30. Transfer substrate to 1M aqueous potassium hydroxide solution heated to 100°C
31. Peel entire PMMA TMD film from substrate using tweezers
32. Scoop PMMA TMD film from potassium hydroxide solution using clean sapphire substrate with polished side facing film
33. Transfer film to beaker of deionized water
34. Perform three sequential transfers of film to fresh deionized water to remove residual potassium hydroxide
35. Obtain 300 nm thick P-type silicon dioxide on silicon substrate patterned with titanium or gold electrodes
36. Immerse patterned substrate in deionized water and attach TMD film to substrate
37. Bake substrate with attached film at 100 degrees Celsius for 3 minutes to evaporate water
38. Cover substrate surface with PMMA to secure film attachment
39. Dry substrate in electronic drying cabinet for eight hours
40. Remove PMMA layer from substrate
41. Fabricate TMD heterostructure transistor using photolithography and reactive ion etching

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Thread 20 G needle into one end of polyethylene tubing
2. Place tubing in self-heal pouch for gas sterilization
3. Place collagen into 15 milliliter conical tube
4. Keep collagen-containing tube on ice
5. Add 128 microliters of 10x alpha MEM per milliliter of collagen to tube
6. Mix solution by repeatedly pipetting while minimizing bubble formation
7. Mix solution by repeatedly pipetting while minimizing bubble formation
8. Add 0.8 M sodium bicarbonate to neutralize collagen
9. Continue adding sodium bicarbonate until solution turns light pink (target pH ~7.4), using approximately 30-60 μL per mL of collagen
10. Store neutralized collagen on ice to prevent gelling until ready for use
11. Transfer bag containing sterilized polyethylene tubing to biological safety cabinet
12. Cut off sealed end of bag while maintaining tubing inside bag
13. Use sterilized tweezers to attach 3ml syringe to 20-gauge needle pre-inserted in tubing end
14. Insert free end of tubing into neutralized collagen in conical tube until tip reaches bottom (conical tube maintained on ice)
15. Slowly retract syringe plunger to draw collagen into polyethylene tubing (avoiding air bubble introduction)
16. Remove 20-gauge needle and place both tubing ends back into bag
17. Seal bag with tape
18. Incubate sealed assembly at 37°C for 60 minutes to gel collagen
19. Place collagen-filled tubing in sterile tray in front of cutting device
20. Load tubing into outlet of cutting device
21. Set up 50 milliliter conical tube containing 25 milliliters of endothelial cell media
22. Set cutting device to cut two millimeter lengths of tubing
23. Cut tubing using cutting device
24. Incubate cut modules in 50 milliliter conical tube at 37 degrees Celsius for 60 minutes
25. Vortex to remove collagen modules from tubing
26. Vortex 50 liter conical tube containing modules at medium speed for 10 seconds
27. Allow modules to settle to bottom of tube for 5 minutes
28. Transfer settled modules to 15 milliliter conical tube using wide mouth 10 milliliter serological pipette
29. Vortex 15 milliliter conical tube
30. Allow modules to settle after first vortex
31. Transfer settled modules from second vortex to 15 milliliter tube
32. Transfer settled modules from third vortex to 15 milliliter tube
33. Adjust media volume in tube to five milliliters
34. Remove media from cells
35. Wash cells with PBS
36. Add 3 ml of trypsin EDTA
37. Incubate at 37°C for 5 minutes
38. Resuspend cells in 7 ml of media
39. Transfer cell suspension to 15 ml tube
40. Count cells using hemocytometer
41. Pellet cells at 300 G for 5 minutes
42. Resuspend cell pellet in 5 ml endothelial cell media
43. Add 5 ml media containing endothelial cells to modules
44. Incubate modules with endothelial cells statically and dynamically
45. Mix modules with endothelial cells by inversion
46. Set tube upright at 37°C for 15 minutes
47. Invert tube again
48. Incubate at 37°C for 15 minutes
49. Rock 15 mL tube gently at 37°C for 60 minutes (dynamic incubation)
50. Transfer modules to non-tissue culture treated plate
51. Incubate overnight at 37°C
52. Transfer modules to new non-tissue culture treated plate with fresh media
53. Incubate at 37°C until endothelial cells achieve 100% surface coverage (~7 days)

Complete step list: Preheat six PDMS stamps to 70 degrees Celsius
2. Place six-well glass-bottom plate on dry bath pre-heated to 75 degrees Celsius

5. Cool setup for 5-10 minutes at room temperature followed by 20 minutes at 4 degrees Celsius
6. Gently peel off PDMS from each well
7. Place embossed plate in light curing system for 3 minutes
8. Fill UV-sterilized macro-wells with sterile PBS
9. Cover plate and wrap with Parafilm for storage at 4°C
10. Remove PBS from each well
11. Gently load 50 microliters of cells of interest onto each hydrogel array

13. Add 6-8 aliquots of 500 microliters fresh medium to macro-well rim outside hydrogel array

16. Lean pipette tip on edge of macro-well plastic bottom adjacent to hydrogel array
17. Remove all medium from around hydrogel array
18. Lean gel-loading pipette tip on edge of array tank carefully
19. Remove medium from array tank without disturbing micro-chambers and spheroids
20. Add two aliquots of 150 microliters collagen mixture at array edge using pre-frozen fine pipette tip
21. Return spheroids to incubator for 1 hour for extracellular matrix gelation
22. Add 400 microliters of pre-warmed 1% low-melting agarose on top of solidified matrix gel
23. Incubate plate covered at room temperature for five to seven minutes followed by two minutes at four degrees Celsius for agarose gelation

25. Load hydrogel micro-chamber array plate onto motorized inverted microscope stage equipped with 37°C and 5% CO₂ humidified incubator
26. Use 4x or 10x objective to pre-determine image acquisition positions across all wells to cover entire array area

Complete step list: Collect green coffee fruits (120-150 days after flowering) from plantations
2. Select approximately 300 uniformly-sized healthy fruits

6. Immerse fruits in 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution
7. Place immersed fruits on shaker at 110 rpm for 10 minutes
8. Rinse fruits three times with clean water
9. Dry fruits using sterile paper towels
10. Place fruits 55 cm from UV source in UV-enabled horizontal laminar flow station for 15-minute irradiation
11. Move fruits every five minutes during irradiation to ensure whole-surface exposure
12. Immerse newly emerged coffee berry borers in 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution
13. Agitate insects slowly with brush for 10 minutes
14. Filter insects through muslin cloth
15. Wash insects three times with sterile distilled water
16. Remove excess water using sterile paper towels
17. Prepare group of 30 green coffee fruits
18. Prepare 30 green coffee fruits per experimental unit
19. Place prepared fruits in plastic box
20. Apply test product concentrations using portable sprayer unit
21. Spray control group of green coffee fruits with water
22. Spray experimental units sequentially with at least three units per treatment
23. Release two coffee berry borer adults in sterile hood
24. Release two disinfected coffee berry borer adults per green coffee fruit in sterile hood
25. Cover boxes after 30 minutes
26. Transfer plastic boxes with infested fruits to dark incubator/room with controlled conditions
27. Count number of borer-infested fruits and living/dead insects outside fruits in each box after specified days
28. Dissect each green coffee fruit under stereo microscope at 10x magnification
29. Count number of healthy seeds and insect-damaged seeds per fruit
30. Count different coffee berry borer biological stages
31. Observe number of dead insects per seed to determine mortality
32. Release coffee berry borer adults in sterile hood
33. Release coffee berry borer adults to disinfected green coffee fruits in sterile hood
34. Allow infestation at two-to-one ratio for three hours at 21°C
35. Select 46 infested fruits with exposed borer abdomens
36. Place selected fruits in 96-well plastic rack
37. Directly spray treatment on borers perforating fruits
38. Spray three racks three times consecutively
39. Cover racks after 30 minutes
40. Dissect green coffee fruits after 20 days
41. Dissect green coffee fruits under stereo microscope at 10x magnification
42. Count number of healthy seeds and seeds damaged by insects in each fruit
43. Count different coffee berry borer biological stages in each seed
44. Count number of dead insects in each seed
45. Release green coffee fruit adults into plastic boxes
46. Count number of coffee berry borers that fly away from boxes
47. Count number of coffee berry borers that infest green coffee fruits

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Expel three 50-microliter drops of media at top, middle, and bottom of glass slide using media-filled filter syringe
2. Transfer 5-10 third instar larvae or early pupae to top media drop using dissecting probe
3. Agitate larvae/pupae gently in media with dissecting probe to remove food/debris
4. Turn single larva/pupa on its side using dissecting probe
5. Identify translucent bilateral testes in posterior third of body to confirm male
6. Discard animals lacking testes (females)
7. Transfer clean male larva/pupa to second media drop
8. Repeat transfer until 3-4 male larvae/pupae are in second media drop
9. Grasp animal at mid-region anterior to testes using forceps
10. Tear animal in half gently using second pair of forceps
11. Secure posterior end of animal on glass slide using forceps
12. Push down on cuticle adjacent to forceps using scalpel edge
13. Move scalpel toward cut end of animal to expel internal organs
14. Gently tease apart testes from surrounding organs
15. Transfer testes individually by inserting scalpel edge under fat body, lifting tissue from media, and moving to third drop
16. Use prewetted glass Pasteur pipette for transfer if insufficient fat body remains attached for scalpel lifting
17. Trim excess fat body from testes using sharp end of scalpel in third media drop, leaving small rim around edges
18. Deposit 30 microliters of Schneider's medium at center of 50mm gas-permeable culture dish using filter syringe
19. Transfer up to five prepared testes to medium drop using scalpel tool or prewetted Pasteur pipette
20. Position testes centrally on gas-permeable membrane using scalpel tool
21. Place four drops of halocarbon oil around medium drop using 1ml syringe, positioned at coverslip corners
22. Align 22mm glass coverslip corners with oil drops and lower coverslip onto media/oil
23. Place culture dish under dissecting microscope
24. Insert corner of delicate task wipe under coverslip
25. Wick away media until coverslip contacts surface of largest testis
26. Place drop of immersion oil on glass coverslip above testes
27. Turn over dish
28. Place dish on microscope stage
29. Move 40X or 60X microscope objective into oil below testes
30. Use transmitted light to locate single testis
31. Bring testis into focus
32. Place 0.5 milliliters of 8% paraformaldehyde in PBSTx into one well of a nine-well dissecting dish
33. Transfer up to 10 testes to the well using a glass Pasteur pipette
34. Ensure testes are positioned at the bottom of the well
35. Transfer testes to a new well containing 0.5 milliliters of PBSTx after 20 minutes
36. Wash testes by agitating gently for five minutes
37. Wash testes two additional times in new wells with fresh PBSTx
38. Transfer testes from dissecting dish to a 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube containing diluted primary antibody
39. Incubate tube overnight at 4°C with gentle rocking or nutation
40. Transfer testes from nine well plate to glass microscopy slide using Pasteur pipette
41. Use scalpel edge to push testes down onto slide surface if necessary
42. Wick away excess liquid from testes using corner of delicate task wipe
43. Remove as much liquid as possible from testes
44. Apply 30-50 microliter drop of microscopy mounting medium to testes
45. Place 22 millimeter coverslip onto drop of mounting medium
46. Allow coverslip to settle and mounting medium to spread under coverslip
47. Apply gentle pressure to coverslip if necessary to squeeze out excess mounting medium and allow coverslip to rest on testes

Complete step list: Dissolve 0.4 millimoles of tetrabutylammonium tetrafluoroborate and 4×10⁻³ millimoles of organic compound in 4 ml dichloromethane
2. Pipette 2 ml of working solution into 3 ml electrochemical cell (glass V vial)
3. Close electrochemical cell with gasket cap
4. Store remaining working solution for later measurements
5. Mount polishing cloth on immobile support
6. Moisten cloth with 0.05 micrometer alumina slurry
7. Polish 1mm diameter platinum disk working electrode for 30 seconds under moderate pressure
8. Rinse polished working electrode with DCM three times to remove residual alumina
9. Insert polished electrode
10. Obtain platinum wire counterelectrode
11. Ignite butane torch
12. Anneal platinum wire counterelectrode in flame until reddening
13. Anneal silver wire reference electrode in flame
14. Allow both electrodes to cool
15. Mount wire electrodes
16. Mount wire electrodes in electrochemical cell through gasket cap
17. Ensure electrodes do not touch each other
18. Connect three electrodes to potentiostat
19. Equip electrochemical cell with argon gas line
20. Bubble argon through working solution for 20 minutes
21. Close argon flow before measurements
22. Open cyclic voltammetry program in potentiostat software
23. Set initial potential to zero volts
24. Set minimum potential to negative two volts
25. Set maximal scanning potential to two volts
26. Set scanning rate to 100 millivolts per second
27. Acquire voltammogram of working solution
28. Note potential values at maxima of anodic and cathodic peaks
29. Calculate average of peak potentials
30. Add approximately 10 milligrams of ferrocene to working solution in electrochemical cell using spatula
31. Bubble argon through solution for 5 minutes to dissolve ferrocene
32. Adjust cyclic voltammetry program parameters: set minimal scanning potential to -1 V and maximal scanning potential to +1 V
33. Acquire voltammogram to record ferrocene redox trace
34. Average anodic and cathodic peak potentials of ferrocene to estimate reversible oxidation potential
35. Determine redox potential of organic compound relative to ferrocene
36. Clean electrochemical cell by filling with DCM and emptying five times
37. Place 2 ml of working solution in clean 3 ml electrochemical cell
38. Clean electrodes as previously described
39. Insert cleaned electrodes into electrochemical cell
40. Reconnect electrodes to potentiostat
41. Deaerate working solution by bubbling argon for 20 minutes
42. Open staircase EIS program in potentiostat software
43. Set potential range to 0.2 volts (0.1 volts on either side of redox potential)

2. Clean all surfaces of dissection area

4. Place euthanized mouse on gauze in dissection area
5. Spray 70% ethanol on mouse's abdomen

8. Remove bladder fundus with scissors along boundary between ureter openings
9. Transfer bladder into sterile dish containing 2 milliliters of DPBS

11. Transfer bladder to 35-millimeter dish in biosafety cabinet
12. Wash bladder with 2 milliliters of sterile DPBS

19. Add collagenase and hyaluronidase mixture to tube
20. Place tube horizontally in 37°C orbital shaker incubator at 200 RPM for 30 minutes
21. Add equal volume of 10% charcoal-stripped FBS and DPBS to digested tissue
22. Transfer mixture into tube
23. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes
24. Discard supernatant
25. Add 2 milliliters of trypsin substitute to cell pellet
26. Mix cell pellet and trypsin substitute thoroughly
27. Incubate tube at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 4 minutes
28. Pipette cells up and down 10 times with standard P1000 tip
29. Add 5 milliliters of 10% charcoal-stripped FBS and DPBS
30. Strain dissociated cells through 100-micrometer sterile cell strainer and collect suspension in 50-milliliter tube
31. Transfer cell suspension to 15-milliliter tube
32. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes
33. Resuspend pellet in 1 milliliter of complete culture medium
34. Count cells using hemocytometer
35. Plate 500,000 cells per well in 24-well plate with 0.5 milliliters fresh complete culture medium
36. Add 2 microliters of adenovirus to 24-well plate
37. Mix adenovirus with dissociated urothelial cells
38. Centrifuge plate at 300g for 30 minutes (spinoculation)
39. Incubate plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 1 hour
40. Transfer cells and medium from well to 15-mL tube
41. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes
42. Discard supernatant
43. Add 50 μL complete culture medium to cell pellet
44. Mix well to resuspend cells
45. Add 140 μL matrix extract to cell suspension
46. Gently mix to avoid air bubbles
47. Pipette 50 μL aliquots to create domes in prewarmed 6-well plate
48. Incubate plate for 5 minutes to solidify domes
49. Flip 6-well plate upside down
50. Continue solidification for 25 minutes
51. Add 2.5 milliliters of complete culture medium to each well
52. Place plate in incubator for organoid culture

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Induce anesthesia with isoflurane
2. Shave the scalp
3. Wipe down skin with Betadine three times
4. Coat animal's eyes with sterile petroleum jelly
5. Administer postoperative analgesia
6. Make 1 cm midline sagittal incision into scalp
7. Expose skull landmarks through incision
8. Prepare tail beginning 1 cm from base
9. Shave 1 cm along ventral surface of tail starting 1 cm from base
10. Wipe shaved tail region with Betadine three times
11. Secure mouse in stereotaxic frame
12. Maintain mouse temperature at 37°C using thermistor-controlled heating pad
13. Monitor temperature using rectal thermometer
14. Attach sterile 27-gauge needle to 1cc syringe on stereotaxic arm
15. Position needle at 2.5mm lateral to BMA on skull surface
16. Rotate syringe to initiate bur hole on skull surface
17. Apply gentle downward force using frame
18. Remove needle from stereotaxic arm after hole initiation
19. Complete bur hole manually until inner table perforation sensation
20. Place paraffin wax paper under the tail
21. Make transverse incision along shaved tail surface
22. Collect two to three large drops of arterial blood on wax paper
23. Apply pressure with sterile gauze to stop bleeding
24. Draw 17 microliters of blood sample into Hamilton syringe
25. Attach syringe to pump
26. Secure syringe to stereotaxic arm on frame
27. Angle stereotaxic arm to 5 degrees medially relative to vertical axis
28. Position needle tip over bur hole
29. Lower needle 3.5 millimeters into brain
30. Wait 2 minutes
31. Withdraw needle 0.5 millimeters to final depth of 3 millimeters
32. Wait two minutes
33. Withdraw needle half a millimeter to position tip at three millimeters depth
34. Wait five minutes for brain re-expansion around needle
35. Inject 7.5 microliters of blood at rate of one microliter per minute
36. Wait five minutes after injection
37. Inject 7.5 microliters of blood at a rate of one microliter per minute
38. Leave needle in place for 25 minutes to allow blood clotting
39. Slowly withdraw needle after clotting period
40. Rinse needle with hot water to prevent clotting
41. Remove mouse from frame
42. Close tail and scalp incisions with veterinary surgical glue
43. Turn off anesthesia
44. Place wet food pellets on cage bottom for easier access
45. Place mouse in warmed recovery chamber with food and water access
46. Return mouse to home cage when ready

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Transfer all needed materials into glove box inside HEPA-filter equipped fume cupboard
2. Weigh desired quantity of nanomaterials into bottle and add water to achieve 1 g/L nanoparticle concentration
3. Seal bottle with lid
4. Ultrasonicate at 43 kHz and 60 watts for 30 minutes to disperse CNTs and GNPs
5. Pour nanoparticles suspension into reactor flask containing magnetic stir-bar
6. Insert ozone bubbler and connect it to ozone generator
7. Place reactor flask into refrigerated bath
8. Stir solution to prevent CNTs from settling
9. Turn on cooling water recirculation unit to cool ozone reactor and ultrasonic cell
10. Turn on oxygen supply to ozone generator
11. Adjust oxygen flow rate to 0.5 liters per minute
12. Turn on ozone generator and allow it to run for 30-60 minutes
13. Turn on peristaltic pump
14. Adjust peristaltic pump to 0.67 Hz
15. Turn on ultrasonic horn
16. Adjust ultrasonic horn power to 60 watts
17. Observe sonication process for at least 30 minutes to ensure stable operation
18. Turn off ozone generator, sonicator, and pump after processing time
19. Stir carbon nanomaterial dispersion for one hour to decompose ozone and solution
20. Sonicate nanomaterial suspension if electrophoresis is not performed within 24 hours of ultrasonicated ozonolysis processing
21. Abrade electrodes using P1000 aluminum oxide sandpaper
22. Clean abraded electrodes in ultrasonic bath for 10 minutes in ultra pure water
23. Dry electrodes in clean oven for 10 minutes at 100 degrees celsius
24. Transfer electrodes to desiccator to cool
25. Weigh cooled electrodes using four-figure analytical balance for anotic deposition
26. Transfer 35 milliliters of carbon nanomaterial dispersion to a 50 milliliter beaker
27. Secure electrode fixture to retort stand
28. Slowly lower electrodes to bottom of beaker
29. Attach positive terminal of DC power supply to central anode
30. Attach negative terminal to one outer cathode
31. Connect two outer cathodes using lid with alligator clips
32. With power supply off, adjust current to maximum setting
33. Adjust voltage to required value for study
34. Prepare stopwatch
35. Turn on power supply for required coating duration
36. Switch off the power supply
37. Slowly raise electrode fixture out of dispersion while ensuring film is not disturbed
38. Disconnect terminals from electrodes
39. Rotate electrode fixture to horizontal orientation
40. Allow film on anode to dry evenly
41. Disassemble fixture
42. Dry anode in oven at 100°C for one hour
43. Place dried anode in desiccator to cool
44. Record anode weight to four decimal places using analytical scale
45. Photograph coded anode

Complete step list: Set up drill press with fence and stop using short dowel to secure tubular seedling container
2. Drill two vertical holes approximately 1 cm apart in container side
3. Cut plastic between drilled holes to create elongated opening (2 cm wide × 5 cm long)
4. Repeat elongated opening cutting process on opposite container side
5. Cut 40 micrometer pore nylon mesh into 9.5 cm × 8.5 cm pieces (one per container)
6. Fasten nylon mesh externally onto containers using industrial hot glue to cover both openings with fabric overlap
7. Apply hot glue around container openings and along long edges of nylon mesh
8. Roll container onto nylon mesh
9. Add hot glue along overlapping mesh edges
10. Press glued mesh edge onto cardboard to seal
11. Attach top and bottom fabric ends to container with flexible tape to prevent loose edges
12. Cover small holes on conical container ends with tape to prevent root growth
13. Place glass marbles in bottom of containers for drainage
14. Assemble pots according to manuscript specifications
15. Use solid unmodified containers for control treatment
16. Add AM fungus inoculum by uniformly mixing 1.2 cm chopped root pieces with soil
17. Prepare soil mixture by blending desired soil with infertile silica sand or glass beads
18. Position filled containers in previously-constructed drilled foam within pots
19. Mix medium particle size sand with small particle size sand in cement mixer to create nutrient-poor silica sand mixture
20. Fill interstitial space between containers with sand mixture using funnel
21. Transplant nurse plants of desired species into each container to sustain AM fungi
22. Grow plants at 24°C in greenhouse for 2-3 months to establish common mycorrhizal networks
23. Sow experimental plant seeds into containers
24. Remove pretreatment nurse plants by clipping shoots after all containers show germinated seedlings
25. Leave designated containers unmoved for intact CMN treatment
26. Rotate designated containers weekly through one full rotation to sever hyphae
27. Heavily water all pots to reestablish substrate-container contact after rotation
28. Fertilize neighboring plants with 0.5% nitrogen-15 enriched potassium nitrate and ammonium chloride
29. Fertilize target individual with nitrogen-14 fertilizer of equal concentration
30. Re-randomize positions of pots monthly during experiment
31. Clip all aboveground tissue from plants
32. Place individual plants into envelopes labeled with treatment, pot, and position
33. Dry above-ground tissues at 60°C in forced air drying oven to constant weight
34. Measure dry weight of each plant tissue

36. Gently brush soil from root systems
37. Wash roots on 250 micron pore size sieve
38. Weigh whole air-dried root system
39. Clip root system randomly to obtain fragments
