question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Grow HTT11619 cells in 25 ml of prepared medium in T-175 flask
2. Aspirate culture medium from T-175 flask
3. Wash cells with PBS without calcium and magnesium
4. Aspirate PBS from T-175 flask
5. Add trypsin drop-wise to T-175 flask using 5 ml pipette
6. Incubate flask to allow cell detachment
7. Tap flask to dislodge cells
8. Quench cells with 8 ml complete medium over flask surface
9. Pipette mixture up and down to break cell clumps
10. Transfer cell mixture to 15 ml conical tube
11. Take 10 microliters from 15 milliliter conical tube
12. Combine sample with 10 microliters of trypan blue
13. Transfer 10 microliters of cell-trypan blue mixture to hemocytometer
14. Count cells in four outer grids of hemocytometer
15. Add 5 milliliters of complete medium and 6 micromolar aphidicolin to each 10-centimeter plate of cells
16. Transfer 100 microliters of re-suspended cell pellet to each centimeter plate
17. Swirl plate gently to mix
18. Incubate plates to synchronize cells at G1/S border
19. Mix RNA components to 45 micromolar concentration in equal molar concentrations
20. Add 6.75 microliters of 200 micromolar CR RNA stock to 1.5 mL centrifuge tube
21. Add 6.75 microliters of 200 micromolar tracer RNA stock to centrifuge tube
22. Add 16.50 microliters of IDT buffer to centrifuge tube
23. Heat mixture at 95°C for 5 minutes in PCR machine
24. Cool samples to room temperature
25. Dilute 2.22 microliters of CR RNA complex and 2.78 microliters of IDT buffer to final volume of 5 microliters
26. Dilute 1.67 microliters of Cas9 protein (60 micromolar stock) in 3.33 microliters of low sera medium to final volume of 5 microliters
27. Mix 5 microliters of Cas9 protein with 5 microliters of complex RNA
28. Aspirate medium from plate
29. Wash plate with 5 ml of PBS
30. Aspirate PBS from plate
31. Add 1 ml pre-warmed trypsin to each 10 cm plate
32. Place plates in incubator
33. Tap 10 cm plate to dislodge cells
34. Quench cells with 4 ml complete medium
35. Pipette mixture up/down to break cell clumps
36. Transfer cells to 15 ml conical tube
37. Transfer 100 microliters of cell suspension to each 4mm gap electroporation cuvette
38. Add 10 microliters of RNP complex to each cuvette containing cells
39. Add 2 micromolar ODN to each sample
40. Transport sample rack to electroporation machine
41. Flick each sample gently to mix
42. Place samples in electroporation chamber
43. Return rack to biosafety hood
44. Transfer each electroporated sample to well containing 2ml complete medium in 6-well plate
45. Incubate plate
46. Electroporate synchronized HTT11619 cells (5 × 10⁵ cells/100μL) with RNP complex (100 pmol) and 72 NTODN (2.0 μM)
47. Transfer electroporated cells to six-well plate
48. Allow cells to recover for 72 hours
49. Sort individual cells into 96-well plates using FACS with 488 nm laser (EGFP+/- selection)
50. Isolate cellular gDNA from wells showing growth using commercial DNA isolation kit
51. Amplify target base regions via PCR
52. Perform DNA sequencing analysis on PCR-amplified samples

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Prepare two clean 200-milliliter glass bottles
2. Add 0.6 grams of sodium chloride to one bottle
3. Add 0.5 grams of peptone to the bottle
4. Add 3.4 grams of agar to the bottle
5. Add 195 milliliters of distilled water to the bottle
6. Add magnetic stirrer to the bottle containing ingredients
7. Add magnetic stirrer to the other empty bottle
8. Autoclave bottles for 15 minutes at 121 degrees Celsius
9. Cool bottles in water bath for 30 minutes at 55 degrees Celsius
10. Add 0.2 milliliters of 1M calcium chloride to filled bottle
11. Add 0.2 milliliters of 5 mg/mL cholesterol to bottle
12. Add 0.2 milliliters of 1M magnesium sulfate to bottle
13. Add 5 milliliters of 1M potassium phosphate to bottle
14. Mix solution by magnetic stirring on hot plate at 55°C
15. Pour half of mixed NGM medium into empty 200-milliliter bottle
16. Add one milliliter of DMSO to one bottle
17. Mix DMSO-containing medium using magnetic stirring
18. Store other bottle in water bath at 55 degrees Celsius
19. Aliquot three milliliters of DMSO-containing medium to 35-by-10-millimeter Petri dishes
20. Remove bottle from water bath
21. Mix bottle contents using magnetic stirring
22. Add 1 milliliter of 75-millimolar etoposide
23. Repeat aliquoting procedure after mixing
24. Cool chemical-containing NGM plates at room temperature for approximately three hours and store at 4°C
25. Spread 100 microliters of heat-inactivated E. coli OP50 on each NGM plate
26. Allow plates to dry overnight in C. elegans incubator at 20 degrees Celsius
27. Transfer age-synchronized C. elegans eggs to NGM plates supplemented with 1% DMSO or 750-micromolar etoposide
28. Place C. elegans plates in incubator at 20 degrees Celsius for four days
29. Observe and take daily microscopic images of C. elegans under stereo microscope
30. Take microscopic image of microscope stage micrometer for worm size calibration
31. Open microscope stage micrometer image in ImageJ
32. Drag 1,000 micrometer line using Straight Line tool on micrometer image
33. Click Analyze menu and select Set Scale
34. Input 1,000 in Known Distance box and 'micrometers' in Unit of Length box
35. Check Global option and click OK
36. Open worm images using File > Open
37. Draw line along each worm using Freehand Line tool
38. Click Analyze > Measure to obtain body length data
39. Repeat measurement steps for 50 worms
40. Incubate age-synchronized eggs on NGM plate containing DMSO or etoposide at 20°C for 64 hours
41. Transfer five adult worms to new NGM plates without chemicals or with same chemical pretreatment
42. Incubate transferred adult worms for 24 hours
43. Transfer adults to new NGM plates
44. Count number of eggs daily and record worms that crawled off, died, or were internally hatched
45. Perform calculations according to text protocol

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Place Nematostella vectensis anemones in temperature- and light-controlled incubator
2. Program incubator for 8 hours of light at 25°C
3. Remove animals from incubator
4. Transfer animals to bench top at room temperature with light on for spawning
5. Use modified transfer pipette to place egg packages from female container into sperm-containing male container
6. Leave egg packages in male container for at least 15 minutes to allow fertilization
7. Place fertilized egg packages in seawater containing 3% cysteine in glass Petri dish
8. Gently agitate dish on shaker for 12 minutes
9. Break up clumps with plastic pipette
10. Continue agitation for 2-3 minutes until completely dejellied
11. Store fertilized eggs in dish at 16°C or room temperature
12. Prepare DNA extraction buffer according to manuscript
13. Vortex DNA extraction buffer to mix well
14. Aliquot 20 microliters of DNA extraction buffer into each PCR tube
15. Dissolve 1% agarose in seawater
16. Pour dissolved agarose into Petri dish to cover bottom
17. Cool agarose on bench top to solidify gel bed
18. Cover solidified gel bed with fresh seawater
19. Transfer 24-hour post-fertilization embryos using pipette into agarose gel bed-containing Petri dish
20. Insert tungsten needle into needle holder
21. Sterilize needle tip by dipping in alcohol
22. Place needle in flame to burn off alcohol
23. Make depression in agarose using tungsten needle under dissecting microscope
24. Remove piece of surface agarose approximately embryo-sized
25. Place embryo into depression using tungsten needle
26. Position embryo with lateral side facing down to restrict movement
27. Surgically remove piece of aboral tissue along oral-aboral axis using tungsten needle
28. Transfer isolated aboral tissue to PCR tube containing 20 microliters of DNA extraction buffer using P20 pipette
29. Transfer post-surgery embryo into well of 24-well plate containing at least 500 microliters of fresh seawater
30. Place plate containing post-surgery embryos in incubator at 16 degrees Celsius
31. Centrifuge PCR tubes containing DNA extraction buffer and embryonic tissues using mini centrifuge
32. Incubate tubes at 55°C for three hours with periodic vortexing (30 seconds every 30 minutes)
33. Inactivate proteinase K by heating at 95°C for four minutes
34. Set up PCR reaction using extracted genomic DNA
35. Set up PCR reaction using extracted genomic DNA as template to amplify locus of interest
36. Set up 20 microliter PCR reaction
37. Run agarose gel electrophoresis to determine size and presence/absence of PCR products
38. Adjust conditions of agarose gel electrophoresis based on expected PCR product size
39. Assign genotype to each post-surgical embryo based on PCR product size and presence/absence
40. Sort embryos according to assigned genotype
41. Perform control PCR using reverse primer binding to wild type sequence

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Remove mouse heart under anesthesia and cannulation
2. Perfuse heart
3. Digest heart with collagenase
4. Dissociate cells
5. Reintroduce calcium
6. Seed cells
7. Culture primary cardiomyocytes
8. Prepare fresh digestion buffer containing type II collagenase
9. Warm digestion buffer to 37°C
10. Soak scissors and forceps in 70% ethanol
11. Set sterilized instruments to dry
12. Weigh mouse to nearest 0.1 gram
13. Record body weight, strain, sex, and date of birth
14. Inject 200 microliters Heparin intraperitoneally
15. Anesthetize mouse after 10-minute wait
16. Check non-responsiveness to tail/toe pinch after 5-10 minutes
17. Secure mouse in supine position by fixing forepaws and hindpaws to work surface
18. Wipe chest and abdomen with 70% ethanol
19. Make midline skin incision from mid abdomen to diaphragm
20. Hold sternum
21. Cut sternum bilaterally
22. Cut diaphragm
23. Retroflect thoracic cage to expose heart
24. Lift heart slightly
25. Dissect heart out of thoracic cavity close to dorsal thoracic wall
26. Transfer heart to cold profusion buffer in 100 millimeter dish
27. Trim away connective tissues (lungs, thymus, bronchi, esophagus)
28. Identify aorta and cranial branches
29. Cut aorta below first branch
30. Grasp aortic wall and lift heart
31. Check perfusion fluid dripping
32. Slip heart onto perfusion fluid-filled aortic cannula with cannula tip above aortic valve
33. Clamp aorta to cannula and ligate with 6-0 surgical silk
34. Perfuse heart with calcium-free perfusion buffer
35. Perfuse heart with calcium-free perfusion buffer at 4 mL/min flow rate
36. Continue perfusion until effluents become clear (4-5 minutes)
37. Switch to digestion buffer containing 50 μM calcium chloride
38. Increase afterload pressure to 70-80 mmHg during digestion (if applicable)
39. Monitor heart until it becomes pale, flaccid, and spongy when pinched
40. Stop digestion when optimal tissue consistency is achieved
41. Transfer heart to 60 mm dish containing 2.5 mL digestion buffer using 70% ethanol-soaked forceps
42. Move to cell culture hood and employ sterile techniques
43. Remove aorta, atria, and great vessels using fine surgical scissors
44. Tease ventricle into 10-12 small pieces
45. Transfer heart pieces and cells to 15 mL conical tube using 10 mL transfer pipette
46. Rinse dish with 7.5 mL calcium solution I and transfer wash to conical tube
47. Disperse heart tissue pieces by gentle pipetting with fine tip transfer pipette
48. Centrifuge for 3 minutes at 20 G to separate non-myocyte cells
49. Aspirate supernatant
50. Resuspend the myocyte pellet in 10 milliliters of calcium solution supplemented with ATP
51. Equilibrate for three to five minutes
52. Transfer duplicate 10 microliter aliquots to a hemocytometer
53. Count the rod-shaped and round myocytes
54. Calculate total number of myocytes and determine percentage of rod-shaped myocytes
55. Pellet myocytes by centrifugation
56. Remove supernatants
57. Wash pellet successively
58. Remove supernatants from pellet
59. Wash pellet successively in 10 milliliters of increasing calcium concentrations
60. Resuspend final myocyte pellet in five milliliters of plating medium
61. Count total number of myocytes
62. Calculate percentage of rod-shaped myocytes
63. Adjust concentration of rod-shaped myocytes
64. Adjust concentration of rod-shaped myocytes to 25,000 per milliliter
65. Gently pipette myocytes to ensure equal cell density for each dish or plate
66. Aspirate off laminin coating solution from culture plates
67. Seed cardiomyocytes
68. Evenly disperse cells by gently sliding dishes forward and backward and side to side in cross-like pattern on culture hood surface
69. Place cultures in 2% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees Celsius
70. Gently aspirate off medium containing unattached myocytes and cell debris
71. Replenish culture medium by aiming at sides of dishes one plate at a time
72. Return cells to culture incubator

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Pre-coat six-well plates with 0.5% gelatin
2. Incubate plates in humidified chamber at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for two hours
3. Transfer aseptically removed canine blood vessels to Petri dishes filled with ice-cold HBSS
4. Remove adherent tissue and fat from vessel exterior using surgical scissors while maintaining vessel integrity and visibility
5. Close vessel branches using ligatures
6. Remove closed branches using surgical scissors or scalpel
7. Insert curved Halsted mosquito forceps into one vessel end
8. Clamp forceps tip at opposite vessel end
9. Retract forceps slowly to invert vessel
10. Complete inversion until vessel is completely inside out with endothelial layer exposed
11. Place purse string sutures at both ends of vessel to close completely
12. Use ligature ends to manipulate inverted vessel
13. Transfer inverted vessel to 50 milliliter tube
14. Rinse vessel twice with HBSS to remove erythrocytes or residual freezing medium
15. Add 30 milliliters of collagenase type two and dispase in canine endothelial cell growth medium
16. Incubate tube at 37 degrees Celsius with gentle agitation for vessel digestion
17. Remove vessel from tube
18. Centrifuge cell suspension at 250 × g for 5 minutes
19. Resuspend cell pellet in CECGM
20. Seed 2 ml of suspension per well in 1-3 gelatin-coated wells
21. Culture cells at 37°C in humidified 5% CO₂ atmosphere
22. Change culture medium twice weekly
23. Wash cells with pre-warmed HBSS to remove dead/non-attached cells
24. Add 200 microliters of recombinant cell dissociation enzyme to each well
25. Incubate dish for 5 minutes or until cell detachment
26. Transfer cells to 15 milliliter tube
27. Add 10 milliliters of CECGM with 10% FCS to stop trypsinization
28. Centrifuge at 250×g for 5 minutes
29. Transfer cells to gelatin pre-coated plate or flask for continued culture
30. Culture cells in CECGM on 0.5% gelatin pre-coated T75 flask
31. Centrifuge cell suspension at 250 × g for 5 minutes
32. Discard supernatant
33. Resuspend cells in 1 ml culture medium
34. Dilute 10 μl cell suspension 1:1 with 0.4% Trypan Blue
35. Count cells using automated cell counter
36. Plate 4 × 10⁵ viable cells in new T75 flask
37. Add 10 milliliters of pre-warmed CECGM to flask
38. Culture flask at 37°C in humidified atmosphere with 5% CO₂
39. Collect ≥1×10³ cells as pellet for RNA isolation
40. Add 20 microliters of sample preparation reagent to pellet
41. Incubate mixture for 1 minute to lyse cells
42. Store lysed sample at -70°C

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Wash worms from growth plates into 15 ml conical tube using M9 buffer
2. Pellet worms by centrifugation
3. Remove supernatant after centrifugation
4. Wash pelleted worms 3-5 times with fresh M9 buffer
5. Perform final wash with double distilled water when supernatant is clear
6. Transfer loose worm pellet to 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube
7. Add equal volume of ice-cold 2X lysis buffer to 300 microliter worm pellet
8. Vigorously mix the suspension
9. Spin down sample to collect mixture at tube bottom
10. Transfer mixture to 1.5 milliliter RNAse-free tube containing metal beads
11. Cap tube tightly
12. Place sample in bead mill homogenizer at 4°C
13. Ensure sample is balanced inside homogenizer
14. Homogenize sample at highest speed for 4 minutes
15. Transfer homogenized sample to new 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube without beads
16. Centrifuge sample to clarify protein extract
17. Transfer supernatant to new 1.5 ml tube on ice while avoiding white cloudy precipitate
18. Set aside 10 microliters of clarified extract for protein quantification
19. Dilute sample to 5-10 mg/ml protein concentration using ice-cold 1X lysis buffer on ice
20. Resuspend magnetic beads by inversion
21. Transfer 150 microliters of 50X bead suspension to 1.5 milliliter tube
22. Magnetize beads on ice against magnetic stand for one minute
23. Discard supernatant when solution is clear
24. Remove tube from magnetic stand
25. Wash beads with three 300 microliter volumes of 1X lysis buffer
26. Resuspend beads in 150 microliters of ice cold lysis buffer
27. Transfer 75 microliters of bead slurry to two milligrams of protein extract sample
28. Incubate at four degrees Celsius with gentle agitation for one hour
29. Place sample tube in magnet on ice for one minute
30. Transfer supernatant to new 1.5 milliliter tube without disturbing beads
31. Set aside 10% of sample for Western blot analysis
32. Add 20 micrograms affinity purified antibody to pre-cleared lysate
33. Incubate antibody-lysate mixture for one hour at 4°C with gentle agitation
34. Add 75 microliters pre-washed bead suspension to antibody-lysate mixture
35. Incubate bead-antibody-lysate mixture for one hour at 4°C with gentle agitation
36. Place sample on magnet for 1 minute
37. Wash beads containing immunoprecipitate three times with 450 microliters of cold wash buffer on ice
38. Resuspend bead pellet in 20 microliters of 2X SDS beta-mercaptoethanol protein gel loading buffer
39. Boil sample at 95 degrees Celsius for 5 minutes
40. Load denatured protein sample onto SDS-PAGE gel without transferring beads
41. Perform Western blot analysis according to standard protocols using appropriate antibodies for proteins of interest
42. Detect protein bands using horseradish peroxidase-based chemiluminescence

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Mount each acrylic chamber on rack inside water bath
2. Position rack so lower portion of chambers is submerged in water bath
3. Install support at back end of chambers to maintain horizontal position and ensure even thermal contact with water bath
4. Connect oxygen tank to vaporizer and flow meter using polyethylene tubing
5. Split tubing into 25 small-diameter resistors of equal length
6. Connect vacuum lines to exit ports of chambers opposite gas inlets
7. Combine vacuum lines at manifold connected to in-house suction line
8. Install pop-off valve along main vacuum line to maintain atmospheric pressure
9. Fill water bath to fully contact bottom of all chambers
10. Turn on water circulation pump
11. Maintain water bath temperature at 37 degrees Celsius
12. Set oxygen flow rate to five liters per minute
13. Submerge each chamber underwater to inspect for bubbles or water entry indicating leaks
14. Replace any leaking chambers
15. Connect 500 milliliter per minute flow meter inline before each chamber
16. Verify balanced flows across each line using flow meters
17. Check tubing for obstructions in chambers not receiving expected flow
18. Calibrate agent analyzer to read 0.0% isoflurane under 100% oxygen flow
19. Sterilize dorsal neck area with Betadine
20. Inject temperature transponders subcutaneously between shoulder blades using sterile needle
21. Monitor injection site daily for infection and transponder migration
22. Place one animal into each chamber
23. Set 100% oxygen flow to the chambers for two-hour exposure
24. Place each animal into individual chambers with 100% oxygen flowing
25. Set isoflurane concentration to 0.4% for 15 minutes
26. Assess each animal's writing reflex by gently rolling chamber until mouse is placed on its back
27. Record state of writing reflex for each mouse within two minutes
28. Scan each mouse for temperature data
29. Record writing reflex state for each mouse
30. Scan each mouse for temperature data
31. Increase isoflurane concentration by approximately 0.05%
32. Maintain concentration for 15 minutes
33. Assess writing reflex as demonstrated earlier
34. Continue increasing anesthetic concentration until all animals lose writing reflex
35. Optionally measure anesthetic emergence by repeating stepwise writing reflex assessment during downward titration
36. Place each animal into individual chambers with 100% oxygen flowing
37. Set isoflurane concentration to 1.2%
38. Gently roll each chamber until mouse remains on its back
39. Confirm loss of righting reflex
40. Maintain isoflurane at 1.2% for intervention duration (30 min to 1 hour)
41. Turn off isoflurane and maintain 100% oxygen flow
42. Measure time until animal places all four paws on floor

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Grow Lactobacillus reuteri in malic enzyme induction medium without cystine at 37°C overnight without shaking
2. Centrifuge 1 milliliter of overnight culture in 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube to harvest cells (target yield: 50-100 μg cellular protein)
3. Completely remove supernatant from cell pellet
4. Add 250 microliters GITC lysis buffer to pellet and resuspend by vortexing
5. Incubate at 95°C for 10 minutes to lyse cells
6. Store lysate at -80°C
7. Prepare BSA standards containing 0, 0.1, 0.2, and 0.4 mg/mL BSA in GITC lysis buffer
8. Aliquot 5 microliters of thawed, well-mixed cell lysates to wells in a clear 96-well plate
9. Aliquot 5 microliters of BSA standards to separate wells in the clear 96-well plate
10. Add 195 microliters of Bradford reagent to each well
11. Measure absorbance at 595 nanometers using a plate reader
12. Calculate protein amount in each well by comparison to BSA standard curve
13. Multiply calculated protein value by 0.05 to determine total protein per sample
14. Add 250 microliters of 95% ethanol to each GITC lysed sample
15. Vortex samples to mix
16. Pipette mixture to silica membrane spin column
17. Place spin column in 1.5 milliliter centrifuge tube
18. Centrifuge at 16,100 g for 30 seconds
19. Discard flow-through
20. Add 750 microliters of tris-hydrochloride, sodium chloride, EDTA, and ethanol mixture
21. Centrifuge at 16,100 g for 30 seconds
22. Discard flow-through
23. Centrifuge spin column at 16,100 g for two minutes
24. Add 150 microliters of 50 mM pH 8 tris-HCl
25. Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes
26. Elute polyP by centrifuging at 8,000 g for 2 minutes
27. Prepare phosphate standards (0, 5, 50, or 200 μM potassium phosphate) in 50 mM pH 8 tris-HCl
28. Aliquot 100 microliters of phosphate standards and 100 microliters of extracted polyP samples into separate wells of a clear 96-well plate
29. Prepare master mix per sample: 30 microliters of 5X ScPPX reaction buffer, 19 microliters water, and 1 microliter purified ScPPX
30. Add 50 microliters of master mix to each well of the 96-well plate
31. Incubate for 15 minutes at 37°C
32. Mix 9.12 milliliters of detection solution base with 0.88 milliliters of 1M ascorbic acid
33. Allow detection solution to reach room temperature
34. Add 50 microliters of detection solution to each sample and standard in 96-well plate
35. Incubate plate at room temperature for approximately two minutes for color development
36. Measure absorbance at 882 nanometers using plate reader
37. Calculate phosphate concentration using potassium phosphate standard curve

2. Establish quiescent cultures by allowing plates to reach confluence with regular medium changes

5. Add ActD at concentration of 15 micrograms per milliliter of medium
6. Mix solution well
7. Aspirate old medium
8. Add medium containing ActD to cells
9. Aspirate off ActD medium
10. Pipette 5-10 milliliters of prewarmed PBS onto 100-millimeter plate (with volume adjustments for smaller plates)
11. Gently aspirate PBS
12. Add phenol-guanidine isothiocyanate solution to tissue culture plate
13. Pipette phenol-guanidine isothiocyanate-cell mixture up and down several times
14. Incubate samples at room temperature for five minutes
15. Introduce spike-in controlled transcripts into each sample at known quantity and concentration
16. Transfer phenylguanidine isothiocyanate solution mixture to 2.0-milliliter microcentrifuge tube using pipette
17. Add 0.2 milliliters chloroform per 1 milliliter of phenylguanidine isothiocyanate solution to mixture
18. Shake microcentrifuge tube vigorously for 15 seconds
19. Incubate chloroform phenylguanidine isothiocyanate mixture

22. Avoid disturbing interphase during aqueous layer transfer

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Ethically euthanize mouse according to institution-approved methods
2. Place euthanized mouse on sterile underpad
3. Press 5/45-style tweezer on orbital area to nucleate eye and induce proptosis
4. Position tweezer at posterior of eye
5. Gently pull tweezer to remove eye
6. Verify optic nerve remains intact after eye removal
7. Submerge eyes in 2-milliliter microcentrifuge tube containing 5% povidone iodine using forceps
8. Incubate eyes in povidone iodine at room temperature for 2 to 3 minutes
9. Remove eyes from povidone iodine and place in Petri dish
10. Wash eyes with Hanks' Balanced Saline Solution using sterile transfer pipette until orange color disappears (approximately 2-3 washes)
11. Use tweezers and/or Vannas scissors to pull or cut away connective tissue in extraocular muscles under microscope
12. Optionally hold cleaned eyes in cold RPE cell culture media for up to one hour
13. Transfer eyes to 2-milliliter microcentrifuge tube containing enzyme A solution
14. Incubate eyes in incubator at 37°C for 40 minutes
15. Remove eyes from enzyme A solution
16. Transfer eyes to new 2-milliliter microcentrifuge tube containing enzyme B solution
17. Place tube in incubator at 37 degrees Celsius for 50 minutes
18. Remove eyes from enzyme B
19. Place eyes in 60-millimeter Petri dish
20. Submerge eyes in complete RPE cell growth media
21. Incubate eyes at 4 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
22. Place dish under dissecting microscope
23. Use M5S forceps and syringe needle to make incision in ora seratta section
24. Grasp inner part of incision with one forcep
25. Grasp cornea with second forcep
26. Slowly tear cornea from retina by pulling in small increments while moving down the tear
27. Remove detached iris and lens with forceps
28. Grasp outer layer of eyecup with one tweezer
29. Position arm of second tweezer between RPE and neural layers
30. Gently pull/scoop neural retina away from RPE layer and discard
31. Move RPE layer to debris-free area of dish
32. Gently scrape inside of eyecup with 5/45-style tweezer to separate RPE from choroid and sclera
33. Aspirate cells using a 3.2-milliliter sterile transfer pipette
34. Transfer aspirated cells to a new 15-milliliter centrifuge tube containing complete RPE cell growth media
35. Centrifuge cells at 300g for 10 minutes at room temperature
36. Aspirate out the supernatant
37. Resuspend pellet in warmed RPE cell growth media
38. Plate cells on 100-millimeter Petri dish
39. Incubate at 37 degrees Celsius and 5% CO2

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Transfer individual Stentor cells into well of glass spot plate containing at least 100 microliters of commercially available pasteurized spring water using one milliliter pipette
2. Transfer 1 milliliter aliquot from Chlamydomonas culture into 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube
3. Centrifuge sample at 2,095 g for 3 minutes at room temperature
4. Resuspend pellet in 1 milliliter pasteurized spring water for second centrifugation
5. Transfer 500 microliters of conditioned medium per culture to individual wells of a glass spot plate
6. Transfer one Stentor cell into each well containing medium
7. Feed each Stentor culture with five microliters of prepared Chlamydomonas every 48 hours
8. Maintain cultures in a shaded area
9. Count new Stentor cells before each feeding
10. Adjust volume of delivered Chlamydomonas based on cell count
11. Gently pipette culture medium a few times to detach Stentor from meniscus of each well
12. Use one milliliter pipette to carefully aspirate media from wells without removing cells
13. Add 500 microliters of fresh conditioned media to each well
14. Add 20 milliliters of pasteurized spring water to autoclaved sterilized wide mouth glass jar
15. Carefully detach Stentor cells from bottom of each well
16. Collect all Stentor cells with one milliliter pipette
17. Gently transfer collected cells to glass jar
18. Add fresh pasteurized spring water to jar every 48 hours to maintain Stentor density at 20 cells/mL
19. Feed Stentor cells with 200-1000 microliters of prepared Chlamydomonas
20. Transfer entire jar contents to two-cup glass container when culture volume reaches 90% capacity
21. Feed transferred culture with 2 milliliters prepared Chlamydomonas per 100 milliliters of culture
22. Add pasteurized spring water every 4-5 days to maintain Stentor density at 20 cells/mL
23. Inspect cultures weekly
24. Inspect cultures under 5X dissecting microscope for rotifers, fungus, and other growth
25. Remove contaminating microorganisms and abnormally shaped/colorless Stentor cells using one milliliter pipette
26. Assess if glass container is approximately 90% full to initiate splitting
27. Add 25 milliliters of pasteurized spring water to culture
28. Detach Stentor from glass using 50 milliliter pipette
29. Transfer approximately 50% of culture to new two-cup glass container
30. Use needle puller to make needles from capillary tubes
31. Collect one healthy Stentor with trumpet shape, blue-green color, and no large vacuoles in a two microliter droplet
32. Place droplet on slide
33. Add two microliters of 4% methyl cellulose to slow Stentor motion
34. Place droplet under dissecting microscope
35. Hold glass needle parallel to cutting surface to prevent breakage
36. Gently press contracted Stentor with side of needle
37. Split anterior end from posterior end of cell
38. Confirm both fragments have at least one macronuclear node
39. Move fragments into individual wells of glass spot plate
40. Transfer 30-60 Stentor cells in 1 ml culture medium to microcentrifuge tube
41. Collect all cells from tube using 200μl pipette in single 125μl draw
42. Dispense cells into tube containing 500μl of 25% sucrose solution
43. Start stopwatch for sucrose treatment timing
44. Flick spin cells in tube rack for 1 minute
45. Collect all cells in single 200μl draw and hold in pipette tip
46. Hold cells in pipette tip until stopwatch reaches 2 minutes sucrose treatment
47. Eject Stentor cells into microcentrifuge tube with 1 ml pasteurized spring water
48. Perform three flick spin washes
49. Add 100 microliters of pasteurized spring water into one well of a glass spot plate
50. Collect one Stentor cell in four microliters of culture media using a 20 microliter pipette tip
51. Deposit the droplet onto the center of a 22x22 mm coverslip
52. Place four additional droplets around the central droplet
53. Invert coverslip and gently place it over the well containing pasteurized spring water
54. Image regenerating cells under light microscope with appropriate time resolution

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Use laryngoscope to expose vocal cords through mouth of 25-30g female Yorkshire pig
2. Spray vocal cords with two puffs of 2% lidocaine topical solution
3. Intubate with 6.5 millimeter tube
4. Use syringe to inflate tube cuff with 3-5 milliliters of air
5. Ensure tracheal tube is in proper position
6. Apply ointment to animal's eyes
7. Protect eyes with cover patch
8. Disinfect neck and abdominal wall with 1% iodine-based scrub solution
9. Make vertical midline anterior neck incision to sternum using number 23 blade
10. Dissect strap muscles
11. Retract muscles laterally using Kelly tissue scissors and Lahey retractor to expose trachea from first ring to thoracic inlet
12. Expose common carotid artery and internal jugular vein for anastomosis
13. Create 1 cm window in second or third tracheal cartilage using number 11 blade
14. Verify endotracheal tube placement through tracheal defect and confirm continuous aeration
15. Design flap on upper abdomen using surgical marking pen
16. Create 4x3 cm elliptical skin incision through anterior sheath using #23 scalpel
17. Position incision on medial side of flap
18. Hold fascia with Allis clamp
19. Elevate flap from rectus abdominis muscle sheath
20. Locate perforators going to skin flap
21. Perform blunt dissection of exposed muscle tissue
22. Follow perforators to superior epigastric vessels
23. Make lateral skin incision in flap using number 23 scalpel
24. Clamp superior epigastric vessels and venae comitantes with haemostat
25. Cut above haemostat using Kelly tissue scissors
26. Ligate vessel below haemostat with three-oh suture to allow haemostat removal
27. Clamp carotid artery with two haemostats placed 1-2 cm apart
28. Cut between haemostats using microscissors
29. Tie superior part of vessel with double suture to prevent leakage
30. Place first two stay sutures 120 degrees apart around vessel circumference using 10-0 simple full thickness interrupted suture
31. Place 2-3 stitches between stay sutures
32. Release vascular clamp
33. Check anastomosis for leakage
34. Create anastomosis between internal jugular vein and venae comitantes
35. Prick skin flap with 18-gauge needle syringe to test viability
36. Observe for blood droplet appearance at puncture site
37. Close tracheal window using 3-0 suture with muscle fascia of superior epigastric artery perforator flap
38. Exteriorize skin panel of flap
39. Suture flap skin panel to cervical midline skin incision
40. Close abdominal skin incision

Complete step list: Use laryngoscope to expose vocal cords through mouth of 25-30g female Yorkshire pig
2. Spray vocal cords with two puffs of 2% lidocaine topical solution
3. Intubate with 6.5 millimeter tube
4. Use syringe to inflate tube cuff with 3-5 milliliters of air
5. Ensure tracheal tube is in proper position
6. Apply ointment to animal's eyes
7. Protect eyes with cover patch
8. Disinfect neck and abdominal wall with 1% iodine-based scrub solution
9. Make vertical midline anterior neck incision to sternum using number 23 blade
10. Dissect strap muscles
11. Retract muscles laterally using Kelly tissue scissors and Lahey retractor to expose trachea from first ring to thoracic inlet
12. Expose common carotid artery and internal jugular vein for anastomosis
13. Create 1 cm window in second or third tracheal cartilage using number 11 blade
14. Verify endotracheal tube placement through tracheal defect and confirm continuous aeration
15. Design flap on upper abdomen using surgical marking pen
16. Create 4x3 cm elliptical skin incision through anterior sheath using #23 scalpel
17. Position incision on medial side of flap
18. Hold fascia with Allis clamp
19. Elevate flap from rectus abdominis muscle sheath
20. Locate perforators going to skin flap
21. Perform blunt dissection of exposed muscle tissue
22. Follow perforators to superior epigastric vessels
23. Make lateral skin incision in flap using number 23 scalpel
24. Clamp superior epigastric vessels and venae comitantes with haemostat
25. Cut above haemostat using Kelly tissue scissors
26. Ligate vessel below haemostat with three-oh suture to allow haemostat removal
27. Clamp carotid artery with two haemostats placed 1-2 cm apart
28. Cut between haemostats using microscissors
29. Tie superior part of vessel with double suture to prevent leakage
30. Place first two stay sutures 120 degrees apart around vessel circumference using 10-0 simple full thickness interrupted suture
31. Place 2-3 stitches between stay sutures
32. Release vascular clamp
33. Check anastomosis for leakage
34. Create anastomosis between internal jugular vein and venae comitantes
35. Prick skin flap with 18-gauge needle syringe to test viability
36. Observe for blood droplet appearance at puncture site
37. Close tracheal window using 3-0 suture with muscle fascia of superior epigastric artery perforator flap
38. Exteriorize skin panel of flap
39. Suture flap skin panel to cervical midline skin incision
40. Close abdominal skin incision

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Harvest spleens and lymph nodes from donor mice
2. Purify CD4-positive T cells from harvested tissues
3. Adoptively transfer purified donor T cells into NOD/SCID recipient mice
4. Monitor recipient mice for type one diabetes development
5. Soak donor mouse fur with 70% ethanol
6. Cut skin and retract to expose front limb muscles
7. Identify axillary lymph nodes near front limb base
8. Grasp lymph node with small tweezers
9. Remove lymph node by trimming connective tissue
10. Make 1-inch peritoneal incision with small scissors
11. Grasp spleen at center
12. Trim connective tissue and attached fat from spleen
13. Remove spleen
14. Place spleen and lymph nodes in 10 ml DMEM on ice
15. Press lymphoid organs through 70 micrometer cell strainer using end of sterile 10 milliliter syringe plunger
16. Rinse strainer with 1 milliliter of DMEM several times
17. Transfer collected cells into 15 milliliter conical tube
18. Centrifuge cells at 400 G for 7 minutes at room temperature
19. Resuspend cell pellet in 3 milliliters of ACK buffer
20. Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes to lyse red blood cells
21. Add 12 milliliters of DMEM
22. Pellet cells by centrifugation
23. Wash cell pellet once in 10 milliliters of DMEM
24. Suspend cells in 5 milliliters of FACS staining buffer
25. Count viable cells using phase contrast microscope and hemocytometer
26. Remove approximately 1×10⁶ cells for staining control
27. Stain cells with mouse monoclonal antibodies for sorting nonactivated transgenic CD4-positive T-cells
28. Incubate samples at 4°C in the dark for 30 minutes
29. Wash cells with 3x staining volume of fax buffer
30. Resuspend cells in fax buffer at 1-2×10⁷ cells/ml for sorting sample
31. Resuspend single stain controls in 300 microliters of fax buffer
32. Pass cell sample through 35 micrometer cell strainer cap tube
33. Collect sorted cells into 3 milliliters of DMEM
34. Record absolute number of sorted cells
35. Centrifuge cells at 400 Gs for 7 minutes
36. Resuspend cell pellets in sterile phosphate buffered saline for adoptive T-cell transfer
37. Prepare 1 mL syringe with 18 gauge needle
38. Gently resuspend FACS-purified CD4-positive T-cells using prepared syringe
39. Load cell suspension into 1 mL syringe
40. Attach 27.5-gauge needle to syringe for injection
41. Restrain NOD-SCID recipient mice
42. Wipe mouse tail with 70% ethanol to disinfect injection site
43. Inject 1×10⁶ purified CD4-positive T-cells per mouse into lateral tail vein
44. Verify proper intravenous placement by monitoring plunger resistance during injection
45. Measure urine glucose using reagent strips according to manufacturer instructions

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Autoclave 15 grams of agar in 900 to 1000 milliliters of water
2. Mix autoclaved agar with 166 grams of wheat germ diet in laboratory blender
3. Pour blended insect diet into dish
4. Allow diet to cool in dish
5. Transfer cooled diet to aluminum foil, wrap tightly, and store at 4°C
6. Place Manduca sexta eggs in glass filter holder with 90mm filter paper in vacuum flask apparatus
7. Pour 250 milliliters of 0.6% bleach solution onto eggs for 2-3 minutes while stirring occasionally
8. Turn on vacuum to drain bleach solution
9. Wash sterilized eggs 3-4 times with 250 ml sterile distilled water per wash
10. Place fresh 90 mm filter paper into Petri plate lid
11. Transfer eggs to filter paper for drying in flow hood until no longer adhesive
12. Place recently prepared insect diet on secured rubber stoppers in plastic containers
13. Transfer approximately 40 eggs into each container
14. Separate eggs from diet to prevent moisture-induced fungal contamination
15. Maintain eggs at 26°C with 16-hour light/8-hour dark photoperiod
16. Pick up each larva by the long black horn using forceps
17. Transfer approximately 25 larvae to new containers with diet at the bottom
18. Incubate larvae for two days under same conditions as eggs
19. Transfer larvae to individual containers with small pieces of food
20. Incubate larvae again
21. Replace food and clean feces from containers every other day
22. Centrifuge 500 microliters of bacterial strains at 17,000 G for 2 minutes at room temperature
23. Wash pellets in 1 milliliter of sterile PBS under same centrifugation conditions
24. Resuspend cells in 0.5 milliliter of sterile PBS
25. Measure optical density of suspensions
26. Prepare six serial tenfold dilutions of bacterial strain in sterile PBS using fresh pipette tips in 96-well microtiter plate
27. Spot 10 microliters of dilutions (10⁻² to 10⁻⁶) along top of Petri plate containing growth medium
28. Tilt plate to spread spots toward center
29. Place insect containers on ice for ~5 minutes after removing diet and feces
30. Rinse syringe needle three times in each of two tubes of 70-100% ethanol followed by one tube of sterile water
31. Quantitate diluted cell suspensions using hemocytometer under microscope
32. Draw 10 microliters from appropriate microtiter well
33. Swab insect injection surface with 95% ethanol
34. Inject 10 microliters of cell suspension into insect at <45° angle behind abdominal proleg
35. Plate 10⁻² to 10⁻⁶ dilutions on middle of agar plate
36. Let samples flow toward bottom of plate
37. Incubate plates at appropriate temperature
38. Count colonies to enumerate inoculum
39. Inject three to five insects with sterile PBS for negative control group
40. Incubate all insects at 26°C with 16-hour light/8-hour dark photoperiod
41. Monitor insect survival over time after injections
42. Inject insects with approximately 50 CFU of wild type or attenuated mutant strain of Xer Haus NLA grown to mid-log phase
43. Observe injected insects for approximately 72 hours
44. Record percentage of injected insects alive at each time point

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Grind defatted cotton seed meal through a 0.5 millimeter steel screen using a cyclone sample mill
2. Retrieve the ground working meal from the grinder's outlet
3. Measure 25 grams of working meal
4. Place measured working meal in blender jar
5. Add 200 milliliters of water to meal in blender
6. Mix water and meal in blender for 3 minutes
7. Centrifuge the resulting mixture
8. Freeze-dry the centrifuged mixture to isolate water-insoluble fraction
9. Place water-washed meal in a vessel
10. Add 25 grams of deionized water per 3 grams of meal to vessel
11. Add stir bar to vessel
12. Seal beaker with paraform
13. Stir mixture for two hours
14. Take prepared slurry for immediate use in bonding specimens
15. Cut wood veneer strips to dimensions: 25.4 mm wide × 88.9 mm long × 1.59 mm thick with length parallel to wood grain
16. Mark line across wood grain 25.4 mm from one end of each strip using pencil
17. Label wood veneer strips for identification
18. Unseal adhesive slurry
19. Unseal adhesive slurry container
20. Apply first layer of adhesive slurry to marked regions of wood veneer strips using brush
21. Air dry adhesive-coated strips for 10-15 minutes until tacky
22. Apply second layer of adhesive slurry over first layer using brush
23. Air dry strips again after second adhesive application
24. Overlap adhesive-coated regions of two specimen strips
25. Place overlapped specimens in press
26. Hot press specimens at specified conditions for 20 minutes
27. Recover specimens from press and transfer to incubator
28. Condition specimens in incubator at 22-23°C and 50-60% RH for 48 hours
29. Put room temperature water in a glass jar
30. Get conditioned specimens
31. Immerse specimens in water for 48 hours
32. Fill water bath with water at 63 degrees Celsius
33. Retrieve conditioned specimens
34. Immerse specimens in 63°C water bath for 4 hours
35. Recover specimens from water bath
36. Dry specimens at 22°C and 50-60% relative humidity for 18-20 hours
37. Immerse specimens in another 63°C water bath for 4 hours
38. Dry specimens for 48 hours before testing
39. Set gripping pressure to 7 MPa
40. Set crosshead speed to 1 mm/min
41. Measure and record shear strength at break for each specimen
42. Average multiple measurements for each test variable
43. Secure bonded wood specimen (30 mm by 25 mm) into grips with width wider than specimen and comparable length

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Mix NK cell isolation cocktail solution with 6-10 milliliters of EDTA peripheral whole blood by several inversions
2. Homogenize sample on tube rotator for 5 minutes at room temperature
3. Place tube into magnetic separator for 15 minutes under sterile conditions with tube label facing magnet back
4. Transfer supernatant into new 15 milliliter tube
5. Bring final volume to 15 milliliters with complete medium
6. Wash cells by centrifugation
7. Resuspend pellet in 1 milliliter of fresh complete medium
8. Count cells
9. Adjust volume to concentration of at least 2×10⁴ NK cells per 100 microliters in complete medium
10. Gently mix isolated NK cells by pipetting at least five times
11. Aliquot 100 microliters of NK cells into appropriate wells of 96-well V-bottomed plate
12. Switch off light
13. Add anti-CD107a antibody to each well of NK cells
14. Carefully mix aliquot of K562 tumor cells (2×10⁴ cells/100μL) by pipetting at least five times
15. Transfer 100 microliters of tumor cells to each stimulation well
16. Add IL-2 and IL-15 to stimulation wells
17. Mix all wells by pipetting at least five times
18. Incubate plate protected from light for 3 hours at 37°C and 5% CO₂
19. Add freshly prepared protein transport inhibitor solution to each well with careful mixing
20. Return plate to incubator
21. Mix co-cultures carefully
22. Transfer mixed co-cultures to corresponding flow cytometry tubes
23. Add 1 milliliter of wash buffer to each tube
24. Resuspend pellets in 99 microliters of PBS and 1 microliter of fixable dead cell marker per sample
25. Vortex cells
26. Incubate for 15 minutes at room temperature in the dark
27. Wash cells in 1 mL wash buffer
28. Resuspend pellets in 84 μL wash buffer plus 16 μL cell surface antibody cocktail
29. Vortex samples
30. Incubate for 20 minutes in the dark at 4°C
31. Wash cells in 1 ml wash buffer
32. Resuspend pellets
33. Resuspend in 100 μl cold fixation solution
34. Mix by vortexing
35. Incubate for 10 minutes at 4°C in dark
36. Wash cells in 1 ml wash buffer
37. Resuspend pellets in 90 μl permeabilization buffer
38. Stain with 10 μl intracellular antibodies and mix by vortexing
39. Incubate for 30 minutes in dark at 4°C
40. Wash cells twice in 1 ml fresh permeabilization buffer
41. Resuspend samples in 400 μl fresh permeabilization buffer
42. Mix by vortexing and place cells on ice for flow cytometry analysis

Complete step list: Weigh 5-8 grams of air-dried sieved soil in 50 mL conical centrifuge tube
2. Record soil mass with four significant figures

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Culture B16-F10 GFP melanoma cells
2. Centrifuge cells and transfer to 1.5 mL micro tube
3. Centrifuge cells again in micro tube
4. Remove supernatant except thin layer above pellet
5. Resuspend cells into thick slurry
6. Remove tip cap from 33-gauge needle on Hamilton syringe
7. Load 20 μL slurry on top of tip chamber
8. Pull back plunger to load 5 μL slurry into syringe
9. Shave the mouse head
10. Remove the hairs on the head and around the ear
11. Rinse with water
12. Fix the proximal edge of the ear on the tip of an index finger using adhesive tape
13. Insert the Hamilton syringe needle between the dorsal dermis and the cartilage of the ear, penetrating the ear from proximal to distal for approximately two to three millimeters
14. Inject the cell slurry
15. Slowly retract the needle from the skin
16. Anesthetize mouse using humidified oxygen and isofluorane mixture
17. Place mouse on 37°C heating pad
18. Perform gentle toe pinch to confirm anesthesia depth
19. Apply ophthalmic ointment to eyes
20. Maintain mouse on rectal thermistor-controlled heating pad throughout procedure
21. Place tumor-bearing ear on stack of glass slides
22. Fixate anterior and posterior ear edges to glass slide stack using adhesive tape strips
23. Cut ventral skin of mouse pinna along anti helix using scalpel
24. Remove adhesive tapes from ear using curved tweezers
25. Peel ventral dermis and cartilage from dorsal dermis
26. Wash ear with Ringer's buffer
27. Apply primary antibodies targeting extracellular matrix molecules in blocking buffer to exposed ear
28. Place cover slip on top of sample
29. Incubate sample for 15 minutes
30. Wash ear twice using approximately five milliliters of Ringer's buffer per wash
31. Apply secondary antibodies or streptavidin in blocking buffer
32. Apply cover slip
33. Incubate for 15 minutes
34. Wash twice
35. Fold open dermal area into the EM mencia conki
36. Dry outer unopened ear dermis with sterile wipes
37. Place ear on stack of glass slides
38. Apply 0.5 microliters of surgical glue to anterior and posterior dorsal edges to immobilize ear
39. Add freshly prepared sterile ascorbate ringers buffer on immobilized ear
40. Apply cover slip
41. Prepare fluorescent stereo microscope with two lens for imaging
42. Use fluorescent stereo microscope with two lens to image ear
43. Place outlet of needle attached to sorbate ringers reservoir under coverslip 0.5 centimeters away from ear
44. Set peristaltic pump to 1 microliter per minute speed
45. Activate peristaltic pump to deliver buffer to chamber
46. Open acquisition software
47. Adjust gain fluorescence intensity, magnification, and exposure time
48. Image multiple fields containing tumor cells and stained extracellular matrix proteins

Complete step list: Add 140 microliters of biological sample to 560 microliters of lysis solution under fume hood
2. Incubate mixture at room temperature for 10 minutes
3. Add 560 microliters of ethanol to sample
4. Apply 630 microliters of total solution to silica column
5. Centrifuge silica column
6. Apply remaining 630 microliters to silica column
7. Centrifuge silica column again
8. Wash column with 500 microliters of AW1 buffer
9. Wash column with 500 microliters of AW2 buffer
10. Add 50 microliters of room temperature AVE buffer to column
11. Close cap and incubate at room temperature for 1 minute
12. Centrifuge at 6,000 g for 1 minute
13. Prepare 22.5 microliters reaction mix per reaction by adding: 12.5 microliters PCR master mix, 20 micromolar forward primer, 20 micromolar reverse primer, 10 micromolar probe, and ultra-pure water to final volume
14. Aliquot 22.5 microliters of appropriate reaction mix to each well of 96-well plate
15. Add 2.5 microliters of sample, extraction negative control, PCR negative control, or positive control sample to corresponding reaction wells
16. Apply adhesive plate seal to cover plate
17. Centrifuge plate at 6,000 g for 10 seconds
18. Place plate in real-time PCR system
19. Select assay template and PCR program settings, then start run
20. Transfer raw data from real-time PCR system to spreadsheet
21. Set amplification threshold above baseline within exponential growth region
22. Plot standard points to generate standard curve for linearity
23. Calculate copy numbers for samples based on standard curve
24. Dispense 90 microliters of ultra-pure water into six tubes
25. Transfer 10 microliters from plasmid working dilution to first water tube
26. Vortex and centrifuge first dilution tube
27. Transfer 10 microliters from first dilution tube to second water tube
28. Vortex and centrifuge second dilution tube
29. Transfer 10 microliters to third water tube
30. Vortex and centrifuge third dilution tube
31. Repeat transfer process for fourth water tube
32. Repeat transfer process for fifth and sixth water tubes
33. Dispense 25 microliters of ultra-pure water into six tubes
34. Transfer 25 microliters from the last positive tenfold plasmid dilution tube into the first water-containing tube
35. Vortex the first dilution tube
36. Centrifuge the first dilution tube
37. Calculate number of positive replicates out of 24 replicates for each plasmid concentration level
38. Determine LOD with 95% confidence (LOD95% PCR) as the concentration level yielding 23 positive replicates out of 24

2. Aspirate media from culture plates while avoiding contact with culture
3. Trypsinize submerged BME2 organoid culture with recombinant enzyme
4. Add two milliliters of recombinant enzyme per well in six-well plate
5. Mechanically break BME2 by repeated pipetting
6. Incubate plates at 37°C in cell culture incubator for five minutes
7. Transfer suspension to 15 milliliter centrifuge tube
8. Centrifuge at 600 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
9. Aspirate recombinant enzyme supernatant carefully without disturbing organoid pellet
10. Resuspend organoid pellet in growth medium
11. Add DNase 1 to suspension
12. Incubate suspension for 5 minutes at room temperature
13. Centrifuge at 600 × g for 3 minutes at room temperature
14. Discard media carefully without disturbing organoid pellet
15. Resuspend pellet in fresh growth medium
16. Preheat new black clear bottom 96-well plate at 37°C in cell culture incubator for 10 minutes
17. Prepare cell suspension in PBS
18. Count cells using cell analyzer
19. Aliquot cells from single-cell suspension
20. Pellet cells by centrifugation
21. Discard media
22. Keep cells on ice for approximately one minute
23. Resuspend cells in BME2

25. Seed five microliters of cell suspension per well
26. Seed cell domes at six o'clock position of well

28. Seed cell domes in remaining inner wells
29. Incubate freshly seeded cell domes in laminar flow hood for five minutes without moving plate

31. Incubate plate at 37 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes
32. Add 100 microliters of growth medium per well to wells containing organoids
33. Add 100 microliters of PBS to outer wells at rim of plate
34. Prepare serial drug dilution of osimertinib in low growth factor media
35. Prepare negative control with low growth factor media and 0.1% DMSO
36. Rotate organoid plate clockwise by 180 degrees to reposition organoids
37. Aspirate growth media using multi-channel apparatus
38. Add 100 microliters of control or drug solution per well
39. Incubate treated organoids at 37 degrees Celsius in cell culture incubator for five days
40. Thaw reagent overnight at 4°C
41. Equilibrate reagent in room temperature water bath for 30 minutes
42. Mix reagent by inverting
43. Add 100 microliters of reagent to each well
44. Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down with tip positioned at organoid dome
45. Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature in dark
46. Transfer approximately 150 microliters of lysate to new white opaque 96-well plate using multi-channel pipette
47. Incubate plate for 25 minutes at room temperature in dark
48. Record luminescence using ELISA plate reader
49. Save data in appropriate table format with raw reads, plate layout, and drug recordings

Complete step list: Plate single-cell suspension of Platinum Eco-packaging cells in 10 cm petri dish
2. Add 15 ml DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS to plated cells
3. Cultivate cells until 50-60% confluent
4. Add 20 μg retroviral construct and 10 μg PCL eco-packaging vector to 1 ml serum-free DMEM
5. Add 75 μl PEI to 1 ml serum/antibiotic-free DMEM in separate tube
6. Incubate PEI mixture for 5 minutes at room temperature
7. Combine contents of both DNA and PEI tubes
8. Incubate combined mixture for 10 minutes at room temperature
9. Replace medium on Platinum Eco cells with 8 ml of fresh 37°C DMEM supplemented with 2% FBS
10. Add transfection mixture dropwise to plate
11. Incubate plate at 37°C for 4 hours
12. Replace supernatant with 10 ml of 37°C DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS
13. Incubate at 37°C for 24 hours
14. Transfer ecotropic retroviral particle-containing supernatant to 15 ml centrifuge tube using 5 ml pipette
15. Remove debris and cells by centrifugation
16. Add 10 ml of 37°C DMEM plus 10% FBS to culture dish
17. Incubate culture dish at 37°C for 24 hours
18. Collect second round of viral particles using previously demonstrated method
19. Use tweezers and scissors in tissue culture hood
20. Remove skin and part of muscles from hind limbs
21. Carefully dislodge acetabulum from hip joint without breaking femur
22. Cut paws at ankle joints
23. Spray bones with 70% ethanol
24. Remove remaining muscle with paper towel
25. Remove residual muscle tissue using a paper towel
26. Place bones in 5 cm petri dish containing PBS supplemented with 2% FBS
27. Bend knee joint of each bone set and separate bones using scissors
28. Remove 1-2 mm of each epiphysis using scissors
29. Flush bone marrow from both ends using 2-5 ml syringe with 30-gauge needle loaded with PBS plus FBS into 15 ml centrifuge tube
30. Pellet bone cells by centrifugation
31. Re-suspend pellet in 2.5 ml red blood cell lysis buffer for 2-3 minutes at room temperature
32. Filter lysed cells through 100 micrometer cell strainer into new 15 milliliter centrifuge tube
33. Restore tonicity with 12 milliliters of PBS plus FBS
34. Re-suspend pellet in DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and antibiotics
35. Plate 5-10 × 10⁶ bone marrow cells in 10-centimeter non-tissue culture treated petri dish with 10 milliliters DMEM supplemented with serum and macrophage colony-stimulating factor (MCSF)
36. Tilt cell culture dish
37. Remove medium leaving approximately 5 milliliters near dish edge
38. Add 20 milliliters of fresh 37°C medium supplemented with FBS and MCSF
39. Return culture dish to cell culture incubator
40. Remove all medium and floating cells
41. Wash culture with 37°C PBS
42. Add 5 milliliters of 0.02% EDTA in PBS
43. Incubate with EDTA for 3-5 minutes at 37°C
44. Gently flush dissociated cells from plate bottom using 5 milliliter pipette
45. Transfer cells to 50 milliliter centrifuge tube containing 25 milliliters fresh PBS
46. Dilute bone marrow cells to 2-5 × 10⁶ cells/mL in DMEM with FBS and antibiotics
47. Plate 1 mL of diluted cell suspension per well supplemented with differentiation cytokine
48. Add 2 mL of collected virus supplemented with polybrene to plated cells
49. Transduce cell cultures by centrifugation
50. Incubate transduced cultures for 4 hours in cell culture incubator
51. Transfer nonadherent cells from each well into individual 15 milliliter centrifuge tubes

question:
Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Add 20 microliters of freshly prepared POPC solution and 4 microliters of freshly prepared biotin-PEG-DSPE solution to a glass vial
2. Evaporate organic solvent by air flow until lipid film forms
3. Place lipid film in desiccator for one hour to completely evaporate organic solvent
4. Add 200 microliters of mineral oil to the vial
5. Cover vial opening with plastic paraffin film
6. Sonicate vial contents in ultrasonic bath at 120 watts for at least one hour
7. Create a lipid film
8. Add 200 microliters of 200 millimolar glucose in LB medium to the lipid film
9. Sonicate the film at 120 watts for at least one hour
10. Extrude resulting giant vesicles into small vesicles using a mini extruder and polycarbonate membrane with 100-nanometer pore size
11. Inoculate E. coli from LB plate into LB medium
12. Incubate culture overnight at 37°C
13. Transfer 20 microliters of culture supernatant to 1.98 milliliters of fresh LB medium
14. Incubate sub-culture for two hours
15. Measure optical density at 600 nm (OD600) using spectrophotometer
16. Mix pre-culture solution with sucrose solution and fresh LB medium according to table one
17. Add 50 microliters of freshly prepared outer aqueous solution to a 1.5 milliliter lidded plastic tube
18. Layer 150 microliters of lipid-containing oil solution over the outer aqueous solution
19. Incubate solution for 10 to 15 minutes at room temperature
20. Verify oil-aqueous solution interface is flat
21. Add 2 microliters of freshly prepared inner aqueous solution of giant vesicles to 50 microliters of sonicated lipid-containing oil solution in 0.6 milliliter lidded plastic tube
22. Tap tube to emulsify components
23. Use pipette to add 50 microliters of water and oil droplet solution to oil-aqueous solution interface
24. Sediment bacterial cell-containing giant vesicles by centrifugation
25. Aspirate top oil layer
26. Collect giant vesicles
27. Drill a seven-millimeter hole with hollow punch on 10x10x1 mm double-faced seal
28. Paste double-faced seal onto 30x40 mm cover glass
29. Add 30 microliters of small vesicle solution to chamber hole
30. Incubate vesicles at room temperature for 30 minutes
31. Gently wash chamber hole twice with 20 microliters LB medium supplemented with 200 mM glucose
32. Introduce 10 microliters of neutravidin in outer aqueous giant vesicle solution to the hole
33. Incubate for 15 minutes at room temperature
34. Gently wash the hole two times with 20 microliters of LB medium supplemented with 200 mM glucose
35. Add entire volume of giant vesicle solution to the hole in the chamber
36. Seal the hole with 18 × 18 millimeter cover glass
37. Select 40X objective on inverted microscope
38. Place chamber on microscope heating stage system
39. Incubate bacterial cell-containing giant vesicles in chamber under static conditions at 37°C for six hours
40. Capture and record images of bacterial cell growth every 30 minutes using scientific complementary metal oxide semiconductor camera
41. Aspirate oil carefully before giant vesicle correction

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Warm S2 media in a 28°C water bath for 30 minutes
2. Place transfection reagents, plasmid, and oligonucleotides on ice
3. Aspirate old media from cells using sterile technique in tissue culture hood without disturbing cells
4. Add equivalent volume of fresh warmed media and rinse cells off flask bottom using pipette
5. Adjust cell concentration to 1×10⁶ cells/mL
6. Add 1 mL of adjusted mosquito cells to each well of 6-well plate
7. Transfer plasmid DNA to sterile microcentrifuge tubes
8. Add 99 microliters of EC buffer per microgram of plasmid DNA and LNA ON
9. Add 8 microliters of enhancer per microgram of DNA
10. Vortex DNA-buffer-enhancer mixture for 1 second
11. Briefly centrifuge solution
12. Incubate samples at room temperature for 5 minutes
13. Add 10 microliters of transfection reagent per microgram of plasmid DNA
14. Vortex mixture for 10 seconds
15. Briefly centrifuge solution to bottom of tubes
16. Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes
17. Bring final volume of DNA-buffer-enhancer mixture to 1000 microliters by slowly adding fresh warmed media
18. Gently mix mixture by pipetting up and down 3-4 times
19. Slowly add 1 milliliter of DNA-enhancer mixture to each cell well one drop at a time
20. Gently swirl plate to ensure uniform distribution of cells and transfection mixture
21. Incubate cells under normal growth conditions
22. Gently aspirate media from cells
23. Slowly replace aspirated media with equivalent volume of fresh warmed media
24. Examine positive control cells transfected with GFP-expressing plasmid under fluorescent microscope
25. Aspirate old media from cells requiring thinning
26. Slowly add equivalent volume of fresh warmed media to thinned cells
27. Detach cells from well bottom by pipetting up and down
28. Discard half volume of cell mixture
29. Add 1 mL fresh warmed media to adjust final volume to 2 mL
30. Aspirate old media from cells
31. Add 1 milliliter of sterile room-temperature PBS to each well
32. Detach cells from well bottom by pipetting PBS up and down
33. Transfer cell mixture to 5 milliliter snap cap tubes
34. Immediately analyze cells via flow cytometry
35. Transfect MS Q4 three and gambier UA five B cells with positive control GFP expressing plasmid
36. Transfect SUA five B cells with: GFP expressing plasmid (positive control), BFP expressing plasmid (negative control), and BFP expressing plasmid with increasing concentrations of BFP-specific LNAO ns

Given the complete step list of the experiment, please predict the next step that will take place after experimental steps shown in this video.
Complete step list: Prepare all stock solutions as specified in text protocol
2. Grow HL-1 cells in T25 flasks
3. Replace medium with 5 ml fresh supplemented Claycomb medium daily
4. Place round 20 mm cover slips into wells of 12-well culture plate
5. Add 1 ml gelatin-fibronectin solution to each well
6. Incubate plate overnight at 37°C
7. Aspirate gelatin-fibronectin solution from wells
8. Incubate plate overnight at 37 degrees Celsius
9. Aspirate gelatin fibronectin solution from wells of culture plate
10. Add 1.5 milliliters of supplemented Claycomb medium to each well
11. Aspirate medium from T25 flask containing HL-1 cells
12. Rinse culture briefly with PBS at 37 degrees Celsius
13. Aspirate PBS from flask
14. Add 3 milliliters of trypsin-EDTA solution to bottom of T25 flask
15. Gently lower and rotate flask to cover cells with trypsin-EDTA solution
16. Place flask in incubator for 1 minute at 37 degrees Celsius
17. Aspirate trypsin EDTA solution from culture without disturbing cells
18. Add 1.3 milliliters of trypsin EDTA solution to flask
19. Incubate flask at room temperature for one minute
20. Observe cells under microscope to verify detachment from flask surface
21. Gently tap flask to dislodge remaining attached cells
22. Add 1.3 milliliters of soybean trypsin inhibitor solution to cells
23. Transfer cell suspension to 15 milliliter centrifuge tube
24. Rinse empty flask with five milliliters of solution
25. Rinse empty flask with 5 milliliters of wash medium
26. Add rinse medium to cells in 15 milliliter centrifuge tube
27. Centrifuge tube at 500 × g for 5 minutes
28. Aspirate supernatant without disturbing cell pellet
29. Resuspend cell pellet in 6 milliliters of supplemented medium
30. Add 0.5 milliliters of cell suspension to each well of culture plate
31. Incubate plates at 37 degrees Celsius
32. Change medium daily until cultures are confluent
33. Dilute Road Two and RH 237 stock solutions 1:1000 in Hank's salt solution
34. Observe 12-well culture plates under microscope to determine HL-1 cell confluency
35. Gently remove cover slip from well and rinse with Hank's salt solution
36. Place cover slip into 35mm Petri dish containing 3ml Road Two working solution
37. Put Petri dish into 37°C water bath
